Timeline of galactic history

Sign In to Save Save Edit
  • History
  • Purge
  • Talk (58)
   

History redirects here. You may be looking for the profession.
This article is about galactic history. You may be looking for Wookieepedia's timeline of canon media, the Galactic Timeline video series, or the fan-made Time Tales timeline.

I have a bad feeling about this…

This article has multiple issues and is in need of major additions and/or work.

  • This article or section is in need of referencing per Wookieepedia's sourcing guidelines.
  • This article has an excess of redlinks in it. ({{Redlink}})
  • Parts of this article have been identified as no longer being up to date. ({{Update}}) The article needs to be updated with Star Wars: The High Republic, Star Wars Outlaws.

Please help Wookieepedia by editing this article. Once you have fixed an issue, you may remove it from the list of issues. See this article's talk page for more information.

"A long time ago in a galaxy far, far away.…"
―Journal of the Whills[1]

This is a timeline of galactic history—a chronological record of events relevant to the galaxy. This timeline uses dates in terms of years before the Battle of Yavin (BBY) and after the Battle of Yavin (ABY).

This in-universe list is incomplete. You can help Wookieepedia by expanding it.

Contents

  • 1 Timeline of galactic history
    • 1.1 Before the Republic (eons–25,025 BBY)
    • 1.2 The Old Republic (c. 25,025 – c. 1000 BBY)
      • 1.2.1 Dawn of the Jedi (c. 25,025 BBY)
      • 1.2.2 Expansionist Period (25,000 BBY)
      • 1.2.3 Millennia before the Imperial Era
      • 1.2.4 22,000–5000 BBY
      • 1.2.5 Wars of the Old Republic (c. 5000 – c. 1000 BBY)
        • 1.2.5.1 Battles with the Jedi (c. 5000 BBY)
        • 1.2.5.2 c. 5000 – c. 1000 BBY
        • 1.2.5.3 Defeat of the Sith (1032 BBY)
    • 1.3 Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY)
      • 1.3.1 Reformation of the Republic (c. 1000 BBY)
      • 1.3.2 c. 1000 – c. 500 BBY
      • 1.3.3 High Republic Era (c. 500 – 100 BBY)
        • 1.3.3.1 Age of Exploration (c. 500 – 330 BBY)
        • 1.3.3.2 c. 330 BBY – 233 BBY
        • 1.3.3.3 Nihil conflict (232 BBY – 228 BBY)
        • 1.3.3.4 Late High Republic Era (227 BBY – 100 BBY)
      • 1.3.4 c. 100 – 32 BBY
      • 1.3.5 Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY)
        • 1.3.5.1 Prelude to galactic war (32–22 BBY)
        • 1.3.5.2 Clone Wars (22–19 BBY)
          • 1.3.5.2.1 22 BBY
          • 1.3.5.2.2 21 BBY
          • 1.3.5.2.3 20 BBY
          • 1.3.5.2.4 19 BBY
    • 1.4 Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY)
      • 1.4.1 Clone Wars (continued; 19 BBY)
      • 1.4.2 "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY)
        • 1.4.2.1 Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY)
        • 1.4.2.2 Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY)
      • 1.4.3 Battle of Yavin
      • 1.4.4 Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY)
        • 1.4.4.1 Renewed hope (0–2 ABY)
        • 1.4.4.2 Empire ascendant (3 ABY)
        • 1.4.4.3 Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY)
        • 1.4.4.4 Return of the Jedi (4 ABY)
        • 1.4.4.5 Fall of the Empire (4 ABY–5 ABY)
    • 1.5 New Republic Era (5–34 ABY)
      • 1.5.1 Galactic Civil War (continued; 5 ABY)
      • 1.5.2 Peace (5–28 ABY)
      • 1.5.3 Cold War (29–34 ABY)
    • 1.6 First Order-Resistance War (34 – 35 ABY)
    • 1.7 New Jedi Order (35 ABY–)
    • 1.8 Thereafter
  • 2 Behind the scenes
  • 3 Sources
  • 4 Notes and references

Timeline of galactic history[]

Before the Republic (eons–25,025 BBY)[]

  • Sentient species prehistory
    • The universe begins forming.[2]
    • The galaxy is formed around a super-massive black hole.[3]
    • Midi-chlorians are birthed from the Wellspring of Life. They are microscopic symbiotic organisms in all living things that connect the Living Force—the energy of all life—to the Cosmic Force, allowing the will of the all-encompassing energy field known as the Force to communicate with lifeforms.[4]
    • The Father, the Son, and the Daughter exist as powerful Force wielders who come to live in the ethereal realm of Mortis.[5]
    • Billions of years before the Imperial Era, mineral matter that would infuse rock walls on the planet Ilum with valuable constituents, including rare crystals such as the Force-attuned kyber, begin to accumulate.[6]
    • The long lived Exogorth species comes into existance. Over one billion years before the Imperial Era, the Exogorth Sy-O is born to Sy-O's father-and-mother. However, at even one billion, Sy-O would be considered young by Exogorth standards.[7]
    • Billions to millions of years before the Imperial Era, lava flows on Dagobah and forms Mount Dagger before disappearing to allow for the flourishing of non-sentient life.[8]
    • A non-sentient species lives on the world of Bravais, eventually evolving into the sentient Bravaisian.[9]
    • A non-sentient species lives on the planet Crul millions of years before they evolve into the sentient Crolute.[9]
    • A primitive species lives on the planet Abednedo, eventually evolving into the sentient Abednedo.[9]
  • Sentient species history (exact chronology undefined)
    • In ancient times, beings from a distant galaxy built a reflex point on the planet Seatos which pointed the way to the extragalactic planet Peridea.[10] The people become known as the Dathomiri.[11]
    • A star map pointing the way to Peridea's galaxy was also stored inside a Nightsisters' Temple on Arcana.[12]
    • 100,000 BBY
      • Beginning of the construction of the first city levels of Coruscant in the Core Worlds.[13]
    • 57,000 BBY
      • Utapau is settled[14] by colonists who eventually evolve into the Pau'an and Utai species.[15]
    • Pre-hyperspace travel
      • Batuu, a planet on the edge of the Outer Rim Territories, is colonized millennia after a great cataclysm resulted in the planet's giant trees being destroyed and eventually petrified.[16]
      • A sentient ancestral species[17] invents the hyperdrive after studying the hyperspace-traveling purrgil,[18] opening up the galaxy for exploration.[17]
    • Post-hyperspace travel
      • c. 30,000 BBY
        • Devaronians, naturally adept as scouts, become active as galactic explorers from their native planet Devaron, independently developing the hyperdrive.[8]
        • The Outer Rim planet Benetage is abandoned.[8]
      • c. 27,000 BBY
        • Felucia is colonized by Gossam Courivers[19]
        • By this point, human civilization had developed on Empress Teta in the Deep Core.[8]

The Old Republic (c. 25,025 – c. 1000 BBY)[]

Dawn of the Jedi (c. 25,025 BBY)[]

  • c. 25,025 BBY
    • The Dai Bendu, the religious group that would be attributed with the first holocrons, exists as the precursor to the Jedi Order.[20]
    • In pursuit of the preservation of peace and justice, an individual who came to be known as the Prime Jedi founds the Jedi Order on Ahch-To.[21][22]
    • The First Jedi Temple is built on Ahch-To,[21] and the Jedi village is constructed on the Temple Island soon afterward.[22]
    • Shortly after the founding of the Jedi Order, the world Ilum is discovered by a Jedi scout through Force-assisted hyperspace navigation.[8]
  • c. 25,020 BBY
    • Professor Huyang powers up.[21]
  • c. 25,000 BBY
    • The Galactic Republic is formed[21][23] by the Core Founders, twenty-two Core Worlds which include such planets as Alderaan.[24]

Expansionist Period (25,000 BBY)[]

  • 25,000 BBY (continued)
    • Expansionist Period
      • The foundation of the Corellian Run helps the formation of the Corellia system. The Corellian Trade Spine and the Perlemian Trade Route are also founded.[25]
      • Duros explorers discover and colonize the planet Neimoidia.[26]
      • The planet Cato Neimoidia joins the Republic.[27]

Millennia before the Imperial Era[]

  • Eons before the Imperial Era (exact chronology undefined)
    • The comet Kinro is predicted to carve its way through the Core Worlds. Before it can enter the Mid Rim, members of the Jedi Order come together and will it apart, although several Jedi lose their lives or minds in the process.[28]
    • By this point, the ice moon Mesula had been shattered.[29]
    • The first Grand Zigoth of Utapau, Krynbalt Kyr, dies.[29]
  • Thousands of years before the Clone Wars or the Imperial Era (exact chronology undefined)
    • The Predori empire opposed the Galactic Republic.[30]
    • A Sibettan constructs a wormhole gate that leads between the seams of time and space, facilitating their exodus from the wider galaxy.[31]
    • The Temple of the Kyber is constructed on the cold, desert moon of Jedha.[32]
    • Hylemane Lightbringer is born and later bludgeoned to death by a chair, disproving the myth that "he could not be killed by mortal weapons."[28]
    • The Zygerrian Slave Empire is defeated by Jedi Knights after their slave operations are outlawed in Republic space.[33]
    • A rogue Jedi[34] leads a group of Force users to seek greater power through embracing the dark side of the Force. They split from the Jedi Order[17] during the schism known as the Hundred-Year Darkness,[35] becoming the rivaling Sith. Defeated, they are exiled by the Jedi Order, which serves the light side of the Force, and flee from known space to Korriban, a world of red sands that was later known as Moraband.[34]
    • Unbeknownst to others, the Sith on Korriban rebuild and prepare their revenge,[34] erecting massive Sith temples and tombs in the Valley of the Dark Lords.[36]
    • Over time, the Sith Empire, with the use of enslaved beings,[37] vies for control of the galaxy. They build gargantuan superweapons powered by kyber crystals to battle the Jedi.[38][39]
    • The carbonite mines of the Empress Teta system are conquered by the Krath.[40]

22,000–5000 BBY[]

  • 22,000 BBY
    • The planet Neimoidia joins the Galactic Republic as an independent world in itself.[26]
  • c. 21,032 BBY[41]
    • The Blood Monarchy is established on Thisspias, the site of many battles during the Great Manifest Period.[20]
  • c. 20,000 BBY
    • An attack on Garn, later known as the Doom of the Ordu Aspectu, occurs—the Fortress of Garn was possessed by the Ordu Aspectu, a splinter group of the Jedi Order. Led by Rur[42] and related to the Massassi,[43] their experiments on immortality led to conflicts with Jedi orthodoxy, beginning the Entruvia Conflict. A dating system based on the signing of the Domancion Accord was used around this time,[44] and the conflict ended with a Jedi—Ordu Aspectu peace treaty: Jedi inspectors arrived at Rur's citadel, and, believing they intended to deactivate him, an artificial intelligence made of Rur's knowledge seized control of the citadel's droids and slaughtered its inhabitants. Rur sacrificed himself to deactivate the machine, hyperspace-jumping the citadel across the galaxy.[42]
  • 15,000 BBY
    • Rodia officially joins the Galactic Republic.[45]
    • The physical distinctions between the Neimoidian and Duros species becomes clear on Neimoidia.[26]
  • 12,000 BBY
    • Settlers from Corellia colonize a planet that becomes an Ordance/Regional Depot known as Ord Mantell.[46]
    • As credited by the Jedi, the planet Ossus comes to be their cultural center, and is made a near-mandatory pilgrimage site.[47]
  • 7977 BBY
    • The Coruscant reckoning calendar is introduced.[40]
  • c. 6000 BBY
    • Civilization on Bardotta begins, with the Frangawl Cult reigning over a militant society.[8]
  • 5500 BBY
    • The Rimma Trade Route is established.[25]
  • c. 5100 BBY
    • The Stybla family of the Chiss unite the Chiss Great Families as the First Ruling Family. With the unification the Chiss Ascendancy is formed, an aristocratic oligarchical nation located inside the Unknown Regions, and an interconnection of individual sovereignties which favores a more libertarian approach and embraces a philosophy of non-intervention and peaceful diplomacy with outsiders, prohibiting first strikes.[21]
  • 5019 BBY
    • The Chiss start exploring the Unknown Regions outside of the Ascendancy, known as the "Chaos" to the Chiss people, begining an era of exploration.[21]

Wars of the Old Republic (c. 5000 – c. 1000 BBY)[]

Battles with the Jedi (c. 5000 BBY)[]

  • c. 5000 BBY
    • Soon after Empress Teta's Unification Wars,[8] the Great Hyperspace War is waged by the Sith Lord Naga Sadow according to the Qel-Droma Epics.[48]
    • The Sith wage a war against the Jedi and[15] the Galactic Republic,[23] which sees the fall of the Republic capital and cityworld Coruscant.[15] This allows the Sith to build a shrine on the planet which exudes the force of the dark side. However, the Jedi eventually repel the Sith and reclaim Coruscant.[17]
    • According to the Qel-Droma Epics, the Great Jedi Library on Ossus is established as the physical embodiment of Jedi knowledge.[49] The library's construction was said to be overseen by the Odan-Urr,[8] a Jedi Master who annotated on a "Phases of Mortis" solution to the Unsolved Theorem of Master Thorpe, which was inscribed on a book dating to millennia BBY.[50]
    • The original Rammahgon was thought to have been destroyed at around this point.[51]
    • The Great Temple is built by the Massassi on Yavin 4.[52][53]
    • The Chiss Stybla family obtains the Starflash, a superweapon created by an unknown alien species that can fire an enormous blast of tachyonic and lightspeed energy at a star, causing a far more powerful return burst. Due to its destructive nature, it is hidden away in the Bastion, the predecessor of the Universal Analysis Group, with the intent of never using it.[21]

c. 5000 – c. 1000 BBY[]

  • c. 4300 BBY[21]
    • An enemy nation of the Chiss conquers almost all of the Ascendancy, driving the Chiss species back to their homeworld of Csilla and nearly driving the Chiss extinct. However, the Ascendancy achieves a pyrrhic victory at the Final Assault on Csilla, a last stand by the remnants of the Chiss Defense Force in the war. The enemy amasses their entire fleet in an attempt to conquer the Chiss homeworld, but Patriarch Mitth'omo'ross "Thomoro" of the Mitth family volunteers the Mitth to sacrifice twenty Chiss warriors, including her four sons, to annihilate the enemy fleet by the Starflash. In the aftermath of the activation of Starflash, all four of Thomoro's sons die, and she is granted the title "The Tragic." Furthermore, the Stybla family, at time the sole Ruling Family of the Ascendancy, so admired Patriarch Thomoro's sacrifice that they bestow a great honor on her, granting her the suffix "odo" meaning "guardian" in the ancestral Stybla language Tybroic. Furthermore, the Stybla agree to step down as Ruling Family, granting Ruling status to the Mitth family and two other Chiss families and stepping back to become a still-important but less powerful Great Family. However, the Starflash weapon also affected Csilla's sun, causing it to cool and make the surface of the planet uninhabitable.[54]
  • c. 4260 BBY
    • The Chiss begin an exodus from Csilla to colony worlds such as Avidich, Rentor and Sarvchi. This exodus to other worlds is kept a secret from most in the Ascendancy. Instead, historical accounts of the event teach that Csilla's population had moved underground, leaving out the fact that the number of Chiss below the surface is far below the population of four billion before Csilla's climate change. Nevertheless, the headquarters of both the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet and Chiss Syndicure, along with many merchant and cargo installations, are kept on Csilla. Additionally, most of the Chiss Ruling Families refuse to abandon their capital world entirely, though some move their homesteads to other planets.[21]
  • Almost a century before c. 4000 BBY
    • Human explorers from Grizmallt in the Core discover a Mid Rim world they name Nabu, later known as Naboo. The native Gungans retreat into their swamps and lakes.[8]
  • c. 4000 BBY
    • Fleeing civil war on Grizmallt, human refugees make Nabu their new home, discovering the presence of Gungans while settling in. The humans and the Gungans make war against each other in the next few decades before agreeing to live separately. Gungan society had flourished on the world in the preceding millennia after the sudden departure of the Elders, a reptilian sentient civilization that colonized Nabu and waged war against the indigenes. Before the Elders' arrival, the Gungans had lived on Nabu in large nomadic tribes for millennia.[8]
    • Later, the Gungan tribes are united under Boss Gallo and commence the building of Otoh Gunga as their capital city. Meanwhile, the humans, calling themselves "the Naboo," split into warring factions; the Time of Suffering is begun.[8]
    • According to the Qel-Droma Epics, the Crusade of Krath occurs in which thousands of Jedi are felled by a new Sith Empire until the redemption of Ulic Qel-Droma.[8]
    • According to the Qel-Droma Epics, Ossus is devastated by the Cron Supernovae, which were triggered by Naga Sadow.[8]
  • c. 3966 BBY
    • By this point, galactic travelers navigate hyperspace using hyperspace sextants.[55]
    • Sith accounts describing the kyber weaponry built by Darth Tanis[50] within the Sith temple on Malachor[56] are made.[57]
      • Eventually, a cataclysmic event[58] at the Battle of Malachor,[59] later known in legends as the "Great Scourge of Malachor,"[58] occurs during the era of great conflict that began around 5000 BBY;[59] Jedi Knights of the Old Republic era[58] uncover the battle station and confront the Sith. All participants are petrified by the superweapon,[56] including a witch who led the Sith forces,[60] although an entity known as the Presence remained in a Sith holocron within the Malachor temple.[56] Afterwards, Jedi are forbidden from traveling to Malachor, and data on the location is removed from the Jedi Archives.[58]
      • By the time of the Great Scourge, the crossguard lightsaber design has become common among Makashi practitioners.[61]
  • 3705 BBY
    • Freia Kallea establishes the hyperspace route known as the Hydian Way, combining multiple smaller routes to create a super-hyperroute, thereby providing traders and privateers with a path across the entire galaxy.[62]
  • c. 3700 BBY
    • Ord Mantell becomes a trading post for spacers.[46]
  • c. 3650 BBY
    • The Chiss Ascendancy venture into the wider galaxy and get involved in the Sith Wars between the Galactic Republic and the Sith Empire. The Chiss will remember their ventures into the greater galaxy as ill-advised affairs.[21]
  • c. 3450 BBY
    • The Chiss withdraw from the wider galaxy, encountering a number of aliens before retreating to their borders, and losing wealth and technology in the process.[21]
  • 3277 BBY
    • The Lothal Calendar is introduced.[63]
  • c. 2500 BBY
    • The Drengir ally themselves with the Sith. However, the latter capture the Great Progenitor on Amaxine Space Station and the Drengir fall into a state of dormancy.[21]
  • 2320 BBY
    • Taanab is colonized by settlers who introduce transplanted farmstock to the world, transforming it into an agricultural outpost.[64]
  • 2200 BBY
    • By this point, the citizens of Begamor had begun to live in peace.[65]
    • By this point, a local corporation had begun their legitimate rule over the Aakaash system.[66]
    • The Fire space station is constructed in the Aakaash system.[67] It expands over a period of two thousand years, housing millions of permanent residents and becoming an integral transit point between the star system's political and economic centers. Fire eventually becomes famous to the wider galaxy for being a wholly artificial construct.[68]
    • A metal currency, the design of which was based on an ancient item of Aakaash system philosophy, is used on Fire. It is eventually phased out in favor of the standard Land & Sky currency as well as Republic credits later on.[68]
  • Over 2019 BBY; millennia before 19 BBY (exact chronology undefined)[69]
    • The Force vergence Acablas on Auratera comes to be a known destination for Jedi Masters and Sith Lords, with Auratera aligning itself with the Galactic Republic or whichever Sith Empire that controlled the surrounding star systems.[8]
    • Bardotta's Jour-un becomes a trading hub and the planet's major spaceport.[8]
    • The Cron Drift asteroid field is established in the Auril sector, preventing expeditions to the ancient Jedi world of Ossus.[8]
    • Iktotch is incorporated into the Galactic Republic.[8]
    • Weik is colonized by sentients.[8]
  • Between 2022 and 2019 BBY[70]
    • The Klorri-clan battle shield is introduced. The Wookiees of Kashyyyk would reserve the battle shield for use in important rituals and ceremonies.[71]
  • c. 2000 BBY
    • Boothi XII is settled by farmers[72] who lead simple lives and whose architecture of sheds would outlast the Republic's existence.[73]
    • The Jedi code used by the Force wielders on Mortis to draw in Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Ahsoka Tano during the Clone Wars is last used around this time.[74]
    • The Dagoyan Order takes hold of Bardotta after a series of successful and strategic small-scale wars; the once ruling Frangawl Cult goes into hiding.[8]
    • The Arakein Monks set in stone a clock atop Mount Arakeirkos. According to legend, whoever watches each swing of its pendulum for a day will have the lifespan of the universe revealed before their eyes.[75]
  • c. 1300 BBY[21]
    • Following the B'ankora homeworld cataclysm, Republic Chancellor Chasen Piian grants the B'ankora survivors sanctuary on Coruscant.[76]
  • c. 1100 – 1000 BBY
    • Momin is trained by Lady Shaa.[21] Eventually, Momin kills his Master and travels the galaxy honing his craft. Later, he and his acolytes are slain by a pair of Jedi during his destruction of a city through the use of a superweapon, although Lord Momin's spirit survives through his mask. His story is deemed heretical by the Sith and is suppressed.[77]
  • c. 1050 BBY
    • The first Mandalorian Jedi, Tarre Vizsla, creates the Darksaber.[21]
  • The fall of the Old Republic[78]
    • The Mandalorians had been waging war against the Jedi[79] and against the Republic,[80] during which the Mandalorian crusades,[79] the devastation of Ubduria,[9] and, in the final stages, the cataclysm that reduced Mandalore to a wasteland occurred.[81]
    • By the time of the Sith's defeat (exact chronology undefined)
      • Before the time of the Galactic Republic, the lushly forested planet Mustafar was inhabited by off-worlder Lady Corvax, who lived in harmony with the native Mustafarians along with her husband. The latter died as a result of an attack on Mustafar, leading to Corvax's theft of the most sacred Mustafarian relic, the Bright Star, in an attempt to use it in a ritual to resurrect her love.[82] The ritual failed; instead, the Bright Star's energies pushed Mustafar into a new orbit, reducing the world to an unbalanced, chaotic state. A volcanic wasteland is created[82] as tiny Mustafar is torn between gas giants Lefrani and Jestefad.[15] Thus, Sith seeking the secrets of eternal life were attracted to the world.[50]
      • Radaki[83] had left the Jedi Order, believing that Jedi should retain their family ties and wealth. Thus he was seduced by the dark side of the force, becoming the Sith Lord Darth Krall.[84]
      • Sith victory at the Battle of Wasted Years; this would be attributed to Darth Krall, who would also be remembered for taming the Nightmare Conjunction and as one of the Lost Twenty.[84]
    • By this point, the Middle Era of the Old Republic has occurred.[78] Bith operas existed at that time.[85]
  • c. 1032 BBY
    • The Four Masters[15] construct the Jedi Temple[21] atop the ruins of the Sith shrine on Coruscant in an attempt to contain and eliminate its power,[17] as well as to take advantage of the area's strength in the Force.[15]

Defeat of the Sith (1032 BBY)[]

  • 1032 BBY[21]
    • The Jedi-Sith War[86] ends with the Jedi Order and Republic forces victorious.[78]
      • Coruscant and the Jedi Temple had been occupied by the Sith as part of the war[78] and the Darksaber stolen from the temple by Mandalorians.[79]
      • By this point, Coruscant and the Jedi Temple are liberated by Jedi and Republic forces.[78]
      • The Brotherhood of Darkness,[87] an army of the Sith in which Darth Bane served,[88] are defeated by the Jedi's Army of Light[87] as a result of infighting amongst themselves.[89] The Sith, who once numbered thousands,[90] are driven into hiding[17] and are eventually thought extinct.[91]
    • The sole Sith survivor, Darth Bane,[92] creates the Grand Plan—a plot to destroy the Jedi and the Republic from within for the coming of Sith rule over the galaxy[93]—and establishes the[94] Rule of Two,[36] a lineage of Sith who would operate in the shadows, preserving the Sith's vendetta against the Jedi whilst preparing for their own return. Centuries before the Clone Wars, the Rule of Two is revealed to the Jedi Order, though still they presume the Sith's eradication and believe that Bane's plan is ruined with his defeat[94] allegedly at the hands of his apprentice, Darth Zannah, in a duel on the Inner Rim planet Ambria. This pattern is repeated by nearly all Sith thereafter.[95]
    • The Sith Lord becomes the last of his kind to be laid to rest on Moraband's Valley of the Dark Lords.[94]
    • The Sith Wars waged between the Republic and the Sith Empire[96] has ended by this point.[21] The Sith Wars saw the participation of the Jedi Order on the side of the Republic[8] as well as the participation of the Chiss Ascendancy, which encountered multiple alien species before retreating to their borders.[96] The Jedi Cervil the Uncanny had left behind testimonies about lightwhips being effective against the Sith's Forbidden Forms,[97] and Ithorian population of Auratera continues restoring the world to its natural state following the Sith Wars.[8]
  • c. 1022 BBY
    • The Amaxine warriors disappear from the known galaxy.[21]
  • c. 1014 BBY[98]
    • A Jedi Temple is erected over the largest mouth of a vast cave system in the mountains of the planet Ilum. It becomes a common pilgrimage site for the members of the Order and home to the ritual known as the Gathering, where Jedi students retrieve the kyber crystals which will power their lightsabers.[98]
  • c. 1000 BBY
    • The Massassi become extinct.[21]
    • The translation of Sith runes is outlawed.[21]
    • In the aftermath of the Corsair Wars, Master Oo'ob breaks with Jedi doctrine, pursuing a pre-emptive method of dealing with threats to galactic peace. He and his partner, noted crystallist Var-Whill, develop the Farkiller, and use it to kill a number of "emergent" despots. Within the decade, the Jedi Council pronounces them apostate and eventually purges them,[99] with Oo'ob's expulsion taking place around 1000 BBY.[21]

Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY)[]

Reformation of the Republic (c. 1000 BBY)[]

  • c. 1000 BBY (continued)
    • Founding of the modern Galactic Republic; with the Republic's definitive victory, its capital resettles on Coruscant, and the time before this rebirth is forgotten as a dark age, the "Old Republic." Official calendars begin anew,[78] and the Old Republic military is disbanded.[17] By this point,[100] the Galactic Senate, newly founded by several star systems,[101] elects Tarsus Valorum[86] as the first Supreme Chancellor.[17]
    • Around this time, the Coruscant Jedi Temple begins serving as the Jedi Order's operational and spiritual headquarters.[102]
    • The Yavin Code, a protocol that dictates prisoners must be blindfolded before their execution, is established at the Yavin Convention.[103]
    • The Bardottan Royal Palace, the political and religious seat of power on the planet Bardotta, is constructed atop a towering stone spire containing a Frangawl Temple deep within its caves. (Approximate date)[104]
    • The construction of the Royal Palace of Alderaan begins.[105] After the initial buildingwork, additional chambers are added every few decades.[106]
    • What will be remembered as the most unpredictable and thrilling instance of the Dragon Void race until the race involving Han Solo occurs.[107]
    • The Anchorite sect, a light side group on Jakku believing that suffering was the basis of life, binds itself to the Jedi Order.[108]
    • Auratera is concealed from official charts and isolated from the galaxy at large by the Jedi and its populace to prevent future Sith or other dark side Force users from accessing the planet's Acablas vergence.[8]

c. 1000 – c. 500 BBY[]

  • c. 997 BBY
    • Marlo is born.[109]
  • c. 982 BBY
    • The Dai Bendu Monastery is constructed on the planet Kijimi, and the settlement Kijimiko is built around the monastery, eventually becoming known as Kijimi City.[110]
  • c. 973 BBY
    • Maz Kanata is born.[111]
  • c. 972 BBY
    • In the wake of the Jedi-Sith War and the ensuing reformation of the Galactic Republic, a convention known as the Galactic Accord of Systems establishes a set of rules of engagement for any future armed conflicts. Among other things, it dictates that a surrendering enemy can not be denied the chance to do so.[21]
  • c. 965 BBY
    • The Dai Bendu Monastery on Kijimi is built.[112]
  • 900 BBY
    • The North River Freight logistics company manufactures for the first time the Sky Three, designed as a universal engine which can be connected into many different starships.[113]
  • 896 BBY
    • Yoda is born.[21]
  • c. 873 BBY
    • Maz Kanata settles on Takodana.[21]
  • 867 BBY
    • Naboo joins the Galactic Republic. The people of Naboo will celebrate this event during the Festival of Light.[21]
  • 832 BBY[21]
    • General Jafan ends Naboo's longstanding Time of Suffering[8] and restores peace to the planet.[15]
    • Theed is founded, and the Theed Royal Palace is constructed in the same year under the direction of King Jafan. Uniter[8] and ruler of all the Naboo, Jafan becomes the first ruler of the Great Time of Peace.[15]
  • 800 BBY
    • Kuat Drive Yards purchases the planet Rothana, eventually leading to the establishment of Rothana Heavy Engineering.[114]
  • 796 BBY
    • Yoda begins to train Jedi in the ways of the Force.[21]
  • 719 BBY
    • The Capitol building of Ryloth's Lessu City, located in the Octagon district, is constructed.[115]
  • c. 682 BBY
    • Thelj is settled as a potential colony of the Galactic Republic.[116]
  • c. 679 BBY
    • A starship that will eventually fall into the hands of the family of the Evereni species known as the Faers is constructed.[117]
  • 620 BBY
    • The Antarian Rangers are founded to assist the Jedi Order as a security force on Antar IV.[118]
  • 600 BBY
    • Jabba Desilijic Tiure is born on Nal Hutta into the Desilijic Tiure clan, an influential Hutt kajidic.[21]
  • 582 BBY
    • Eiram and E'ronoh are in a time of peace. 200 years later this time period is known as the Greatest Age.[119]
  • c. 532 BBY
    • Leric Schmireland, an explorer affiliated with the University of Coruscant, is sent out by the Galactic Republic to create a catalogue of planets, departing Coruscant with a team. He never returns, but his messenger droids will continue to appear at the university to deliver reports into the High Republic Era. [120]
  • c. 522 BBY
    • The Citadel Station, a prison designed by the Republic to hold rogue Jedi, is built on the planet Lola Sayu, placed on a bridge between two yellow lava lakes and powered by geothermal energy.[121]
  • c. 520 BBY
    • The Citadel Station is utilized to imprison the last known rogue Jedi of the Jedi Order.[122]
  • 519 BBY[123]
    • With the Kallidahin believing themselves to be descendants of the extinct Eellayin people, the Archaeological Research Council of Kallidah establishes a Research Base on one of the largest fragments of ancient Polis Massa, which was once home to the Eellayin and had suffered a planetary cataclysm long ago. Archaeologists begin to survey and excavate the entirety of the Polis Massa asteroid belt, seeking to discover more of their ancestors as well as other alien species with cloning skills learned from the Kaminoans.[15]
  • c. 511 BBY
    • The planet Jelucan is colonized by off-worlders exiled from another world for not breaking allegiance to their king.[124]
  • c. 509 BBY
    • Yaddle is born.[125]
  • c. 500 BBY
    • The armor that will become Sabine Wren's is forged.[126]

High Republic Era (c. 500 – 100 BBY)[]

Age of Exploration (c. 500 – 330 BBY)[]

  • c. 500 BBY
    • The last members of the Evereni, known as the Wanderers, remaining after the Great Storm and centuries of conflicts, band together and abandon their homeworld of Everon under the lead of the Xouri family at the Everon exodus.[21]
  • By c. 483 BBY
    • Sachar Rold, a priest of the Guardians of the Whills on the moon Jedha breaks away from the order and forms the Path of the Open Hand on Dalna.[127]
  • c. 482 BBY
    • Jedi Master Barnabas Vim and his Padawan Vix Fonnick undertake a mission to the world of Angcord, in search of the Force artifact known as the Echo Stone that has been a object of legend, capable of amplifying connections to the Force. They find the object in the possession of the native citizens, and after a small investigation, Master Vim detects that the Stone has been corrupted and amplified a dark side power within the Lady of Angcord. He is forced to order his apprentice to destroy the Stone, breaking the dark side hold over the planet. With the Echo Stone shattered into 3 pieces, Vim and Vix set out separately to hide the pieces of the powerful object.[128]
  • c. 437 BBY
    • Vildar Mac is born in a village on the planet Kiffex.[129]
  • c. 433 BBY
    • A member of the Sorcerers of Tund, who had been expelled from the his order after embracing the dark side of the Force, attacks Vildar Mac's village and kills many Kiffar before he is stopped by the Selkath Jedi Master Larti. Vildar barely survives the attack, and is taken as a Padawan by Larti.[130]
  • c. 431 BBY
    • Pan Eyta is born on Dowut.[131]
  • c. 430 BBY
    • "Seismic Events and Their Role in Force Confluences" is written after a survey of Dalna's Maawat Mountains by the Galactic Republic.[132]
  • c. 429 BBY
    • Hintis—the red star of the planet Hintis IV—begins to inflate and die, leading to Hintis IV's inhabitants starting to leave the system.[133]
  • c. 428 BBY
    • Kaktorf is born.[134]
  • c. 422 BBY
    • Creighton Sun is born.[135]
  • 413 BBY
    • Axel Greylark is born.[136]
  • c. 412 BBY
    • Gella Nattai is born, and very young she is dropped off by her parents at the Jedi temple on Devaron to be trained as a Jedi.[137]
  • c. 407 BBY
    • The Ikkrukkian Porter Engle and the Kage Barash Silvain train as Jedi younglings.[138][139][138]
    • Phan-tu Zenn is born on the planet Eiram into a poor family living in the Rayes Canal area of Erasmus Capital City.[140]
  • c. 402 BBY
    • Dietrix Jago is born.[141]
    • Enya Keen is born.[142]
  • c. 401 BBY
    • Fel Ix is born.[143]
  • c. 400 BBY
    • Rumors persist of Planet X.[21]
    • Thomas Toov is born.[144]
  • 399 BBY
    • Padawans Barash Silvain and Porter Engle are separated by their Jedi Masters, who are concerned by their close bond.[145][146]
  • c. 397 BBY
    • Rooper Nitani is born on the agriworld of Rohm.[147]
    • Savina Besatrix Malagán is born on the planet Phatrong, and given to the to the Jedi Order shortly afterward.[148]
    • Sky Graf is born into the powerful Graf family.[149]
    • During a storm and flood on Eiram, Phan-tu Zenn and his family are swept out to sea, with Phan-tu being the only survivor. Following this, the boy is found and adopted into the Eiram royal family by Queen Adrialla, who has been unable to have children with her wife, Queen Consort Odelia. As a result, Phan-tu becomes the heir to Eiram's throne.[150]
  • c. 396 BBY[151]
    • Students of Reena University Axel Greylark, Leyli Romero, and Kozmo Sundrel IV sneak into a party for the Queen's Bloom Festival in Reena's Gorag Tower, only to discover that it is being robbed by Elecia Zeveron's crew of thieves. As Romero is secretly in on it, the thieves are successful.[152]
  • c. 395 BBY
    • Senator Lexxir Greylark, his son Axel Greylark, and a Jedi Knight travel to the astronomical object Melida/Daan to release the leader of the Daan, who has been captured by the opposing Melida. When it comes time to extract the leader, the Melida set off an explosion, injuring Axel and trapping Lexxir beneath rubble. Although Axel tries to rescue his father, the Jedi Knight forces Axel to leave Lexxir behind to die in order to save the others from the Melida. As as result, Axel blames the Jedi for his father's death and comes to resent them.[153][154]
  • c. 394 BBY
    • Dass Leffbruk is born into hyperspace prospectors Spence Leffbruk and his mother, who dies shortly after in a shuttle accident.[155]
  • c. 393 BBY
    • Jerid is born.[156]
  • c. 392 BBY
    • Republic and Jedi pathfinder teams start exploring the Outer Rim.[21]
  • c. 389 BBY
    • Rooper Nitani becomes the Padawan of the Jedi Silandra Sho.[157]
  • 387 BBY
    • The Forever War between Eiram and E'ronoh is reincited[21] after the death of E'roni Prince Niko A'lbaran during a diplomatic mission to Eiram. As a result, the first battle of the Eiram and E'ronoh War is fought.[153]
    • Tey Sirrek, a Sephi member of the Guardians of the Whills stationed as a guard at the Temple of the Kyber, kills Anst Wozo, an Inleshat criminal wanted by the Namoi criminal syndicate, who exploited children of the streets of Jedha eventually causing their deaths. Sirrek leaves the Guardians for their inaction in dealing with Wozo and protecting the people of the Holy City.[158]
  • 385 BBY
    • Elecia Zeveron becomes the mother of the Path of the Open Hand.[21]
  • c. 384 BBY
    • Kevmo Zink becomes the Padawan of Jedi Master Zallah Macri.[159]
  • c. 383 BBY
    • The Great Hyperspace Rush occurs,[21] making hyperspace travel safer.[97]
  • 382 BBY
    • By this point, it has been established on Jedha the Convocation of the Force, an advisory body and council designed to promote understanding between the various Force religions based on the desert moon. Religions with representatives on the Convocation include the Disciples of the Whills, the Fallanassi, the Lonto, the Matukai, the Jedi Order, the Church of the Force, and the Sorcerers of Tund.[21]
    • The Force artifact known as the Rod of Seasons is stolen from the Hynestian Royal Treasury on the planet Hynestia Prime by the Children of the Open Hand, a team within the Path of the Open Hand led by Yana Ro tasked with "liberating" such objects by Elecia Zeveron.[160]
    • In the aftermath of the robbery, the queen of Hynestia appeals to the Jedi Order for the retrieval of the artifact, and Jedi Master Zallah Macri and her Padawan, Kevmo Zink, are assigned to investigate the theft.[160]
    • The smuggler Radicaz Dobbs travels to the planet Sophros with prospectors Spence Leffbruk and Dass Leffbruk, where he retrieves the egg of a Shrikarai. While flying through the atmosphere of the planet, Spence and Dass' ship is damaged, and Dobbs agrees to help them off the planet for half the reward of discovering the planet. Instead, Sunshine strands them on the planet Gloam. He is later approached by Elecia Zeveron, and the egg ends in possession of the Path of the Open Hand.[160]
    • The Children of the Open Hand are ambushed by the Graf family's forces on the planet Thelj while they attempt to steal an artifact called Jedi Tears from the Graf family compound on the planet. Yana Ro is the only member of the Children who survives.[160]
    • The Great Leveler hatches on Dalna instigated by Elecia Zeveron as Jedi Zallah Macri and Kevmo Zink burglarize the Path compound in search of the Rod of Seasons, and the Leveler petrifies both Jedi to death. The Leveler is hailed as a gift from the Force by the members of the cult, and Yana Ro learns to control it through the Rod of Seasons.[160]
    • Left vulnerable on Dalna, The Path of the Open Hand leaves the planet in the Gaze Electric, a starship the cult had been building for years. Alerted by Sunshine Dobbs, they travel to Jedha in search for the Rod of Daybreak, an artifact that can help them better control the Leveler, hidden in a vault in the Holy City controlled by the Jedi.[160]
    • Jedi Knight Azlin Rell begins investigating the deaths of Zallah Macri and Kevmo Zink,[160] leading him to research Jedi Master Barnabas Vim and the Echo stones.[128]
    • Republic Pathfinder team Outer Rim Expedition Two-Five-Two, led by Jedi Master Rok Buran and including his Padawan Maliq, is sent to the planet Aubadas in response to a request for assistance from the Katikoot species, who face an energy crisis and need help setting up a new, sustainable power source after halting mining operations on Aubadas' twin planet, Gloam. Hearing legends of monsters in the mines of Gloam, the team decides to visit the planet to investigate. Their guide, Rillik, who is sick with the Miner's curse and seeks to secretly sell out the planet's resources to gain wealth for himself with what time he had left, betrays them and sets them up to be ambushed by the monsters, and only Master Buran survives. He sends away their communications droid EX-8C to seek help.[161]
    • Jedi Master Silandra Sho's Pathfinder team travel to Gloam and rescue Buran. They also rescue Spence and Dass Leffbruk, who were stranded on Gloam by Sunshine Dobbs.[161]
    • A skirmish is fought in the Eiram system between forces of the warring planets Eiram and E'ronoh, sparked by a sabotage attempt carried out by members of the Path of the Open Hand against a starfighter they believed would be flown by E'roni princess Xiri A'lbaran during a mission to escort an ice hauler due to make a delivery to E'ronoh. However, A'lbaran had reassigned the Corellian devilfighter to Bly Tevin, a new recruit in E'ronoh's fleet, leading to his death after an attempt to stop the sabotaged ship from attacking the Eirami. Jedi Masters Char-Ryl-Roy and Creighton Sun alongside Jedi Knight Gella Nattai and Padawan Enya Keen arrive aboard the Alif-class Longbeam on a mission to deliver food and medical supplies in a Republic relief shipment to Eiram, and are caught up in the fight. Gella Nattai intervenes in the skirmish aboard her Alpha-class starfighter, hailing both Princess Xiri, Captain of E’ronoh’s Thylefire Squadron, and the Eirami General Nhivan Lao.[153]
    • The Chancellors of the Republic negotiate a peace between Eiram and E'ronoh during a summit aboard the Republic Longbeam Paxion, stationed between the two planets.[21] The Jedi on the relief mission attend the summit, as does Chancellor Orlen Mollo himself. Chancellor Kyong Greylark sends her son Axel Greylark to represent her. During the summit, Prince Phan-tu Zenn of Eiram and Princess Xiri A'lbaran of E'ronoh agree to marry.[153]
    • After the summit, Jerrod Segaru, an E'ronoh military leader, is assassinated aboard the Paxion by Rev Ferrol, son of Viceroy Ferrol and member of the Children of E'ronoh, an extremist group that believe their planet should not make peace with Eiram.[153]
    • A funeral for Jerrod Segaru is held at The Rook, the capital city of the planet E'ronoh. The E'ronoh royal family, the members of the Jedi Order in the system, Chancellor Orlen Mollo, Axel Greylark, and the Eiram royal family are present, and the latter is attacked by members of the Children of E'ronoh during the procession, only surviving thanks to the action of the Jedi.[153]
    • Xiri Al'baran and Phan-tu Zenn begin a customary aid mission on E'ronoh, joined by Nattai and Greylark.[153]
    • The Children of E'ronoh attack E'ronoh's mining village of U'ronoh in an attempt to frame the Eirami, and kill two of its inhabitants before being stopped by Gella Nattai and Axel Greylark.[153]
    • As a secret operative of the Path of the Open Hand, Axel Greylark kills Abda, a Path agent who was captured by the Jedi and E'roni, and attempts to frame E'roni Viceroy Ferrol for it. He takes over Abda's mission of retrieving an Eirami poison for the Path, successfully doing so with the help of Phan-tu Zenn. After Viceroy Ferrol confronts Axel about his actions, Axel kills him, but he is caught in the act and flees.[153]
    • The Wedding of Phan-tu Zenn and Xiri A'lbaran, the heirs of Eiram and E'ronoh respectively, is held in a two-part ceremony; first at The Rook on the planet E'ronoh, and then in Erasmus Capital City of the planet Eiram.[153]
      • Several bounty hunters and mercenaries attack the wedding hoping to claim a bounty on Axel Greylark, and Creighton Sun leads a force of multiple Jedi in a defense. They are able to repel the bounty hunters, but Jedi Knight Laan is killed in the fight.[153]
      • Concurrently, the Children of E'ronoh attack those gathered for the wedding in the hopes of taking over E'ronoh, but they are thwarted and their leader, Rev Ferrol crashes his ship into the sea.[153]
      • After deciding that the poison is too awful to deliver to the Mother, Axel Greylark begins a plot to destroy Eiram's capital city but is talked down by Gella Nattai and taken into custody.[153]
    • Jedi Masters Porter Engle and Barash Silvain[139] undertake a mission to the planet Gansevor to intervene in a conflict between the city-states of Bethune and Firevale[21]. Bethune army, led by Field Marshal Tozen, and supported by the mercenary army of General Abediah Viess, sieges Firevale hoping to claim for Bethune the only deposits of Baudraxite, a mineral crucial to the energy infrastrucure of the planet. Eventually, General Viess decides to betray Tozen, killing him and several other of his staff to be able to destroy and plunder the whole city by themselves. Through the united effort of the Jedi Masters, Sicatra, the heir to the bethunian throne, and Bethune's army, the mercenaries are repelled and forced off-world. Silvain blames herself for the losses at the battle, and vows to distance herself from the rest of the Jedi Order, including her brother, Engle.[162]
    • Engle later breaks the siege of Bardotta, earning the nickname "Blade of Bardotta."[162]
    • A turf war is fought between two gangs of pirates; Pirate Queen Maz Kanata's crew; and the Dank Graks, a group of criminals whose members are mostly rejects from dark side of the Force cults, led by the force-sensitive Geonosian Arkik Von. During the conflict, Jedi Padawan Sav Malagán goes undercover and joins Maz Kanata's crew.[163]
      • A skirmish near Nymeve Lake occurs.[163]
      • After a skirmish fought above the planet Takodana, the Dank Graks escape with the Venomed Scabbard, Maz Kanata's flagship, and Maz Kanata's pirate crew escape in the Grim Devourer, the Dank Graks' ship.[164]
      • The Dank Graks try to acquire Spiral-class Viz-Core Eviscerators from Frander's hidden depot, a space station converted into a shipyard and workshop.[165]
    • Jedi Master Vildar Mac arrives on Jedha[166] and learns of an ongoing spree of artifacts being stolen from Padawan Matthea Cathley and vigilante Tey Sirrek.[167]
    • A summit is held at the Second Spire in the Holy City on Jedha for a peace treaty to be signed to officially end the war between Eiram and E'ronoh, with Ambassador Lian Cerox as the Eiram representive, Ambassador Tintak as the E'ronoh representive, and Morton San Tekka and Tilson Graf as mediators.[21] The summit is bombed resulting in the deaths of Ambassador Tintak and Morton San Tekka, and dozens wounded. The treaty between Eiram and E'ronoh is postponed indefinitely, which leads to the resumption of hostilies between the two planets.[168]
    • The Temple of the Kyber is bombed, Disciples of the Whills archivist Zumeg is killed and many others wounded, including Jedi Knight Vildar Mac.[169]
    • The Battle of Jedha occurs.[21]
      • Werth Plouth, the Herald of the Path of the Open Hand, incites a riot outside the Convocation Chambers in the Square of the Supplicants and releases the Great Leveler into the crowd to cause chaos.[170]
      • The riot reaches the location of the Eiram-E'ronoh peace summit, which along with attacks from members of the Brotherhood of the Ninth Door at the behest of the Path of the Open Hand, turns both planets against each other, both beginning to attack the other and unleashing an all-out battle.[168]
      • Rioters bring down the massive Final Protector Jedi statue.[171]
      • The Herald and the Children of the Open Hand use the battle as a distraction to attempt to steal the Rod of Daybreak from a vault hidden beneath the Final Protector, but they are foiled by Vildar Mac and Tey Sirrek.[172]
      • Sav Malagán and Maz's crew come into conflict with the Dank Graks and the Path of the Open Hand.[173]
      • After discovering the Brotherhood of the Ninth Door's involvement, Jedi Master Silandra Sho and Church of the Force adjunct Keth Cerepath fight and defeat the Brothers, resulting in Cerepath's death.[168]
      • After Jedi reinforcements arrive Jedi Master Creighton Sun talks the E'roni and the Eirami down.[168]
    • The Path of the Open Hand retreats to Dalna. Some of its members, led by Radicaz Dobbs, undertake a mission to Planet X to retrieve eggs of the Nameless species. One of their three starships is destroyed by the planet's Veil, while one crash lands. One member of the team, a Force-sensitive Setaran tracker called Calar, foresees their actions will have grave consequences for the galaxy and the Force itself and tries to sacrifice their ship, but is shot by Dobbs before he can reach the controls. After retrieving many of the eggs, Path member Bokana Koss experiences a Force vision matching Calar's, claiming that massive beings called the Under-Dwellers want the Path to escape Planet X so as to begin a blight upon the galaxy. The group is attacked by an Under-Dweller, knocking Koss out of the vision, but is subdued by the protector, another massive creature. However, the protector then attacks the group, and Koss sacrifices to take down the protector and allow the Path members to escape on the Silverstreak, a ship that had previously crash-landed on the planet.[172]
    • The Klytobacter incident occurs, orchestrated by the Path of the Open Hand, helping spur tensions between Eiram and E'ronoh.[174]
    • The turf war with the Dank Graks ends with the Dank Grak's defeat following the Rescue of Maz Kanata on Takodana.[175]
    • The Hyperspace Chase, a contest to route Hyperspace lanes sponsored by the San Tekka and Graf prospecting families, occurs. Sky Graf attempts to use it as an excuse to find Planet X with the help of Jedi Padawan Rooper Nitani and Dass Leffbruk, but eventually gives up their quest.[176]
    • In the aftermath of the Battle of Jedha, several different missions of Jedi traveling to Dalna to investigate the Path of the Open Hand and their involvement occur, including a diplomatic mission and one undertaken by Creighton Sun and Jedi Aida Forte. The Path, under the leadership of Marda Ro, found the Path of the Closed Fist and use the Jedi's arrival as an excuse to attack the investigators.[172]
    • The Republic Pathfinder ship Witherbloom, housing Jedi Masters Rok Buran and Sula Badani and Badani's Padawan Coron Solstus, crashes into an Path of the Open Hand ship containing Nameless eggs.[177] The Jedi and Republic members face members of the Path of the Closed Fist and the Nameless in the crash site. Budani is killed, and Buran takes Solstus as his Padawan.[178]
    • The battle known as the Night of Sorrow occurs when the Path attack the Jedi on Dalna.[174]
      • The Path of the Closed Fist unleashes many recently acquired Nameless and many Jedi are killed.[172]
      • Axel Greylark briefly joins the Path of the Open Hand from the influence of the Mother, but soon turns against them. Having resigned as Chancellor to prevent the Path from using her and her son against the Republic, Kyong Greylark arrives on Dalna with reinforcements and fights alongside her son. Greylark and Nattai take down a path leader named Binnot Ullo.[174]
      • Phan-tu Zenn and Xiri A'lbaran rally the forces of Eiram and E'ronoh to work together to help the Jedi on Dalna.[174]
      • Jedi Knights Oliviah Zeveron and Azlin Rell, joined by Padawan Matthea Cathley, enter the Path compound caves and defeat the Path's Herald, Werth Plouth. Oliviah duels her long lost sister Elecia Zeveron, the Path's Mother.[172]
      • A massive explosion rigged by the Mother destroys the caves of the Path compound.[172]
      • The Jedi and their allies defeat the Path.[21]
    • The Path of the Open Hand is dissolved.[179]
    • Having discovered that the Mother was secretly a Force-sensitive fraud, Marda Ro kills her with the Leveler and leaves Dalna[172] to found what will become the Nihil pirate organization.[180]
    • A Funeral at the Jedi Temple on Coruscant is held for those killed in the Night of Sorrow.[174]
    • Creighton Sun and Yoda agree to cover up the existence of the Nameless so they cannot be used against the Jedi.[174]
    • After encountering the Nameless several times and being driven mad during the Night of Sorrow, Jedi Azlin Rell is placed and asylum as his former Master Arkoff tries to help him. Rell is sought after and attacked by Path member Vol Garat, who wants Rell's notes on the Nameless, but Arkoff and Rell stop Garat. After Rell goes missing, Arkoff hides his journal on the planet Banchii.[181] Rell then falls to the dark side after devoting his life to studying the Nameless.[182]
  • c. 381 BBY[183]
    • Marda Ro visits the planet Dantooine as part of her search for others of her species and recruits the Twi'lek Fori Nagor into her group of marauders.[184]
  • c. 380 BBY
    • A Jedi temple is constructed on the planet Dalna.[185]
  • c. 379 BBY
    • Marda Ro attacks a Jedi at a marketplace during her search for others of her species and feeds him to the Great Leveler.[184][186]
    • A Salmaca performs a series of attacks on the Batuu village Peka[187][188]
    • Rooper Nitani takes and passes her Jedi Trials on Batuu.[187]
  • 377 BBY
    • Jedi Knight Rooper Nitani, Jedi Master Silandra Sho, and the church droid P3-7A go on a mission to the floating city Penumbra.[189] Nitani makes a pilgrimage to the Holy City on Jedha and visits the bar Enlightenment.[189]
  • 362 BBY
    • A new Outer Rim Exploration Team of the Republic Pathfinders investigates the death of a Jedi Master.[190]
  • c. 357 BBY
    • Beginning of the cold war between Eiram and E'ronoh.[191]
  • 339 BBY
    • The Chiss Ascendancy's Clarr family defeats a numerically superior Irizi family force by disabling one of their warships' engines and comm and putting the vessel in a long, elliptical orbit that took it near the Irizi force.[192]
  • c. 332 BBY
    • The Jafan family leads a people to colonize what became known as the planet Jafan in the Chommell sector. The planet is ruled by the Jafan family until at least 32 BBY.[193][194]
    • By this point, the mining industry on Elphrona,[195] a world located on the edge of the Unknown Regions in the Outer Rim,[196] is established.[195]
  • By c. 330 BBY
    • Mari San Tekka, a girl who is member of the San Tekka clan with a gift to see paths through hyperspace, is kidnapped by an Evereni named Ro of the Ro family[21]

c. 330 BBY – 233 BBY[]

  • c. 319 BBY
    • The third volume of the Aionomica book is subject to a forgery scandal.[197]
    • Mustafar comes into the possession of the Techno Union.[198] The Union charges the native Mustafarians rent[50] whilst providing the technology for them to mine their mineral-rich world.[71] The natives' labor serves to pay their rent, and the planet becomes known for its mining activities.[50]
  • c. 318 BBY[199]
    • The modern Chiss Syndicure is formed, the supreme ruling body of the Chiss Ascendancy, composed of the Nine Ruling Families. The Syndicure has both executive and legislative authority over the Ascendancy and serves as collective head of state of the Chiss. The political body called the Aristocra is a part of the Syndicure, which includes representation from the Forty Great Families. Its fundation marks the ending of an era where armed conflicts between rival Chiss families was common.[54]
  • c. 300 BBY
    • Many Gotal in the Oplovis sector disapear.[68][200]
  • c. 285 BBY
    • The Chiss Stybla family discovers a second Starflash weapon, and decides to keep it hidden from the public's eye on the Chiss Ascendancy planet Sposia, where the Stybla family manages the Universal Analysis Group, a secretive research project of the Ascendancy dedicated to reverse-engineering alien technologies acquired by the Chiss Defense Force. No member of the Chiss Ascendancy know about the second Starflash except trusted members of the Stybla family.[21]
  • c. 280 BBY
    • Thandeka is born.[201]
  • c. 279 BBY
    • The Galactic Republic assists with an evacuation of Hintis IV due to its star Hintis's death.[202]
  • c. 278 BBY
    • Orlis is born.[203]
  • c. 272 BBY
    • Invaders surface on Pamarthe for the last time until at least 28 ABY.[204]
    • Lourna Dee is born into the First family of the world Aaloth, one of the families that govern Twi'lek colony planets. Her father, Yudiah Dee, rules the planet as Keeper.[205]
  • Between 272 BBY and 255 BBY
    • Aaloth has been in a state of famine for several seasons, causing the hungry populace to lead demonstrations and riots against the ruling family. Yudiah Dee opposes developing Aaloth's economy around spice mining, but Lourna Dee believes it necessary to feed and control the populace. Lourna's boyfriend, Bala Waleen, a spicerunner in league with the Zygerrian Alliance, induces Lourna to support a coup d'état to depose her father, promising he would install Lourna in her father's place. Lourna unlocks the doors of the First family's palace to rioters in league with the Palace Guard, who storm the place led by Waleen. He then orders the guards to execute Lourna's family and takes the title of Keeper. Waleen drugs Lourna with glitterstim and sells her as a slave to the Zygerrian enslaver Lady Vardem Kroleyic.[206]
  • c. 270 BBY
    • The song "Corellia for Corellians" is released by around this year.[207]
    • Dal Azim is born.[208]
  • 266 BBY
    • The Hayananeya, a singing stone sacred to the Twi'lek people of the planet Ryloth, goes missing.[209]
  • c. 265 BBY
    • Faddaff Davenspon is born.[210]
  • c. 262 BBY
    • Avar Kriss is born.[211]
  • 258 BBY
    • The Jedi Order carries out a mission to Wadi Raffa.[212]
  • c. 257 BBY
    • The Jedi Order's T-1 shuttle sees action in[213] the Eiram–E'ronoh crisis.[214] Fillithar Jedi Master Simmix is killed in a ship crash, leaving his Padawan, Cohmac Vitus, conflicted over the Jedi ways of non-attachment. Vitus nevertheless resolved the crisis alongside Master Laret Soveral and her Padawan, the Umbaran Orla Jareni.[213]
  • c. 255 BBY
    • Jedi Master Oppo Rancisis and his Padawan Dal Azim attack a camp of Zygerrian enslavers on the planet Zygerria, freeing a number of enslaved individuals including Lourna Dee. She takes advantage of the Jedi attack to incite a rebellion among her fellow enslaved individuals. Master Rancisis later recognized and commended Dee for leading the revolt, and by his suggestion she joins the Carida military academy.[21]
  • c. 253 BBY
    • Xylan Graf is born into the powerful Graf family. As the grandson of Catriona Graf, the family's matriarch, he is a member of the inner family line and considered the primary heir.[215]
  • c. 252 BBY
    • Innamin is born.[216]
    • Asgar Ro, descendant of Marda Ro, becomes upset at how long his mother Shalla Ro is waiting to combat the Republic and the Jedi. Onboard the Gaze Electric, he challenges Shalla's waiting, leading to an argument where he pushes her over a railing to her death.[217][218]
    • Asgar Ro reveals the existence of the Paths, secretly provided by Mari San Tekka, to the Nihil[21] and proves their effectiveness by having his son, Marchion Ro, use Battle Paths to defeat a group of them. Asgar then takes the title of Eye of the Nihil.[217]
  • c. 251 BBY
    • Jordanna Sparkburn is born on Tiikae.[219]
  • c. 250 BBY
    • By this point, Pra-Tre Veter had joined the Jedi High Council.[220] While Veter is still a new member, Grandmaster Yoda responds to the raids on the Scalvi, teaching him patience and trust and inspiring the conflict between the Scalvi and the Crulkon to be resolved peacefully.[221]
    • Bell Zettifar is born on the planet Chandrila.[222]
    • Lourna Dee joins the Nihil.[21]
  • c. 249 BBY
    • Affie Hollow and Reath Silas are born.[223]
  • c. 248 BBY
    • Vernestra Rwoh is born.[224]
  • 247 BBY
    • Bell Zettifar joins the Jedi Order.[222]
    • Lourna Dee saves Asgar Ro from an assassination attempt and is promoted to Tempest Runner.[21]
  • c. 246 BBY
    • Imri Cantaros is born.[225]
  • c. 245 BBY
    • Ronn Blythe is born into Ottoh and Erika Blythe.[226]
  • c. 244 BBY
    • A largely isolated colony is established on Sedri Minor.[227]
    • Avon Starros is born into Republic senator Ghirra Starros and into the Starros Clan.[228]
    • Honesty Weft is born on the planet Dalna into a Corellian woman and Weft, who serves as ambassador for their people.[229]
    • A civil war erupts on the aquatic planet Helikoprion, forcing the Heliko Haze Delik and her parents to depart from the chaos aboard their starship, the Stingray.[230]
  • c. 243 BBY
    • Driggit Parse is born.[231]
    • Zyle Keem is born, child of the pirate Tanana Keem and grand child of the pirate Saya Keem.[231]
    • Gavi Takitaken is born.[231]
    • Kildo is born.[231]
    • Emelsine Tarkin is born into the wealthy and influential Tarkin family of Eriadu.[232]
  • c. 242 BBY
    • Serj Ukkarian is born.[233]
    • The Nihil Tempest Runners plan to assassinate Asgar Ro, believing Ro's son Marchion would be easier to control.[21] Lourna Dee stabs Asgar,[234] but Marchion finds Asgar's body. However, Marchion chooses to strike the final killing blow to his father,[21] and proves harder for the Tempest Runners to control than Asgar.[234]
  • 241 BBY
    • Klias Teradine and Ty Yorrick visit the Yallow Shrine on Alaris Prime. After Teradine's mind is affected by a trap and he attacks her, Yorrick is forced to kill him,[235] leading to her leaving the Jedi Order.[21]
    • Chandrila Star Line's MPO-1400 Purgill-class star cruiser, Halcyon, is constructed and sails on its maiden voyage.[21]
  • c. 240 BBY
    • Dez Rydan passes the Jedi Trials and becomes a Jedi Knight.[236]
  • 238 BBY
    • Jedi Master Sskeer trains his Padawan, Keeve Trennis, on Kirima,[21] teaching her how to Force jump.[237]
  • 237 BBY
    • Xu Bo is born.[238]
  • c. 236 BBY
    • Nihil Tempest Runner Lourna Dee trains Sylvestri Yarrow in self-defense for a year.[239]
    • Orphan Affie Hollow starts to work for the Byne Guild.[21]
  • 235 BBY
    • Senator Ghirra Starros gives the droid J-6 to her daughter Avon.[240]
    • Bell Zettifar becomes Loden Greatstorm's Padawan.[241]
    • Affie Hollow joins the Byne Guild.[21]
  • c. 234 BBY
    • Lina Soh is elected Supreme Chancellor of the Republic.[21]
  • c. 233 BBY
    • The pirate Taborr Val Dorn and his gang raid the Hap's Sap Tap cafe in the town of Kublop Springs on the planet Tenoo, but are stopped by the Jedi younglings Kai Brightstar, Nubs and Lys Solay.[242]
    • The people of Tenoo celebrate the Kublop Springs festival, with members of the Jedi Order in attendance.[243]
    • The Skirmish in Kublop Springs marketplace occurs.[244]
    • The Kublop Classic racing skiff competition is held at the river around Kublop Springs, with Team Crimson Bolt, led by pilot Nash Durango, winning the race.[245]
    • The Jedi younlings track down the Star Seeker, an old hyperspace prospector Renning-class shuttle that had crash-landed on the planet Fobaris while en route to Tenoo. They rescue the ship's pilot droid 0G-LC, who flies the ship away through the asteroid field that had trapped them on the planet.[246]
    • The Search for the Yara leaves occurs.[247]
    • A herd of the Wellagrin creatures of the planet Andraven migrate through a mountain and a snowstorm into the orchard open field.[248]
    • The Jellyfruit Festival is held in the town of Kublop Springs.[249]
    • A Discovery of burrowberry bird occurs.[250]
    • Jedi younglings Kai Brightstar, Nubs, and Lys Solay take a test flight of their Jedi Vectors, with the guidance of pilot Nash Durango, to Tenoo's big moon of Dedoon. At the moon, an exogorth captures Lys Solay, Nubs and Nash Durango, but are rescued by Kai Brightstar.[251]
    • The Search for Figgles occurs.[252]
    • The Gargantua Garganchie is accidentally released from its cage in the Sil Gohtta's Nature Preserve on the planet Kundu Minor by the Besalisk Braygh. The Jedi younglings, helped by members of the Galactic Society of Creature Enthusiasts, retrieve the Gargantua back to its cage.[253]
    • The Search for Bruff occurs.[254]
    • Taborr Val Dorn attempts to steal Hazun Princess Inaya's gift for her father.[255]
    • Jedi Initiate Kai Brightstar travels to the planet Tatooine in order to deliver a hyperdrive capacitor to the Zekodoan Mycho Zala, a hyperspace prospector and associate of Jedi Master Zia Zaldor Zanna.[256]
    • The planet Tenoo hosts a Visitor's Day, a holiday that attracts guests from all over the galaxy.[257]
    • Explorer droid 0G-LC leads Jedi Initiates on a mission to recover the Tharnakan Story Stone on Tharnaka.[258]
    • Jedi Initiates from Tenoo attend the Dunkutu Day, an annual celebration celebrated by the people of the planet Omaka, traditionally celebrated with a big feast in the center of town.[259]
    • At the annual Life Day celebration on Kashyyyk, Kai Brightstar, Lys Solay, and Nubs help Wookiee Johgeshakka find her Life Day Orb.[260]
  • c. 233 – 232 BBY[21]
    • A Starship Show is held on Tenoo.[261]
    • An Ancient Sith Runestone brought to the Batuu Jedi Temple corrupts the Jedi there and causes them to kill each other, leaving only Padawan Ady Sun'Zee alive. Sun'Zee overcomes her fear at the Batuu Jedi temple with the help of Grand Master Yoda and the two seal the Sith Runestone away.[262]

Nihil conflict (232 BBY – 228 BBY)[]

  • 232 BBY[21]
    • At this point, the Outer Rim contains few well-mapped hyperspace lanes that allows those seeking adventure, as well as those seeking to evade the Republic's laws, to do so.[263]
    • By this point, the droid revolutionary Ajax Sigma has led a droid uprising on Kligson's Moon, where the entire population of the moon was wiped out, including thousands at the hands of Sigma himself. Eventually the Jedi stopped Ajax Sigma's army, with the Jedi Master Loden Greatstorm strucking down the droid leader.[264]
    • The Coruscant-based Republic Chancellor Lina Soh envisions a galaxy of Great Works that ranged from infrastructure to culture in an effort to expand the Republic's reaches, better connect the peoples of the galaxy, and bring inspiration and peace for all citizens. Efforts are made to negotiate a new peace treaty between the Quarren and the Mon Calamari, as well as to mass-produce bacta—a "miracle" substance[263] primarily made from a barley originally cultivated on Thyferra[265] that was intended to replace juvan—in the hopes of revolutionizing medicine.[263]
    • As part of Chancellor Soh's pledge to maintain the Republic's hold on[263] the hard-to-navigate Outer Rim,[266] outreach programs are established in the Rim.[263]
    • In the Galactic Frontier,[21] the Jedi maintain order and justice at independently operated outposts, working closely with the Republic without direct assistance from the Coruscant Jedi Temple.[263]
      • The Starlight Beacon space station is constructed as part of the series of outreach programs,[263] featuring a Jedi temple designed by Palo Hidalla and located in the center of the Rim's dark zones to guide travelers[266] and boost the region's transmission times, intended to be the first Beacon of a new galactic communications relay network.[263]
      • A conclave between the Jedi Order and the Republic involving Jedi Master Avar Kriss and Admiral Pevel Kronara of the Republic Defense Coalition is held at Starlight Beacon.[263]
    • The strain from the Jedi mission to Zeitooine leads Dez Rydan to request a transfer to the Starlight Beacon.[213]
    • Marchion Ro instigates the Great Hyperspace Disaster as leader of the Nihil raiders, based in his No-Space territory.[263] He allows the Nihil to use special hyperspace Paths, which he controls by keeping the hyperspace navigator Mari San Tekka captive.[24]
      • In hyperspace, the Legacy Run, a Kaniff Yards Class A modular freight transport of the Byne Guild, encounters a Nihil ship and is destroyed.[263] The Byne Guild's Kezarat Convoy disappears on the same day.[267] Starships across the galaxy are abruptly knocked out of hyperspace by missiles of debris from the Legacy Run, which also threaten to destroy space stations, moons, as well as planets. The Republic turns to the Jedi for help in saving lives and ending the crisis,[263] and Jedi activity sees a significant increase.[196]
      • Due to being delayed at the Starlight Beacon, Master Avar Kriss and Admiral Pevel Kronara become the first to respond to the disaster in the nearby Hetzal system, coordinating efforts to save citizens from aboard the Third Horizon.[263]
    • Vernestra Rwoh, having been the Padawan of Stellan Gios, is made a Jedi Knight at the age of sixteen, becoming one of the youngest Knights of the Jedi Order in a generation.[268]
    • The Jedi Wayseeker Orla Jareni, Jedi Knight Dez Rydan, Jedi Padawan Reath Silas, and Jedi Master Cohmac Vitus, as well as the cargo ship Vessel's Captain Leox Gyasi, co-pilot Affie Hollow, and the navigator Geode are ripped out of hyperspace as a result of the Great Disaster. They take refuge in a seemingly abandoned Amaxine Station.[213]
    • Aboard the Amaxine Station, The Jedi passengers of the Vessel are disturbed by dark visions. Believing that a set of four binding statues contained the dark side influence, the Jedi take away the Force artifact for purification in the Sith shrine located beneath the Coruscant Jedi Temple. However, they are proven wrong, and realize that the statues had contained something else.[213]
    • Even though the combined efforts of the Republic and the Jedi Order managed to prevent a majority of the damage at Hetzal, further fragments of the wreckage of the Legacy Run continue to emerge from hyperspace in a series of Emergences, that threaten star systems across the galaxy.[263]
    • In the First Emergence, seven pieces of the Legacy Run emerge in the Ab Dalis system. While six of them pass through the system harmlessly, the seventh strickes a glancing blow to Ab Dalis, destroying a city on the planet and killing approximately twenty million people. The devastation leads Larence Garello of Garello Technologies to evacuate his employees and research from the planet. However, the evacuation convoy is attack by the Nihil before they can make it out of the system.[263]
    • One of the Emergences strickes the planet Trymant IV. The threat is reported by Republic Security Zone Monitoring Satellite 777B-Alpha, in the orbit of the planet. The only Jedi Order or Republic starship within range is the Padawan Academic Cruiser Star Hopper, which immediately diverts to assist. At the same time, the Nihil take advantage of the situation to evacuate select individuals from Bralanak City, including members of the Elders of the Path, before launching an attack. Force-sensitive resident of the planet Zeen Mrala joins Lula Talisola's group of Jedi Padawans in defending Bralanak City. The group of Padawans confront the pirates and use their Spider Cruiser to evacuate civilians, and Marchion Ro orders the Nihil to execute the Padawans. They are saved by Jedi Masters Yoda and Torban Buck, and while Buck and the Padawans battle in the city streets, Yoda board the Spider Cruiser in an attempt to rescue Mrala's friend Krix Kamerat, who had been hustled onboard. However, Kamerat, upset over the revelation that Mrala had lied to him about her Force-sensitivity, refuse to leave. A Galactic Republic rescue fleet arrives to assist and set up a blockade, but the Nihil ship is able to escape to hyperspace after Ro has a medical frigate fired upon as a distraction.[269]
    • The San Tekka clan, having risen from a family of hyperspace prospectors in the outer reaches of the galaxy to a dynasty privy to leading technology that allows Chancellor Soh's Republic to continue expanding safely to new areas about a century ago, operates alongside the galactic government as it responds to the Great Disaster.[263]
    • After the 40th Emergence shoots out from hyperspace the portion of the Legacy Run bridge containing its black box, the Jedi and Republic, hoping to retrieve the flight recorder, clash with the Nihil Lourna Dee and her tempest, who are attempting to destroy it. Eventually, the Republic retrieves the Legacy Run flight recorder, but Jedi Knight Te'Ami is killed aboard her Vector, and Republic pilots Marcus Augur and Beth Petters are also lost.[263]
    • The tragic poet Omar Berenko is reputed for residing at the Varykino artists' retreat on Naboo.[263] Centuries before the year 22 BBY, the controversial Berenko was kidnapped from a scenic balcony overlooking the lakes after penning the disturbing and visionary epic poem titled Defense of Naboo in the Room of Morning Mists.[15]
    • The Jedi High Council convenes to discuss the threat of the Nihil, and after a contested debate between Grandmaster Xo Lahru and Jedi Masters such as Yarael Poof, Rana Kant, Oppo Rancisis, Keaton Murag, Ada-Li Carro, and the stand-in of Grandmaster Yoda—who was on sabbatical from Council affairs—Ephru Shinn, approves a joint effort between the Jedi and the Republic in a mission against the Nihil as proposed by Chancellor Soh.[263]
    • The Nihil conduct a raid on Elphrona, capturing Jedi Master Loden Greatstorm and farmer Ottoh Blythe. Marchion Ro executes the farmer but keeps Geatstorm for future plans. Porter Engle returns to active Jedi service.[263]
    • Attack on Tanalorr.[270]
    • The Battle of Kur occurs. Marchion Ro sends Kassav's Tempest to death in the Kur Nebula, intending to trick the Jedi and the Republic that they had destroyed the Nihil. The Trandoshan Jedi Master Sskeer loses his left arm and blames himself for the death of Master Jora Malli.[263]
    • While restoring the statues aboard the Amaxine Station, the Jedi are attacked by the Nihil. To defend themselves against overwhelming forces, the Jedi are forced to destroy the statues to unleash what it contained. A group of Drengir, who were put into stasis by the Sith through the binding statues long ago, are thus awakened from their slumber. While the Vessel's crew escape amidst the chaos, several Drengir are released into open space in the process.[213]
    • Vernestra Rwoh is stranded on the jungle moon Wevo with several other individuals, including Avon Starros of the Starros Clan and Padawan Imri Cantaros, when their transport ship, the Steady Wing, is sabotaged by the Nihil.[97]
    • Rwoh and the others fight Nihil that follow them to Wevo. Rwoh prevents Cantaros from falling to the dark side and takes him as her Padawan.[97]
    • The medical substance kolto is in common use at this point.[213]
    • Jedi Grandmasters Pra-Tre Veter and Yoda join other Jedi and Republic personnel, amongst them Chancellor Lina Soh, the newly minted Jedi Knight Keeve Trennis, and the newly appointed marshal of the Starlight Beacon, Avar Kriss, aboard the latter station in its dedication ceremony for light and life.[271]
    • Sskeer hides a devastating secret that was tearing him apart,[272] drawing a private emotional outburst from him soon after Starlight's dedication.[271]
    • Surik, which formed a part of a phrase used by the Jedi Sskeer, existed by this point.[265]
    • Jedi Padawan Wes Vinik joins the other Jedi at the Jedi temple on Tenoo. Soon after, he and the Jedi younglings from the planet help stop the Ganguls pirate gang from stealing starships on Deppani.[273]
    • Kai Brightstar helps retrieve a runaway fellow Jedi youngling named Bren from the clutches of pirates at Yarrum Tower.[274]
    • Jedi younglings Kai Brightstar, Lys Solay, and Nubs help settle a conflict between Mayor Brooks and rustlers on a desert world.[275]
    • Jedi younglings Kai Brightstar, Lys Solay, and Nubs stop pirate Taborr Val-Dorn from kidnapping a gangul creature on Tenoo.[276]
    • A grand opening event is held for a library on Hazun.[277]
    • Nubs discovers the lightsaber of the ancient Pooba Jedi Master Barabo, in an old Jedi temple in a hidden room behind a wall sculpture depicting Barabo saving the town from a chirodactyl. Yoda and Nubs take the lightsaber to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant.[278]
    • Padawan Wes Vinik is kidnapped by the Ganguls pirate gang but is rescued by Jedi younglings.[279]
    • Jedi younglings Kai Brightstar, Lys Solay, and Nubs join Nash Durango to search for Kaliah Kublop's staff. They ultimately find on the abandonded ship Terror of Tenoo in the possession of the long-lost droid pirate Blackbolt, who they inadvertently reactivate and release into the galaxy.[280]
    • Cyrus Vuundir balances and switches between his identity as Prince of Vuundalla and the pirate Taborr Val Dorn to help his Jedi youngling friends stop the Comet Runners pirate group from stealing fathiers on Vuundalla.[281]
    • The band Feb Rozo and the Sap Tap Trio are kidnapped on Tenoo by the Ganguls for the crime lord Bulcha the Hutt. Padawan Wes Vinik, alongside his Jedi friends and Tenoo local Hap successfully rescue them from Bulcha on Yarrum.[282]
    • A party is held on Ootoo Prime. Prince Cyrus Vuundir nearly accidentally ruins it, but Jedi younglings stop armored amberflys from attacking.[283]
    • The Andraven Ancestors Circuit is held on the planet Andraven, where kids of the Andraven species complete challenges to honor their ancestors who settled the world.[284]
    • Dunkutu Day is once again held on Omaka, and resident Fiorna and Jedi youngling Nubs search for gomgourds.[285]
    • Life Day celebrations are held on the planet Kashyyyk, and Jedi younglings stop the Gangul pirates from ruining them.[286]
    • Jedi younglings Kai Brightstar and Djovi Resmia locate Tanglevine Base on Tenoo, and stop droid pirate Blackbolt from raiding it.[287]
    • A skirmish occurs at Docking Bay 7 on the planet Batuu after Jedi youngling Lys Solay's lightsaber is stolen.[288]
    • The Ganguls pirate group successfully take over Yarrum Tower on Yarrum, but the Tenno Jedi younglings rescue Chigg from it with the reluctant help of pirate Taborr Val-Dorn.[289]
    • The Ganguls and Comet Runners team up to perform a raid on the planet Vuundalla for the Vuundir family jewels. They are successful, and Vuundallan prince Cyrus Vuundir's identity as the pirate Taborr Val-Dorn is exposed to his Jedi youngling friends trying to help.[290]
    • Jedi younglings stop the Comet Runners pirate group from stealing coaxium from Zephyr.[291]
    • Raxlo, anlongside Jedi younglings, stop the Ganguls pirate group from stealing his harvester on Andraven.[292]
