    • Padawan Wes Vinik and youngling Kai Brightstar help the Besalisk Braygh search for B3-C4, a water scout droid she built to find underground water on Kundu Minor.[293]
    • The Tenoo Grand Tour, a monopod race, is held on the planet Tenoo. The first introduction of the sport on Tenoo, the race is organized by the Monopod Circuit, and consists of five monopod teams, four of which are a sentient being paired with an engineering droid. The fifth team consists of the Pantoran Wiston Freng and the human Nash Durango, who fills in for Freng's missing engineering droid JK-46. Freng and Durango win the race.[294]
    • Bulcha the Hutt attempts to invade the Jedi temple on Tenoo to take it for himself while the older Jedi are away, but the young Jedi thwart him anyway.[295]
    • Jedi youngling Lys Solay and her friends stop K-2PL, a droid serving the Moldwarp thief and collector Draiven Bosh, from stealing a firehawk on Tenoo.[296]
    • Jedi younglings Nubs and Lys Solay help the Pooba Zeepohz and the droid NP-87 deliver a cargo pod to Kuplop Springs on Tenoo.[297]
    • Jedi younglings Kai Brightstar, Lys Solay, and Nubs locate a Tazi sloth at the Tenoo Jedi temple.[298]
    • Jedi younglings led by Kai Brightstar, alerted by a Twi'lek and a human workers of a starship factory on the planet Deppani, stop the pirate Taborr Val-Dorn from stealing from the factory a thrust accelerator to complete the upgrades to his ship, the Iron Talon.[299]
    • The scrapper Eren Kitt and their droid M1-M1 team up with Jedi younglings Kai Brightstar, Lys Solay, and Nubs on Bracca to rescue pikpik in the Bracca Badlands from Nihil Jaay and Fleever, who plan to use them to find a navicomputer.[300]
    • Jedi younglings Lys Solay and Zotti search for missing dunnels on Tenoo.[301]
    • Jedi younglings Kai Brightstar and Nubs are assigned a mission to search for Seba's journal at the same time Lys Solay and Nash Durango are assigned to help the individual Roon retrieve missing banthas, however, their destination coordinates get mixed up and they end up completing each other's missions.[302]
    • The Nihil set a diversionary trap in the Bright Jewel system leading to a battle between them and the Jedi that the Jedi easily win.[303]
    • The Battle on Quantxi is fought on the junk moon Quantxi between members of the Jedi Order and their allies, and the Nihil. Zeen Mrala had come to the moon in search of her former friend Krix Kamerat, taken by the pirates during their attack on Trymant IV. When Padawans Lula Talisola, Farzala Tarabal and Qort follow Mrala to Quantxi, they find the Nihil attempting to massacre the local Junk Mavens, and Tarabal and Qort stop to help while Talisola follows Mrala. Jedi Masters Yoda, Torban Buck and Kantam Sy, who had just fought a space battle against more Nihil forces, follow the Padawans to the moon and assist in the fight. With the pirates antagonizing the Junk Mavens defeated by the Mavens, the Padawans, and a Mantellian Savrip allied with them, Talisola, Mrala, Sy and Buck attempt to prevent Kamerat from escaping with Marchion Ro, but the Spider Cruiser Squall Spider is able to assist in the pirates' escape.[303]
    • Yoda and Tromak of the Elders of the Path embark on a mission to Vrant Tarnum to retrieve the artifact hidden there before the Nihil can, but they are shot down and Marchion Ro retrieves the artifact anyway.[304]
    • Jedi Initiates Kai Brightstar, Lys Solay, and Nubs, alongside pilot Nash Durango, rescue Yoda from Vrant Tarnum.[305] However, he disappears afterward.[306]
    • Explorer droid OG-LC performs an excavation of a raka fossil on Tenoo and thwarts businessman Ozon Nimbee's attempt to take over the land.[307]
    • Kai Brightstar, Lys Solay, and Nubs help droid MB-4 find his owner Freet Chaddio and return to him.[308]
    • The Battle of Tenoo is fought between the Jedi Order and an alliance of pirates led by the Ganguls. Seeking to drive the Jedi out from the Bri'ahl sector, the Ganguls' leader Sellaccc Orryak leads an attack on the Tenoo Jedi Temple. Despite capturing the temple, she meets resistance from the Jedi trainees led by Padawan Wes Vinik and youngling Kai Brightstar. With the help of several allies including Ace Kallisto, Raxlo, the Durangos and Taborr's gang, the Jedi are able to defeat the Ganguls and defend the Tenoo Jedi temple.[309]
    • By this point, the Hutt Clan is sanctioned from trading openly in Republic space.[265]
    • Drengir crisis
      • The Drengir abduct children on Sedri Minor.[265]
      • Mission to Sedri Minor[265]
        • The Nihil attack a Hutt Clan ship in the recently–Republic-aligned Kazlin system, prompting the Jedi Sskeer, Keeve Trennis, and the Kotabi bond-twins Terec and Ceret to investigate the resulting wreckage. Tracing the Hutt craft's origin to the Sedri system, Sskeer and Ceret take a T-1 shuttle to Sedri Minor. Avar Kriss and Vernestra Rwoh take the Ataraxia to join Trennis aboard the Hutt craft.[265]
        • On Sedri Minor, Ceret is captured by the Drengir, and Kriss, Terec, Trennis—traveling aboard the Ataraxia—join Sskeer on the moon. Ceret yields to the powerful shadow cast by the Drengir and joins their collective mind. Sharing the same mind with Ceret, Terec also succumbs to the Drengir's influence and becomes crazed. While Kriss and Trennis confront the Drengir at their underground dwelling, Sskeer dispairs that he could not help Terec, who was uncontrollable and inconsolable.[310]
        • While Avar Kriss and Keeve Trennis free Ceret as well as a local boy from the Drengir, they are surrounded by more Drengir, who are accompanied by Sskeer. Sskeer had had his mind joined with the Drengir's collective consciousness, and had grown a botanical, plant-like left arm.[310] Sskeer captures Kriss' crew, and the secret history of the Drengir is revealed.[237]
        • The Hutt Cartel, led by Myarga Anjiliac Atirue, arrive on Sedri Minor attempting to claim it. After a misunderstanding initially pits the Hutts and Jedi against each other, the Hutts agree to ally with the Jedi against the Drengir.[304]
        • Keeve Trennis uses a Mind trick on a Drengir-connected Sskeer to get the Drengir to retreat.[304]
      • Starlight Beacon is attacked by the Drengir, as a result of the Jedi Order bringing aboard the corpse[237] of a Hutt trader whose starship had been attacked by the Nihil after departing the planet Sedri Minor with a cargo of Vratixian barley.[265] The Hutt's system had been infected by the Drengir, who burst from the corpse while the Jedi were performing an autopsy. Jedi Master Estala Maru and Jedi Knight Vernestra Rwoh proceed to fend off the Drengir as Maru attempts to contact Marshal Avar Kriss for assistance; Kriss, however, is occupied with events on Sedri Minorr.[237] The Drengir retreat after Trennis's mind trick.[304]
      • The Hutt world of Nal Hutta, including its capital Bilbousa, is attacked by the Drengir, transforming its metropolises into jungles and swamps.[24]
  • 231 BBY
    • Drengir crisis continues
      • The Drengir spread throughout the Outer Rim, partially due to being seeded by the Nihil. The Hutts and the Jedi work together to combat them.[24]
      • As a consequence of Menir's bitterness and unwillingness to accept any change, the Drengir plague the village Skirl on the planet Batuu.[311]
      • The Drengir slaughter many on Daivak, but the Jedi and Hutts fight against them.[312]
      • Keeve Trennis utilizes Sskeer's connection to the Drengir to learn that their homeworld and hiding place of their Great Progenitor is Mulita.[312]
      • The Drengir attack Nal Hutta's capital of Bilbousa. Jabba the Hutt attempts to use the event and the concurrent arrival of the Jedi for negotiations to thwart the alliance between the Hutts and the Jedi, but is thwarted.[313]
      • Mercenary Ty Yorrick is hired by farmers of the planet Safrifa to defeat a member of the Drengir that had destroyed their crops and killed many of their children. She slices the beast to pieces with her lightsaber, before lighting the remaining strips of Drengir on fire so that it can't regrow and return.[24]
    • The Nihil Tempest Runner Zeetar attempts to raid the Halcyon cruiser, in order to gain information from the passengers onboard. Jedi Knight Nib Assek and her Padawan Burryaga Agaburry are able to repeal the attack with the help of the late arrival of Jedi Master Stellan Gios and several Jedi Vectors.[314]
    • While avoiding joining the fight against the Drengir on Mulita, Keeve Trennis joins Orla Jareni to fight the Nihil on Chortose.[315]
    • Marchion Ro acquires the Great Leveler, a powerful Nameless creature that specifically targeted Jedi.[24]
    • The Nihil raid the Cyclor Shipyards above the planet Cyclor. Sarn Starbreaker, a Cloud of Pan Eyta's Tempest, wanted to make a strike against the Republic by destroying the Innovator, an Elite-class vessel being built at the shipyards to be a showpiece of the upcoming Valo Republic Fair. The Cyclorrian workers, alongside Jedi Knight Indeera Stokes and her Padawan Bell Zettifar, fight back against the attackers. After the Cyclorrians manage to get a signal for help past the Nihil's scrambler droids, a drift of Jedi Vectors led by Jedi Master Porter Engle arrive from Derra IV to reinforce them. Ultimately, Zettifar is able to destroy Sarn Starbreaker's Cloudship despite having been shot in the gut with a harpoon, killing the Cloud amidst the defeat of all his forces.[24]
    • The Valo Republic Fair is held on Valo by Supreme Chancellor Soh to show how the galaxy had recovered from the Great Hyperspace Disaster. However, the Valo Atrocity occurs, in which the Nihil attack, and having distracted the Jedi with the Drengir, devastate the planet and kill hundreds of thousands.[24]
      • Bell Zettifar rescues Lina Soh's son Kitrep Soh and the Lonisa City mayor's son Jom Lariin from the experimental Republic ship the Innovator as it sinks into Lake Lonisa[24]
      • Elzar Mann and Ty Yorrick fight the Nihil together and help handle escaped beasts from the Lonisa City Zoo[24]
      • Jedi Archivist OrbaLin defeats Nihil Tempest Runner Lourna Dee in a duel.[24]
      • Stellan Gios protects Chancellor Lina Soh and Togruta queen Elarec Yovet from the Nihil.[24]
      • Jedi Padawan Ram Jomaram restores communications on and from the planet at Crashpoint Tower, fighting off Drengir planted by the Nihil there.[306]
      • The Nihil are eventually driven from the planet, but achieve a psychological and tactical victory.[24]
    • Drengir crisis, continued
      • As part of the Nihil's attack on Valo, the Drengir are sent to attack Crashpoint Tower.[306]
      • The Drengir attack a farming settlement on the planet Banchii, after sprouting from a container of seeds transported to the planet by the farmer Kooba. They are able to kill several farmers, incuding Kooba himself, before being stopped by members of the Jedi Order under the command of Jedi Master Arkoff and Jedi Knight Lily Tora-Asi.[316]
      • At the Battle on Mulita, the Jedi Order and the Hutt Clan, led by Jedi Master Avar Kriss, assault the planet Mulita, homeworld of the Drengir. The location of the Great Progenitor, the first of the Drengir, had previously been learned by Keeve Trennis through a link to the Drengir in the Force. Kriss subsequently summoned as many Jedi as possible, helped by Master Estala Maru on Starlight Beacon. The Jedi defeat the Drengir and capture the Great Progenitor, ending the Drengir crisis.[317]
    • After the Valo Atrocity, where the Tempest Runner Pan Eyta left his fellow Tempest Runner Lourna Dee for dead when Republic reinforcements arrived, she returns to the Nihil base on Grizal, where she witness Eyta be poisoned by Marchion Ro for challenging his authority and for his previous failures. Dee saves Eyta with an injection of cypanid, a substance from Ryloth that does not cure him but instead prolonges his agony, and she convinces him to launch a second attack on the Republic. Eavesdropping on a communication from Senator Tia Toon to the Cyclor Shipyards, Eyta learns the Jedi Order is withdrawing its forces to Hetzal Prime and Starlight Beacon, and so chooses to attack the shipyards, only for him to find an entire battle fleet waiting for his cruiser, the Elegencia, when it arrives, leading to the Second Battle of Cyclor. The Republic had recently discovered that the Nihil were tapping into their communications and laid a false trail to ambush the Nihil forces, and Dee had also gone behind Eyta's back and convinced the majority of his Tempest to abandon him as he had done to them on Valo. Upon realizing that he was betrayed, the severely-ill Eyta flees the Elegencia in a secret escape pod and leaves the rest of his forces to be wiped out by the Republic.[24]
    • The Battle of Grizal is fought in the aftermath of the attack on Valo when the Jedi attack the Nihil base on the planet Grizal. The Jedi were able to locate the base after allowing the pirate Quin Amarant to escape Valo in a shuttle aboard which Jedi Knight Indeera Stokes and her Padawan Bell Zettifar were hiding, allowing the two to activate a homing beacon upon their arrival on the planet. Once the signal is received, Jedi Master Stellan Gios and his Red Group depart the ongoing skirmish at Cyclor, arriving at Grizal and assaulting the base. During the attack, Zettifar is briefly reunited with his thought-dead Master, Loden Greatstorm, who had been a prisoner of the Nihil. However, Marchion Ro unleashes the Great Leveler against the Jedi, killing Greatstorm and traumatizing Zettifar. In the aftermath, the Jedi assume that the Tempest Runner Lourna Dee, who escapes the battle, is the true leader of the Nihil.[24]
    • In the aftermath of the Battle of Grizal, Stellan Gios sends Jedi Master Emerick Caphtor to investigate the mysterious death of Loden Greatstorm. Caphtor travels to the moon of Vrant Tarnum and attacks a Nihil base there with a unit of Galactic Republic troopers. Most of the pirates stationed at the base flee upon the Republic group's approach, with Caphtor and his soldiers fighting those Nihil who had not managed to escape, taking as many as they can alive as prisoners. Upon inspecting the Kharvashark ruins where the Nihil were stationed with his droid, Q-2, Caphtor discovers a cave with the lyrics of an old nursery rhyme he had found to be in some way connected to his investigation carved around it.[318]
    • Chancellor Soh then assigns investigator Sian Holt and Caphtor to work together in uncovering an attempt to sell the creature by criminal Arathab Fal and Nihil doctor Kisma Uttersond.[319]
    • Krix Kamerat, the leader of a Nihil specialized cell, is ordered by Marchion Ro to find a way to prove himself after the debacle at Vrant Tarnum, which had caused him to lose much of his unit's strength. Kamerat chooses to attack the planet Takodana, that has a Jedi Temple, in the hopes of destroying it and demonstrating to the pirate Maz Kanata that the Nihil are the only true marauders in the area. The lone Jedi at the Takodana temple, Sav Malagán, is able to drive off the first wave of the attack, but calls for reinforcements from Starlight Beacon as she knows the Nihil will return. Kamerat retreats to Dol'har Hyde to gather more of his forces, vowing to destroy Malagán's temple.[320]
    • Ram Jomoram's friends throw a party on Starlight Beacon for him after noticing his homesickness.[321]
    • Sylvestri Yarrow and Jordanna Sparkburn fall in love on Tiikae, but they are forced to separate as Sylvestri leaves the planet with her mother, Chancey Yarrow.[322]
    • Chancey is believed by her daughter to have been killed during a Nihil raid on their ship, the Switchback. Chancey had been presented an opportunity to pursue her personal gravity well projector project by the Nihil Lourna Dee with the help of Mari San Tekka.[322]
    • Jedi Cohmac Vitus, Vernestra Rwoh, Reath Silas, and Imri Cantaros assist in repelling the Nihil attack on Tiikae.[322]
    • Genetian civil war[322]
    • A Twi'lek assassin member of the Nihil assassinates the Gungan Professor Thaddeus Wolk on Coruscant for correctly theorizing that they have created a Gravity well projector superweapon in the Berenge sector. Shortly before the assassination, Wolk's building on Coruscant is bombed, leading to Jedi Vernestra Rwoh and Imri Cantaros attempting to rescue Wolk from the building. However, Wolk is shot and killed by the assassin before they can escape.[322]
    • The Nihil ambush the Jedi on the Graf family space station Everbloom, after they went there to investigate Wolk's assassination and the Graf's dealings with the Nihil. Reath Silas, Imri Cantaros, and Sylvestri Yarrow are captured by the Nihil.[322]
    • A battle is fought at the Nihil space station Gravity's Heart, located in the barren Berenge sector, and contained the gravity well projector developed by Chancey Yarrow, where the Republic and the Jedi Order destroy the station and rescue Reath Silas, Imri Cantaros and Sylvestri Yarrow, who had been unaware of her mother's plotting with the Nihil. During the battle, Jedi Knight Vernestra Rwoh encounters the dying hyperspace prospector Mari San Tekka, who gives her one final route to a location on the edge of the galaxy.[322]
    • Resulting from the Nihil's devastating raid on the Valo Republic Fair and the Jedi's defeat of the Drengir, the Republic Defense Coalition and Jedi Order launch Operation: Counterstrike, a counter-piracy military operation headed by Jedi Masters Stellan Gios and Avar Kriss against the Nihil.[234]
      • A battle is fought between the Nihil and the Republic Defense Coalition on the planet Carlac, ending with a Republic victory and the capture of several pirates as prisoners, who are taken to Starlight Beacon.[234]
      • Forces of the Republic Defense Coalition and Jedi Order led by Marshal Avar Kriss engage the Nihil on Magaveene. As the battle is ongoing, the Frozian Jedi Knight Nooranbakarakana is sent to assist Kriss with a drift of Jedi Vectors from Starlight Beacon, resulting in a victory for the Jedi and Republic forces.[234]
      • The Republic engage the Nihil in the Dreighton Nebula, resulting in a victory for the Republic Defense Coalition.[234]
      • The Unified Republic Task Force and members of the Jedi Order battle the Nihil on the planet Aris, ending with a major victory for the Jedi and Republic.[323]
      • The Jedi and the Republic Defense Coalition defeat the Nihil on the planet Golrath.[323]
      • The Unified Republic Task Force achieves a mayor victory against the Nihil on Port Haven.[323]
    • Combined forces of the Republic and the Jedi Order fight Nihil raiders lead by Lourna Dee, who are attempting to capture Relay Post Epsilon One at the direction of Marchion Ro. When it becomes clear that the Nihil will not be able to capture the station, Dee fires on the station with a turbolaser, setting off a chain reaction leading to its destruction. After the battle ends with a Galactic Republic and Jedi Order victory, the Republic captures most of the Nihil forces, including the entire crew of the cruiser Lourna Dee and Lourna Dee herself. But the Republic is unaware Dee is in custody, believing her to have died in the battle.[234]
    • Lourna Dee and other prisoners of the Restitution defend it against Pan Eyta and his Tempest. Dee claims the Restitution for herself, reforms her Tempest, and rejoins the Nihil.[234]
    • The Infiltration of the Nihil Tempest occurs.[323]
      • The Jedi stage a skirmish in the Soola system so that Jedi Knights Keeve Trennis and Terec are brought into Zeetar's Tempest on Xais by the Nihil.[323]
      • After having their identities revealed by a captive Myarga the Hutt, Trennis and Terec fight the Nihil and attempt to escape, but are stopped by Lourna Dee using the Great Leveler.[324] Jedi arrive to rescue Trennis and Terec,[325] but Dee escapes.[326]
  • Between 231 BBY and 230 BBY
    • The Nihil attempt to assassinate Chancellor Soh on Corellia as part of preparation for their increased efforts on Corellia.[327][328]
    • The Nihil perform a raid on a vault on Quilken, however, it is secretly a trap set by Porter Engle, who duels Lourna Dee and captures the Storm Slombo.[329][330]
  • 230 BBY
    • Nihil pirates from Kara Xoo's Tempest destroy, raid, and loot the spaceport on the planet Haileap. They also break into a lab and kidnap the inventor Avon Starros who was working there with scientist Glenna Kip. Jedi Master Jorinda Boffrey leads Jedi and civilians in fighting against the attackers, with Jedi Knight Kohta Jarik flying a Jedi Vector in the fight, and the Nihil quickly depart after causing massive damage to Port Haileap.[185]
    • The Nihil attack the Maawat Mountains on Dalna.[185]
    • The Kara Xoo's Tempest, including Deva Lompop, attack the people of Hon-Tallos, using flamethrowers and a Spider Cruiser to destroy the city.[185]
    • Vernestra Rwoh and Imri Cantaros take a mission to the planet Dalna to rescue Avon Starros from the Nihil.[185]
      • The Nihil attack the Dalnan capital city of Saludad.[185]
      • The Nihil and their scientist Zadina Mkampa carry out the Destruction of Dalna, a volcanic cataclysmic event triggered by Mkampa using a crystal matrix built into the planet's crust that produces groundquakes.[185]
      • Imri Cantaros organizes efforts on Starlight Beacon to evacuate the Dalnans off their planet. The station is towed through hyperspace to the Dalnan system by several starships, including the luxury liner Halcyon, allowing the population to be evacuated.[185]
    • A cyclone disaster as large as a continent devastates Eiram. In response, the Republic send Starlight beacon there to assist the world, as the Dalnan evacuation had proved the space station could be effective at disaster relief upon arriving over the site of a crisis.[331]
    • The Eiram celebration occurs as Starlight Beacon provides aid to Eiram.[331]
    • Marchion Ro and a group of Nihil devoted to him called the She'ar undertake a mission to Planet X to retrieve many members of the Nameless species. Driven by knowledge passed down through the generations of his family, Ro begins the mission by sacrificing many Nihil strikeships and their Nihil scout pilots to the planet's tempestuous outer layer he called the veil. By preoccupying the veil with the strikeships, Ro and the She'ar manage to sneak through it in the Cloudship Ikoru. After activating the Rod of Seasons in order to call the Nameless to him, Ro and the She'ar are attacked by many creatures and fauna of the planet, but they secure several Nameless aboard the Ikoru and escape the planet while many members of the She'ar are killed.[332]
    • The Jedi win a battle in the Nefitifi system against the Nihil. Multiple Jedi onboard a Longbeam cruiser are able to board and capture a Nihil starship, and the pirates swiftly surrender.[331]
    • The Jedi conduct many raids against the Nihil in the Ocktai system, capturing Nan and Chancey Yarrow, who are imprisoned on Starlight Beacon.[331]
    • The Great Jedi Rumble Race is hosted by Jedi Masters Torban Buck and Kantam Sy for the Padawans and other denizens of Starlight Beacon to serve as a relieve for the stress caused by the ongoing Nihil conflict.[333]
    • Despite the protests of her friend Master Gios, Avar Kriss leaves Starlight to lead the Battle for No-Space and gives into her rage, nearly executing Lourna Dee.[334]
    • A Jedi-Republic task force, led by Jedi Master Torban Buck and Padawan Lula Talisola, begins a hunt to bring Krix Kamerat to justice.[335]
      • After the Jedi are informed of Kamerat's location, a Nihil Encampment on the planet Dol'har Hyde, by the Padawan Bibs who had been sent to investigate the source of a distress call, the Republic sends the task force to assault the Nihil Encampment and capture Kamerat. A battle is fought between the task force and Krix Kamerat's cell, and after an engagement in the planet's atmosphere, Padawans Talisola, Qort, and Farzala Tarabal, as well as Zeen Mrala, make their way to Kamerat's saferoom where they are able to capture him.[335]
    • The Nihil, on the orders of Marchion Ro, begin an infiltration of the planet Corellia. Aided by the Father of Finances Nomar Tralmat, the Nihil are able to hide hundreds of themselves amongst the Coronet municipal police, with Tralmat hiring the undercover marauders on the claim they will help with security for new starships being built in the Santhe Shipyards of Coronet City.[336]
      • A Nihil attack in Coronet City tips the Jedi off to the Nihil's infiltration.[336] Zeen Mrala leaves her friends to help investigate. Farzala Tarabal and Qort are knighted.[337]
      • A union dispute on Gus Talon is manipulated by the Nihil.
        • The Nihil secretly carry out a bombing at Gus Talon University to draw the Jedi to the union dispute and distract them from their operations on Corellia.[336]
      • The Battle of the Corellian Shipyards is fought on the planet Corellia. Alys "Crash" Ongwa and members of her company fight alongside Jedi Masters Kantam Sy, Cohmac Vitus, and Yoda who returns from his absence, Padawans Reath Silas and Ram Jomaram, and Zeen Mrala against Nihil forces led by Sabata Krill, who attempt to steal ships from Coronet City's shipyards. The Nihil forces are prevented from stealing the Republic ships and their plot to ambush Starlight Beacon foiled. After the battle is over, Reath Silas is knighted and Cohmac Vitus leaves the Jedi Order, after expressing doubts towards the Jedi Order's way of dealing with the Nihil threat.[336]
    • The Nihil conduct a series of simultaneous raids and attacks to draw people toward Starlight Beacon, including:[331]
      • The Attack on Aleen[331]
      • The Attack on Japeal[331]
      • The Attack on Tais Brabbo[331]
      • The Attack on Yeksom[331]
      • The Attack in the Echerta system[331]
    • Jedi Emerick Caphtor and his partner Sian Holt lead an attack on a Nihil convoy and capture Kisma Uttersond.[338]
    • The Nihil decimate the Jedi Temple on Chespea and kill all Jedi within.[331]
    • Destruction of Starlight Beacon[331]
      • The Nihil unleash the Nameless on the station, mentally disturbing the Jedi on the station and preventing them from sensing their sabotage of the station. The Nameless kill many Jedi who attempt to investigate.[331]
      • Nihil saboteurs blow up the station, sending it in a collision course with Eiram and cutting off all of its communications.[331]
      • Avar Kriss and her team return from No-Space on the Ataraxia as the station explodes.[331] They board in an attempt to help, but are attacked by the Nameless. Sskeer stays behind to fight them off and Trennis rescues Terec and Ceret from the station.[339]
      • Emerick Caphtor and Sian Holt board the crashing station and help Torban Buck and Qort save a large group of Jedi younglings. Caphtor faces a Nameless and retrieves one of its limbs and a sample of its blood, but Dr. Uttersond escapes.[340]
      • Jedi Master Estala Maru holds the station together with the Force for an extended period, but it eventually splits in two and the top half burns up.[331]
      • Bell Zettifar uses an explosion to detonate the station's medical tower, which is safely pulled away by a medical frigate.[331]
      • Elzar Mann wrongly assumes that Chancey Yarrow is attempting to sabotage the station when she's trying to save it. He kills her, dooming the station.[331]
      • Stellan Gios sacrifices himself to stay on the station and pilot it away from Barraza City on Eiram. After it crashes into the ocean, many other Jedi remain missing.[331]
    • During the Nihil celebration, Marchion Ro makes a grand declaration to the galaxy in which he reveals himself as the leader of the Nihil and gloats of his victory to the Republic and the Jedi.[331]
    • The Nihil attack the Jedi Temple on Banchii in an attempt to retrieve Azlin Rell's journal, but are forced to retreat after killing many.[341]
    • Wookiee Jedi Master Kelnacca takes Yarzion Vell as his Padawan after the latter's master Lulad died on Starlight Beacon.[342]
    • The Nihil, led by Baron Boolan, attack the planet Valo. During the attack, the Jedi youngling Gavi Takitaken witness his Jedi Master, Aebal, being killed by a Nameless, though he manages to escape and live in the ruins of the Lonisa City Zoo for months along with fellow younglings Kildo and Tep Tep. As a result of the attack, Valo is consumed by the Nihil and placed behind its Stormwall in the Occlusion Zone. Along with Aebal, Master Eicho and all Jedi Masters of the Valo Temple are killed.[343]
    • The Nihil expand their territory using the Stormwall, engulfing ten sectors of space within their Occlusion Zone. Avar Kriss and Porter Engle are among the Jedi trapped behind the Stormwall.[182] When the Nihil deploy the Stormseeds that create the Stormwall, the Republic Defense Coalition Outreach Fleet is destroyed.[344]
    • The Hutts take advantage of the chaos caused by the Nihil to annex many systems and locations, including most of the Kastolar sector.[345]
    • The Jedi Council recalls all of the order's members across the galaxy to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant in response to the threat posed by the Nameless.[182] Most Jedi comply, but some, like Bell Zettifar, who begins his search for Burryaga Agaburry on Eiram, refuse.[346]
  • 229 BBY
    • A week after the fall of Starlight Beacon, the Jedi Council invokes the Guardian Protocols, accelerating Jedi learners' elevation and preparing the Jedi for more active strikes against the Nihil.[182]
    • Ten days after the fall of Starlight beacon, Elzar Mann launches his search for Avar Kriss[347]
    • The Nihil, at the direction of Marchion Ro, excavate the Banchii Jedi temple in search of Azlin Rell's journal hidden there. Ro recruits Vol Garat, an old member of the Path of the Open Hand and the last person to see the journal, to contribute to the search. Garat finds the droid ZZ-10, who along with Jedi Master Arkoff, stop the Nihil's efforts and kill Garat.[181]
    • 1 month after the Fall of Starlight Beacon
      • Affie Hollow reorganizes the Byne Guild into a new iteration designed to help protect traders from the Nihil and assist the Republic in stopping them.[348]
      • The Jedi Order stops building ornamental lightsabers and led by Jedi armorer Master Govena, produce battle sabers instead.[182]
    • Six weeks after the fall of Starlight Beacon, Bell Zettifar recovers Burryaga Agaburry from a cave at the bottom of the Maw of Shareen on Eiram.[346]
    • Two months after the fall of Starlight Beacon, Bell Zettifar and Burryaga Agaburry return to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant and are knighted.[346]
    • 3 months after the fall of Starlight Beacon
      • The Jedi Council rejects Elzar Mann's request to launch a recue mission behind the Nihil Stormwall to rescue trapped Jedi.[182]
      • Yoda reveals he has found fallen Jedi Azlin Rell, and presents him to the rest of the council, announcing his plans to utilize Rell's vast research on the Nameless.[182]
    • 4 months after the fall of Starlight Beacon
      • Yoda works with Azlin Rell to uncover the mystery of the Nameless and discovers the man's instability.[182]
      • Ghirra Starros convinces Marchion Ro to establish the Occlusion Zone as a proper Nihil state and appoints herself leader of a Ministry of Information.[349]
      • A resistance effort on the planet Sarumo against the Nihil begins. Abediah Viess, a retired mercenary and resident of the planet, agrees to lead their forces against the Nihil. Instead, once control of their weapons is given to her, she kills the planet's leader and decimates the resistance. Knowing Marchion Ro was watching, she contacts him and requests to join the Nihil as one of his leaders. Viess becomes a General and the leader of a Nihil Ministry of Protection.[349]
      • After discovering Avar Kriss is on the planet Allard, Nihil General Viess launches an attack on the planet and launches a campaign to ravage it in search of her. Kriss remains hidden.[347]
    • 5 months after the fall of Starlight Beacon
      • By this point, the Nihil main base has been established on Hetzal Prime, placed on the agricultural planet to control food shipments throughout the Occlusion Zone.[349]
      • Chancellor Soh creates teams designed to protect Republic space from Nihil emerging from the Stormwall, and requests the Jedi join them.[182]
      • Marchion Ro recruits scientist Boolan from a prison the Nihil captured to experiment on the Nameless as leader of a Ministry of Advancement and gives him the title of Baron.[349]
    • 6 months after the fall of Starlight Beacon
      • The Jedi join Soh's RDC forces in a Jedi-Republic task force to the edge of the Stormwall. The Jedi are led by Grand Master Pra-Tre Veter and include Bell Zettifar and Burryaga Agaburry and the RDC forces are led by Admiral Kronara.[346]
      • Azlin Rell and Yoda discover a way to the Nameless's homeworld may be hidden with the Leffbruk family on Travyx Prime. Behind Yoda's back, Rell mind controls the pilot of a massive starship and makes it crash into Travyx Prime's city, preventing the way to the Nameless from being recovered.[182]
    • Seven months after the fall of Starlight Beacon, Elzar Mann successfully sends a message to Avar Kriss through the Force using a Tythonic resonator. Kriss, still on Allard, uses this as motivation to attack the Nihil head-on on the planet.[347]
    • Nine months after the fall of Starlight Beacon, the RDC learns of a raid at Solunus, a location on the planet Asternin and dispatch Pra-Tre Veter, Bell Zettifar, and Burryaga Agaburry to help. The Jedi are ambushed by Nihil forces led by Melis Shryke, who use a 47 lightsaber nullifier to gain the upper hand. Zettifar and Agaburry evacuate the citizens and bring them to safety, but Veter is captured by the Nihil.[346]
    • After a battle near Genetia, Vernestra Rwoh gives up on the Jedi effort against the Nihil and becomes a Wayseeker.[350]
    • A civil war occurs on Corellia between those wanting to embrace Nihil rule and those wanting to reject it.[21]
      • Jedi Master Conithr and her Padawan Ruu defy the Jedi recall order to defend the Corellians from the Nihil.[351]
      • At some point, Conithr is killed by the Nihil and Ruu falls to the dark side, using a Nihil mask as a disguise to continue attacking them.[351]
      • Ruu discovers a Nihil factory constructing scav droids on Corellia. Crash Ongwa and Svino Atchapat find him there and convince him to help them take down the factory and then join their crew.[351]
    • Porter Engle ambushes a Nihil patrol on Exu Prime and steals their shuttle.[352]
    • The people of Hetzal attempt an uprising but are defeated by General Viess's forces[352]
    • Vernestra Rwoh helps Tanabi Yar defeat a monopoly held by the Confederation of Haulers on Miekos. Yar shows Rwoh passage to the peaceful planet Ibbe to heal.[350]
    • Kelnacca and Yarzion Vell fight the Nihil on the Occlusion Zone planet Vianax.[342]
    • The RDC and Jedi combat the Hutts in an effort to free the worlds retaken by them after the Nihil's establishment of the Occlusion Zone.[345]
      • RDC forces and Jedi led by Keeve Trennis liberate the planet Ena from Tregarra the Hutt.[353]
      • After combatting the Hutts on Gallimere, Keeve Trennis protects it from a subsequent Nihil attack.[354]
      • The RDC recaptures Ubrikkia from the Hutts.[345]
      • The RDC recaptures Ookbat from the Hutts.[345]
      • The RDC combats the Hutts on Cols.[345]
    • The Nihil Occupation of Valo continues[343]
      • On Valo, the Nihil host a Dark Fair, a week-long celebration leading up to the anniversary of the destruction of Starlight Beacon intended as a cruel parody of the Republic Fair. Ram Jomaram, now operating on the alias "Scarlet Skull," attacks it. He meets younglings Gavi, Kildo, and Tep Tep.[343]
      • Master Kunpar Vasivola is captured by the Nihil from the Valo Jedi temple. Ram Jomaram and his youngling friends infiltrate Lonisa City's city hall to learn where they are holding him. Gavi retrieves the datapad of the Nihil Commander Vark Tarpalin from his office, obtaining Master Vasivola's location.[343]
      • Ram and his crew, which begin calling themselves the Scarlet Skulls and are joined by kid pirate Zyle Keem, rescue Kunpar Vasivola from an underground prison. The group manages to escape the facility with the weakened Jedi Master by using micronite explosives to bypass the normal escape route.[343]
      • The Scarlet Skulls' hideout in the Lonisa City Zoo is attacked by the Nihil.[343]
      • As Boolan's protégé Niv Drendow Apruk attempts to retrieve mycopram from the sunken Innovator starship in Lake Lonisa, Ram and the Scarlet Skulls raid it for bacta and successfully make it airborne again, reclaiming it for the Jedi and Republic. Gavi remains on Valo while Kildo and Tep Tep go to help the Jedi elsewhere in the galaxy.[343]
    • The Nihil bombard the planet Ribento[352]
    • On the anniversary of the fall of Starlight Beacon, Marchion Ro broadcasts a live execution of Jedi Grand Master Pra-Tre Veter.[352]
    • Stuck in the Occlusion Zone, Avar Kriss and the pilot Belin undertake a mission to the planet Prandril to deliver grain.[352]
    • Porter Engle goes on a mission on the planet Daedus to retrieve an old EX communication droid, believing it could potentially pass through the Stormwall.[352]
    • After discovering a cryptic message from the Nihil warlord Tarktak Vil, Zeen Mrala goes on a mission to the moon Bracront. There, she discovers that Vil is actually her long lost lover Lula Talisola,[355] who experienced amnesia after Starlight's fall.[356]
  • 228 BBY[357]
    • The Jedi launch a raid on the Nihil prison ship Korvix Vorn and rescue its captives, including many Jedi believed to have died on Starlight Beacon such as Torban Buck and Farzala Tarabal.[358]
    • The Republic Occlusion Zone attack fleet launches an assault on the Stormwall, destroying several of the Stormseeds creating it. However, when they then try to enter the Occlusion Zone, they discover that the Stormwall has already healed itself and the fleet is destroyed.[352]
    • In response to the assault on the Stormwall, Marchion Ro expands it, bringing more worlds into the Occlusion Zone.[352]
    • Joss and Pikka Adren are sent by Admiral Kronara on a mission to recover an abandoned Nihil ship at the edge of the Stormwall. The expansion of the Stormwall occurs while they are there, trapping them within the Occlusion Zone.[359]
    • After being trapped in the Occlusion Zone, Joss and Pika Adren's ship the Aurora III is attacked by Nihil scav droids.[360]
    • When Avar Kriss and reporter Rhil Dairo meet with a Nihil defector named Quith Meglar on Hetzal Prime who promises them a way out of the Occlusion Zone, they are ambushed by General Viess and her forces, who kill Meglar, but retreat once Jedi Master Porter Engle arrives.[352]
    • Above Seswenna, Avar Kriss and Porter Engle assault the Nihil starship Cacophony in the hopes of capturing it and using its Path drive to escape. After Kriss captured the ship, the Path drive was destroyed, and Engle was lost, Kriss used the hyperspace data from an EX communications droid to successfully escape the Occlusion Zone in the ship, with Melis Shryke in custody.[352]
    • The Battle of Seswenna occurs between the Nihil and Republic on Seswenna, which results in a Nihil victory.[361]
    • On Ibbe, Vernestra Rwoh is approached by Avon Starros, who reveals that Rwoh's Padawan Imri Cantaros is alive and in the Occlusion Zone. She agrees to join Starros and others on a trip behind the Stormwall using a ship provided by Starros's friend, the Nihil Deva Lompop.[361]
    • After discovering Cantaros on Aricho, Rwoh joins him and others in an attack on a Nihil processing facility to free children and civilians being forced to work there. They are successful, and also capture a Nihil leader named Kara Xoo. Afterward, Rwoh knights Cantaros, and leaves him on Aricho so he can continue helping the people there.[361]
    • Jordanna Sparkburn joins Starros's trip into the Occlusion Zone, taking a mission to find her lost family members. After being joined by her cousin Cair San Tekka on Seswenna, the pair track their family to Arli's moon. Once there, they discover that the moon has been overrun by a blight that causes an effect similar to the Nameless's calcification of Force-sensitives. They successfully rescue their family, but Cair's hand is infected by the blight and is amputated and contained in a stasis field by Jordanna.[361]
    • Avon Starros and Xylan Graf travel from Seswenna to Lightning Crash station, where the Nihil are controlling the Stormwall. The Nihil scientist running it reveals that the station is rigged to cause disaster across hyperspace in the event of her death, thwarting their mission to destroy it.[361]
    • After Starros, Graf, San Tekka, Sparkburn, and Rwoh regroup on Seswenna, they flee scav droids in Deva Lompop's ship. After discovering the Path drive is unusable, Starros provides her Sunvale device, which allows them to travel through the Stormwall but only with the help of Rwoh manually performing precise hyperspace jumps with the Force. Rwoh returns to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant, Starros remains on Coruscant under the alias Avon Sunvale, and Cair San Tekka's hand is delivered to the Jedi for study.[361]
    • Jedi and their allies prevent the Korvix Vorn's Warden from landing on Eriadu in the Star Hopper, which is now controlled by the Nihil.[358]
    • The Jedi Padawan Ruu, under orders of Alys Ongwa, kidnappes the Togruta Nihil pirate Haakon Pav and interrogates him for coordinates to traverse across the Stormwall. After obtaining the coordinates, Ruu kills him.[362]
    • Ruu and the Nihil Driggit Parse, travel to the hidden depot operated by the Lasat Frander, in order to obtain the schematics for constructing a Spiral-class Viz-Core Eviscerator. Ultimately the schematics are retrieved by the Nihil.[363]
    • The Massacre at the Greens occurs on Corellia, ending the civil war with the Nihil presence on the planet being wiped out.[364]
    • Crash Ongwa and her crew take a mission to Rekelos in search of the former Nihil Krogger Sheem, hoping to get info out of him. He provides them with the name of the person who led the infiltration of Corellia: Krix Kamerat.[365]
    • Crash Ongwa and her crew, primarily her bodyguard Ruu, take a mission to Ersham Prison with the intention of killing Krix Kamerat. However, once there, Ongwa realizes Kamerat may be able to help them kill Marchion Ro, so she frees him instead.[365]
    • With the Stormwall at the edge of Eriadu, the Jedi begin to evacuate children and non-essential personnel from the planet. While doing so, Jedi come under fire from Nihil fighters over the Eriaduan city Bri-Phrang.[366] The Jedi ship explodes, but all of the Jedi escape. Afterward, Zeen Mrala proposes marriage to Lula Talisola.[364]
    • The Nihil invade the planet Eriadu.[367]
      • As part of the invasion, the Nihil blockade Bri-Phrang City, trapping inside many Jedi and Republic operatives.[368]
    • Jedi Knights Burryaga Agaburry and Bell Zettifar undertake a mission to the moon Felne 6 to investigate reports of Drengir, where they find a dying member of the species.[369]
    • Burryaga Agaburry and Bell Zettifar travel to the planet Oanne to convince the local Elia-An to evacuate before the Stormwall expands and leaves the world inside the Occlusion Zone.[369]
    • Bell Zettifar and Burryaga Agaburry go on a mission to the planet Ronaphaven in order to investigate the Drengir, but they find the blight on the planet instead.[369]
    • The Jedi Order and Galactic Republic carry out missions across the Stormwall in order to rescue individuals who are trapped in the Nihil's Occlusion Zone region of space and return them to Republic space.[369]
      • Vernestra Rwoh undertakes a six-day mission during which she rescues a resistance cell from the Occlusion Zone.[369]
    • The Republic Defense Coalition and Jedi Order attack the Lightning Crash, a Nihil space station which houses a crystal array that powers the Stormwall barrier, in order to take out the Stormwall's power. Once the attack is a success and the station destroyed, the Republic Defense Coalition and Jedi begin operations to round up any surviving Nihil as prisoners. After Marchion Ro learns that the Lightning Crash had been destroyed, he orders for his flagship, the Gaze Electric, to become the Stormwall's new power source.[369]
    • Naboo is occupied by the Nihil.[369]
      • The planet Naboo is conquered by forces led by General Abediah Viess. The regional and planetary governments ultimately surrender to Viess, and the reigning Naboo queen manages to escape with the help of her guard and goes into hiding.[369]
      • Forces from the Jedi Order and the Republic Defense Coalition led by Jedi Master Avar Kriss liberate Naboo from the Nihil occupation, but General Abediah Viess escapes.[369]
    • Hunt for Viess.[369]
      • Mission to Viess' manor.[369]
      • Nihil Minister of Protection Abediah Viess and Jedi Master Porter Engle duel on the planet Isolt. Eventually Viess escapes.[369]
    • The Jedi Order undertakes a search in an attempt to hunting down and stopping the Eye of the Nihil Marchion Ro. After they are provided a series of locations Ro had visited by Nihil Minister of Information Ghirra Starros, the Jedi determine he is investigating the plague known as the blight, which they also believe can be linked with a reappearance of the Drengir.[369]
      • A skirmish is fought when the Jedi Order undertakes a mission to the planet Vixoseph I as part of its search for Marchion Ro, who was on Vixoseph trying to find out whether he could replant the blight and use a Drengir to spread it through the carnivorous plant's hive mind. When Ro notices that the Jedi arrive, he sends one of his Nameless creatures to kill them. Through the efforts of Burryaga Agaburry, and Avar Kriss and Elzar Mann, however, the Jedi are able to kill the Nameless. The Eye is confronted by the arrival of Jedi Knight Vernestra Rwoh. After she attempts to kill a Drengir that had began to calcify as a result of the Nameless' effects, Ro interrupts and stopps Rwoh, telling her to not interfere. While the Drengir died, because of its hive mind, it spread the husking to another Drengir, successfully completing what the Eye had wanted. Ro is picked up by his secretary Thaya Ferr in his flagship, the Gaze Electric, and departs the planet.[369]
      • Thraisai mission.[370]
    • A battle is fought at the planet Ballum, at the edge of the Occlusion Zone, between Jedi and Republic forces led by Commander Velko Jahen and Jedi Master Keeve Trennis, against the Nihil pirates, led by Envoy Affanar, and their allies of the Hutt Clan represented by Skarabda the Wise. During the fight, the Jedi Santar is killed, but the Nihil forces are defeated with the loss of Envoy Affanar, the Child of the Storm Lycos, and one of their Nameless.[345]
    • The Star Cruiser Ataraxia is captured from Lourna Dee by Keeve Trennis with support from Republic Defense Coalition and Hutt Clan forces. Following the battle on Ballum, in which Skarabda learned that Dee killed her sister Myarga, Skarabda orders Dee eliminated and enters an alliance with the Republic and Jedi. In the space above Ballum, Dee attempts to flee with the Ataraxia, which she named the Lourna Dee, as the combined forces pursue. Trennis manages to breach the Ataraxia's hull, where she fights various droids send by Dee. Meanwhile, RDC Commander Velko Jahen uses the Longbeam cruiser Gios's tractor beam to hold the Ataraxia in place. Dee counters by straining the ship's engines and dumping crates filled with civilians into the path of the tractor beam, forcing Jahen to turn it off. Jedi Ai-Dan Yelooc uses the Force to direct the loose crates of people into the Gios for medical care. Following a confrontation in the Ataraxia's bridge, Trennis stuns Dee, captures her, and secures control of the Ataraxia.[371]
    • Mission to Kindosorn.[372]
    • Funeral of Goonral Monshi.[373]
    • Members of the Jedi Order, including Keeve Trennis and Sskeer, along with the marauder Lourna Dee, and the Sephi Tey Sirrek carry out a mission to rescue a Yacombe girl from Shingcu Station, a space station within the atmosphere of the gas giant Chenley Prime that serves as a base of operations for Baron Boolan, the Minister of Advancement of the Nihil.[374]
    • Rescue of Sskeer.[375]
    • Mission to Cethis.[212]
    • Jedi Master Eve Byre and her Padawan Zaph Mora undertake a mission to deliver supplies to the Oisinians of the planet Oisin, transported there by Affie Hollow and her crew aboard the Vessel. During the mission, Mora is killed by the Nihil Child of the Storm Sicarus, who had been sent by Baron Boolan to eliminate the Jedi, while Sicarus decides to capture Byre and take her to Boolan.[212]
    • Former Jedi Master Cohmac Vitus travels to the planet Thall to investigate the Tolemite, a lost civilization which resided on Thall. Concurrently to Vitus' mission, the Child of the Storm Sicarus arrives to Thall to hunt the former Jedi. Cohmac Vitus learns the location of the Tolemite homeworld, and manages to escape Sicarus.[376]
    • A team consisting of 13 Republic Defense Coalition personnel, along with Jedi Master Mirro Lox and his Padawan Amadeo Azzazzo go on a mission to a Nihil base on the planet Angoth, where they successfully capture a Nameless specimen, but Mirro Lox dies defending his Padawan from a Nameless.[212]
    • Cohmac Vitus and Reath Silas undertake an expedition to the planet Tolis, to investigate the knowledge of the Tolemites, who lived on the world before being destroyed, about the Nameless and blight. While on the world, they defeat the Jedi hunter Sicarus, who is killed along with two of the Nameless.[212]
    • Attack on Tortle Cove Outpost.[376]
    • Members of the Jedi Order and the Republic Defense Coalition go on a mission to investigate a distress beacon from the supposedly evacuated planet of Palagosal. During their time there, the Jedi rescue Churo, as well as the survivors of the attack on Tortle Cove Outpost, and capture three live Nameless, but Jedi Master Ada-Li Carro dies in the process.[376]
    • Mission to Mynos Three.[376]
    • The Jedi Reath Silas, Cohmac Vitus and Amadeo Azzazzo go on a mission to the planet Varovia, where they recover the missing Rod of Ages.[377]
    • The monster hunter Ty Yorrick rescues several Force-sensitive children in the Osler community on the planet Temerancé who has been abducted by a Nihil Cloud, killing several Nihil in the process, including the Cloud leader.[378]
    • Kitrep Soh, the son of Republic Supreme Chancellor Lina Soh, and his boyfriend Jom Lariin are rescued on the planet Abtin, inside the Occlusion Zone, by Ty Yorrick and Drewen Qweebjillan. During the mission, Ty Yorrick confronts her former Master Cibaba, now part of the Children of the Storm, and convinces her to leave the Nihil.[379]
    • Jedi Master Savina Besatrix Malagán, the ZZ-model droid ZZ-10, and the Banchiian Shosho undertake a mission to the moon Astenban to prevent the Nihil from taking control of a base there. On Astenban, the group encounters the Force-sensitive creature Zimtok, who decides to assist the group. Malagán successfully destroys the base, but while fighting the Nihil afterword, Zimtok is captured by a Nihil and Shosho stowes away on a Nihil starship to attempt to save her.[380]
    • Nihil pirates led by the scientist Niv Drendow Apruk go on a mission to the planet Banchii where they face the Jedi Order, the native Banchiians, and the Drengir. The Jedi and their allies are able to seal Apruk's Force-consuming Nameless creatures within bunkers, forcing the Nihil to retreat.[380]
    • Grand Master Yoda goes a mission to a Nihil satellite after he feels a disturbance in the Force that had come from the station. After arriving, Yoda discovers he has been lured to the satellite by the Nihil, who intend to kill him with the Great Leveler. While there, Yoda rescues a family of Twi'leks who had been captured by the Nihil to lure Yoda to the station, ultimately escaping despite the Nihil's attempts to kill him. During his encounter with the Leveler, Yoda is emphatic towards the creature's plight, which allows him to escape its affects and communicate with it through the Force. Through the encounter, Yoda learns it is haunted by the deaths it had been forced to carry out from its hunger and simply wishes to return to its homeworld, Sophros.[381]
    • The Battle on Banchii is fought when the Nihil, led by Minister of Advancement Baron Boolan, attack the planet Banchii. Boolan initially travels there in hopes of recruiting the various Jedi there, including Jedi Knights Lily Tora-Asi and Reath Silas, fallen Jedi Azlin Rell, and Jedi Masters Savina Besatrix Malagán and Arkoff for his experiments, with the promise that he will not harm the people of Banchii if they complied. When the Jedi in charge, Tora-Asi, refuses, Boolan sends his horde of Nihil pirates to attack the planet and the Jedi. As Tora-Asi fights the Nihil, the plants of the planet begin to attack them too, as members of the Drengir trigger the plant life to protect Tora-Asi in their stead after the Jedi helped them flee Banchii. As the Baron starts to flee, he releases Nameless at the Jedi. However, the Jedi are assisted by a large group of non-Force-sensitive Banchii natives as well as a group of Banchiians led by Shosho, a Nihil defector named Maktan Plynt, and Malagán's friend Dexter Jettster, who defeat the Nameless. Meanwhile, Boolan makes one final plea to Rell to join him and study the Nameless, but Rell refuses and choses to protect Jedi Initiates Viv'Nia Nia'Viv and Nima Allets instead. With the Nameless defeated, the Jedi drive the Nihil away, winning the battle and successfully protecting the planet.[382]
    • A Jedi and Republic team, including Jedi Master Eve Byre, Padawan Cam Lindon, and Jedi younglings Kildo and Tep Tep, carry out a scientific mission to investigate the blight. While on the mission, they receive a distress call from the planet Eiram from Jedi Master Ackmora, requesting assistance in dealing with a situation that had arisen in Barraza City.[383]
    • Escape on Eiram.[383]
      • An incident takes place on the planet Eiram when the Drengir, previously believed to have been defeated at Mulita, begin kidnapping citizens of the Eirami city Barraza City in an attempt to escape the planet, as the spread of the blight heads to Eiram. The Drengir are ultimately defeated by citizens of the city, as well as several Jedi, Republic Defense Coalition members, and former Nihil that had formed the Hurricanes gang. Resulting from the joint effort, tensions between the citizens of Barraza and the Jedi ease.[383]
      • Jedi Master Eve Byre, her Padawan Cam Lindon and the two Jedi Initiates Kildo and Tep Tep undertake an expedition to the wreckage of Starlight Beacon, to investigate if the Great Progenitor is still alive.[384]
      • A battle is fought in Arovia park, at the center of Eiram's Barraza City, between two factions of the Drengir; one led by the Great Progenitor and supported by the Jedi Order; and one controlled by Saxl Neth, a human Nihil. One Saxl Neth is defeated, the Drengir and the Jedi make peace, the Drengir escape Eiram in the Innovator, heading for the Wookiee homeworld of Kashyyyk.[384]
    • Capture of Boolan and Sskeer.[385]
    • Jedi Masters Keeve Trennis and Avar Kriss, alongside bounty hunter and former Nihil Tempest Runner Lourna Dee, spearhead a search for ailing Jedi Master Sskeer and Nihil Minister of Advancement Baron Boolan, after the two are captured by Muglan Tarantyne's crew and brought into the custody of Marchion Ro on the planet Heiklet. However, Sskeer and Boolan eventually escape Heiklet on their own and travel to Boolan's former home on the planet Dalna. There, they are recovered by the Jedi and brought to the planet Protobranch for medical attention, and so that Boolan can help the Republic researchers there find a solution for the blight spreading across the galaxy. However, Ro and the Nihil pursue Sskeer and Boolan to Protobranch, where Dee duels and defeats Ro, who warns that Boolan has created a virus that will kill all life and is pretending it is a cure for the blight. With Dee's warning, the Jedi and the Republic are able to contain the virus, provide an antidote to those already infected, and arrest Boolan.[385]
      • Skirmish on Port Hunavon.[385]
      • Massacre on Waskiro.[385]
      • Escape from Heiklet.[385]
      • Duel on Protobranch.[385]
    • The Republic Defense Coalition and Jedi Order undertake a joint relief mission to the planet Eroudac. Cerean Jedi Knight Sul-Nath-Rune is part of the mission, though becomes separated from his unit and encounters the Hutt Churo who had traveled to Eroudac in search of a way to heal the planet Nal Hutta.[386]
    • On the planet Valo, multiple Valons, aided by Jedi Initiate Gavi Takitaken, Jedi Master Kunpar Vasivola, Jedi Knight Farzala Tarabal, and Padawan Ram Jomaram, along with the pirate crew led by the Zabrak Tanana Keem, revolt against the occupying Nihil forces led by Herwint Tiks, with several Nihil dying in the fight.[387]
    • Alys "Crash" Ongwa and Ruu infiltrate the stronghold of the Nihil warlord Herwint Tiks and steal an EX droid from the fortress.[388]
    • The Nihil Marlem Glay leads her Cloud on a raid to the planet Baori Minor, though it is ultimately unsuccessful due to the blight.[370]
    • The moon Inad Komesh is evacuated by a team of Jedi and Republic Defense Coalition personnel led by Jedi Master Adampo, and including Padawan Amadeo Azzazzo, and Jedi Knights Burryaga, Vernestra Rwoh, Dez Rydan, Reath Silas, Indeera Stokes and Bell Zettifar. Although the citizens of the moon are successfully evacuated, Master Adampo perishes when he is crushed by a collapsing tower.[370]
    • Hundreds of Jedi Order gather for a service on the roof of the Jedi Temple on Coruscant to honor the Jedi Master Adampo of the Jedi High Council after he is killed during the evacuation of Inad Komesh. In many respects, it is felt the service essentially is for all of the Council members who had fallen during the Nihil conflict.[370]
    • Thraisai mission.[370]
    • The Jedi Order carries out a mission to the planet Kashyyyk to stop the spread of the blight after it is discovered there. The Kashyyyk team is led by Jedi Knight Reath Silas, and includes the Wookiee Jedi Burryaga Agaburry and Kelnacca, Jedi Knight Dez Rydan, Padawan Amadeo Azzazzo, the former Jedi Azlin Rell and Cohmac Vitus, the scientist Avon Starros, and the former Nihil Nan Hague. The team are transported to Kashyyyk by the crew of the Byne Guild starship Vessel, which consists of Affie Hollow, Leox Gyasi, and Geode. They also work to stop the Nihil scientist Doctor Zadina Mkampa from acquiring a Sith artifact known as the Thornseed. Both missions are ultimately successful, the blight threat is thwarted, Zadina Mkampa is taken into custody, and the Drengir threat is ended, but Geode sacrifices himself to complete the mission.[370]
    • Jedi Master Kantam Sy is involved in a hostage negotiation on the planet Ferrix, leaving Sy unable to join a delegation of Jedi Masters in traveling across the Stormwall into the Occlusion Zone to attend the wedding of their former Jedi Padawan Lula Talisola to the Mikkian Zeen Mrala on the planet Eriadu.[389]
    • A wedding is held in Bri-Phrang City on the planet Eriadu, where the brides Lula Talisola and Zeen Mrala are married, and officiated by Maz Kanata. The celebration is interrupted by the begining of the Battle of Eriadu.[389]
    • The Battle of Eriadu is fought between the Republic Stormwall Defense Fleet led by marshal Keeve Trennis, multiple Jedi on the ground led by Knight Farzala Tarabal, and the Eriaduans, mainly organized by the Tarkin family, against the Nihil Occlusion Zone Occupying Force led by Herwint Tiks and the Nihil fleet led by General Abediah Viess, for the control of the planet Eriadu. Outside factions who are opposed to the Nihil also arrive and join the battle, including the Togruta government, the Hutts, and the Mon Calamari. The battle is a violent affair as the Eriaduans, already a combative people, were enraged with the Nihil occupation of their homeworld and finally have a chance to overthrow their occupiers. The battle results in a Nihil surrender and the end of the Nihil conflict, with the deaths of Herwint Tiks, Abediah Viess, the Children of the Storm Sister H'Tar, and many of the Nihil Irregulars.[390]
    • The Tempest Breakers guild of bounty hunters founded by Lourna Dee and Quin Carree, as well as the Sephi Tey Sirrek, carry out a mission to the planet Naboo, under Nihil occupation,to find surviving members of Nihil Baron Boolan's Children of the Storm, who have scattered following Boolan's arrest. While on Naboo, the three are found by members of the Nihil, though Jedi Master Kelnacca and his Padawan Yarzion Vell arrive to help them. Afterward, the Jedi agree to help the three in their mission, with the Tempest Breakers and Sirrek agreeing to help liberate the planet in return.[391]
    • The Luminous Nine, a team of Jedi consisting of Masters Elzar Mann, Avar Kriss, Torban Buck, Knights Reath Silas, Bell Zettifar, Burryaga Agaburry, Terec, and former Jedi Ty Yorrick and Azlin Rell, undertake a mission to return the Nameless to their homeworld of Sophros to stop the Blight, while Marchion Ro travels to Sophros with the objective of kill all the Nameless and destroy the galaxy. During the mission, Master Buck is killed by one of the Nameless, and Ro produces the death of all Nameless brought to the planet by the Jedi. As a distraction for the Republic, Ro destroys the Gaze Electric and in the process brings down the Stormwall, but is eventually defeated by Bell and Burryaga and captured. Avar Kriss and Elzar Mann remain on Sophros to hold back the Blight's spread and maintain the balance between light and dark.[390]
    • Disaster on Estarvera.[390]
    • A trial for Marchion Ro is held in the Republic's Senate Building on the planet Coruscant. Following his capture on Sophros, Marchion Ro stands trial for numerous crimes, including the destruction of Starlight Beacon and the attempted murder of every being in existence. The trial concludes with Ro being sentenced to life in solitary confinement, with his prison to be constructed by droids in a randomly chosen location in deep space.[390]
    • A memorial service is held at the Jedi Temple on Coruscant for those who died in the Nihil conflict. The service is joyful, and hundreds of Jedi attend to honor the dead and laud their heroes. After the ceremony is over, the Guardian Protocols are rescinded.[390]
    • After Azlin Rell escapes in a ship during the mission to Sophros, Reath Silas undergoes a search for him, departing in a Jedi Vector and bringing both his shield and his lightsaber.[390]

Late High Republic Era (227 BBY – 100 BBY)[]

  • c. 227 BBY
    • Yarzion Vell tells Kelnacca about the meaning of his tattoos. Vell is knighted after saving the child Yesula, her father, and their droid Zeekay from a fire.[392]
  • c. 220 BBY[21]
    • By this point, the Blue Dragon pirates have begun wrecking significant damage on the city of Fire, remaining the single most destructive force to plague the station for the next twenty years.[393]
    • Zhang Jincheng begins to ascend the hierarchy of Fire.[394]
    • Jamie Brasson becomes the President of Begamor and is elected to represent the planet as a senator of the Republic Senate. While Brasson appears to be a benevolent politician, he indulges in corruption and transforms Begamor into a dictatorship. Brasson develops a cult of personality, brainwashing citizens with holographic programs worshipping himself, isolating his world from the Republic HoloNet, and enforcing strict surveillance over Begamor. An appearance of prosperity for the planet's capital city is maintained while poverty, slavery, and organ trading is left to fester in the streets.[395]
    • Ma, an executive of the Land & Sky Corporation that rules the Aakaash system, dies of suspicious circumstances.[396]
    • The Jedi Knight Ady Sun'Zee and her padawan Nooa travel to a training exercise on the Sacred Garden of the planet Valron.[397]
  • c. 217 BBY
    • Sean is born.[398]
    • Zhuang Yuanying is born.[399]
  • 205 BBY
    • A Mandalorian is employed as a gatekeeper at Jamie Brasson's Presidential manor.[400]
      • Huang begins paying off his debts to Li Yu by working as a member of Li's Knights of Silver Dawn.[401]
      • Zhang Jincheng ascends to the position of the Fire station's manager.[402]
  • 203 BBY
    • Jedi Master Mostima takes Sean as her Padawan learner. (Approximate date)[403]
    • The Devaronian Begamor underworld–serial killer Bellon is offered a shelter by Jamie Brasson, who hires Bellon as one of his chefs.[404]
  • 201 BBY
    • On Fire, the Knights of Silver Dawn, led by Li Yu, clash with the Blue Dragon, led by its leader, the dragon head. Li lets the dragon head escape with his life in the belief that it would be more profitable for the head to be alive than dead.[405]
    • Nan Moyou and Nan Holi go on a short vacation at the Nan family villa.[406]
    • Mostima and Sean study the Outer Rim's sectors, including Oplovis sector. In particular, the two Jedi study its Aakaash system.[407]
    • The industrial output of the distant, loosely Republic-affiliated Aakaash system exceeds that of the famous industrial world Metalorn.[68][408]
  • 200 BBY
    • At some point during the High Republic Era, Nihil raiders have torched the paradise planet Nelgenam[409] and the Jedi abandon the Jedi outpost on Elphrona.[196]
    • Chewbacca is born on Kashyyyk.[410]
    • The Jedi Mostima and Sean undertake a mission to Begamor.[68][411]
    • By this point, Sean had constructed his lightsaber with a crystal that he bonded with on Ilum.[68] As Ilum and its crystals grew in prominence for the Jedi, the number of Padawans traveling to Zoph for its Zophis crystal is decreased.[86]
    • Mostima and Sean undertake a mission to Aakaash, revealing the corruption of the ruling Land & Sky Corporation.[68]
    • By this point, legends of Jedi using the Force to destroy entire planets have spread to Nan Holi's star system.[68]
    • By this point, the Judicial Department's peacekeeping fleet had been established as military arbiters of the Republic.[68]
    • The Mining Guild had been founded and operational by this point.[68]
    • By this point, bacta had become a common medical substance in the Aakaash system and human replica droids were invented.[68]
  • 196 BBY
    • As Chelli Lona Aphra would later claim, Vaale's sewers were used for the last time by around this point. The Architects of Vaale had also inscribed Old Republic iconography in their sacred workshop in the city, warning of the dangerous potential of the Rings of Vaale.[412]
    • By this point, the cathedral-like ceiling of the landing pads of Basteel's Eladro City is carved.[413]
    • By this point, the Jowloon community had settled on Jaresh because they did not want change. They farm fertilizers on the fertile lands of Jowloon.[414]
  • c. 186 BBY
    • Indara is born.[415]
  • c. 184 BBY
    • Taibik Kajok is born.[416]
  • c. 181 BBY
    • Wiltoo Kajok is born.[417]
  • c. 180 BBY
    • Sol is born.[418]
  • c. 176 BBY
    • Sol joins the Jedi Order.[419]
  • c. 173 BBY
    • Torbin is born.[420]
  • 170 BBY
    • Torbin is given to the Jedi Order.[421]
  • c. 166 BBY
    • Cabuck is born on Drez.[422]
  • c. 163 BBY
    • Republic Senator Jakob Garn attempts to pass the Jedi Annexation Act, a bill to declare the Jedi Order an official branch of the Republic, in an attempt to claim Galactic Senate oversight of the Order.
  • c. 162 BBY
    • Con Star Mining Corporation establishes mining colonies on Parnassos.[423]
  • 161 BBY
    • A second wave of settlers arrive on the planet Jelucan and establishes mining operations. The second wave consider the ways of the initial settlers to be backward. The second-wave of settlers use the profits from their mining operations to become wealthy aristocrats, while the first wave settlers remain largely destitute.[424]
    • Indara becomes a Jedi Knight.(Approximate date)[425]
    • Five members of the Jedi Order, including Indara, undertake a mission to the planet Seswenna to mediate with miners who are upset with working conditions. While inside the mines of Seswenna, the roof collapses and four of the Jedi are killed instantly by rubble, while Jedi Knight Indara survives and is rescued a week later by a droid crew.(Approximate date)[425]
  • c. 160 BBY
    • The Strafes, a pirate group led by a Toydarian pirate, carries out attacks against the city of Norna, capital of the planet Cerifisis, stealing food rations arriving to the city.[426]
      • Jedi Master Vernestra Rwoh, supported by a security team from Norna led by the soldier Kavil, leads an attack on the Strafes after being contacted for help by Cerifisis' Council of Elders.[426]
      • Vernestra Rwoh, this time with Jedi Knight Indara, carries out a second attack against the Strafes at their stronghold on Cerifisis, capturing their leader.[426]
    • The Jedi Order begins an investigation into the production of four-seven lightsaber nullifiers, a prototype device that disrupts energy weapons, including blasters and Jedi lightsabers, preventing them from working, being manufactured by the arms dealer Nilsson Summach.[426]
      • Vernestra Rwoh and Indara undertake a mission to the planet Thelj, with the objective of safely escorting Senator Jakob Garn back to the planet Coruscant. In exchange, Garn will turn over the arms dealer Nilsson Summach. However, Garn is killed by Nilsson's crew before he can turn over the dealer.[426]
      • Rwo and Indara travel to the planet Ruathi, where they recruit the help of former Jedi Ty Yorrick, facing in the process the Gigoran Hinra, an enforcer from Nilsson's crew.[426]
      • Vernestra Rwoh, Indara and Ty Yorrick travel aboard Rwo's VKCE-7100x Starcutter Cantaros to the planet Pinara.[426]
      • Rwoh and her team are able to stop Nilsson Summach from manufacturing 47 lightsaber nullifiers at an engagement at the Kolnare Mine on the planet Septra, where Summach is killed by his employee Grizela.[426]
      • A trial is held for the Pantoran criminal Grizela on the planet Coruscant. Indara testifies in the trial in the place of Vernestra Rwoh.[426]
      • Florinda Jackard's trial.[426]
  • c. 158 BBY
    • Fillik is born.[427]
    • Mog Adana is born.[428]
  • c. 157 BBY
    • Yord Fandar is born.[429]
  • 156 BBY
    • Verosha Aniseya and Mae-ho Aniseya are born on the planet Brendok.[430]
  • c. 153 BBY
    • Jecki Lon is born.[431]
  • c. 151 BBY
    • Bazil is born.[432]
    • Tasi Lowa is born.[433]
  • 150 BBY
    • The Chiss Ascendancy's Syndicure removes the Coduyo family from the Ruling Families, demoting them to a Great Family.[434]
    • Rhos Arthyst is born on the planet Siline to Enea Arthyst, the Allvoice of the Silinese War Council.[435]
  • 149 BBY
    • Yarzion Vell dies.[436]
    • Kelnacca takes on Ordea as his Padawan.[436]
  • 148 BBY
    • Four Jedi—Indara, Sol, Torbin, and Kelnacca—embark on a mission to the planet Brendok to investigate a vergence in the Force.[437]
    • The four Jedi discover a coven of witches. After a series of misunderstandings involving one of the coven's younglings, Verosha Aniseya, wishing to join the Jedi, most of the coven is killed.[437] Verosha joins the Jedi Order[438] and her sister Mae-ho Aniseya, who is presumed dead, swears revenge against the Jedi.[439]
  • 147 BBY
    • Torbin becomes a Jedi Knight.[440]
  • c. 146 BBY
    • The Artiodac chef Strono Tuggs hosts a competition at Maz Kanata's Takodana Castle to uncover Robbs Ely's murder.[21]
  • c. 142 BBY
    • By this point, Jedi Master Torbin has taken the Barash Vow and entered a silent state of meditation to atone for his role in an incident on the planet Brendok.[441]
  • c. 138 BBY
    • Osha Aniseya leaves the Jedi Order.[442]
  • 136 BBY
    • The Force-sensitive Celantes Chippin Jat hatches.[443]
  • c. 135 BBY
    • Cabuck Junior is born.[444]
  • 134 BBY
    • Jedi Masters Sol and Elishe and their Padawans, Jecki Lon and Yord Fandar, go on a mission to the planet Siline, to solve a diplomatic crisis produced by another Jedi.[445]
      • A Crowning Convocation is held, a championship held on the planet Siline between the planet's youth every seven to ten planetary years, when thermal vents in the continental crust of Siline bust and spew red metallic vapor into the atmosphere, meant to test the strengths and abilities of the combatants. Padawans Jecki Lon and Yord Fandar participate representing the Jedi Order including in the great hunt, where the combatants hunt the ericadi, the planet's most dangerous predator.[445]
      • Jecki Lon and the human warrior Lionine Graf are captured by a group of mercenaries during the great hunt. The mercenary leader, Orisent, was hired by the young Graf to sabotage the great hunt by releasing lyleks into the arena and poaching ericadi so that the Silinese traditions lose their air of purity and help usher in a less traditional future. When the operation is discovered by Lon and her allies, sullying Orisent's potential for profiting on ericadi hunting, she pivots to abducting Graf and Lon in hopes of making ransom money. After knocking Lon and Graf unconscious, the mercenaries bring their captives to the Heart of Siline, a sacred place, as they wait for the ransom. As Graf and Lon fight the mercenaries, fellow Padawan Yord Fandar protects the Silinese Sitia Arthyst, who has been severely injured by a trap sprung by the mercenaries. Soon, the Silinese government arrives with Masters Sol and Elishe, and the mercenaries are defeated.[445]
      • Rescue of Jecki Lon.[445]
  • c. 133 BBY
    • Kelnacca settles on Khofar.[446]
  • 132 BBY
    • Mae Aniseya, now a Sith acolyte, begins a quest for revenge against the Jedi from Brendok, beginning by murdering Indara on Ueda.[439]
    • Osha Aniseya, as Mae's twin, is blamed for Indara's murder and Jedi Knight Yord Fandar and his Padawan Tasi Lowa are sent to arrest her.[439]
    • A prisoner mutiny on the ship Osha was being held, the Palwick, occurs, leading to Osha being stranded on the planet Carlac[439]
    • Jedi Master Sol, his Padawan Jecki Lon, and Yord Fandar find Osha on Carlac.[439]
    • Believing that Mae is alive and behind Indara's murder, Sol and Osha team up to locate her. They track her to Olega, but do not prevent her from encouraging Torbin to commit suicide. Mae learns that Osha is alive.[447]
    • Mae travels to Khofar to kill Kelnacca, who is hiding there. However, she decides against it after reckoning with Osha being alive and turns against her accomplice, Qimir, who is secretly her Sith master. However, Qimir, as "the Stranger", kills Kelnacca anyway.[448]
    • Sol, Osha, Jecki, Yord, and a large contingent of Jedi travel to Khofar to try and reach Kelnacca before the assassins do. They arrive too late,[448] and all of the Jedi except for Sol and Osha are killed. The Stranger kidnaps Osha, and Mae disguises herself as Osha and goes with Sol.[449]
    • After discovering Mae has disguised herself, Sol takes her to Brendok while Osha and the Stranger, who have formed a tentative alliance, also travel to Brendok to prevent Mae from killing Sol. Both the sisters and Sol and the Stranger duel. After Sol admits to killing her mother, Osha kills him. The Stranger erases Mae's memory and Osha agrees to train with Qimir on a planet where Darth Plagueis lurks in the shadows.[450]
    • Jedi Master Vernestra Rwoh, the Stranger's former Jedi Master, discovers Sol's death and a mind-wiped Mae on Brendok. Despite sensing the Stranger's involvement, Rwoh blames all of the Jedi murders on Sol to stave off a Senate investigation of the Order before resolving to find her former apprentice and reporting back to Master Yoda.[450]
  • c. 119 BBY; nearly a century before the Imperial Era[451]
    • By this point, a treaty ensuring Mandalore's independence from Galactic Republic intervention had been put in place.[452]
    • The Skree space station is established within a nebula.[453]
  • c. 118 BBY
    • A High-density electronics memory unit is brought to the Universal Analysis Group[454]
    • The Coduyo family is removed as a Ruling Family and demoted to Great Family of the Chiss Ascendancy.[455]
  • c. 116 BBY
    • Kedpin Shoklop is born on Werma Lesser.[456]
  • c. 106 BBY[457]
    • Janyor is born on Garel.[78]
  • c. 104 BBY
    • Ramil is born on the planet Serenno into Count Gora and Countess Anya, the rulers of the planet. As the firstborn, he is expected to be the future Count of Serenno.[458]
  • 102 BBY
    • Dooku is born[21] on the planet Serenno[459] into House Serenno. The child's Force-sensitivity manifests itself rapidly, scaring his father, who hates and fears those he dismissed as "freaks." Gora contacts the Jedi Order and tells them to take his son. However, instead of waiting for the Jedi seekers to arrive, Gora abandons the infant outside his palace on the edges of the forest without clothes or any form of identification. A seeker rescues Dooku before he freezes to death or is eaten by spine-wolves, and brings the child to the Jedi Grand Temple on Coruscant.[84]
    • Sifo-Dyas is born[21] on Minashee.[84]
    • Arath Tarrex is born[460] to the Jedi Yula Braylon. Tarrex's lineage is kept a secret from the Jedi High Council.[84]
  • c. 101 BBY
    • Jenza is born on the planet Serenno into House Serenno, the younger sister of Dooku and Ramil.(Approximate date)[461]
    • The last reported sighting of a Loth-wolf on Lothal until the Liberation of Lothal occurs.[462]

c. 100 – 32 BBY[]

  • c. 99 BBY
    • The Cosmatanic Steppes, a layer of mantle suspended above a gas giant,[73] becomes a center of archaeological digs and scavenging as part of the historical art rush,[463] with participants seeking the reasons for the Steppes' existence and selling relevant artifacts to the Archaeological Association at Archaeo-Prime.[73]
    • Wat Tambor acquires the Triple-Zero protocol personality matrix[464] with the intent to study it. Tambor is soon frightened of the matrix and sends it to quarantine.[465]
  • c. 94 BBY
    • Meggone is born.[466]
  • 91 BBY
    • At or after this point, Maz Kanata sees a holocron that she had no knowledge of and had put into storage for the last time for the next 125 years.[467]
  • 90 BBY[21]
    • Along with Jedi younglings including Dooku and Sifo-Dyas, Jedi High Council members Yula Braylon, Tera Sinube, and Yoda visit a festival held in the city of Carannia, capital of Serenno, with the objective of showing what the Outer Rim Territories planets can offer to the Galactic Republic, and presided over by the count of Serenno Gora.[84]
    • While on the festival, Dooku uses his Force powers and an electroblade to save two kids being harassed by a trio of thugs that are threatening to kidnap them, who turn out to be Jenza and Ramil.
