  • The Separatists launch an attack on Kamino, hoping to eliminate the cloning facilities in Tipoca City and to deprive the Republic from creating more clone troopers for their army, resulting in the Battle of Tipoca City. The Separatists, led by General Grievous and Asajj Ventress, are repelled by the Republic. However, the attack on Kamino proves the Republic had intelligence leaks and that every Republic planet is vulnerable to assault.[715][59]
  • Actress Risha Synata, a spy for the Confederacy, orchestrates a plot invite senators, who were unconvinced of joining the Separatist Alliance, to her ship and gas them to advance her goals. She is stopped by Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker.[716]
  • The Trade Federation establishes a blockade above the moon Pantora, orchestrated by Sib Canay, Nute Gunray, and Lott Dod in an attempt to force Pantora to join the Separatist Alliance. Because he disownes the blockade as a rogue action, Federation Senator Dod is able to keep his position in the Galactic Senate.[717][718]
  • To increase pressure of the blockade, the daughters of the Pantoran Supreme Chairman Notluwiski Papanoida are kidnapped. With the assistance of Ahsoka Tano, Pantoran Senator Riyo Chuchi and Chairman Papanoida are able to rescue both daughters and uncover the conspiracy, forcing the Trade Federation to lift the blockade.[717][718]
  • Bounty hunters employed by the Hutt Clan, led by Cad Bane, held Chancellor Palpatine and several senators hostage in order to free Ziro the Hutt from the Republic Judiciary Central Detention Center. Cad Bane and his team escape with Ziro.[719][718]
  • A mission to capture the Ziro leads to a showdown between Cad Bane and Obi-Wan Kenobi and Quinlan Vos on Teth. Both sides were on the hunt for Ziro, who escaped from the custody of both the Republic and the Grand Hutt Council, but found him dead on Teth after he was killed by Sy Snootles at the behest of Jabba the Hutt.[720][718]
  • A Jedi ceremony is held for Ahsoka Tano at the Jedi Temple on Coruscant, in which she is given new Padawan beads by Grand Master Yoda to signify her growth as a Jedi.[721][718]
  • Senators Padmé Amidala and Mina Bonteri attempt a Confederate–Republic peace initiative, but it is disrupted by Count Dooku with the bombing of Coruscant's central power distribution grid.[722][59]
  • Sabine Wren is born on Krownest. (exact chronology undefined)[723][63]
  • Jyn Erso is born to Galen and Lyra Erso on Vallt. (Approximate date)[724] Soon after, the Erso family is detained by the native Koorivar, sympathizers of the Separatist cause. Having captured Morseerian scientists for a prisoner exchange, Krennic leads Republic forces in rescueing the family, who then move to Coruscant.[76]
  • The Confederacy of Independent Systems besiege Grange for a period of over two standard months.[76]
  • A farming accident on Raada injures the Togruta Selda, where he loses his left arm, leg, and lekku, and kills Kaeden and Miara Larte's parents. (Approximate date)[725]
  • By this point, the Republic Special Weapons Group's plans for both an automated battlemoon asteroid and a torpedo siege platform—developed after the Naboo Crisis—were ruled out at the design stage as a result of the Republic's then pacifistic leanings and the consequent lack of funding.[76]
  • In the aftermath of the bombing of Coruscant's central power distribution grid, mandatory blackouts are put into place. Senator Onaconda Farr is assaulted by bounty hunters Robonino and Chata Hyoki during one of the blackouts.[726][727]
  • After the bombing of Coruscant's central power distribution grid and breakdown of peace talks, Count Dooku alleges the Republic launched an attack on Confederate people, killing many including Separatist Senator Mina Bonteri. While Republic spies attribute Bonteri's death to Separatist agents, Dooku withdraws Bonteri's peace proposal offered by the Separatist Senate.[726][727]
  • Between 21 and 20 BBY
    • A funeral is held for Senator Onaconda Farr on Coruscant, after he is poisoned.[728][729]
    • Senatorial aide Lolo Purs is arrested for the murder of Farr and other high-profile political murders and attempted killings.[728][729]
  • Construction of the Death Star begins over Geonosis.[730]
20 BBY[]
  • Gedeek Obaz is born on Cantonica.[502]
  • The Republic and the Confederacy fight for control over the planet Sullust. During this battle, Asajj Ventress is targeted by Count Dooku, who wished to prove his allegiance to his Sith Master, Lord Sidious by killing his aspiring apprentice. Ventress is presumed dead in the conflict, but escapes.[578][679]
  • Asajj Ventress returns to her homeworld Dathomir. There, Mother Talzin agrees to aid in getting revenge on Dooku. Nightsisters go on a mission to assassinate Dooku, but he repells the attack.[578][731]
  • Dooku's new Sith apprentice, Savage Opress, attacks the Temple of Eedit on the planet Devaron, resulting in the Devaron massacre.[732][731]
  • After failing to kidnap Toydarian king Katuunko alive, Savage Opress is punished by Dooku to the point of Opress siding with Asajj Ventress. The two of them confront Dooku together but are unable to kill him, fall apart, and escape.[733][731]
  • Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Ahsoka Tano travel to Mortis, where they encounter three powerful Force wielders: the Father, the Daughter, and the Son. Anakin is put through a number of tests by them, proving that he is the Chosen One who will bring balance to the Force.[734][731]
  • Jedi Master Even Piell and the crew of his Star Destroyer are captured by Confederacy forces, after beeing attacked and boarded while returning from the Outer Rim Territories.[735][731]
  • Jedi and clone troopers launch a mission to infiltrate the Citadel, a Separatist prison on Lola Sayu, in order to rescue Even Piell, Captain Wilhuff Tarkin, and their clone forces. Though Piell is killed, Tarkin and his forces are rescued[735][731]
  • A funeral is held for Even Piell on Lola Sayu.[736][731]
  • Having been forced into a retreat at the First Battle of Felucia, the Republic re-established a presence on the planet after an offensive into Separatist-held territory.[737][731]
  • Ahsoka Tano is taken captive to the planet Wasskah by a group of Trandoshan hunters to hunt her, and other Jedi, for sport. She takes command of a team of Jedi youngling who had been evading the hunters, and they attack the floating fortress of the hunters and free the Wookiee Chewbacca.[737][731]
  • On the planet Mon Cala a schism confronts the Mon Calamari and Quarren, as the later rebel against the Mon Calamari with the support of the Confederacy of Independent Systems. The Grand Army of the Republic, Jedi Order, and the Gungan Grand Army came to the defense of the Mon Calamari to fight the invading Separatist Droid Army. The Mon Calamari and the Quarren are reunified, and the Separatists repelled.[738][679]
  • A battle is fought on Horain in the Crantori system between the Republic and Confederacy. During the battle, B1-series battle droid B1-0516 is hit by a blaster bolt, which causes the droid to develop a conscience, and fight for the Republic.[739]
  • The Gungans' Boss Lyonie's minister, Rish Loo, influences the Boss to seek an alliance with the Separatists. Anakin Skywalker, Padmé Amidala, and Jar Jar Binks arrive to Naboo to advise against the arrangements. After an attempted assassination, Lyonie is injured and the Gungan Grand Army prepares for an attack on the Naboo by Grievous and his army. The Gungan militia, lead by General Roos Tarpals, disables the droid forces. Grievous is captured by the Gungans, while Dooku captures Anakin Skywalker, and the two generals are subsequently freed in a prisoner exchange.[740][731]
  • After a series of devastating groundquakes rocked the world of Aleen, Clone Commander Wolffe and 104th Battalion are sent to deliver relief supplies, with R2-D2 and C-3PO providing assistance in installing the new equipment and translating for the clones.[741][731]
  • The flagship of Jedi Master Adi Gallia comes under attack by a fleet of Confederacy warships led by Grievous. Caught off guard while en route to Republic territory, the Separatist fleet manages to destroy Gallia's Destroyer and take her captive.[742][731]
  • Plo Koon and clones of the 104th Battalion board the Separatist dreadnought where Adi Gallia was being held, and rescue her from Grievous.[742][731]
  • Following the death of Umbara's senator Mee Deechi, the Umbarans withdraw from the Republic and commit their militia to the Confederacy. The Republic sends Jedi Master Pong Krell, Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi and Saesee Tiin to retake Umbara with their troops. Pong Krell is executed for treason against the Republic, after it's revealed his ultimate goal was recognition by Dooku to become his new apprentice. The Galactic Republic occupies Umbara.[743][731]
  • The Republic sents help to the the Togrutan colony planet of Kiros, neutral in the war, after learning it was being invaded by Confederate forces. However, by the time they arrive, the Confederacy had already occupied Kiros's capital and transferred the entire population to the planet Zygerria to be sold as slaves. The Republic forces fight through the capital, only to find the whole population missing.[744][731]
  • Anakin Skywalker, Ahsoka Tano, and Obi-Wan Kenobi undertake an undercover mission to Zygerria to rescue the Togrutan populationfrom Zygerrian slavers. Kenobi and Captain Rex are captured and forced into slavery at the Kadavo slave processing facility.[745][731]
  • After escaping the Royal Slave Auction, Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano learn from the Zygerrian Queen, Miraj Scintel, that the colonists had been transported to Kadavo. Calling on Jedi General Plo Koon for reinforcements, Skywalker and Tano traveled to the Kadavo slave processing facility. In the ensuing battle, the surviving Togruta captives are rescued and Republic forces destroy the Kadavo facility.[37][731]
  • The Confederacy and the Republic held a peace conference on the neutral world of Mandalore. Hoping to reach a diplomatic end to the war, delegations from the Republic Senate and the Separatist Senate meet in the throne room of Duchess Satine Kryze's Royal Palace. However, the negotiations quickly collapse.[746][731]
  • Lux Bonteri, Ahsoka Tano and R2-D2 arrive on the planet Carlac after the Confederate–Republic peace conference, which Bonteri had interrupted. He had gained the location of Count Dooku and wanted revenge on him for the death of his mother, Mina Bonteri, by allying with Pre Vizsla's Death Watch warriors. But after they start to torch Ming Po Town, Bonteri gets disappointed with them and escapes with Tano and R2-D2.[746][731]
  • A faux funeral is held for Obi-Wan Kenobi, as part of a Jedi Council's plot to send him undercover as the bounty hunter Rako Hardeen. Amongst those in attendance is Anakin Skywalker, who is unaware of the plot.[747][731]
  • Cad Bane instigates a riot at the Republic Judiciary Central Detention Center on Coruscant in order to provide a cover for his escape. He flees with Moralo Eval and Obi-Wan as Rako Hardeen in a Panther police interceptor.[747][731]
  • Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano track down the trio of escaped bounty hunters to the planet Orondia and confront them, but they escape. Anakin and Ahsoka discover that Obi-Wan is alive.[748][731]
  • Moralo Eval, a criminal working for Count Dooku, works out plans to kidnap Chancellor Palpatine. To find the most suitable recruits, Dooku and Eval host a tournament on Serenno. The bounty hunters face multiple tests in the Box, a floating cubic structure designed by Eval.[749][731]
  • The 847th Festival of Light is held, a Naboo tradition that celebrates the anniversary of the planet Naboo joining the Republic.[750][731]
  • A skirmish takes place in the Royal Palace of Naboo between Count Dooku and Obi-Wan disguised as Rako Hardeen and Anakin Skywalker. The Jedi were attempting to rescue Chancellor Palpatine, whom Dooku was attempting to kidnap after the failure of his plan to have a team of bounty hunters do it. Kenobi and Skywalker rescue the Chancellor, but Dooku is able to escape.[750][679]
  • As a retaliation for the actions of Mother Talzin and Ventress against Dooku, the Confederacy launches an invasion led by Grievous on the planet Dathomir that results in the almost total annihilation of the Nightsisters.[751][752]
  • The safe cargo objective operation is carried out by a group of six bounty hunters, Latts Razzi, Dengar, Asajj Ventress, C-21 Highsinger, Bossk and Boba Fett, who was leading the posse. They were hired by the Belugan Rigosso to help him deliver a chest safely to Otua Blank.[753][752]
  • Nightbrother Savage Opress finds his lost brother, Maul, on Lotho Minor. The forgotten Sith Lord is brought back to his homeworld of Dathomir. Mother Talzin restores Maul's sanity and memories through magick and rebuilds him with droid parts. Thinking Obi-Wan Kenobi had robbed him of his destiny at the Battle of Naboo, Maul takes Opress as his apprentice and ventures to take revenge on the Jedi.[754][752][595]
  • Lead by General Flebek, the Separatists look to find the rumored secret to invisibility on the planet Retta. Yoda and Anakin Skywalker are sent to stop them. After the Jedi discovered that the secret to invisibility was the power of the Segredo Kreeda, in order to protect it they convince Flebek that the whole thing was a Jedi trick, and she orders her forces to leave Retta.[755]
  • In order to draw the attention of Obi-Wan Kenobi, Maul and Savage Opress massacre the inhabitants of a village on Raydonia.[756][752]
  • Obi-Wan Kenobi, who had been captured by Maul and Opress after responding to the massacre they committed, is rescued from their ship by Opress's former master, Asajj Ventress. After engaging the brothers in combat, Ventress and Kenobi flee using a escape pod.[756][752]
  • The Onderonian Civil War, fought between the Separatist-backed Royal Court of Onderon and the Republic-backed Onderon rebels, ends after two years with the death of King Sanjay Rash and the re-installation of King Ramsis Dendup.[757][758]
  • A funeral is held for Steela Gerrera, the leader of the Onderon rebels, after she is killed in the final battle against Onderon's droid garrison.[757][758]
  • Jedi younglings Byph, Ganodi, Gungi, Katooni, Petro and Zatt, escorted by Ahsoka Tano, travel to the planet Ilum to claim the kyber crystals they would use to construct their lightsabers, following the Jedi ritual known as The Gathering.[759] [758]
  • The Ohnaka Gang attack the Paladin-class corvette Crucible while it was transporting the Jedi younglings who had undergone the Gathering back to Coruscant, attempting to seize their kyber crystals and sell them on the black market. Although Ahsoka Tano drives the pirates off the ship, she is taken captive.[760][761]
  • Grievous attacks a Republic fleet under the command of Obi-Wan Kenobi. Launching a surprise assault, Confederate battleships and droid starfighters eliminate the Republic Star Destroyers and their outmatched starfighter squadrons. Kenobi mounts a defense of his flagship, the Negotiator, with Commander Cody and clone troopers from the 212th Attack Battalion. Yet they are forced to evacuate from the Negotiator and conced it to Grievous's control.[762][761]
  • The Jedi younglings attempt to rescue Ahsoka Tano from the Ohnaka Gang on Florrum. They infiltrate Ohnaka's camp and successfully retrieve her. However, when returning to the Crucible, it is shot down by a WLO-5 battle tank and Ahsoka and the younglings are recaptured.[762][761]
  • Still angered by his capture at the hands of Hondo Ohnaka, Dooku orders for a Separatist invasion of Florrum,[763][761] nearly crippling the Ohnaka Gang.[764]
  • Shortly after yet another naval battle against Separatist forces, the Republic prepares to convene a conference over Carida. The Republic also assembles D-Squad, their secret weapon to steal the Confederacy's encryption module, which scrambled all Separatist communication in the region.[765][761]
  • D-Squad steals the encryption module aboard Aut-O's flagship.[765]
  • Apailana is elected Queen of Naboo, succeeding Queen Neeyutnee, after being supported in her campaign by Padmé Amidala.[21]
  • Despite having stolen the encryption module, D-Squad are left stranded on Abafar.[766][21]
  • With the help of the amnestic clone CC-5576-39 "Gregor," D-Squad escape from Abafar.[766][21]
  • As the Republic strategy conference is held aboard the space station Valor, the Carida incident occurs as the result of a plot by the Confederacy to load the Venator-class Star Destroyer Renown with enough rhydonium to destroy the space station. The plot is discovered and foiled by D-Squad.[767][21]
19 BBY[]
  • Kay Vess is born in the city of Canto Bight on the planet Cantonica to Riko Vess.[502]
  • Maul and Opress duel two Jedi at an Outer Rim spaceport, killing both of them.[764][21]
  • Maul and Opress raid the Cybloc Transfer Station and depart by commandeering an Aurore-class freighter.[764][21]
  • A skirmish takes place on the world of Florrum between the pirate forces of Hondo Ohnaka, aided by the Jedi Order, and renegade pirate forces under the command of Maul and Opress. Jedi High Council member Adi Gallia is killed by Savage Opress.[764][21]
  • Determined to conquer Mandalore, Maul recruits allies to form the Shadow Collective.[21]
    • Maul and Opress travel to Mustafar with their new allies of Death Watch to convince Black Sun to join them in conquering Mandalore. After they refuse, Opress beheades most of the Black Sun's leadership, and the sole surviving crime Vigo, Ziton Moj, agrees to supply Death Watch.[768][679]
    • Maul and the Shadow Collective travel to Nal Hutta to gain the backing of the Grand Hutt Council. After Maul's threats, Jabba orders their bounty hunters to kill him, causing a fight to break out. The bounty hunters and few surviving guards flee.[768][667]
    • Landing on the grounds of Jabba's Palace on Tatooine, Death Watch warriors attack Jabba's men and enter the main chamber, Jabba concedes defeat and gives the Hutt Clan's backing to Death Watch's coup on Mandalore.[768][667]
    • Maul renders the influencial Morubas Crime Family powerless, thereby eliminating their potential complication in the expansion of his Shadow Collective.[769]
  • The Shadow Collective takes over Mandalore from the pacifist New Mandalorian government of Duchess Satine Kryze;[770] a Mandalorian civil war erupts between Maul loyalists and a Mandalore resistance.[771]
    • Maul betrays Pre Vizsla of Death Watch and kills him, taking the Darksaber with the intent to rule over Mandalore.[770] He captures Duchess Satine Kryze in order to lure his nemesis and her lover, Obi-Wan Kenobi; though Maul murders Kryze and takes Kenobi captive during the Battle of Mandalore, the Jedi is freed by Death Watch dissidents led by Satine's sister, Bo-Katan Kryze, who continues resisting against the Shadow Collective.[771]
    • Darth Sidious secretly travels to Mandalore, killing Savage Opress and seizing his former apprentice, Maul.[771]
  • The planet Cato Neimoidia, housing the headquarters of the Trade Federation, is invaded by the Confederacy of Independent Systems. The Republic launches a defense of the planet, after having taken control of it previously. Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano take part of the battle, but are recalled to Coruscant following the bombing of the Jedi Temple hangar.[772][667]
  • A funeral is held for the victims of a bombing of the Jedi Temple. Various Jedi and Republic dignitaries are in attendance, including Padawan Barriss Offee, who is later revealed to be behind the attack.[773][667]
  • After her arrest for the murder of Letta Turmond, Ahsoka Tano manages to effect an escape from the Republic military base where she was being held.[773][667]
  • Ahsoka Tano is captured in the Coruscant underworld by Coruscant Guard shock troopers under the command of Plo Koon and Anakin Skywalker.[774][667]
  • Ahsoka Tano is put on trial for her alleged role in the bombing of the Jedi Temple hangar. She is represented by Padmé Amidala, who seaks desperately to find a way to clear her client, but with little success. Ultimately, Tano is cleared of the charges as Anakin Skywalker discovered that the crime had been committed by Barriss Offee, disillusioned with the behavior of the Jedi Order during the Clone Wars.[775][667]
  • After Anakin Skywalker learned that Offee framed his Padawan for the bombing of the Jedi Temple hangar, he fights her in the Jedi Temple and brings her to Tano's trial.[775][667]
  • Separatist Admiral Trench captures Ringo Vinda's orbital graving docks, prompting a Republic effort to retake the world. Republic forces advance on Trench's command center, but lose their momentum when clone trooper Tup abruptly executes Jedi General Tiplar due to a malfunction in his behavioral modification biochip causing him to carry out Order 66 prematurely.[776][667]
  • ARC trooper Fives escapes from Tipoca City on Kamino while trying to uncover the mystery of why his friend Tup had suddenly killed a Jedi Master, and after discovering he was in danger of memory-wiping for what he knew about the chip he had discovered in Tup's head.[777][667]
  • Fives escapes from the Grand Republic Medical Facility on Coruscant, where he had been taken for evaluation by Supreme Chancellor Palpatine. He is pursued by several Republic clone troopers and Jedi.[778][667]
  • Anakin Skywalker and Rex go to Coruscant's Level 1325 to find Fives, who is on the run after being framed for an attempted attack on Palpatine. Fives is eventually killed by Clone Commander Fox of the Coruscant Guard, responding to a report from a probe droid.[778][667]
  • By this point, the Midorian Era had occurred, with some academics believing that a trade relationship was established between the Vaks and Garwians during that time.[96]
  • General Yiv the Benevolent, the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states.[96]
    • Two Paccosh refugee starships escape from their homeworld of Rapacc while it is under blockade by General Yiv, rendezvousing in a quadruple star system, but one is unable to travel further. The working ship enters the territory of the Chiss Ascendancy, but the Nikardun locate it at its exit point in the Dioya system due to its Void Guide navigator and destroy it. The Chiss witness the incident, but have their attention drawn away when the Nikardun orchestrate an attack on the Chiss homeworld of Csilla. Later, Senior Captain Mitth'raw'nuruodo, looking into the attack on Csilla, investigates the wreckage in the Dioya system, leading to his discovery of the Nikardun conquests. The attack helps cover up the Sunrise's civil war orchestrated by Lord "Jixtus" of the Grysk.[96]
    • A battle is fought between the forces of the Chiss Ascendancy and three attacking warships from the Paataatus Hiveborn, though they are unidentified at the time of the attack. The Hiveborn have been ordered to attack the Chiss homeworld and capital of Csilla by their Nikardun conquerers, to distract from the massacre of refugees at the Dioya system at the Ascendancy's edge. In turn, the Nikardun have conquered the Paataatus and other nations near the Ascendancy at the behest of the Grysk Hegemony, who seek to encircle, undermine, and ultimately invade the Chiss Ascendancy. The attackers are quickly defeated by the Chiss Defense Force. However, the attack happens during a legislative session of the Chiss Syndicure, sending the Ascendancy's ruling Aristocra into an uproar. The Syndics become single-minded about identifying and punishing the attackers, as no-one had dared to attack Csilla in decades. Supreme General Ba'kif of the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet thinks the question of why Csilla had been attacked is of more interest than who had attacked it. Nevertheless, Ba'kif consents to a retaliatory strike.[96]
    • The Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet conducts a retaliatory strike on Nettehi, the homeworld of the Paataatus, shortly after the attack on Csilla. At the time, the [hiss Syndicure does not have definitive proof that the Paataatus government is behind the attack on Csilla, but justifies the attack as a means to punish the Paataatus for proven encroachments into Ascendancy territory. The Paataatus military is far more prepared to defend their homeworld than most officers of the Chiss Picket Force Six anticipate. Only Senior Captain Thrawn expected their level of readiness, he disobeys the orders of Admiral Ar'alani, breaking off and drawing most Paataatus forces away from the bulk of the Chiss force. In doing so, Thrawn buys the other Chiss warships time to regroup and eliminate most of the Paataatus fighters, securing a Chiss victory.[96]
    • Senior Captain Thrawn undertakes a mission in order to investigate the point of origin of the refugee starship destroyed by the Nikardun Destiny in Chiss territory. After locating a second derelict vessel, Thrawn identifies the vessels' origin in the Rapacc system. With the assistance of Admiral Ar'alani and the Vigilant, Thrawn manages to discreetly maneuver the heavy cruiser Springhawk into the system, boarding an abandoned mining station with the caregiver Mitth'ali'astov. The Nikardun, having noticed the Vigilant's intrusion, send a group of subjugated Paccosh to board the station. Thrawn speaks with the Paccosh, learning the identity of the Nikardun and their leader, General Yiv. The Paccosh let Thrawn leave without reporting him to the Nikardun, allowing Thrawn to pursue further investigation into Nikardun activities. While aboard the asteroid mining station, Thrawn and Thalias meet the Paccosh leader Uingali foar Marocsaa, who gives Thrawn a double ring depicting a chimaera.[96]
    • Thrawn orchestrates a battle in order to capture a blockade frigate of the Nikardun Destiny in the Rapacc system, as he wishes to study them further. Thrawn hires the Pathfinder navigator Qilori to bring the Springhawk into the system near the Nikardun starships. Once there, Thrawn distracts all but one of his pursuers with an automatic shuttle, managing to disable and capture the remaining vessel.[96]
    • The Ascendancy is asked to send Ambassador Ilparg in a diplomatic mission to the planet Urch, capital of the Urchiv-ki, with whom the Ascendancy have a treaty, and the syndics Thurfian and Zistalmu choose Admiral Ar'alani's Nightdragon man-of-war Vigilant to escort the ambassador. The Syndics insist on sending Thrawn on the mission to prevent him from visiting the Lioaoin Regime as part of his investigations into the Nikardun conquests. However, the invitation is a trap, as the Urchiv-ki have been subjugated by the Nikardun, refusing to allow the Chiss to land. Thrawn notices the presence of a Lioaoin vessel and a Nikardun frigate. In retribution for Thrawn's earlier capture of a Nikardun blockade frigate, allied Lioaoi, Urchiv, and Nikardun ships triy to set up a web to ensnare and capture the Vigilant. However, the Vigilant evades the Urchiv, Lioaoi and Nikardun ships, escaping into hyperspace.[96]
    • Due to the Lioaoin presence at Urch, Ambassador Ilparg demands that the Vigilant visit the Lioaoin heartworld for an explanation. Upon the Chiss arrival at the heartworld, the Lioaoi deny involvement, while a Nikardun Battle Dreadnought approach the Vigilant. Thrawn identifies himself to the Lioaoi, sparking a brief reaction from several Lioaoin gunboats, that use a pincer maneuver, a decidedly non-Lioaoin tactic associated with with the Nikardun military. These factors allow Thrawn to deduce that the Lioaoi are allied with the Nikardun Destiny and have standing orders regarding him. Thrawn, now convinced that the Nikardun Destiny have been conquering, coercing, and courting alien species near the Ascendancy, devises a plan to trick Yiv into believing he has inadequate information about the Chiss.[96]
    • A diplomatic reception is hosted by the Vak Combine on the Vak homeworld of Primea. Primea serves as a hub for trade and diplomacy in the region, and as such, many nations send representatives to the reception. General Yiv seeks to conquer the Vak Combine, and uses the event to make political inroads with the planet's leaders while also maintaining a fleet of warships in orbit to intimidate his hosts and their guests. Meanwhile, the Garwian Unity sends representatives to attend the event, with Second Defense Overlord Frangelic accompanying them. Thrawn attends the reception under the alias of "Artistic Master Svorno", Chief curator of the Nunech Art Collection, accompanied by Thalias to act as Thrawn's family hostage. Thrawn had made up the concept of Chiss family hostages in the hopes that word of the supposed cultural practice would reach Yiv and cause the Nikardun to question his knowledge of Chiss culture. To Thrawn's pleasant surprise, Yiv is actually present at the reception, and Thrawn asks Qilori of Uandualon to introduce the two men.[96]
    • After Thrawn travels aboard a Garwian Unity diplomatic mission to Primea to look into General Yiv's attempts at subjugating the planet, Yiv intends to orchestrate Thrawn's death as the Garwians leave Primea. Unplanned for by Yiv, Thrawn and Thalias remain on the planet when the Garwian vessel departs, and the Chiss Ascendancy sends Admiral Ar'alani's Vigilant to collect them. Syndic Irizi'stal'mustro, an Irizi family official wary of Thrawn's inquisition into the Nikardun activities, insists on coming along on the mission. The Vigilant arrives three days after the Garwian diplomat's departure, and is confronted by several Vak patrol ships. One of the starfighters has been commandeered by Thrawn, who uses modulated laserfire to make contact with the Vigilant, sending a schematic of the Vak fighters in case Ar'alani needd to disable them without harming the occupants. However, before the Chiss can take action, four cruisers of the Lioaoin Regime, being sent in place of Nikardun forces by General Yiv, emerge from hyperspace and open fire on the Vigilant. Thrawn attacks one of the cruisers in his Vak fighter, and the Lioaoin respond too late, unsure whether to open fire on a Vak ship. The Vigilant brings Thrawn's fighter aboard, and jumps to hyperspace.[96]
    • The Chiss Ascendancy launches an attack on the heartworld of the Lioaoin Regime in response to the attack against the Chiss warship Vigilant at Primea. Upon arrival at the heartworld, the Vigilant and Thrawn's Springhawk are confronted by twelve Lioaoin warships, four of which directly attack the Chiss vessels. The Vigilant deploys two decoy shuttles, one hidden behind the other, toward the Lioaoin, a tactic that Thrawn had previously used against the Nikardun. The Lioaoin recognize the tactic and prevent the Vigilant from remotely controlling the shuttle; however, Thrawn had intended for them to do so, firing at the shuttle and releasing the breacher missiles hidden within it. The acid from the missiles damages the hull]s of the Lioaoin vessels, and the Vigilant destroys the second shuttle, similarly releasing breaching missiles into the Lioaoin. The Lioaoin refuse to concede, and the four damaged ships fall back, replaced by two more. The Chiss are ultimately victorious, though not without damage to their own vessels.[96]
  • Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala conduct a mission to Batuu. Skywalker encounters Senior Captain Mitth'raw'nuruodo of the Chiss Ascendancy, whom he joins forces with.[779][58]
  • Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker, travel to the planet Mokivj to investigate and later neutralize a Separatist droid factory. Thrawn aids them, with the objective to learn about the Clone War and whether technology or nations in the wider galaxy may be able to aid the Chiss in their war against the Nikardun Destiny. During the mission, they discover that the Separatists are manufacturing the C-B3 cortosis battle droid, a cortosis-armored model of B2-series super battle droids, on the planet, using cortosis sourced to the nearby cortosis mine. During their attack on the factory, Thrawn procures a deflector shield generator for the Ascendancy. The mission concludes with a catastrophe. In an effort to collapse the Separatists' cortosis mine on Mokivj, Skywalker accidentally sends lava, ash, and smoke into the planet's best cropland and water, as he did not account for the cortosis in the mine redirecting the heat of the explosions. As a result, the explosive force sends down the planet's crust, triggering volcanic activity and leading to widespread devastation, deforestation, and desertification on Mokivj.[779]
  • End of the Nikardun Conquests
    • Thrawn sends the caregiver Mitth'ali'astov and the sky-walker Che'ri to return the Vak fighter he had previously commandeered in the escape from Primea, along with a message to the Vak leadership. Vak forces aligned with the Nikardun organize for the capture of the two Chiss and instead deliver a false message, both of which are expected by Thrawn. Yiv sends a threat to the Ascendancy, demanding that Thrawn deliver him a ransom for the issues he has caused. The Ascendancy's Chiss Syndicure decides that the capture of a sky-walker requires a military response. Thrawn travels to Yiv's location in an unarmed freighter with the Pathfinder navigator Qilori, who, having been allied with Yiv, changes the arrival coordinates from above Primea to further out in the system. As Yiv confronts Thrawn with four Nikardun Battle Dreadnoughts, including the flagship Deathless, five Chiss warships, led by Admiral Ar'alani arrive at Primea, informing the Vaks of the locations of orbiting Nikardun vessels, as Thrawn had offered to do in his message. The Vaks allow Ar'alani to fire on the Nikardun, and Yiv sends his three dreadnought escorts to assist his blockade of Primea. With the Springhawk taking damage, the vessel goes dark, and Admiral Ar'alani requests humanitarian aid from nearby Vak patrol boats. The Vak comply, but the Nikardun vessels shoot down the patrol ships, turning the Vak Combine against the Nikardun. Yiv attempts to pull Thrawn's freighter in with a tractor beam, but Thalias and Che'ri release tava mist into the bridge of the Deathless, allowing Thrawn to break free of the tractor beam and instead crash his freighter into the viewport, with his ship rendered unharmed due to the energy shield acquired from Mokivj. Thrawn boards the bridge, rescuing the two Chiss prisoners and capturing Yiv, who becomes a prisoner of the Ascendancy. Following Yiv's disappearance, the Nikardun Destiny is left without a suitably competent successor, and their plans of conquest fall apart.[96]
  • Padmé Amidala travels to the planet Scipio to uncover evidence of corruption within the InterGalactic Banking Clan. Amidala and Baron Rush Clovis are targeted by the bounty hunter Embo, who was hired by Darth Sidious to intimidate and drive Clovis off-world.[780][667][58]
  • Having helped uncover corruption in the InterGalactic Banking Clan, Rush Clovis is appointed as the new head of the bank, only to be coerced by Count Dooku into raising interest rates for the Republic, at which point Separatist forces occupy Scipio to cement their position. The Republic responds with its own invasion.[781][667]
  • Mace Windu and Jar Jar Binks are dispatched to the planet Bardotta to investigate the mysterious disappearance of the people of Queen Julia, eventually determining that Mother Talzin is responsible for the disappearances.[782][667]
  • Mace Windu and Jar Jar Binks travel to the moon Zardossa Stix to rescue Queen Julia of Bardotta, who had been kidnapped by the Frangawl Cult. They defeat High Seneschal Peteen and Mother Talzin.[783][667]
  • Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker travel to the planet Oba Diah to investigate the Pyke Syndicate and what they may have known about the death of Jedi Master Sifo-Dyas. They discover that the Pykes had killed Sifo-Dyas at the behest of the Sith. Count Dooku, arrives on Oba Diah and engages in lightsaber combat with the Jedi. During the duel, the Jedi learn that Dooku is the man named Tyranus who selected Jango Fett as the clone template, and that the Sith had been involved in the creation of the clone army.[593][667]
  • Yoda undertakes a mission to Moraband, while on a journey to discover the secrets of eternal consciousness within the Force. Upon his arrival, he is confronted by illusions of Sith wraiths, as well as the specter of Darth Bane, the ancient Sith Lord who created the Rule of Two. After facing his fears as part of his trials he is deemed worthy to learn the secrets of immortality.[36][784]
  • Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker travel to the planet Utapau to investigate the death of the Jedi Master Tu-Anh whilst she was on the planet. They discover a Separatist conspiracy to create a powerful weapon using a massive kyber crystal. They pursue and eventually destroy the crystal.[785][679]
  • A funeral is held for Jedi Master Tu-Anh, who had perished as part of an arms deal between the Confederacy and the Sugi crime lord Endente.[785][784]
  • Ahsoka Tano meets the Martez sisters at their repair shop on Level 1313.[786]
  • After being stationed on Coruscant with the Open Circle Fleet, Anakin Skywalker takes part in the Outer Rim Sieges campaign.[786][59]
  • As the Outer Rim Sieges begins to turn the Clone Wars in the Republic's favor, the Separatist Bracca Invasion occurs.[6][59]
  • Begining of the Battle of Anaxes[787], an engagement that would last for several months. The Harch Separatist Admiral Trench launches an assault on Anaxes, which serves as one of the Republic's strongholds and the location of a crucial shipyard for the Republic during the war.[788][784][59]
  • Clone Force 99 end an insurrection on Yalbec Prime. Under the command of Clone Sergeant Hunter, the clone commandos Tech, Crosshair and Wrecker kill the Yalbec queen.[788][784]
  • Anakin Skywalker leads Captain Rex and the Bad Batch to a unsanctioned operation to the planet Skako Minor, which is neutral in the Clone Wars, to rescue ARC trooper Echo from the Techno Union in the city of Purkoll.[789][784]
  • The Confederacy targets the planet Mahranee for its plentiful resources, laying waste to it's capital city. Separatst forces kill all the Mahran refugees who were escaping from their embattled homeworld, in what is later known as the atrocity on Mahranee.[790][784]
  • Asajj Ventress captures Moregi, a Volpai with a bounty from the Rang Clan on his head, on the planet Pantora with the aid of undercover Jedi Master Quinlan Vos.[790][784]
  • Asajj Ventress and Quinlan Vos rescue the family of Marg Krim, the new head of the Pyke Syndicate, from Black Sun captivity on Mustafar. Black Sun retaliates by attacking the Pyke Syndicate's stronghold on Oba Diah.[790][784]
  • Asajj Ventress and Quinlan Vos go on mission to assassinate Count Dooku, who is attending a gathering on the Confederate capital world of Raxus Secundus. Dooku defeates Vos and takes him prisoner. Ventress escapes to warn the Jedi of Vos' capture.[790][784]
  • Maul escapes from Darth Sidious' facility on Stygeon Prime to a Shadow Collective camp on Zanbar with the assistance of the Mandalorian super commandos Rook Kast and Gar Saxon. Through Maul's escape, Sidious hopes to track him to the Nightsisters' Mother Talzin and end her threat to the Sith.[791][784]
    • Separatist forces led by General Grievous battles the Shadow Collective at the latter's camp on Zanbar. The Collective is defeated and retreats to Ord Mantell.[791][784]
    • Anticipating a major Separatist strike spearheaded by Count Dooku and General Grievous, the Shadow Collective prepares a trap for the two pair. As a Battle of Ord Mantell ensues, Maul springs his trap, capturing both Dooku and Grievous.[792][784]
    • A team of Jedi, led by Mace Windu and Obi-Wan Kenobi, carriy out an assault on the supply outpost Vizsla Keep 09 in an effort to capture Maul and Count Dooku. Jedi General Tiplee is killed, and after Death Watch forces incapacitate the Jedi, Maul and Dooku are able to escape.[793][784]
    • The Second Battle of Dathomir occurs; Darth Sidious, Dooku, and Grievous kill Mother Talzin, who sacrifices herself for Maul to live.[794][679]
  • Maul adds Dryden Vos' Crimson Dawn to the Shadow Collective's ranks.[473]
  • Asajj Ventress hires Boba Fett's syndicate to assist her in the rescue of Quinlan Vos from Dooku's Serenno fortress. Shattered by Dooku's well-woven words and confused by the lying of Ventress that occurred prior, Quinlan Vos furiously battles his lover instead, joining Dooku and forcing the infiltrators to escape.[790][784]
  • The Jedi, assisted by Asajj Ventress, rescue Quinlan Vos from Dooku's Providence-class Dreadnought, without knowing that he had already embraced the dark side and become Dooku's apprentice.[790][784]
  • The Republic mounts a mission to capture a strategic Separatist supply storage base containing medications, weapons, ship repair materials, and foodstuffs vital to worlds in need. However, the facility and all of its supplies are destroyed by Quinlan Vos, secretly serving Dooku.[790][784]
  • The Republic launches an attack on a Confederate listening post on the planet Vanqor. However, Vos leaked intelligence about the operation to the Separatists and they are able to evacuate before Republic forces arrive.[790][784]
  • A battle on Christophsis occurs; Asajj Ventress guides Quinlan Vos to return to the light with her sacrifice.[790][784]
  • Obi-Wan Kenobi and Quinlan Vos brings the recovered body of Asajj Ventress to the Nightsister lair on Dathomir. Under Kenobi's watch, Vos lets go of Ventress and returns her to her kin, resting his lover's body in the waters of the Nightsister cavern, leaving her to the embrace of green waters and voices of her sisters.[790][784]
  • A bombing at the Jedi Temple is conducted by the Separatist Captain Rackham Sear. However, Jedi Master Depa Billaba and initiate Caleb Dume sense the explosives as they go off, and they foil the bombing and Sear perishes in the process. Depa Billaba decides to take Caleb Dume as his Padawan.[795]
  • Depa Billaba and her battalion are sent to the planet Kardoa, where they fight Separatist forces led by Colonel Coburn Sear. After the sacrifice of clone trooper CT-1157 to save Caleb Dume, the Separatists are forced to retreat.[796]
  • The planet Mygeeto, which was aligned with the InterGalactic Banking Clan, became host to a Confederate military presence, with Grievous and Coburn Sear overseeing a detachment of the Separatist Droid Army. The Republic leads an invasion, but its troops become outnumbered and require the reinforcements of Depa Billaba and her battalion, who are helped by Skull Squadron, a Mandalorian unit led by Fenn Rau. Caleb Dume beheades Sear and Grievous escapes.[797]
  • A funeral is held for clone trooper Stance on Mygeeto by Depa Billaba, Caleb Dume, and Stance's fellow clone soldiers.[798]
  • Anticipating the fulfillment of Darth Sidious' Grand Plan with Anakin Skywalker as the Sith Lord's apprentice, Maul enters the Mandalore city of Sundari in an attempt to lure Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker and prevent the Plan's completion by killing them.[799]
  • The Battle of Yerbana concludes. Lady Bo-Katan Kryze and Ahsoka Tano request assistance from Kenobi and Skywalker to capture Maul and take Mandalore.[452]
  • The Battle of Coruscant begins. General Grievous storms the Galactic City and kidnaps Chancellor Palpatine.[800] Republic forces led by Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker are recalled from the Outer Rim Sieges to rescue the Chancellor.[15]
  • Ahsoka Tano and Rex lead a division of Skywalker's 501st Legion to Mandalore, initiating the Siege of Mandalore.[452]
  • Maul tells the Shadow Collective leaders to go into hiding,[799] and the syndicates soon split up.[801]
  • Kenobi and Skywalker rescue Chancellor Palpatine at the Battle of Coruscant, where Skywalker executes Count Dooku.[802][679]
  • Senators concerned with the Chancellor's growing powers join the Delegation of 2,000 and take part in the Cantham House meetings hosted by Senator Bail Organa. Key figures at Cantham House include Padmé Amidala and Mon Mothma, who sign the Petition of 2,000 requesting Chancellor Palpatine's relinquishment of his emergency powers.[567]
  • The Jedi Council assigns Anakin Skywalker to secretly spy on Palpatine.[799]
  • Maul attempts to recruit Ahsoka Tano as an ally against Darth Sidious, revealing his insight that Anakin Skywalker would become his new apprentice, but instead Tano defeats and captures Maul.[799]
  • Bo-Katan Kryze's Mandalorians and the clones take Sundari from Maul's forces, capturing Rook Kast and Gar Saxon.[799]
  • The Siege of Saleucami, one of the longest lasting campaigns of the Outer Rim Sieges,[803] comes to an end. Jedi General Quinlan Vos moves his troops to Boz Pity,[802] the siege of which was one of the most intense of the Clone Wars.[787] Stass Allie, a Jedi High Council member and cousin to the late Adi Gallia,[804] leads the 91st Mobile Reconnaissance Corps in securing Saleucami.[803]
  • The Battle of Kashyyyk occurs.[802] Jedi Generals Luminara Unduli,[802] Quinlan Vos,[805] and Yoda lead Republic forces in defending Kachirho from the Separatists alongside Wookiee warriors,[802] who are led by Grakchawwaa, King of Kashyyyk,[806] and Chieftain Tarfful.[805]
  • The Battle of Utapau occurs. General Grievous, now leader of the Separatists, is killed by Obi-Wan Kenobi shortly after the other leaders are sent to the Klegger Corp Mining Facility at the order of their secret master, Darth Sidious.[802]
  • The three-year long Mygeeto Campaign continues with the Fourth Battle of Mygeeto, with Marshal Commander Bacara and the 21st Nova Corps eventually capturing the planet.[802][807]
  • Aayla Secura, Commander Bly, and the 327th Star Corps are deployed to the planet Felucia to capture Shu Mai, Presidente of the Commerce Guild and member of the Separatist Council, and to rescue Ormo Hyn, a relative of a Felucian leader who supported the Republic that had been taken hostage by Shu Mai.[802]
  • Plo Koon led the 442nd Siege Battalion in the capture of the Neimoidian Purse World of Cato Neimoidia, headquarters of the Trade Federation.[802]
  • The conquest of Kaller occurs under the leadership of the Jedi Depa Billaba, CC-10/994 "Grey," and Billaba's Padawan, Caleb Dume.[808]
  • With the end of the Clone Wars in sight, the Jedi Council instructs Anakin Skywalker to confront Palpatine about his emergency powers and term as Chancellor.[802]
  • Ahsoka Tano meets with the Jedi Council, but decides not to relay Maul's words about Sidious' plans. Tano is tasked with escorting the captive Maul to Coruscant.[799]
  • The duel in Palpatine's office occurs; realizing that Palpatine himself was Darth Sidious but promised the power to save Padmé Amidala, Skywalker disobeys the Jedi Council and sides with the Sith Lord.[802]
  • Anakin Skywalker is anointed Darth Vader, Dark Lord of the Sith.[802]
  • Per the Supreme Chancellor's command, Order 66 is executed by clones throughout the galaxy, spelling the end of nearly all Jedi.[802][809]
  • Maul, Ahsoka Tano, and Rex escape from the clone forces sent to Mandalore,[809] with the latter two faking their own deaths.[810]
  • Snoke, created by Darth Sidious,[50] had memories reaching back to at least this point.[811]

Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY)[]

Clone Wars (continued; 19 BBY)[]

  • 19 BBY
    • Archduke Poggle the Lesser instigates an uprising on Geonosis where Geonosian drones riot, allowing him to escape the custody of the Republic and flee to the Confederacy. The riot also sabotages the top-secret construction project in the orbit of Geonosis.[76]
    • A battle begins on the planet of Lokori between the Bright Nebula Fleet of the Republic Navy and a Separatist invasion force. The Erso family gets caught up in the invasion.[76]
    • The Sith Empire is restored as the Republic is reorganized into the First Galactic Empire by Supreme Chancellor Palpatine—secretly the Dark Lord of the Sith Darth Sidious—who declares himself Galactic Emperor and restrains the powers of the new Imperial Senate.[802][679][812]
    • The mission to Mustafar occurs; Darth Vader wipes out the Separatist leaders on Mustafar and orders the deactivation of the droid armies at the orders of the Emperor, thus ending the Clone Wars.[802]
    • Yoda and Obi-Wan Kenobi infiltrate the Jedi Temple, which had fallen to the 501st Legion during the execution of Order 66 and Operation: Knightfall.[802]
      • Kenobi changes the beacon luring Jedi to the Temple into a message warning them away.[802]
      • The ambush in the Coruscant system occurs, with Caleb Dume receiving Kenobi's message and then retreating back to Kaller.[813]
      • Kenobi and Yoda learn Anakin Skywalker has fallen to the dark side.[802]

"The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY)[]

Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY)[]

  • 19 BBY (continued)
    • Darth Vader fights Obi-Wan Kenobi on the planet Mustafar. Vader is severely wounded, requiring the use of a cybernetic suit of armor for the remainder of his life.[802]
    • A lightsaber duel is fought between Darth Sidious and Yoda within the heart of the Senate Chamber. Ultimately, Yoda retreats and goes into exile on Dagobah.[802]
    • Skywalkers Luke and Leia[814] are born to Padmé Amidala on Polis Massa. Amidala dies after childbirth due to heartbreak at her lover's fall to the dark side, though she remains adamant that there is still good in her husband. In order to protect the Skywalker twins from the Sith, Obi-Wan Kenobi leaves Luke with Anakin's stepbrother Owen Lars on Tatooine while Bail Organa adopts Leia into the Alderaanian royal house.[802]
    • A funeral for Padmé Amidala is held on Naboo, where she is mourned by thousands of Naboo citizens. Her body is made to still look pregnant in order to keep Luke and Leia safe from Vader.[802]
    • After the funeral, Bail Organa secretly meets Ahsoka Tano and gives her a line of contact in the case of emergency. Tano then departs with Rex,[815] and the pair soon part ways.[816]
    • Obi-Wan Kenobi and Yoda go into exile on Tatooine and Dagobah respectively.[802] Kenobi is tasked with ensuring the future of the Jedi by watching over Luke Skywalker, while Yoda uses Dagobah's strength in the Living Force to ponder ancient Jedi texts and the mysteries of the Force, as well as to communicate with other Jedi exiles and monitor the Skywalker twins.[15]
    • Ezra Bridger is born to Mira and Ephraim Bridger on Lothal.[63][817]
    • Rex meets Cut Lawquane on Saleucami, speaking about inhibitor chips.[818]
    • Ahsoka Tano settles on the world Thabeska with the powerful and wealthy Fardi clan, working for them as a courier and taking on the name "Ashla."[816]
    • With the Jedi Order's overseer of Kamino's clone army facilities, Shaak Ti, having been murdered by Darth Vader in the Jedi Temple,[679] Governor Tarkin arrives in Tipoca City to assess the viability of using clones in the New Order.[819]
    • A public rally is held on Coruscant to celebrate the destruction of the Jedi Order. Led by Grand Vizier Mas Amedda, who presents a speech to a massive audience on the steps of the Jedi Temple, shows the incineration of the Jedi's lightsabers, including Yoda's.[820]
    • Darth Vader recaptures a starship gifted to him by the Emperor on the planet Gattering, after it was stolen by a group of enslavers and held at their base.[820]
    • Vader attacks the Jedi Order's space station Brighthome in order to access its archives and discover the location of Jedi Master Kirak Infil'a.[821]
    • Clone Force 99 is dispatched to end an insurgency on Onderon. However, the clones let go of the insurgents, who were mainly refugees led by Saw Gerrera, and decide to desert the new Empire.[819]
    • Vader travels to the river moon of Al'doleem, where he finds and kills Jedi Kirak Infil'a. He takes his lightsaber in order to bleed its kyber crystal.[822]
    • Clone Force 99 returns to Kamino and, with the exception of Crosshair, who remained loyal to the Empire, rescue Omega with the assistance of Kaminoan cloner Nala Se. Wary of the Empire's intentions, the Kaminoans keep secret the clones' defection.[819]
    • Aboard the Marauder, the clones escape to meet Cut Lawquane on Saleucami. They help the Lawquane's family escape the Empire.[818]
    • Seeing that the most effective of clone troopers were disloyal to the Empire, Vice Admiral Edmon Rampart's proposes a new army of loyal, enlisted soldiers, to the dismay of Kaminoan Prime Minister Lama Su. Intrigued, Governor Tarkin trials Rampart's proposal, which is named Project War-Mantle. Elite Squad, led by CT-9904, is thus sent to handle Saw Gerrera's rebellion on Onderon.[823]
    • The Kaminoans hire bounty hunter Fennec Shand to track down Omega. The Bad Batch and Omega escape Pantora and ask informant Ciddarin Scaleback to identify the hunter on their tail. In return, the clones retrieve the rancor Muchi for Cid, who in turn gives Muchi to Jabba's majordomo, Bib Fortuna, for a payment.[824]
    • The Bad Batch and the Martez sisters undertake a mission to Corellia at the behest of the information broker Ciddarin Scaleback to steal the head of a tactical droid from a decommissioning facility in Coronet City.[825]
    • Vader and the Grand Inquisitor are sent by Palpatine to hunt the Jedi Master Jocasta Nu so she could tell him the location of the Kyber memory crystal list of Force-sensitive children. After she reveals the Emperor's plans to Vader, he kills her and destroys the crystal.[826]
    • The Bad Batch are sent by their informant Ciddarin Scaleback to retrieve a lizard from the Rhokai gang of thieves, who had stolen it from the criminal Roland Durand.[827]
    • The clones escape from Elite Squad on Bracca, but Omega is captured by Cad Bane, hired by the Kaminoans.[828]
    • Prime Minister Lama Su sends Taun We to obtain Omega's genetic material and then terminate the clone. However, Nala Se hires Fennec Shand to rescue Omega from Taun We and Cad Bane at the Bora Vio cloning facility. Ultimately, Omega is rescued by the Bad Batch at Bora Vio.[597]
    • The Batch extract Senator Avi Singh from the Imperial-occupied Raxus Secundus, the former Separatist capital.[829]
    • On Imperial-occupied Ryloth, Admiral Rampart frames the freedom fighter Cham Syndulla for attempting to assassinate Senator Orn Free Taa,[830] and sends Captain Howzer to pursue Syndulla's daughter, Hera. Together with Howzer and the Bad Batch, Hera and the Ryloth freedom fighters rescue Cham and Eleni Syndulla.[831]
    • Roland Durand, a former client of Scaleback's, attempting to make it in the criminal underground gets involved with the Pykes, taking over Cid's Parlor in the process. In retalliation, Cid and the Bad Batch steal sansanna spice Durand was keeping in his office, which he owed to the Pyke Syndicate, in order to get him in trouble with them.[832]
    • Rex sends the Bad Batch to rescue Gregor from an Imperial installation on Daro. While Gregor is rescued, Hunter is captured by the Empire's Clone Commandos and new stormtroopers and brought before Crosshair.[833]
    • The Empire cancels all its contracts with the Kaminoans and mobilizes its clone forces for departure from Kamino. Fearing their own doom, the Kaminoans attempt to evacuate Kamino with some of their clones as well, but are caught by Rampart's forces. Rampart thus takes away Nala Se for her expertise and deposes Lama Su.[833]
    • The Bad Batch rescue Hunter and confront Crosshair in Tipoca City. While Crosshair maintains his belief that they must serve the Empire, he turns on the Elite Squad, wishing to join his brothers-in-arms.[834]
    • As per Tarkin's command, Rampart's forces destroy Tipoca City's cloning facilities and other Kaminoan cities. The Bad Batch flee the city alongside Crosshair, Omega, and AZI-345211896246498721347.[834]
    • Secure as Emperor, Darth Sidious begins to delve deep into dark side arcana, including the hunt for immortality. (exact chronology undefined)[50]
    • Shortly after the rise of the Empire, entire populations of one species or planet are enslaved[17] as manufacturing worlds such as Riosa of the Inner Rim are annexed and pushed to produce components for Imperial military projects, including the Death Stars.[643] Meanwhile, dissent and resistance emerges with the beginning of the Ryloth Insurgency.[76]
    • Tarkin Massacre occurs. During a protest in the capital city of Palmo, Moff Wilhuff Tarkin attempted to land his cruiser in a crowded plaza. Despite the presence of peaceful demonstrators attempting to block the landing, Tarkin landed his cruiser on top of the crowd and killed five hundred unarmed civilians.[835]
  • Between 19 BBY and 18 BBY[836]
    • The Bad Batch take a job retrieving a package for Ciddarin Scaleback from the planet Aynaboni, facing a pod of aggrocrabs in the process. They succeed and deliver the package to Scaleback's contact, Phee Genoa.[837]
    • Amid the Empire's occupation of Serenno, the Bad Batch launch a mission attempting to steal part of the late Count Dooku's war chest from Castle Serenno before the Empire could confiscate the wealth for itself.[837]
    • The Empire dispatches forces, led by Commander Cody and his squad, to the former Separatist planet Desix to rescue Grotton, the Imperial-appointed governor of the planet, from the Separatist holdout commanded by the original governor Tawni Ames.[838]
    • The Safa Toma Classic, a major Riot Racing event is held on the planet Serolonis. The Dowutin gangster Grini Millegi oversees the event, and personally backs the Nosaurian racer Jet Venim. After Cid loses a bet with Millegi and he holds her as ransom, Tech runs in and wins the race, winning a bet that releases Cid.[839]
    • Clone Force 99 and the pirate Phee Genoa discover an ancient superweapon walker, long believed to be a city built by the Ancients containing a valuable crystal called the Heart of the Mountain.[840]
    • Kashyyyk's Wookiees face persecution by Trandoshan hunters who are aided by Imperial clones. The Bad Batch safely return the Wookiee Jedi Gungi to Kashyyyk.[841]
  • 18 BBY[836]
    • Rampart is exposed for the ruin of Kamino in the Imperial Senate and is arrested, but senators nevertheless approve his Imperial Defense Recruitment Bill at the Emperor's persuasion, formalizing the transition from a clone military into one made up of stormtrooper recruits.[842]
  • During or after 18 BBY[836]
    • Echo leaves the Bad Batch to join Rex in secretly supporting fellow clone troopers facing decommissioning.[842]
    • The Bad Batch’s ship gets stolen on Ipsidon whilst they are on a mining mission for Cid, but they steal it back and commence the liberation of Mokkotown.[843]
    • Cid sends the Bad Batch on a job to get valuable cargo from a crashed transport. It turns out to be an Imperial science vessel, and the Batch encounters a cloned Zillo Beast that terrorizes a village for energy to grow before being abducted by imperial forces and taken to Mount Tantiss.[844]
    • Imperial chief scientist Dr. Royce Hemlock and his assistant Dr. Emerie Karr arrive to take over the secret work on Mt. Tantiss, where Hemlock visits with the imprisoned Kaminoan scientist Nala Se, and requests Lama Su to come to the base. He tells Hemlock that Se’s weakness is her attachment to Omega.[844]
    • Crosshair is assigned to an outpost on Barton IV, where he meets clone Mayday. They thwart raiders’ attempts to steal TK trooper armor, but get badly injured in an avalanche and snowstorm. Crosshair kills Lieutenant Nolan, his commanding officer, when he refuses to help Mayday, then he is taken to Mt Tantiss.[845]
    • Clone force 99 helps as Phee Genoa muscle during with an artifact trade. She then takes them to Pabu, a peaceful ocean world where the Batch helps town get to high ground during a Tsunami, and is tempted to settle down on the planet to give Omega a normal life.[846]
    • Auction on Hosnian Prime.[847]
    • Skirmish on Dallow.[847]
    • Mission to Tryth.[847]
    • Echo and members of the Clone Underground attack an Imperial prisoner transport ship en route through the Balmorra system to the planet Wayland, in order to liberate Howzer and two surviving members of Howzer's squad, who had defied the Empire at Ryloth. Echo extracts portions of Advanced Science Division data before they destroy the ship and flee, with Imperial Captain Pearce dying in the process, along with most of his crew.[848]
    • The Galactic Empire's top strategists and scientists gather on the planet Eriadu to discuss their plans for the future of the Empire. The summit take place in Governor Wilhuff Tarkin's fortified compound, Raven's Peak, and attendees also include Admiral Barton Coburn, Chief Scientist Royce Hemlock, Commander Orson Callan Krennic, and General Hurst Romodi.[849]
    • Clone Force 99 and Saw Gerrera's Partisans independently infiltrate Raven's Peak, the bad batch seeking to locate Doctor Hemlock in order to find and rescue Crosshair, and the partisans seeking to assassinate Governor Tarkin. [849]
    • Capture of Omega.[850]
    • Search for Omega.[851]
    • Escape from Tantiss.[852]
    • Cargo Dock Clash.[853]
    • Two senators, Riyo Chuchi of the moon Pantora, and Avi Singh of the planet Raxus, meet at a tea parlor on Pantora, where they discuss forming an alliance to oppose the Galactic Empire despite having been on opposite sides of the Clone Wars. The meeting is infiltrated by the Clone X trooper CX-1, who is attempting to assassinate the senators. However, Rex and his Clone Underground foil the plot and capture CX-1.[854]
    • A operative of the Clone X trooper division, tracking a fellow operative captured by the Clone Underground, discovers the Underground headquarters in the B'omarr Order Monastery on the planet Teth.He infiltrates the monastery and calls for a Recovery Strike Team to reinforce him after identifying the presence of Omega in the compound. In the ensuing firefight the assassin kills many insurgents and forces the remaining clones to flee into the jungle, where they are cornered by the Strike Team. Despite Commander Wolffe allowing the clones to escape, the battle marks the fragmentation of the Clone Underground movement and the destruction of their primary headquarters.[854]
    • Hunt for Sylar Saris[855]
    • The Empire raids the island Pabu to retrieve Omega. After Clone X trooper CX-2 tracks her down by interrogating her former associate Ciddarin Scaleback and stealing the island's coordinates from Phee Genoa's starship, CX-2 finds Omega, destroys Clone Force 99's shuttle the Marauder, and calls for reinforcements. The forces that arrive lock down the island by destroying any starships and sailing ships in the docks and begin searching the city's houses one by one. After Hunter fail to commandeer an Imperial Low Altitude Assault Transport to escape the blockade, Omega is forced to surrender to the Imperials in hopes that her allies can use her to track down Tantiss Base's coordinates and rescue her. Following Omega's capture, all Imperial troops withdraw from the planet.[855]
    • Rescue of Edmon Rampart.[856]
    • Mission to Imperial Station 003.[857]
    • Skirmish on Wayland.[858]
    • Rex would eventually join fellow clones Wolffe and Gregor on Seelos.[859]
    • Queen Apailana's reign ends, after she refuses to become a puppet of Emperor Palpatine, placing her duty to her people first.[21]
    • Following the defeat of the Nikardun Destiny and General Yiv, the Chiss Ascendancy undertakes a military effort to seek out and destroy the scattered Nikardun forces. Though the Ascendancy typically does not permit military action without having been attacked first, the threat that the Nikardun has posed leads the Chiss Syndicure to allow the defense forces to bend the protocols regarding preemptive strikes.[860]
      • A Commander of the Nikardun Destiny military forces, who had blockaded and occupied the planet Rapacc under orders of General Yiv, is defeated by Commander Uingali foar Marocsaa of the Paccian Governance, and the blockade is lifted.[860]
      • Picket Force Six of the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet undertakes a mission to eliminate any remaining Nikardun military presence at the Vak Combine territory. Admiral Ar'alani and Senior Captains Thrawn and Lakinda believe the Ascendancy has nearly eliminated the Nikardun from the region, as they have cleared out most of the Nikardun bases listed on General Yiv's questis. After defeating the Nikardun forces in an unidentified system, Ar'alani gives Thrawn and his Springhawk permission to undertake his next mission. While the Springhawk travels to Rapacc, Ar'alani's Vigilant and Lakinda's Grayshrike investigate listening post KR20 acquired from interrogating Yiv. To their surprise, they find all Nikardun forces already destroyed by weaponized asteroids that double as missile launchers. They visit the last Nikardun base, Listening post KR21, and investigate it but find that it as well is destroyed.[860]
      • Thrawn travels to the planet Rapacc, homeworld of the Paccosh species, to investigate the remnants of the Nikardun. He and Caregiver Thalias encounter their old friend Uingali foar Marocsaa, who tells them of a group of 200 refugees who have settled on Rapacc. Thrawn and Thalias meett the refugees and their leader, known only as the Magys, who seeks suicide for her people so they can join "The Great Beyond." During the mission, Thrawn returns the Marocsaa subclan ring bearing a chimaera that Uingali had entrusted him with. Thrawn and Thalias also see a chimaera painted on the hull of a Nikardun blockade frigate that Uingali has captured.[860]
      • The Springhawk is attacked above the planet "Sunrise" by three formations of five missile boats. Soon thereafter, the Grayshrike, commanded by Lakinda arrives in the system to deliver a message from Admiral Ar'alani to Thrawn, and Lakinda orders her Grayshrike to join the battle to protect the Springhawk. Both Chiss warships are attacked by the HopeBreaker apparently accompanied by a repair ship which turns out to be a freighter. The Battle Dreadnought keeps the Chiss forces occupied while the freighter escapes to hyperspace. Thrawn disables or destroyed all of the missile boats as well as the armaments and sensors on the Battle Dreadnought's port side. After the freighter escapes into hyperspace, the Battle Dreadnought also flees, but first destroys all the intact missile boats so the Chiss can not learn anything about their design or schematics.[860]
      • A skirmish is fought above the planet Nettehi, homeworld of the Paataatus, between the Chiss Ascendancy and the Nikardun Destiny. It initially appears that the native Paataatus will attack the inbound forces of the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet, but the Paataatus military ally with the Chiss Ascendancy against the remnants of the forces of General Yiv. The Paataatus were subjugated by the Nikardun, and the skirmish above Nettehi frees them from Nikardun occupation.[860]
    • "Jixtus", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military, begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy.[860]
      • Members of the telepathic Agbui species undertake an attempt to precipitate a civil war in the Chiss Ascendancy, on behalf of the Grysk Hegemony.[860]
        • The Vigilant and the Grayshrike come across an asteroid missile platform in a nearly-circular orbit above Sunrise which was not there two weeks prior., and encounter the same Unidentified Battle Dreadnought from the first skirmish over Sunrise, bearing the damage inflicted to its port side weapons and sensor installments. The Battle Dreadnought's commander demands the Chiss leave the area, and a skirmish ensues using breachers, dibbers, plasma spheres, spectrum lasers, and the asteroid missile platform. The asteroid missile platform's outer shell detonates when it believes it comes under attack. Ar'alani keeps the Battle Dreadnought occupied with and leads the enemy commander to believe the Grayshrike destroyed by the asteroid missile launcher. Ar'alani damages the Battle Dreadnought from the Starboard flank, and Lakinda eventually attacks the already-damaged port flank. Ar'alani attempts to gain information from the damaged enemy, at which point he launches a missile that destroys the asteroid missile platform and initiates self-destruct, destroying his Battle Dreadnought.[860]
        • A skirmish is fought between Chiss forces and the Watith crew of a freighter, whose Captain Fsir has been hired by Jixtus to destroy or delay Thrawn so he can not prevent a battle between Chiss Great Families over a supposedly valuable mining world. While exploring leads related to the Nikardun campaigns, Thrawn encounters a distress call from a freighter identifying itself as the Saltbarrel, whose captain claims to be under attack by Vagaari pirates, being attacked by three gunboats. Thrawn notes, however, that little damage is actually being done to the Saltbarrel and suspects that the attack is a trap, so he boards the Watith freighter and find remote-piloting systems aboard, as the Watith crew has been piloting the Watith gunboats. Upon returning to the Springhawk, Thrawn orders his crew to fire to disable the Saltbarrel, and they capture the ship, fourteen Watith gunboats, and the Watith crew.[860]
        • A battle is fought above the planet Hoxim between Chiss forces as a result of the machinations by Jixtus. The culmination of Jixtus' plot to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy was to draw rival Chiss Great Families, each allied with a different Ruling Family, into open conflict; which would spread to the Ruling Families and then the entire Ascendancy. The skirmish nearly accomplishes this, but the actions of Thrawn and Lakinda of Picket Force Six prevent such an outbreak. Agents of Jixtus manipulate ambitious officials from the three rival Great Families of Xodlak, Pommrio, and Erighal into gathering their family fleets in an open confrontation to lay claim to the supposedly nyix-rich mining world of Hoxim. Hostilities nearly break out between the forces of the Great Families above the Agbui mining world, until Lakinda and Thrawn are able to convince the Great Families' fleets to work together against a supposed threat, which is in reality a force of remote-controlled gunboats controlled by Xodlak, Pommrio, and Erighal family members aboard Thrawn's Springhawk. Thrawn has his warriors and officers put on a light show, targeting their families' vessels with "maximum ferocity but minimal actual damage". At the end of the skirmish, Thrawn has his sky-walker navigator Che'ri use her powers of Third Sight to guide the hands of weapons officer Afpriuh. Che'ri and Afpriuh aim at the commandeered Watith freighter at the exact angle needed to destroy the false Agbui nyix mine, and make it look accidental. As a result, the Great Families can not fight over the fake nyix deposit, believing it destroyed.[860]
      • Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war.[54]
        • The armed freighters of the Chiss Tahmie and Droc families exchange fire at Csaus inside the Ascendancy, before Chiss Defense Force Admiral Ers'ikaro's Firewolf man-of-war Bellicose stops the battle in order to prevent further escalation of the crisis.[54]
        • A skirmish takes place over the Ascendancy planet Jamiron between the Tahmie and Droc families, soon after the two families' previous engagement over Csaus. The Chiss Syndicure dispatches the Nightdragon man-of-war Vigilant, under the command of Admiral Ar'alani, to resolve the situation. When the warship reaches Jamiron, eighteen starships belonging to both families are engaged in battle. After the families ignore the admiral's orders to cease hostilities, Ar'alani's vessel fird plasma spheres, weapons that disable vessels, at all the combatants. After all eighteen Tahmie and Droc ships are neutralized, the Vigilant uses its tractor beams to haul the disabled ships away from each other. After the skirmish, Ar'alani meet with Supreme Admiral Ja'fosk of the Ascendancy's Defense Hierarchy Council, who informs her the Syndicure is infuriated by her intervention as it has humiliated both families.[54]
        • A standoff is fought between freighters of the Chiss Chaf and Ufsa families at the Ascendancy planet Sarvchi.[54]
        • A transport of the Erighal family and a patrol ship of the Boadil family fight above the planet Shihon.[54]
        • At the Ascendancy planet Ornfra, the Orisson, a light cruiser of the Clarr family commanded by Captain Clarr'os'culry, engages at least two destroyers of the Dasklo family, before the heavy cruiser Grayshrike of the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet intervenes.[54]
        • Jixtus, who has allied himself with the Kilji Illumine nation, sends General Crofyp's task force to the Rapacc system to capture a group of refugees hiding on the planet Rapacc. The general's task force, consisting of Crofyp's Kilji war cruiser Anvil and Colonel Tildnis's Kilji picket cruiser Hammer, carry out an attack, confronting the Paccosh-commandeered Nikardun blockade frigate Aelos. Crofyp instructs the blockade frigate's captain, Uingali foar Marocsaa, to move aside and allow the Kiljis to capture the refugees they have come for; however, Marocsaa refuses, prompting the general to prepare a cross fire attack on the Aelos. The Anvil and the Hammer move along the frigate's starboard and portside respectively, and once they are in position, the Springhawk arrives at the outer system, firing two plasma sphere weapons at Crofyp's flagship before joining the confrontation in the inner system. Thrawn instructs Crofyp to move clear of the Aelos, angering the general, who fires spectrum laser salvos at the Springhawk. Shortly after, the plasma spheres slamm into the Anvil's starboard while the war cruiser shields the Hammer so that the picket cruiser can escape. Thrawn allowes the Hammer to escape and destroys Crofyp's flagship with breacher missiles.[54]
        • Jixtus has several falsified patrol ships, supposedly from the Erighal and Xodlak families, attack a Clarr family convoy carrying valuable technology at the Ascendancy planet of Jamiron. Though the Clarr freighters are highly damaged, their escort cruiser, the Orisson, commanded by Captain Roscu, as well as local Clarr patrol ships, fight back. The heavy cruiser Grayshrike, under the command of Senior Captain Ziinda which has been posted to defend at Jamiron, also step in to assist in the battle. After two of the false patrol ships are destroyed, the others self-destruct, leaving nothing intact to be captured. However, from analyzing the wreckage, both Ziinda and Roscu determine the patrol ships to be fake, though the latter assumes that the rival Dasklo family is responsible.[54]
        • After the attack on Rapacc, the Kilji picket cruiser Hammer flees, and travels to the Sunrise system according to General Crofyp and Jixtus's instructions, where keeps station with one of Jixtus's warships. The Paccosh-commandeered Aelos and its complement of Paccian commandos arrive shortly, and request permission from the Grysk vessel to dock with the Hammer, which is granted through comm. Docking with the picket cruiser, the blockade frigate is able to board the Kilji warship and kill all aboard, including the ship's commander, Colonel Tildnis. As the Chiss heavy cruiser Springhawk arrives at the system seeking the capture of Thrawn, who is aboard the Aelos, the Grysk warship fires at it. However, after Thrawn retrieves data on the Illumine Kilhorde, the Kiljis' battle fleet, the Grysk are forced to concentrate their laser fire on the rogue Hammer. Shortly after the remaining individuals—the Hammer's navigator Sarsh, Thrawn, Paccosh Commander Uingali foar Marocsaa and his Paccian commandos—aboard the Hammer flee aboard the Aelos and Bogey One, as the Springhawk has codenamed the Grysk vessel, destroy the Hammer and jump to hyperspace.[54]
        • The Paataatus Hiveborn nation carries out an attack on the Illumine Kilhorde, after the fleet conquered several small nations to the north and northwest of the Hiveborn's territory as part of the Illumine's conquests of enlightenment. with data on the Kilhorde's design and battle doctrine retrieved from Tildnis' warship. The Hiveborn obliterate the Kilji battle fleet and free the nations the Illumine's forces had conquered. The destruction of the Illumine's battle fleet prompts Jixtus, who had allied himself with the Illumine's ruler, Generalirius Nakirre, to murder him, as the Kilhorde's destruction renders Nakirre useless to his ally.[54]
        • A battle is fought between a Chiss Ascendancy fleet led by Thrawn and Jixtus's fleet. As Jixtus attempts to instigate a civil war in the Ascendancy, Thrawn realizes that if the Grysk's fleet is not destroyed, its commander will be able to use it to destroy the Ascendancy. Thrawn and his colleagues form a large fleet and bring it to the Sunrise system, where they deactivate it, making it seem as if it is destroyed. Once Jixtus's fleet and the Kilji war cruiser Whetstone arrive at Sunrise, Jixtus's Grysk warships move toward the Chiss; however, that separates the vessels and surrounds them with enemy ships that "revive" and attack the Grysks shortly afterward. As the Grysk StoneCrusher-class WarMasters and Prism-class BattleChiefs are destroyed by Thrawn's forces, fifteen starships of the Garwian Unity and Vak Combine nations arrive and attack the Grysk forces, destroying many of Jixtus's warships. His flagship, the Shatter-class WarMaster, then tries to flee. As the FateSpinner can destroy the Whetstone before fleeing so that there are no non-Chiss witnesses to the Sunrise confrontation, the war cruiser's navigator, Pathfinder Qilori of Uandualon, instructs the cruiser's crew—the Kilji vassals of the Kilji Illumine nation—to destroy the WarMaster before it can kill him. The vassals then fire all of the vessel's weaponry at the Fatespinner, destroying the Grysk warship's starboard aft flank. The Kilji attack prompts Jixtus to activate the self-destruct mechanisms aboard his seven remaining vessels, destroying all of them but his flagship. After telling Thrawn that the Grysks will return, the Grysk destroys the FateSpinner, killing himself.[54]
        • As Thrawn violated Chiss law that prohibits fighting if an enemy has not attacked the Ascendancy, the Syndicure remove him from the Expansionary Defense Fleet and turns him into a member of his family, the Mitth family, without rank. Although the Syndicure seeks to punish everyone who fought at Sunrise, they only press charges against Thrawn, who is exiled to a world in Wild Space. Supreme General of the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet Ba'kif and Thrawn view the exile as a way to send the senior captain to the region beyond the Unknown Regions known to the Chiss as "Lesser Space." Thrawn is to contact the Galactic Empire and determine if the government can help the Ascendancy fight the Grysks.[54]
    • The Graf family is pursued by the Empire. Auric Graf and his wife Rhyssa are arrested by Captain Visler Aqorian Korda III of the Empire, forcing their two children, Lina and Milo, to escape on their own.[861][862]
