    • Caleb Dume is rescued from capture by clone troopers on the planet Lahn by Kalleran smuggler Janus Kasmir and former Separatist general Kleeve. Caleb changes his name to Kanan Jarrus and begins a new life.[863]
    • Darth Vader eliminates a Geonosian queen and her group of Geonosians who were attempting to sabotage the Empire's Death Star project. Vader then inspects the Death Star plans on Scarif without neither Tarkin nor the Emperor's permission.[864][21]
    • Imperial forces, led by Wilhuff Tarkin, carry out arrests, executions, and massacres on Antar 4, as punishment for Antar 4's allegiance to the Confederacy during the Clone Wars, in what the media would call the Antar Atrocity.[865]
    • A multi-week campaign is fought at the Salient system. Moff Tarkin leads an Imperial invasion force, and are opposed by Saw Gerrera's Partisans and the rebeling factions on Salient's worlds, Salient I and Salient II.[76]
    • Former Padawan Ferren Barr begins influencing the King of the planet Mon Cala, Lee-Char, in negotiations with the Galactic Empire. When the Emperor recognizes these negotiation techniques, Vader is dispatched alongside three Inquisitors and a death squad of Purge Troopers to investigate the possible Jedi. Shortly after Lord Vader's arrival, Imperial Ambassador Telvar is assassinated by Barr leading to Governor Wilhuff Tarkin, aboard the Imperial I-class Star Destroyer Sovereign, sending forces down to invade Mon Cala.[866]
      • During the initial battle at Dac City, Vader and his troops capture and interrogate Lee-Char. However, they are interrupted by a tsunami created by a school of large fish-like creatures which wipes out the Imperial forces on the surface of Mon Cala.[866]
      • While Vader and his Inquisitors regroup, the Imperial Colonel Bergon is fighting in Mon Cala's northern regions aboard an aerial landing platform. The Mon Calamari Gial Ackbar leads an assault on the platform and destroys it with Bergon aboard.[867]
      • In the southern regions, the Mon Calamari Admiral Raddus interlinks the shields of merchant ships from the Mon Cala Mercantile Fleet to combat Imperial forces.[867]
      • Vader and his group attack Barr and his disciples and pursue them to the underwater settlement Bel City. While Vader is requested by Tarkin to track down Lee-Char, the Inquisitors and the Purge troopers corner Barr and his last remaining disciple. Barr, though, uses a mind trick to turn the Purge troopers against the Inquisitors, allowing the Padawan to escape..[868]
      • Vader goes to capture Lee-Char and forces him to watch as Tarkin begins an orbital bombardment of Mon Cala. Barr soon arrives and fights Vader. When the Jedi reveals he deliberately kick-started the war, the King panicks and orders a cease-fire of his forces, although the governor continues it for a while. Vader then kills Barr and arrests Lee-Char. At the same time, Raddus escapes with a handful of his merchant ships.[869]
      • After being arrested, Lee-Char is imprisoned on Strokill Prime. The Empire proceeds to subjugate Mon Cala. The Mon Calamari, however, launch a mass exodus aboard spaceworthy structures disguised as city buildings.[869]
    • By this point, the first Empire Day had occurred, marking exactly one year after the initiation of the Jedi Purge, the end of the Clone Wars, and the founding of the Galactic Empire.[816]
    • The Empire begins a large terraforming project on Ilum after finally claiming it;[50] the planet's core is exposed and exploited by Star Destroyers for kyber crystals.[816]
    • An uprising takes place against the Empire on the agricultural moon of Raada. In response to Imperial restrictions and the introduction of soil-destroying crops, the local farmers organize a resistance movement under the leadership and tutelage of Ahsoka Tano. Tano's plans go awry when the farmers Kaeden Larte and Hoban launch an unsanctioned attack on the Imperial administrative building, where several fighters including Hoban are killed by the Imperial garrison. Tano uses her Force powers to save the rebels but exposes herself to both the Empire and the Jedi sympathizer Senator Bail Organa, who is organizing a rebel movement. Tano and the surviving rebels flee to a network of cave hideouts near the moon's main settlement. After rescuing Kaeden from Imperial captivity, Tano goes into hiding offworld at Kaeden's advice. However, the Empire send the Inquisitor known as the Sixth Brother to hunt Tano. The Sixth Brother discovers the rebel hideout, captures Kaeden, and kills most of the rebels except Kaeden's younger sister Miara Larte. At the request of Senator Organa, Tano returns to Raada to investigate the Sixth Brother. After killing the Sixth Brother, Tano manages to rescue Kaeden again and takes part in helping to evacuate the moon's population with the help of Senator Organa's rebel forces.[816]
    • At this point, the Empire continues to deploy Clone Wars-era prefab buildings and walkers of the Republic's military.[816]
    • Bail Organa and Ahsoka Tano organize a rebel network.[816]
    • The Western Reaches pacification operations begin in response to rebellion from remaining Separatist holdouts.[76]
    • Darth Vader and Governor Ahr lead Imperial attacks on insurgents with a Venator-class Star Destroyer and its complement of V-wings. Vader is ordered to follow Ahr's orders to the letter by Emperor Palpatine to teach him a lesson.[870]
      • The Imperials attack the Mid Rim world of Namzor, with the aim to secure coaxium supplies and to destroy the insurgents near the area. Ahr sends out V-wing starfighters to annihilate them, resulting in most of the insurgents dying. As Ahr sees the coaxium supplies he recalls all of his fighters, though Vader in his starship attacks, causing the destruction of the coaxium that the Moff wanted.[870]
      • Governor Ahr leads the Imperial forces to attack the Mid Rim planet of Ahn Krantarium, in order to destroy deadly droids that had overrun the world long before the Imperial Era, and that had rebuilt themselves to be more dangerous than regular security droids. Ahr sends out a stormtrooper patrol, but all are killed, so instead of sending out more to die for nothing, the Governor sends Vader alone. He survives and destroys all of the droids in the vicinity.[870]
      • The Imperial forces launch an attack against insurgents based on the moon Phelzepham. As Vader and the stormtrooper detachment he leads push their numerical advantage and are on the verge of victory, Ahr decides to test his hold over Vader by ordering the stormtroopers to withdraw. Although he is suddenly left alone, Vader completes the mission and defeats the insurgents.[870]
      • The governor decides to head to the Kankalo Belt Containment Zone despite the protests of a subordinate of his. He sends out Vader in his starship to destroy the greatest threat there, the Kankalo Belt creature. Since Vader sees Ahr as his biggest threat, when he sees the creature, he turns back toward the Star Destroyer, with the creature following him, to eliminate the Moff. When Vader returns, Ahr is angry with him and orders that he kneel before him as the Emperor commanded. When Vader kneels, the creature claws into the Star Destroyer's bridge to grab Ahr away, killing him and multiple other personnel in the vacuum of space. The Emperor then senses the governor's death and sees that Vader has learned his lesson.[870]
    • Mon Mothma begins to organize military and intelligence movements for an anti-Empire coalition known then as the "Alliance".[871]
  • 17 BBY
    • The Erso family flees from Coruscant during the chaos of Coruscant's All-Species Week, after they discover that Galen Erso's research has been weaponized by Orson Krennic and the Empire, with the help of Saw Gerrera and Has Obitt.[76]
    • Navik the Red, a Rodian of the Chattza clan from the city of Iskaayuma on the planet Rodia, strikes against his rival clans, overthrowing them in an insurrection in which he captures the Rodian capital, Equator City. Declaring himself Grand Protector of the planet, he switches the planet's capital to his home city, which is also the seat of his clan.[872]
    • Sanver, the Imperial Senator for the Barabel Senator is killed alongside 3 Jedi Knights he hid in his private quarters by the Inquisitorius.[873]
    • Darth Vader discovers the wreckage of the Tribunal along with the bodies of the 332nd Division and Ahsoka Tano's lightsabers on a unidentified moon.[874]
    • Gwellis Bagnoro is born on Onod I. (Approximate date)[875]
    • Verge is born.[876]
    • Leonart Beehaz is born to Roboda Beehaz and Jayhold Beehaz, an Imperial Military Officer.[877]
  • 16 BBY
    • Unkar Plutt is born on Crul.[878]
    • Nifera Shu is born.(Approximate date)[879]
    • The Lars moisture farm is attacked by mercenaries. Obi-Wan Kenobi tries to defend young Luke Skywalker, who was playing outside, and together with Owen lars, drive the bandits away. However, Lars then punches Kenobi and tells him not to go anywhere near Luke.[880]
    • The chain code of bounty hunter Boba Fett is encoded into a set of armor once worn by his father, Jango Fett. (Approximate date)[881]
  • 15 BBY
    • The Western Reaches pacification operations are brought to a close.[17]
    • As orchestrated by the Emperor himself, Gallius Rax joins the Imperial Starfleet with the rank of commander.[30]
    • The Axis network is founded.[882]
    • Asara Deyn and Calys are born.[502]
    • Kitwarr is born on Kashyyyk.[883]
    • Wyl Lark is born on Polyneus.[884]
  • c. 15 BBY
    • Vader goes to the planet Benath in Wild Space where he fights the Benathy after his demands are not fulfilled. The Benathy are subdued, but Darth Vader is unable to kill their god, a massive Zillo Beast.[21]
    • Thrawn is discovered on his "exile" planet by the Galactic Empire and, upon meeting Emperor Palpatine, joins the Imperial Military. Over the ensuing years, he will rise through the ranks, with Eli N. Vanto always at his side.[885][886]
  • 14 BBY
    • Fortress Vader begins construction on Mustafar, designed by Darth Momin.[887]
    • Xea dies of an incident caused by the rebel-conducted theft of an air scrubber farm on Eriadu. (Approximate date)[888]
    • By this point, the mother of the individual who came to be known as TK-462 had passed away. (Approximate date)[889]
    • Several members of the Free Ryloth Movement led by Pok undertake a mission to hijack an Imperial weapons shipment at the Yaga Minor shipyards. They are all killed by Vader, and Cham Syndulla and Isval vow to accelerate their rebellion against the Empire.[890][891]
    • Darth Sidious and Darth Vader undertake a mission to quell the growing rebellion on Ryloth, and to root out a traitor within the Imperial ranks.[890][891]
      • The Free Ryloth Movement led by Cham Syndulla attempts to assassinate the two Sith Lords.[890][891]
      • Deaths of Belkor Dray and Isval.[890][891]
    • Former Galactic Republic Intelligence officer Berch Teller leads a rebel campaign against the Galactic Empire.[17][892]
      • Berch Teller's rebel cell launches an attack against the Empire's Sentinel Base. Using communications equipment supplied by Vice Admiral Dodd Rancit, the rebels send a fake holotransmission claiming that Rampart Station is under attack. Tarkin detect the message as a fraud and sends his Venator-class Star Destroyer, Electrum on a microjump to the edge of the system. Shortly after, a modified Providence-class Dreadnought enters the system. Tarkin then deploys the Venator and orders it to ambush the intruders. The Providence cruiser retreats to hyperspace shortly afterward.[17]
      • Vader and Tarkin travel to the planet Murkhana, where a cache of communications equipment has been discovered, which is in fact bait meant to lure the pair to the world so that a Berch Teller's cell can steal Tarkin's personal starship, the Carrion Spike. Upon reaching Murkhana, and accompanied by a dozen stormtroopers, they retrieve the equipment, which is then left to the troopers to document and transport to the Carrion Spike. Vader and Tarkin visit the Murkhana City Imperial compound, where they meet with the planet's Imperial ambassador and the Koorivar Intelligence asset who has discovered the cache. While the meeting is taking place, the rebel cell creates a false communication to the troopers in which they impersonate Tarkin and tell the Imperials to return to the cache. The cell then boards the Carrion Spike and kills its crew before stealing it. Tarkin and Vader are alerted to the theft, and visit a local Sugi crime lord named Faazah, who they blackmail into giving them a ship, the Parsec Predator. However, the cell manages to escape the Murkhana system and the Imperials.[17]
      • After stealing the Carrion Spike from Tarkin, Teller's cell attacks an Imperial space station orbiting the planet Galidraan III in the Galidraan system. The starship manages to cause large amounts of damage to the station, and the stations defenses have trouble returning fire. Tarkin and Vader arrive in the system on board the Parsec Predator and proceeded to try and attack the Carrion Spike alongside starfighters from the station. The corvette is damaged by the Predator, but manages to disable the Imperial's vessel before escaping the system.[17]
      • Berch Teller and his cell attack TaggeCo's ore mining facility on Lucazec, while Tarkin is chasing the Carrion Spike through hyperspace. Extensive damage is made to the mining facility and several V-wings and ore haulers are destroyed. The dissidents then broadcast their attack on a holovid that is circulated throughout the Imperial HoloNet.[17]
      • A battle takes place in the Phindar system between Berch Teller's cell and Imperial forces under Tarkin and Vader. The Imperials intercept the stolen Carrion Spike as is docking with a tanker to refuel above Phindar. Vader and Tarkin's Yellow Squadron of V-wing starfighters attack the Carrion Spike, and the tanker dispatches Aggressive ReConnaissance-170 starfighters to intercept Tarkin's starfighters. Before the Imperials can fire at the Carrion Spike, the ship flees into hyperspace. The tanker is then destroyed by a rogue fuel cell, which destroys several V-wings.[17].
      • Berch Teller transmits disinformation that the Carrion Spike is traveling to the Obroa-skai system. Knotts, one of Teller's dissidents, hires the Koorivar captain of the YT-1000 freighter Reticent to travel to the Obroa-skai system as a decoy. Acting on this information, Tarkin and Vader deploy three Imperial interdictor vessels into the Obroa-skai system, hoping to recover the stolen Carrion Spike. However, the Immobilizer 418's gravity well projector malfunctions and yanks several civilian ships out of hyperspace, including the luxury MC80 Star Cruiser Stellar Vista, the smuggling vessel Truant, and the Reticent. The Stellar Vista collides with a Detainer CC-2200, damaging both ships and causing the deaths of 1,100 civilians. Despite failing to capture Teller's dissidents, the Imperials manage to destroy the Truant and capture the Reticent. After interrogating the captain and his crew, Vader and Tarkin learn about the identity of Teller and his dissidents.[17]
      • Berch Teller and his rebels attack the Imperial facility in the Nouane system. In response to the insurgents, Vice Admiral Dodd Rancit fortifies the facility. Despite the stolen Carrion Spike's stealth technology, the insurgents are forced to retreat by a continuous onslaught of long-range lasers. After barely escaping with their lives, Teller and his dissidents deduce that their contact Rancit has betrayed them. Suspecting a trap at Carida, the dissidents instead send the Carrion Spike on autopilot to the Carida system, and transfer to Berch Teller's warship.[17]
      • A battle takes place over an unidentified moon near the Gulf of Tatooine. Berch Teller chooses this location for his rebel cell to ambush an Imperial convoy, transporting the hyperdrive for the secret Death Star project from Desolation Station to Sentinel Base. However, Tarkin learns about their plan and leads Imperial Navy reinforcements which defeat the dissidents. However, Teller manages to escape into hiding.[17]
    • Moff Wilhuff Tarkin enunciates the Tarkin Doctrine.[17][892]
    • A major shift in the Imperial bureaucracy occurs as a result of the Berch Teller campaign; the Deputy Director position of Imperial Intelligence's Naval Intelligence Agency is filled by Colonel Wullf Yularen of the Imperial Security Bureau whilst ISB Deputy Director Harus Ison is reassigned to the Ubiqtorate. Additionally, Admiral Nils Tenant is promoted to the Joint Chiefs, Wilhuff Tarkin is appointed to the position of Grand Moff and Governor of the Outer Rim, and the Imperial Ruling Council members Conan Antonio Motti and Cassio Tagge are also promoted.[17][892]
    • With its newly installed hyperdrive recovered from Berch Teller's insurgency, the Death Star leaves Geonosis' orbit and moves into deep space.[17][892]
    • Secret mission to rebuild the Jedi Order[6]
      • The Inquisitorius senses an echo in the Force from the former Padawan Cal Kestis in hiding on Bracca, and the Second Sister and Ninth Sister move to apprehend him. Following a tense confrontation, Kestis escapes the planet aboard the Stinger Mantis.[6]
      • Cal Kestis sets out with former Jedi Cere Junda, pilot Greez Dritus, and the droid BD-1 to recover a holocron containing a list of Force-sensitive children sealed within a vault on Bogano by the Jedi Master Eno Cordova. Following Cordova's instructions to open the Vault, Kestis travels to Zeffo, where a Project Auger outpost, by order of the Emperor himself, excavates the tombs of Zeffo Sages Eilram and Miktrull. Kestis is then tasked to find Chieftain Tarfful, a friend of Cordova's.[6]
      • Saw Gerrera and the Partisans attack an Imperial Refinery on Kashyyyk. With the help of Cal Kestis, they successfully free numerous Wookiees, though Gerrera retreated from the planet in thinking that Kashyyyk was already lost. Several Partisan fighters choose to stay; led by Mari Kosan, they flee into the Shadowlands to join Tarfful's resistance.[6]
      • Kestis, in addition to having a large bounty placed on him by the Haxion Brood, is wanted by the Imperial Inquisition. Nevertheless, the Jedi learns to let go of his fear as well as to keep rising up from the Origin Tree.
      • Kestis finds that the Bogano Vault could be unlocked by an astrium from the Tomb of Kujet on Dathomir. However, the Jedi is accosted by the nightsister Merrin and the mad Jedi Taron Malicos on Dathomir. Eventually arriving at Kujet's tomb, Kestis is overwhelmed by regret and anger over his past and loses control, destroying his master's lightsaber.[6]
      • After building a new lightsaber on Imperial Ilum, which now houses a world-wide artificial trench, Cal Kestis returns to Dathomir, where he and Merrin realize that they were both survivors who shared a similar past of loss and aim to restore their kind. They thus join forces, allowing the intricate Force of Dathomir to consume the fallen Taron Malicos.[6]
      • While Merrin expresses her concern that recruiting the children of the Force would instead endanger the potential Jedi, Kestis presses on his quest for the holocron at Bogano as the Empire approaches. While Kestis overpowers the Second Sister, the echo of her past and suffering overwhelms Kestis, allowing the Inquisitor to steal Eno Cordova's holocron for the Empire.[6]
      • Cal Kestis and Cere Junda discuss the Second Sister's past, with Junda accepting the pain of the past, letting go and willing to move on. Kestis is knighted, and together, the two Jedi storm the Fortress Inquisitorius on Nur to recover Eno Cordova's holocron. The Second Sister dies, though not before she sees pass her hate for Junda's failure regarding Padawans, instead desiring her kind to be avenged. Kestis and Junda barely escape the facility with their lives and the holocron, and back aboard the Stinger Mantis, Kestis destroys the holocron; the Jedi chooses to let go of forging the destinies of children, instead letting their destinies lie before the will of the Force.[6]
    • Berch Teller fails in attempting to assassinate Wilhuff Tarkin on Eriadu's Tarkin family–owned Carrion Plateau.[17][892]
    • Tarkin's tightening of the Empire's grip on the Outer Rim, including his homeworld Eriadu, ensures a safer environment for Imperial citizens on Eriadu. One such citizen was the brother of Xea, who leaves home to enlist as a stormtrooper after Governor Tarkin's visit to his farm despite his father's warnings. The brother of Xea joins the Junior Academy in Phelar, Eriadu. (Approximate date)[893]
  • 13 BBY
    • While hiding on the planet Lah'mu with his wife, Lyra, and his daughter, Jyn Erso, Galen Erso is taken away by Orson Krennic to continue his work on the Death Star. Jyn is taken in by Saw Gerrera.[894]
    • Goll, a former member of the Free Ryloth movement, along with Hera Syndulla, Karthakk Group member Trae Baratha and the crew of the freighter Eclipse go on relief mission to deliver gattis-root extract to Twi'lek elders on Ryloth stricken with bybbec fever.[895]
    • Han and Qi'ra partake in an auction on Corellia for a data cube with plans for a shield generator; originally won by the Droid Gotra, the Kaldana Syndicate ultimately wins the bid.[896]
    • Han and Qi'ra attempts to escape from the Imperial– and White Worm Gang–controlled Corellia; only Han is successful, with Qi'ra being later rescued by Crimson Dawn's Dryden Vos and enlisted as his lieutenant.[897]
    • Han enrolls in the Imperial Starfleet with the hopes of being a pilot. As Han lacks a family name, the enroller gives him the surname "Solo."[897][598]
    • Han Solo trains alongside the brothers Lyttan and Tamu Dree, Kanina Nico, and Beilert Valance at the Imperial Carida Academy as TIE pilot cadets.[898]
    • During the final stages of the mission on Qhulosk, Han Solo disobeys Instructor Yurib Nakan's orders to rescue Valance, saving his life. Solo's crew becomes disillusioned with the Empire and lets Ghular miners to escape from both their Qhuloskian slavers and the Empire. Valance is fitted with cybernetics to compensate for his injuries whilst Kanina Nico rejoins her love in the nearby Huru system to escape from the oppression of the Empire.[899]
    • The brother of Xea graduates from the Junior Academy to the Imperial Academy of Eriadu. He eventually becomes the stormtrooper TK-462 and requests to be reassigned to combat rebels on Lothal. (Approximate date)[900]
    • A crisis begins on Moltok when a Makurth boss shoots at a Ho'Din settlement. This leads the Ho'Din to request Imperial support to prevent the crisis from becoming a full-fledged war. At the time of the crisis the Gozanti-class cruiser Blood Crow is assisting the freighter Dromedar when it is requested to help settle the matter. While members of the Blood Crow's crew stay behind to fix the Dromedar, the Blood Crow jumps into hyperspace.[885]
    • The Dromedar is raided and captured by the pirate band Culoss, led by the insurgent leader Nevil Cygni. The pirates take the crew prisoner and capture the ship's tibanna cargo. When an Imperial boarding party led by Lieutenant Thrawn answers the Dromedar's distress call, they are greeted by Cygni, who trapps the Imperials. However, Thrawn turns the tables on his captors by using a buzz droid to cut a hole in the cage and expose parts of the ship to the vacuum of space. Despite his efforts, Cygni and some of his followers manage to escape with the smuggled tibanna canisters.[21][885]
  • 12 BBY
    • Lord Momin constructs Fortress Vader on Mustafar's Gahenn Plains for Darth Vader,[901] who then kills the dark side servant and artist after a battle against Mustafarians disturbed by the castle's construction.[902] Vader then enters the portal within the Sith cave beneath his fortress, encountering apparitions of his past and losing his lover, Padmé Amidala, once again in his inability to let go. After witnessing the future coming of a figure who was, unbeknownst to the Dark Lord of the Sith, his son Luke Skywalker, Darth Vader destroys the portal in his anger, accepting that it gave him what he needed.[903][904]
    • Ephraim and Mira Bridger are captured by the Galactic Empire.[905]
    • Hera Syndulla leaves Ryloth to fight against the Empire.[679]
    • K-2SO is constructed on Vulpter.[32]
    • Darth Sidious creates a being later known as Dathan on his secret Sith world of Exegol.[906]
  • Between 12 BBY and 11 BBY[907]
    • Cal Kestis and Merrin duel the Fifth Brother on Murkhana.[908] Within years, the crew of the Stinger Mantis break up.[909]
  • 11 BBY
    • A conflict takes place between a small group of rebels on the planet Gorse and its moon, Cynda, and the Empire, represented by the ambitious industrialist Count Denetrius Vidian. After learning that Vidian is visiting the Gorse system, the rebel operative Hera Syndulla travels there to tail the Count. In an attempt to meet the Emperor's new thorilide quota and to discredit his rival Baron Lero Danthe, Vidian decides to detonate Cynda under the pretext of accessing the moon's substantial thorilide resources. In response, Hera forges an informal rebel cell consisting of the dissident miner Skelly, the former Jedi Padawan Kanan Jarrus, and the former surveillance operator Zaluna Myder to stop the Count. After learning that Vidian has tampered with his research on Cynda's detonation, the rebels are able to convince the Imperial Captain Rae Sloane that Vidian's destruction of Cynda will cause the moon's thorilide crystals to disintegrate. Sharing a common enemy, the rebels and Sloane takw action to stop Vidian's plan. The Count is killed during a showdown above Cynda. Due to the rebels' actions and Sloane's help, Cynda is saved from destruction.[543]
    • Hera Syndulla and Kanan Jarrus join forces.[543]
    • Temmin Wexley is born on the planet Akiva to Norra and Brentin Lore Wexley.[28]
    • Death of Enfys Nest's mother,[910] leader of the Cloud-Riders, the mantle of which passes down to the teenage Enfys Nest.[911]
    • A battle erupts above the planet Umbara between naval forces of the Empire and forces controlled by the mysterious individual known as Nightswan. The battle ends in the Empire's favor when Captain Thrawn relays orders to the other vessels of Admiral Carlou Gendling's task force to target Nightswan's Vulture droids while they are within the transmission shadow of the individual ships.[21][885]
  • Between 11 BBY and 9 BBY[912]
    • A land dispute takes place on the planet Cyphar between the local Cyphari Afe clan and human colonists based on the Hollenside Enclave, rooted in the colonists' desire to harvest a pre-spice material used in the manufacture of scarn that is running in a vein beneath the Afe territory. After a brief battle, Commander Thrawn and his aide Eli Vanto manage to arrest Clay Tanoo and a group of other colonists that entered Afe lands in the dark of night to collect the pre-spice, but they can not be brought on charges because possession of pre-spice is not considered to be illegal. To destroy the colonists' source of profit, Thrawn uses turbolaser fire to burn out the entire pre-spice vein, which angers the Afes, whose farmland suffers a considerable amount of damage in the process. In response to angry report of Afe Chief Joko, Thrawn has to explain his actions for a court-martial panel, but is cleared of all misconduct over the incident, and soon after Thrawn and Vanto both receive promotions to higher ranks in the Imperial Navy.[885]
    • A large espionage operation is conducted by members of the Higher Skies Advocacy Group and Yinchom Dojo on Coruscant, involving collecting information on Imperial projects and targets on behalf of the insurgent leader Nevil Cygni. This spy network is exposed by the Lothalite expatriate Arihnda Pryce, who becomes a double agent under the guidance of Commander Thrawn and the Head of the Imperial Security Bureau Colonel Wullf Yularen. As a reward for her services, Pryce is made Governor of the Lothal sector by Grand Moff Tarkin.
    • The troop carrier Sempre is attacked by two squadrons of stolen V-19 fighters and the frigate Castilus while transporting Wookiee slaves. The attackers damage and board the transport, and the Sempre's captain transmits a distress call that is received by the Imperial Star Destroyer Chimaera, Commodore Thrawn's flagship. Imperial authorities ordered the Chimaera's stormtrooper commander Major Ayer not to tell the Chimaera's crew that the Sempre is transporting slaves. However, Thrawn and Lieutenant Commander Eli Vanto insist on inspecting the Sempre and discover its true cargo.[885]
    • After the attack on the Sempre, insurgents and freed Wookiee slaves attack the space station Lansend Twenty-Six in an attempt to liberate their companions. With their communications jammed, Lansend Twenty-Six can not call on the nearby Baklek Base for support, but are aided by the Chimaera under the command of Thrawn, who had anticipated the attack. The rebels are defeated soon after.[885]
  • 10 BBY
    • The Gorothites of Goroth Prime are enslaved by the Empire and mine hyperbarides. This releases a dangerous amount of radiation onto the planet and its inhabitants.[913]
    • The Galactic Empire establishes the Imperial security complex on Scarif's surface.[914]
    • "Vader's Many Prosthetic Parts," a song written by Nakari Kelen's mother and performed by the group Hakko Drazlip and the Tootle Froots, that mocked Darth Vader, is published. Just days after its release, the Empire sent the entire membership of the group to the spice mines of Kessel.[915]
    • Lando Calrissian and L3-37 take up a weapons smuggling job to Kullgroon. They supply the local Petrusian resistance,[916] assisting Kristiss to liberate her people from the Empire. Later, Calrissian denies Kristiss' offer to pioneer a new future for Petrusia, and instead tracks down the Millennium Falcon, which had been captured by the Empire and impounded on Vandor. Calrissian and L3-37 arrive at Vandor's Fort Ypso one week after leaving Kristiss' people.[917][918]
    • Han Solo is expelled from the Imperial Flight Academy for insubordination. He is reassigned to Mimban as an Imperial Army trooper.[630][919]
    • The Empire wages a planetary war against the Mimbanese Liberation Army on the swamp world of Mimban, in an effort to secure the planet's vital resources while establishing a regime loyal to the Empire. The 224th Imperial Armored Division engages the Mimbanese, with the latter eager to drive the Imperials from their homeworld at all costs. The campaign sees the deployment of numerous swamp troopers and wet-weather gear stormtroopers, with the Imperial ground forces making use of trench warfare against the Mimbanese.[897]
      • In one of the bloodiest battles of the campaign, Major Staz leads a group of Swamp troopers including Corporal Han Solo in a ground assault to capture a ridge from the enemy. The Mimbanese fire explosives which kill Major Staz. Han and several soldiers regroup in a trench under the command of "Captain" Tobias Beckett, the last senior officer in the group. Beckett leads the Swamp troopers in a successful assault on the ridge.[897]
      • During a break in the fighting, Solo encounters "Captain" Beckett and his gang associates Val and Rio Durant, who are planning to commandeer a Y-45 armored transport hauler. Recognizing an opportunity to leave behind the Empire, Solo offers them his services as a pilot. When Beckett rebuffes Solo, he threatens to expose them as infiltrators, so Beckett convinces Lieutenant Bolandin they caught Solo trying to desert the Imperial Army. Bolandin throws Solo into a pit to be devoured by "the beast," a Wookiee called Chewbacca. Solo use his knowledge of Shyriiwook to convince the Wookiee to help him escape. After breaking out they go to a nearby airfield where Beckett and his gang are preparing to depart on their stolen transport hauler. Impressed by his determination, Beckett allows Solo and Chewbacca aboard his stolen hauler.[897]
    • Seeking to repay his debts to the Crimson Dawn crime lord Dryden Vos, Beckett leads his gang in a heist on the planet Vandor to recover coaxium from an Imperial conveyex. The criminals face a group of range troopers, led by Captain Denwade Banevans, guarding the transport, forcing them to detach several of the conveyex's carriages to lose them. The group is later ambushed by Enfys Nest and her Cloud-Riders, who intend to seize the cargo for themselves. During the gun battle, Rio is fatally wounded while repelling boarders from his AT-hauler. Val, who had set up position on a nearby bridge overlooking the tracks, is attacked by several 11-3K viper probe droids. Realizing there is not enough time for her to abandon her position, she blows the bridge up and is killed in the ensuing explosion. The coaxium wagon falls into a deep gorge and explodes the Luftgriff Peak in half.[897]
    • Beckett and his crew raid the spice mines of the planet Kessel controlled by the Pyke Syndicate, in order to retrieve raw coaxium for Crimson Dawn.[897]
      • Enslaved workers, organics and droids alike, are liberated from the Spice Mines of Kessel, including the Wookiee Sagwa. L3-37 is destroyed while leading the rebellion, and Han Solo makes the Kessel Run in just under thirteen parsecs, which Solo rounds down to twelve.[897]
    • A showdown breaks out on the planet Savareen between Crimson Dawn, the gang of Tobias Beckett and the Cloud-Riders to determine who would take control of the coaxium stolen from Kessel. Dryden Vos is killed by Qi'ra and Tobias Beckett is killed by Han Solo.[897]
    • Qi'ra joins Maul, leader of Crimson Dawn, on Dathomir.[897] The Dawn disappears a few years later.[801]
    • Han Solo wins the Millennium Falcon from Lando Calrissian in a game of sabacc on Numidian Prime.[897]
    • Sebulba and his crew; consisting of podracer Volo Bolus, Luuda Fluxion and Trizz Vezcro, escape from a facility belonging to Sorbee Manufacturing after stealing a binder coil.[920]
    • Sebulba's crew attempts a heist to steal credits at the Hutt colosseum on Nar Shaddaa. They fail, resulting in Sebulba being captured by Grakkus Jahibakti Tingi.[920]
  • 10/9 BBY
    • The Empire sterilizes Geonosis, eventually leaving Klik-Klak and Karina as the only remaining Geonosians on the planet.[921]
    • Construction of the Death Star moves from Geonosis to Scarif.[914][32]
  • 9 BBY
    • Cal Kestis' adventures
      • Kestis faces Senator Daho Sejan on Coruscant, retrieving data for Saw Gerrera and killing the Ninth Sister.[909]
      • Kestis finds the High Republic Jedi Dagan Gera on Koboh, but Gera had fallen to the dark side and the Bedlam Raiders rallies to Gera. While the Empire battle the raiders, Kestis kills Gera with the help of Bode Akuna and acquires the Abyss compass.[909]
      • Akuna, an Imperial agent and fallen Jedi, betrays Kestis; the Empire raids Cere Junda and Eno Cordova's Jedi Archives on Jedha, battling Hidden Path insurgents while Vader kill the Jedi.[909]
      • Kestis and Merrin duel Akuna on Tanalorr. The pair then begin preparing the world to host the Hidden Path.[909]
    • Miara Larte is promoted to the Rebel Alliance rank of captain at a ceremony on Alderaan.[922][923]
    • Admiral Jyrom Ottdell of the Imperial Navy is murdered with poisonous zolall extract.[40]
    • Imperial Security Bureau's Agent Andressa Divo investigates the Malkite poisoners as a result of Jyrom Ottdell's murder, though Divo receives "Malkite poisoner kits" with an inscription warning against continuing the investigation.[40]
    • Jak is born on Coruscant.[28]
    • The Jedi Nari is hunted down on Tatooine by the Grand Inquisitor, the Fifth Brother, and the Third Sister. The Inquisitors kill him and hang his deceased body in a village for the residents to look upon.[924]
    • Leia Organa is kidnapped on Alderaan by Vect Nokru and his crew at the behest of the Third Sister; Bail Organa travels to Tatooine and begs Obi-Wan Kenobi for help to find his beloved daughter.[924]
    • Obi-Wan Kenobi searches for Leia at the planet Daiyu while being hunted down by the Inquisitorius. They successfully escape the planet, and Kenobi learns that Anakin Skywalker is alive at the process.[925]
    • Obi-Wan and Leia arrive at the planet Mapuzo on instructions from the criminal Haja Estree, who contacts them with Imperial Captain Tala Durith, an undercover agent of the Hidden Path. Vader arrives on the planet and confronts Kenobi, injuring and burning him.[926]
    • Kenobi, alongside Tala Durith, infiltrate the Fortress Inquisitorius on the moon Nur to rescue Leia after she was captured by the Third Sister. They escape with the help of members of the Hidden Path.[927]
    • Vader leads an attack on Jabiim and its Hidden Path fighters. Tala Durith sacrifices herself while the Third Sister reclaims her identity as Reva Sevander and betrays Vader.[928]
    • Vader and Kenobi duel at a labyrinth of rock pinnacles on a barren moon; the latter is victorious and leaves. The hunt for Obi-Wan Kenobi ends after Palpatine questions Vader.[929]
    • Reva Sevander makes an assault on the Lars Homestead in order to kill Luke Skywalker, but she spares him and redeems herself to the light side of the Force with the help of Obi-Wan Kenobi.[929]
    • Obi-Wan Kenobi, having found his inner peace, is finally able to see and converse with the Force ghost of Qui-Gon Jinn.[929]
    • Governor Quesl of the planet Botajef attempts a secession from the Empire, as a cover to steal the planet's valuable art collections for himself. Commodore Thrawn leads Imperial forces in a battle against the Botajef Defense Force that ends in an Imperial victory, Quesl is arrested and Botajef returns to the Empire.[885]
  • 9/8 BBY
    • Obi-Wan Kenobi saves Luke Skywalker when the boy confronts Jabba the Hutt's henchmen about their master's "water tax," under which the local farmers are suffering during the Great Drought. Kenobi carries the unconscious Skywalker to the Lars family homestead and leaves before he wakes up, but the child's courage reassures Kenobi that the Jedi have not yet met their end; his waning hope is renewed.[930]
  • 8 BBY
    • Keln, a loved one of Boushh's, is born.[931]
    • Un'hee is born.(Approximate date)[932]
  • 8/7 BBY
    • A year after the Great Drought, Pagetti Rook and a Klatooinian guard take the Wookiee bounty hunter Black Krrsantan to the Dune Sea where Obi-Wan Kenobi stopped Jabba's water tax. Rook tells Krrsantan about how five of Jabba's saltiest warriors were taken down while not one of them saw the man who did it. Krrsantan roars and starts off towards the moisture farm in front of him. At the Lars Homestead, Owen Lars just recently returned from Tosche Station with some T-16 skyhopper parts. He looks for his nephew, Luke Skywalker in hopes of repairing their busted speeder with him and mend their already strained relationship. All of a sudden, as Owen calls out to his wife Beru, Krrsantan kidnaps him. Meanwhile, unbeknownst to Owen, Luke has run away from home with the intention of going to Mos Eisley and getting off of Tatooine. He does not make it that far as he senses something is wrong. He runs back to the homestead to find the place a mess and his aunt Beru wounded on the floor with a rifle. Luke asks her what happened and where uncle Owen is. She tells him that it took him and orders him to hide in the maintenance bay until he hears her voice.[933]
  • Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY[934]
    • A crew led by Khel Tanna, Han Solo and Chewbacca carries out a heist on the planet Galator III on behalf of Jabba Desilijic Tiure. According to Tanna, Solo and Chewbacca blew the crew's rendezvous, which almost gets the crew killed. Despite this, Chewbacca and Solo prevent Tanna's crew from being placed in shackles, and the heist is pulled off. Afterwards, Tanna, Solo, and Chewbacca visit Jabba on Tatooine, who praises Solo for his actions, to the displeasure of Tanna, who questions why Solo is credited for the success of the heist as it had been her crew that had pulled it off. After the discussion with Jabba, Tanna informs Solo it is the last job she will pull with him as she leaves Jabba's Palace.[935]
    • Jabba Desilijic Tiure sends Han Solo, Chewbacca, and the bounty hunter Greedo to travel to Coronet City on Corellia, to steal an urn of pure verillix containing the ashes of Krestrel D'Naran, the archrival of Jabba. They fail to recover the urn and learn that is has been sold by Augustus Graves, the CEO of the Corellian Engineering Corporation, to Madelin Sun on the planet Antillion.[935]
    • Han Solo meets Corbus Tyra, who claims to be his father, Ovan.[935]
    • Solo, Chewbacca, and Corbus Tyra travel to Antillion where Solo and Tyra are able to locate the urn, but before they can leave with it, they are intercepted by the Wookiee bounty hunter Krrsantan, who is working for Augustus Graves. He stunnes Tyra, and takes Solo to his ship. Chewbacca engages in a firefight with Krrsantan but the bounty hunter is able to escape, though not before Chewbacca places a homing beacon on his ship.[936]
    • Chewbacca and Tyra follow Krrsantan to the planetoid Mollo Tanka, though before Tyra could accompany Chewbacca on the rescue, he pretends to be dazed by the stun blast, and Chewbacca leaves him aboard the Falcon. Chewbacca makes his way to Graves' building, and after scaring off a pair of security guards, makes his way to Graves' office, where he discovers Solo being interrogated for the location of the urn. Chewbacca manages to avoid a blast by Krrsantan, and engages him in a Wookiee death-duel. As the two fight, Chewbacca pulls out a thermal detonator and sets it off at the feet of the bounty hunter, which allows Chewbacca and Solo to fire at security guards before they make their escape from Graves' office. The two, being chased by Krrsantan and security guards, arrive to where the Millennium Falcon had been parked, only to discover that it had been stolen by Tyra.[936]
    • Tyra, in possession of the urn, makes his way to the planet Iakar, and while waiting to speak with an Imperial supervisor, is arrested by Marshal Buck Vancto for various prior crimes. The marshal takes Tyra to the Benelex Marshal Service Headquarters on the planet Paqualis III. The Falcon ends up in an Imperial Impound Facility on Iakar.[937]
    • Khel Tanna's crew, Han Solo, Chewbacca, and Greedo extract Corbus Tyra from the Benelex Marshal Service Headquarters. However, Chewbacca is captured and imprisoned on Gulhadar, and Solo is shot by Greedo and dumped onto the planet Escalan.[937]
    • Tanna's crew make plans with Greedo and Tyra to recover the Falcon and the urn. Tyra and the bounty hunter T'onga go undercover as Imperial officers in the facility on Iakar, while Tanna, Akko, and Ooris Bynar hide in a smuggling compartment as Tanna's ship is captured by the Empire and taken to the impound facility. Greedo arrives at the facility in an attempt to reclam the Falcon and causes enough of a ruckus to be escorted out of the facility by a stormtrooper. At that moment, Greedo caused an explosion and T'onga lockdowns the facility and discovers where the Falcon is being held, and passes on the location, Impound Paddock XM139, to Tanna and Bynar. The pair go to the paddock and find the Millennium Falcon in a docking clamp. Bynar sets to remove the clamp, but they are attacked by stormtroopers. The Thisspiasian is able to remove the clamp, and Tanna calls in Akko to use her ship to attack the stormtroopers, allowing Tanna and Bynar to escape the facility with the Falcon. They make their way to a rendezvous point, and Tyra learns that the urn had been taken from the Millennium Falcon.[938]
    • Solo washes up on the shore on Escalan and is found by Doctor Aber, who heards him mutter his own name as she brings him to a spare bed. After healing, Solo goes to a local bar, but is kicked out due to not speaking the native language. He then sees a broadcast reporting on the impending executions of three prisoners in the Benelex Marshal Service prison on Gulhadar, including Chewbacca. He finds a local who speaks Basic and gets them to fly him to Gulhadar. Upon reaching the prison, they pick up Chewbacca, the criminal Phaedra, and the pirate Maz Kanata, who were in the midst of escaping.[939]
    • After dropping off Kanata at her castle on Takodana, Solo and Chewbacca fly to Tatooine along with Phaedra. At Chalmun's Cantina they find Greedo and interrogated him for the Falcon's location. Greedo confesses that the ship is in a docking bay in Mos Entha. Before split from Tanna's crew, Greedo had cheated in a game of Sabacc to gain ownership of the Falcon. He also admitts that the urn had been confiscated by Grand Moff Wilhuff Tarkin and stored within his office on Coruscant. Once punching Greedo and leaving him unconscious in the street, Solo reclaims the Falcon.[940]
    • Solo, Chewbacca, and Phaedra travel to Coruscant, and after Phaedra calls in a couple of favors, the trio acquires uniforms of waste disposal personnel, as the Empire does not do background checks on those who work in the sewers. Through sewer network they arrive to Tarkin's building, and Phaedra disguises herself as the RA-7 protocol droid 6D6-RA-8 and persuades Tarkin to leave the office to speak with the Emperor. Solo and Chewbacca arrive and kill the Grand Moff's guard of death troopers. Solo finds the urn, but soon stormtroopers arrive, and the trio jumps from the window and land on a passing train, as Phaedra had planned. A trooper shoots Chewbacca, and the Wookiee falls off of the train and into the city.[940]
    • As he falls Chewbacca grabbs hold of a speeder, turning it to the side and causing an Imperial officer to fall out of it, before the Wookiee gets into the speeder. Meanwhile, Solo and Phaedra are still aboard the train, though Solo dropps the urn on the train, which splits it open. Phaedra grabbs the bottom half whilst Solo collects the top, and she learns that what is inside the urn is not ashes as they been informed and asked him what it is. The three returned to the Falcon and at the suggestion of Solo, they visit former Sava Korin Pers on Nar Shaddaa, who informs them that they have the neural core of the droid insurgent Ajax Sigma, destroyed during the High Republic by the Jedi Order. They decide to bury the neural core.[941]
  • 7 BBY
    • The mechanic Salman Paak from Ferrix attends a Separatist meeting on the planet Jondora where he meets a woman who tells him that, if he is serious about politics, he can act as a liaison for Ferrix to, unknowingly, the Axis network via the use of a fractal radio unit, which he will be paid to keep alive. He eventually meets the network's leader, Luthen Rael, and introduces him to local mechanic Bix Caleen.[942]
    • Bodhi Rook enters the Terrabe Sector Service Academy to train to be a pilot.[642]
    • Jyn Erso goes on a solo mission to the planet Horuz, tasked by Saw Gerrera, with the goal of assassinating Imperial scientist Dorin Bell. Jyn suspects that Gerrera sent her to the mission as a way to celebrate her fifteenth birthday, and that the Partisan Staven helped convince Gerrera to trust her with a mission following her failure when Gerrera tested her with a detonator kit. En route to Horuz, Staven briefs Jyn on the terrain, the drop point, and the rendezvous point. Jyn arrives hours early to Bell's small transport unit. When he arrives, he is killed by land mines, an insurance that Gerrera had planned for. Jyn feels some shame and some relief that she has not been the one to kill Bell.[943]
    • The Imperial Military brutally suppresses an uprising on the Lasat homeworld of Lasan. Alexsandr Kallus of the ISB orchestrates an invasion that sees the near-extinction of the Lasat species. After Kallus defeats a member of the Lasan High Honor Guard, the guard gives him his J-19 bo-rifle in accordance with the Boosahn Keeraw, the Lasat warrior way. A number of Wookiee soldiers armed with weapons whittled from the wroshyr trees of their home planet also take part in the battle, sacrificing their own lives to try to prevent the massacres on Lasan. Thanks to the Wookiees' intervention, some Lasat are saved. The conflict causes the Imperial Senate to ban T-7 ion disruptor rifles, after Taris's senator Tynnra Pamlo sees evidence of the atrocity.[944]
    • Obi-Wan Kenobi rescues 12-year old Luke Skywalker and his friend Windy after they are left unconscious during a sandstorm.[945]
  • 6 BBY
    • The Partisans, using invitations forged by Jyn Erso, infiltrate the sakoola blossom festival on the planet Inusagi alongside Arane Oreida and her partisans. They massacre all the guests at the festival using flechette launchers.[943][21]
    • The warlord Malkhan of the planet Crucival goes into conflict against other factions that seek to control the planet on the Malkhani Insurrections. Eventually, Malkhan dies, and his lieutenants each stak a claim to his cache of offworld weapons. An unprecedented bloodletting ensues, and all the former warriors of Malkhan, who now lack an army to serve, fall into misery and deprivation.[3]
    • The Mandalorian Gar Saxon, a former member of Death Watch who fought under Maul in the Clone Wars, becomes governor of Mandalore as an Emperor's Hand and leader of the Imperial Super Commandos.(Approximate date)[21]
    • As a cadet in the Imperial Academy, Sabine Wren builds an Arc Pulse Generator which reacts with the beskar alloy inside the Mandalorian armor, incinerating the armor and disintegrating their wearers. As Wren witnesses the Empire subjugating Mandalore, she is wracked with guilt and escapes the Imperial Academy with the help of her friend, Ketsu Onyo, and deserts the Empire.(Approximate date)[21]
    • The Leonis family migrates to Capital City on the planet Lothal, where Leo and Tepha work as agricultural scientists for Imperial Ministry of Agriculture.[946]
    • Imperial forces on Lothal under the command of Lieutenant Piers Roddance massacre a gathering of peaceful demonstrators in the Westhills near Capital City.[946]
    • Dhara Leonis, daughter of Tepha and Leo, becomes a cadet of the Academy for Young Imperials. Inside she is identified as a Force-sensitive and kidnapped by the Grand Inquisitor, in order to be forcibly inducted into Project Harvester on the planet Arkanis and be trained as an Inquisitor.[947]
    • After witnessing the Westhills massacre, Lothalite Beck Ollet joins a three-member rebel cell, and they infiltrate a mining camp in Lothal's Westhills and plant detonators on Imperial mining droids.[946]
    • The Dizonites of Dizon Fray are massacred using Sabine Wren's weapon.[948]
    • An Imperial masking unit is stolen from the Imperial naval yard on Lozash,[949][950] one of many high-level thefts of Imperial technological items during the early rebellion against the Galactic Empire.[951] The masking device finds its way into the hands of Anto Kreegyr's rebel group.[949]
    • The Empire's mining activities on Jelucan lead to the planet becoming polluted.[649]
    • Jelucan people start using similar clothes and house decorations, reducing the division between the first and second-wavers.[649]
    • Chio Fain and his partner Rone undertake a job which involved slicing into property of the Empire. The two slicers were caught, and Rone lets TIE fighters catch up to her, which allows Chio to escape into hyperspace. Rone is killed, which makes Chio hold a hatred towards the Empire, and wanting to kill Darth Vader.[952]
    • Kuat Drive Yards designs the Raider-class corvette.[953]
    • Miramir is born on the planet Hyperkarn.[954]
    • Tiras Or'un is born.[955]
  • 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined)
    • Cassian Jeron Andor is embarked on a search for his long lost sister Kerri, whom he had not seen since she was six years old on their homeworld of Kenari. Having discovered a lead on a Kenari girl working at the Leisure Zone brothel on the planet Morlana One, Cassian travels to the brothel but discovers from a hostess that the woman in question has departed months earlier. He enters in a conflict with Preox-Morlana sentry guards Kravas Drezzer and Verlo Skiff, who both end up dead at his hands.[956]
    • Deputy Inspector Syril Karn begins to investigate the incident on Morlana One and is able to determine a male from Kenari as the perpetrator of the crime. After receiving a tip from a Ferrix resident identifying the suspect as Cassian Andor, Karn and three Pre-Mor squads led by Sergeant Linus Mosk arrive on Ferrix to detain him. Andor is however aided by rebel agent Luthen Rael and the pair are able to kill several Pre-Mor agents and escape, while other townsfolk destroy the East Team's Tac-Pod. Andor and Rael then rigged a speeder bike with a bomb and send it towards the remaining Pre-Mor agents while escaping in another, causing it to explode and kill at least two. In the chaos that ensued, they are able to reach Rael's ship and escape the planet, leaving the police agents scattered and the city in disarray.[957]
    • The Galactic Empire annexes the Morlani system and overall Morlana sector from Preox-Morlana following the disastrous results of the Pre-Mor Security's attempt to capture Cassian Andor. The Empire occupies Ferrix, and an Imperial garrison is established on the planet, headquartered in Hotel Rix. The occupation is overseen by ISB Supervisor Blevin, who has Chief Inspector Hyne, Deputy Inspector Syril Karn and Sergeant Linus Mosk transferred away from Ferrix.[951]
    • As Supervisor Blevin departs Coruscant to oversee the takeover of the Preox-Morlana Corporate Sector, he misses a conference on the ISB headquarters office, resulting in a speech he was meant to give being agreed to be moved up.[958]
    • A rebel cell comprised of leader Vel Sartha, Taramyn Barcona, Arvel Skeen, Karis Nemik, Cinta Kaz, and Imperial double agent Lieutenant Gorn plan a mission to the planet Aldhani, with the goal of stealing a quarter's worth of the Imperial payroll for the entire sector. Cassian Andor, operating under the name "Clem," is forcibly installed by Luthen Rael on the job. On the day of the phenomenon known as the Eye of Aldhani, Barcona leads Skeen, Nemik, and Andor to the Aldhani dam, the main Imperial base, disguised as Imperial Army troopers. Meanwhile, Sartha and Kaz travel through the water, climbing up the dam before disabling communications. Gorn allows the squad of four men to pass by and take the Imperial commandant, Jayhold Beehaz, and his family, hostage. Rendezvousing with the other two members of their group, Gorn watches their backs as Sartha, Barcona, Nemik, Skeen, and Andor go down to the vault to locate the credits. As Kaz watches the hostages, the others force the Imperial soldiers there to load the credits onto a Max-7 Rono freighter. However, by the time Gorn arrives in the vault to escape as well, a group of Imperials has discovered them. After a shootout that leaves Gorn and Barcona dead, Andor is able to launch the ship, although Nemik is injured during takeoff. After escaping from three TIE fighters in the chaos of the Eye of Aldhani, a brilliant meteor shower, Sartha, Skeen, and Andor visit a doctor to help Nemik while Kaz leaves the Imperial base disguised as an Imperial officer. Nemik can not be saved, and when Skeen suggests that he and Andor take the payload and abandon Sartha, Andor shoots and kills him. He tells Sartha of this before taking his cut and departing.[951]
    • The Empire ramps up its security measures in response to the events on Aldhani. The Public Order Resentencing Directive results in tribute tax of 400 million credits levied on any system harbouring partisan activity, re-evaluation or doubling of all sentences for crimes directly or indirectly affecting the Empire, suspension of fine and levy postponements, changes in the Imperial prison system including removal of oversight measures, and classification of all anti-Imperial offenses as a Class One Offense. The Imperial Emergency Act results in increase of Imperial Security Bureau authority with officers allowed to request personnel and material from the Imperial Army and Navy and increased focus of Supervisor responsibilities, imediate revocation of local festivals and customs suspected of harbouring rebel activity, and increase of Imperial Army and Navy authority.[959]
    • Andor travels to Niamos, a tropical world that had once been a popular site for fugitives who needed to lie low but since had fallen under Imperial occupation. There, he goes under the alias Keef Girgo and stays in a hotel with the woman Windi. After a run-in with a shoretrooper, who accuses him of being involved in anti-Imperial activity, Andor is arrested and brought to the Niamos Courthouse. Waiting in a long line of citizens waiting to be heard by a judge and prosecutor. Under the Empire's recently enacted Public Order Resentencing Directive, Andor is sentenced to six years in prison on Narkina 5 and given no chance to protest.[959]
    • The Imperial Security Bureau initiates a hunt for Cassian Andor for his role in the ambush on Ferrix and his links to the rebel operative Axis, unaware that Andor is already incarcerated under a false name on the moon Narkina 5. The operation is headed by Supervisor Dedra Meero, who questions former Preox-Morlana Deputy Inspector Syril Karn to find out any information about Andor. She sets up an active base in the Ferrix main city.[960]
    • Luthen Rael evades capture above Segra Milo after his ship engages Imperial Captain Elk and his Cantwell-class Arrestor cruiser.[961]
    • A prisoner uprising occurs in the Imperial Prison Complex on Narkina 5. Cassian Jeron Andor and Ruescott Melshi are among the prisoners that escape.[962]
    • Former Confederacy of Independent Systems member and Neo-Separatist rebel leader Anto Kreegyr, with thirty or fifty insurgents from his rebel cell, launch a raid against an Imperial power station on the planet Spellhaus. Luthen Rael attempts to recruit the Partisans into the effort and asks them to provide air support, but Saw Gerrera refuses to put his soldiers at risk for another group. By the time Gerrera has changed his mind, the Imperial Security Bureau has learned of the attack, forcing Rael and Gerrera to agree to let Kreegyr and his men fall into the trap. They reason such a sacrifice will give the rest of the rebellion an opening and protect Rael's ISB spy, Lonni Jung. The raid ends in a complete destruction of Kreegyr's forces. To the Empire, the victory is a way to recover from the rebel attack on Aldhani, much to the disapproval of Dedra Meero, who believes that capturing prisoners who might've had valuable information was ignored.[963]
    • In order to hide her illicit rebel fundings from the Empire, Mon Mothma begrudgingly lets her daughter, Leida, meet Stekan Sculdun, the son of oligarch Davo Sculdun.[963]
    • A spontaneous riot and citizen uprising against the Empire takes place on Rix Road in Ferrix City during former Daughters of Ferrix president Maarva Carassi Andor's funeral procession. After the planet came under Imperial occupation, Ferrixians including Maarva Andor become increasingly weary and restless against their reign. Inspired by the recent attack on Aldhani, Andor decides to take action against the Empire, using her farewell speech to condemn the Empire and to urge her people towards action against them. As contempt and anger towards the Empire had been building for weeks, her speech is the spark that ignited a full on riot against the Imperial forces led by Dedra Meero and Prefect Vanis Tigo. When the latter tries to interrupt and cut off her recorded anti-Imperial speech, the denizens of Ferrix attack the Imperial Army trooper units guarding the Hotel Rix headquarters attempting to storm the hotel and lynch Tigo. During the uprising, the son of a recently executed Ferrix denizen threw a homemade bomb against the troopers, killing and injuring many. In response, Tigo orders the stormtrooper forces stationed in the facility to open fire against the protestors, resulting in heavy casualties.[963]
    • Saw Gerrera, along with Partisans Jyn Erso, Reece Tallent and Codo go on a mission to the planet Tamsye Prime to scout out an old Clone Wars munitions factory that the Empire is using in hopes of a future, larger attack. Establishing a cover as tertiary units on a propaganda documentary filming on the site, Erso forges the scandocs for their cover. The crew enters the factory to capture information on film and send it to other rebel groups, but are betrayed by Tallent, who suspects Jyn's true identity as daughter of Imperial scientist Galen Erso. The remaining Partisans fight their way out the trap and the Empire's planned destruction of the factory, but with the Empire behind them, Gerrera gives Jyn a small blaster and orders her to hide in an old shell turret until daylight. Believing that he will come back for her, Jyn watches from the bunker as Gerrera deals with the traitorous Tallent, and then leaves her alone.[943]
    • Assisted by street orphan Ezra Bridger, Bossk is hailed as an Imperial hero on Lothal.[964]
    • The Spectres rebel cell raid Imperial shuttles on the occupied worlds of Ryloth, Christophsis, Kashyyyk, Toydaria, and Lothal, downing TIE fighters, Imperial Troop Transports, and All Terrain Defense Pods defending the supply ships. Hera Syndulla pilots the Spectres' ship, the Ghost; Kanan Jarrus fires its laser cannon turrets; Sabine Wren drops her ion bombs; and Garazeb Orrelios transfers cargo from the shuttles to the Ghost, defended by its detachable fighter, the Phantom. The raids conclude with the Spectres' stay on Lothal, having struck a significant blow against the Empire and strengthening the growing rebel movement.[965]
    • The rebel crew of the Ghost undertakes an operation to attack and destroy over two dozen Imperial interrogator droids near an Imperial center on Lothal. The rebels, including Sabine Wren and Garazeb Orrelios, attack an Imperial convoy and successfully destroy the shipment using a paint bomb. After the attack, the Empire releases a propaganda report via the Imperial Holovision claiming that a large group of insurgents destroyed Timely Tutor education droids bound for Lothal's neediest schools.[966]
    • Kanan Jarrus comes up with a plan to attack what he and the Spectres believe is an Imperial cargo convoy, but it is a trap set by the Empire where at least four TIE/ln space superiority starfighters attack the Ghost. Outnumbered and sustaining damage, Jarrus and Hera Syndulla struggle to work together as a communication disruption prevents the two from coordinating their responses. Their astromech droid, C1-10P—or "Chopper"— uses Phantom, to destroy the remaining TIE fighters, allowing the Spectres to escape.[967]
    • Sabine Wren attacks an Imperial airfield on Lothal, as a diversion to allow the Ghost, to lift off nearby without detection from Imperial forces. Wren paints her crest, the starbird, onto a TIE fighter and places a paint bomb in the center of it. The resulting explosion destroys the TIE fighter and draws the attention of the Empire, allowing the Ghost to go undetected. As a result of her attack, the Empire names a person matching Wren's description as one of Lothal's most-wanted criminals.[968]
    • Ezra Bridger witnesses a battle between the Ghost and a TIE fighter piloted by Baron Valen Rudor, which is shot down and crashes to the ground. Bridger steals several components from the TIE fighter that he will later sell in the Black Market, including the pilot's flight helmet. Rudor opens fire on Bridger with the TIE fighter's laser cannons, but Bridger incapacitates Rudor with his energy slingshot.[969]
    • The Spectres, contracted by crime lord Cikatro Vizago, undertake a heist to steal blasters from the Empire at the Capital City of Lothal. They are interrupted by Ezra Bridger, who steals one of their sets of crates, leading to a chase throughout the city, with the rebels attempting to outrun the Empire, and Bridger attempting to outrun both. They make it out of the city, fighing off stormtroopers and TIE fighters as they flee. Bridger eventually gets away from the rebels, but is saved by the Ghost, which shoots down an approaching TIE fighter. Kanan Jarrus offers Bridger a chance to escape as more fighters approached, and he leaps onto the ship. The Ghost is able to escape into hyperspace before returning to Lothal, where it scrambles its signal so it can not be recognized. The rebels bring many of the crates, also containing food and other supplies, to a refugee settlement called Tarkintown, where the rebels provide it for free to the refugees. Jarrus and Hera Syndulla deliver the blasters to Vizago, who pays them in money and in information, including the potential whereabouts of Wookiees who had been enslaved by the Empire.[970]
    • The Spectres attempt to rescue the Wookiees aboard Transport 651, an Imperial Gozanti-class cruiser, however they walk into a trap set by Agent Alexsandr Kallus of the Imperial Security Bureau. They soon realize the Wookiees are not aboard the vessel, and are forced to flee from Kallus and his forces. During the escape Ezra Bridger is captured, and the rest of the crew has to return and rescue him. While a prisoner, Bridger learns that the Wookiees are being taken to the spice mines of the planet Kessel. The rebels soon arrive at the mines, where the Ghost fires upon the stormtroopers guarding the slaves, freeing the prisoners. They are confronted by Kallus and his troops, who open fire on the rebels and the Wookiees. As the Ghost engages with TIE fighters, Kanan Jarrus ignites his lightsaber and reveals himself as a Jedi. While Jarrus and other members of his crew secure most of the Wookiees, Bridger goes in pursuit of the Wookiee child Kitwarr, who is being chased by a stormtrooper. Jarrus saves them both from Agent Kallus, and the Ghost flees from Kessel with the Wookiees.[970]
    • As Cikatro Vizago is willing to pay for weapons, the rebel crew of the Ghost travels to the planet Garel with the objective of stealing T-7 ion disruptor rifle from the Empire, aboard a star commuter shuttle. Aboard the shuttle is Minister Maketh Tua, who is attempting to procure the weapons from their manufacturer, Amda Wabo, with the help of R2-D2 and C-3PO, but the droids are secretly working for Senator Bail Organa of Alderaan to destroy the weapons. The rebels learn the location of the weapons in the Garel City Spaceport, and steal them after a brief firefight over Garel. They escape back to Lothal, where they attempt to sell the weapons to Vizago, but are discovered by Agent Kallus. This leads to a brief battle against Imperial forces, in which the rebels destroy the disruptors so they can not fall into Imperial hands. The Spectres escape Agent Kallus' forces and return the droids to Senator Organa, who is able to learn about them from R2-D2.[971]
    • Ezra Bridger and Garazeb Orrelios are send on a supply run by Hera Syndulla, where they attempt to steal meiloorun fruits purchased by the Empire, but are discovered, leading to a chase throughout the settlement of Kothal. Orrelios hijackes a TIE fighter operated by Baron Valen Rudor, and the two escape aboard it away from the settlement. Originally planning to return to the Ghost, they discover Imperial Troop Transports carrying three prisoners: Morad Sumar, an old friend of Bridger's parents, as well as Sumar's wife and an Aqualish. The rebels mount a rescue, freeing the prisoners and afterwards hidding the TIE fighter on Lothal.[972]
    • The Spectres learn that Jedi Master Luminara Unduli survived the Great Jedi Purge and is being held in the Spire, a prison on the planet Stygeon Prime, and attempt to rescue her. However, Unduli in reality was executed by the Empire years earlier and the residual Force essence within her bones is used to lure surviving Jedi to their deaths. The Grand Inquisitor intends to trap the rebels in the Spire and kill Kanan Jarrus and Ezra Bridger. The rebels infiltrate the Spire and make their way through the facility, Jarrus and Bridger locate Unduli's cell, and find the Inquisitor waiting for them. Jarrus and the Inquisitor engage in a duel during which the Inquisitor attempts to turn Bridger to the dark side of the Force. When the boy refuses, the Inquisitor aimes to kill them both, but they are able to flee and, ultimately, escape the facility with the other rebels.[973]
    • An Imperial Gozanti-class cruiser engages the Ghost near an ion storm. A pair of TIE fighters deployed by the transport follow the rebel ship while it tries to escape the pursuers in the ion storm, where Sabine Wren is able to shoot them. With only the cruiser remaining, lightning hits the Ghost but Ezra Bridger manages to recover energy and Sabine eliminates the cruiser. The Ghost leaves the ion storm and Hera Syndulla jumps to hyperspace to meet with Kanan Jarrus and Garazeb Orrelios, who simultaneously raid an Imperial transmission centre aboard the Phantom.[974]
    • The crew of the Ghost embarks on a mission to locate and destroy a powerful kyber crystal held by the Empire. To locate it, they need an Imperial decoder from the office of ISB Agent Kallus at the Imperial Headquarters on Lothal. To gain access to the complex, Ezra Bridger is tasked with infiltrating the Imperial Academy below to steal the decoder, where he spends several weeks training with the Imperial Army. He finally locates the decoder and steals it with the help of fellow cadet Zare Leonis, who is looking for his sister, cadet Dhara Leonis, disappeared from the academy months before. Bridger provides the decoder to the rest of the rebels, allowing Kanan Jarrus and Hera Syndulla to go to the coordinates of the kyber crystal. The Spectres then pass the decoder to Leonis' girlfriend Merei Spanjaf, who needs it to access several classified ISB files on Dhara at the Imperial Transportation Ministry of Lothal. Bridger remains behind to save fellow cadet Jai Kell, who is in danger from the Grand Inquisitor after showing signs of Force sensitivity. Leonis and Kell commandeer an All Terrain Defense Pod and the three are able to make it out of the academy before the Inquisitor arrives. Bridger and Kell flee, but Leonis remains to find out more about his sister, under the pretense of having tried to stop the other two. Jarrus and Syndulla track down the Imperial convoy carrying the kyber crystal and destroy it.[947]
    • Imperial forces led by the Grand Inquisitor arrive at a village on the planet Vyndal searching for Kanan Jarrus, who had been taken in by the villagers after landing on Vyndal. The Imperials first visit the villager's protector Yeleb, who lies and claims that they had not seen Jarrus. Sensing the lie, the Inquisitor orders his stormtroopers to burn down the village, drawing out Jarrus and causing both the Jedi and Yeleb to attack. Yeleb receives a mortal wound during the battle, causing Jarrus to flee through the village, drawing the Imperials away from fighting villagers. Jarrus then boards one of the Imperials' Gozanti-class cruisers and sets it to leave the planet before exiting the vehicles. The Imperials chase the vehicle off world believing Jarrus is still aboard.[975]
    • Capture of Jovan.[976]
    • The crew of the Ghost attack an Imperial convoy on Lothal using intelligence from Ahsoka Tano, who secretly coordinates with cells such as the Ghost crew under the codename "Fulcrum". The information about the convoy's timing and route is accurate, but Tano's intelligence underestimates Imperial defenses. This leads to at least four TIE fighters chasing the Phantom, the Ghost attack shuttle, which is carrying Hera Syndulla, Sabine Wren, and Ezra Bridger. Syndulla is able to outfly and destroy the TIE fighters, allowing the Phantom to escape. Fulcrum's identity and the intelligence she provides become a point of contention for Wren, who wants to know why the rebels are risking their lives on operations like the convoy attack. Syndulla assures her that they are part of something bigger and that there is a plan for fighting the Empire.[977]
    • Mission to Fekunda Outpost.[978]
    • The Spectres steal Imperial research crates carrying biological weapons from the Kessel Bunker within the Kessel system.[979]
    • An armed rebellion is fought by the Liberators in the Atrivis sector, who manage to seize the Imperial Mantooine garrison. Shortly afterward, the Imperial Navy counterattacks, swiftly defeating the Liberators on Mantooine. The Atrivis Resistance Group, after due deliberation, soon joins the Alliance to Restore the Republic.[980]
    • Beaumont Kin is born.[981]
    • Mapo is born.[982]

Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY)[]

  • 4 BBY
    • Mission to Kaller.[808]
    • Rescue of Morfizo.[983]
    • The Spectres disrupt the Empire Day celebration in Lothal's Capital City by carrying out a bombing that destroys an experimental TIE/ad v1, prompting an Imperial crackdown on Lothal.[817][984]
    • TK-462 is killed in action on Lothal. (Approximate date)[985]
    • The Spectres rescue Tseebo, a Rodian defector from the Imperial Information Office, escaping Lothal while being pursued by agent Alexsandr Kallus of the ISB. They safely deliver him and his knowledge to Ahsoka Tano.[817]
    • Ezra Bridger passes his trial to overcome his fears and receives a kyber crystal. He uses it in the construction of his lightsaber, which is completed several weeks later.[986]
    • The Ghost crew accompany Lando Calrissian to his farm on Lothal, fighting their way past an Imperial blockade to get there, after aiding him in acquiring a puffer pig from the crime lord Azmorigan. There they are confronted by Azmorigan and repel him and his enforcers. Calrissian, with the puffer pig, can begin a mining operation.[987]
    • The Empire carry out an assault on the headquarters of the Gray Syndicate, located in the former Ake's Tavern in Lothal's Capital City. Multiple Gray Syndicate members are killed.[984]
    • ISB agent Kallus laids a trap in the Lothal City Capitol Building to the Lothal rebels, using the fake rebel Senator Gall Trayvis. The rebels manage to elude the trap thanks to warning from Zare Leonis.[988]
    • The Specters raid an Imperial installation and steal supplies near the town of Jalath, pursued by Commandant Cumberlayne Aresko and Taskmaster Myles Grint and their forces but managing to escape. Due to their failures, Aresko and Grint are executed by the Grand Inquisitor on Tarkin's orders.[989]
    • Wilhuff Tarkin launches a raid Lothal's Imperial Communications Center during a rebel mission to hack into the communications tower to send out a transmission of rebellion to the galaxy.[989]
      • Kanan Jarrus lets himself to be captured by Grand Moff Tarkin to allow the rest of the Specters to escape.[989]
      • Ezra sends out a message inciting rebellion which is heard on Lothal and in surrounding star systems.[989]
    • The Spectres stage a mission to rescue Kanan Jarrus. With the help of the Devaronian crime lord Cikatro Vizago, the rebels send Chopper to infiltrate an Arquitens-class command cruiser in order to find Jarrus' location. They discover that he is being transported to the Mustafar system aboard Wilhuff Tarkin's flagship Sovereign. Using a stolen Gozanti-class cruiser, the rebels manage to infiltrate the Sovereign and use detonators to unleash a massive electro-magnetic pulse that disables the Star Destroyer. Ezra Bridger then frees his master. While escaping the ship, Jarrus defeats the Grand Inquisitor in a duel which damages the Star Destroyer's reactor core, setting of a chain-reaction and destroying the Star Destroyer. The Spectres manage to escape the stricken vessel before it explodes but are pursued by several TIE fighters. Several rebel ships from Phoenix Cell arrive and rescue them. Following Jarrus' rescue, the rebels meet the former Jedi Ahsoka Tano and learn that they are part of a greater rebellion. For the rebels, the skirmish marks one of their earliest victories in the Galactic Civil War, as well as an escalation of rebellious activity in the Outer Rim Territories.[990][567]
    • A tribunal is held at the Arkanis Academy against Zare Leonis for crimes against the Empire while training as one of its cadets. He is charged with numerous offenses, plead guilty to them all, and sentenced to death. However, he escapes with help of The Spectres.[991]
    • Dhara Leonis is rescued from Imperial custody, in Area Null of the Arkanis Academy, by her brother Zare Leonis, aided by Beck Ollet, Merei Spanjaf and the Spectres.[991]
    • Hera Syndulla, Ahsoka Tano and Sabine Wren attempt to rescue Janard, an old contact of Sabine prisoner of the Empire in a freighter crashed in the oceans of the aquatic world of Quila. He is killed by stormtroopers during the escape.[992]
    • Commander Jun Sato leads a rebel mission to steal shield generators from an Imperial convoy. Three of his blockade runners and two pilots from Phoenix Squadron distract the convoy's TIE escorts and the three freighters while the Spectres attack the main ship to knock the cargo into space. Hera Syndulla pilots the Ghost to capture the cargo, but two Arquitens-class command cruisers arrive to fire on the rebels, and Sato orders a retreat after the blockade runner's shields are disabled. Syndulla captures the cargo and the rebels flee into hyperspace. When they rendezvouse later, they discover that although the cargo holds vital supplies, it does not have shield generators as their intelligence suggested.[993][59]
    • The Empire orders a lockdown of Lothal as a reaction to the ongoing insurgency and the unrest on the planet, following the success of the rebel operation in the Mustafar system and the destruction of the Sovereign. As Lothal is an industrial world vital to the Imperial conquest of the Outer Rim, the Emperor sends Darth Vader to put an end to rebel activities on the world. The Empire cracks down on Lothal, bringing numerous Star Destroyers over Capital City as an intimidation tactic, and the demands on the local authorities to produce results in finding the rebels and their sympathizers are intensified. Minister Maketh Tua, acting in place of the absent Governor Arihnda Pryce, is unable to produce the results that Darth Vader and Governor Tarkin expect. Worried that she will be punished, she contacts the rebels and requests assist in defecting from the Empire in exchange for a list of names of those on Lothal who sympathize with the rebellion. The crew of the Ghost agrees to rescue Tua, they return aboard a commandeered Star Commuter 2000 shuttle and intercept the minister. However, the Empire tracked her communications and knew she was planning to defect, and the shuttle she takes explodes in an act of Imperial sabotage, which Kallus blames the rebels for in order to turn public opinion against the rebellion.[993]
    • With Lothal on lockdown, and no ship leaving the surface of the planet without Imperial authorization, the rebels are trapped and forced to hide within Capital City. They infiltrate the Imperial Complex in order to steal a ship and escape the planet.
