They find a shuttle, as well as a number of military-grade shield generators to steal on behalf of the rebellion, but as they prepare to escape, are confronted by Darth Vader, who engages Kanan Jarrus and Ezra Bridger in a lightsaber duel. Vader quickly overpowers them but allows them to leave, as the shuttle has a tracking device on it intended to find the rest of the rebel fleet. The rebels contact Lando Calrissian for help getting off of Lothal, who in exchange for three of their shield generators, gives the rebels a number of masking transponders that can duplicate the shuttle's signal and scatter the Imperial forces. The Empire, meanwhile, rounds up the citizens of Tarkintown, a refugee settlement the rebels helped in the past, and burns the town to the ground as punishment for the rebellion's activities. Vader tracks them to the fleet using his TIE Advanced x1 and engages the rebel A-wing starfighters of Phoenix Squadron in combat, and mortally damages the rebel command ship, Phoenix Home. The Lothal cell along with Ahsoka Tano boarded the Ghost and during the fighting, Jarrus and Tano use the Force in an attempt to learn more about the Sith Lord's identity. What Tano senses overwhelms her, while Vader realizes that her former Padawan is still alive. The command ship is destroyed and the entire fleet retreats.[993]
    • Darth Vader dispatches the Fifth Brother and Seventh Sister to hunt down the Jedi Ahsoka Tano, Kanan Jarrus, and Ezra Bridger.[993]
    • The Spectres undertake a series of tactical missions on Lothal, Garel, and an infiltration of an Imperial I-class Star Destroyer.[994]
    • The Empire launches an assault on the planet Seelos, against the Ghost's crew and former clone troopers Rex, Gregor and Wolffe. The two groups, having recently met, defeat the Imperial forces and escape Seelos. Rex joins the Phoenix Cell.[859]
    • Kanan Jarrus and Garazeb Orrelios go on a mission to the Sienar Fleet Systems factory on Absanz to acquire intelligence regarding Sienar's bomb updates on their newest TIE prototypes.[995]
    • Members of the Lothal rebel cell fight against two Inquisitors aboard an abandoned Republic Haven-class medical station. They manage to escape and warn their fellow rebels that the Empire has more Inquisitors.[996]
    • Members of the Spectres, temporarily allied with the pirate Hondo Ohnaka, fight the crime lord Azmorigan and his forces in Nixus Hub 218 on the planet Nixus.[997]
    • Imperial forces blockade the planet Ibaar, cutting the Ibaarian people off from vital supplies. The rebellion, through the Phoenix Cell and the Spectres, launch operations to deliver supplies to an underground network of Ibaarian resistance forces.[998]
    • A prototype B-wing created by the Mon Calamari engineer Quarrie on the planet Shantipole is given to Phoenix Squadron after they use it in the blockade of Ibaar.[998]
    • Two former Imperial officers, Swain and Cogon, flee to the planet Thrad seeking to defect to the rebels. They are met by Beneda, a Thradian assistant of Senator Nadea Tural, who agrees to aid them in return for the intel they bring with them. Agent Alexsandr Kallus, who was conducting an investigation into Imperial personnel on Lothal, suspecting rebel sympathizers, indentifies them and has their shuttle tracked. He arrives aboard the Star Destroyer Relentless, and descends to the planet with and escort of stormtroopers. After a fight in a spaceport, he caughs up to the two fugitives, capturing both of them and retrieving the intel.[999]
    • Members of the Phoenix Cell, led by Sabine Wren, join forces with Black Sun bounty hunter Ketsu Onyo to retrieve the droid EG-86, carrying valuable intelligence for the rebellion, at the Garel City Spaceport. They deliver the droid to R2-D2, working on behalf of Senator Bail Organa, at the rebel rendezvous known as Havoc Outpost.[1000]
    • Kanan Jarrus and Rex rescue Commander Jun Sato, Ezra Bridger, and other rebels of the Phoenix Cell from a experimental Imperial Interdictor, who had been captured when the Interdictor used gravity well projectors to pull their ship out of hyperspace. The rebels destroy the experimental vessel to prevent it from disrupting the movements of the rebel fleet.[1001]
    • The Phoenix Cell fights Imperial forces in deep space. The rebels are victorious, but lose Phoenix Three to a TIE fighter.[1002]
    • The rebel cell known as the Maya Pei Brigade is ambushed, leading to devastating losses among the group, including 40 fighters, 6 transports, and their leader, Maya Pei. The survivors become stranded on the moon Yavin 4.[1003]
    • Cassian Andor infiltrates Sienar Test Facility 73 on the snowy Sienar Fleet Systems planet of Sienar TF73 and steals a TIE Avenger prototype.[1003]
    • Director of Advanced Weapons Research Orson Callan Krennic hosts a secret conference between various Imperial officers and employees at a clandestine Imperial facility on the planet Maltheen Divide. Attended by members of the Imperial Navy, Imperial Security Bureau, Ministry of Enlightenment, and Tarkin Initiative, the conference ses the planning and discussion of how to justify and execute the planned suppression of Ghorman and it's people to allow Imperial gouge mining of the planet for a rare form of the mineral Kalkite to coat reactor lenses in the Emperor's "Energy Initiative", which in reality is Project Celestial Power, a project part of Project Stardust that focuses on the development of the Mk I Superlaser. Following the conference, the already running anti-Ghorman whispering campaign continues, and the suppression of Ghorman begins under the command of ISB Supervisor Dedra Meero.[1003]
    • A a group of Maya Pei Brigade survivors has a series of disagreements on the moon Yavin 4. The dispute turns violent when Crab shoots and kills Rika, causing the entire group to begin firing upon one another. They eventually split into two sides, and send out individuals to scout in the jungle. Cassian Andor is caught in the midst of the turf war, which broke out after he was taken prisoner by the brigade, who believed him to be an Imperial pilot. After a long standoff, both sides emerge from hiding, lower their weapons, and agree to settle the conflict through a Roski rules variant of the game Five Hands. Their game is interrupted when a Yavinian doodar charges through the clearing and swallow members of the brigade. Through the commotion, Andor escapes capture and flees in his stolen TIE Avenger prototype.[1003]
    • The Empire initiates an Imperial Supply Census on Mina-Rau to audit agricultural output and to verify the visas of the workers on the agriworld. A communications blackout is enforced, isolating illegal refugees that included Ferrixians Bix Caleen, Brasso, Wilmon Paak, and the droid B2EMO, who had been hiding among undocumented laborers. In visit to their mobil-haus, Lieutenant Krole targets Caleen during inspection. After Krole attempts to sexually assault Bix in the dwelling, she defends herself, killing him with a tool. When Krole's companion Corporal Pyke tries to arrest her, Caleen kills him after Pyke and Paak exchanged blaster fire. As Brasso escapes the Imperial forces that had arrested him, Cassian Andor arrives in the stolen TIE Avenger prototype to extract the survivors. Andor kills several Imperial Army troopers and stormtroopers, but Brasso is fatally wounded during the escape.[1004]
    • Lieda Mothma and Stekan Sculdun have their arranged wedding on Chandrila. Mon Mothma attempts and fails to talk her daughter out of it. At the wedding, Rael and Mothma determine that Mothma's business partner Tay Kolma is a liability to the network, and Rael has him assassinated by agent Cinta Kaz.[1004]
  • 3 BBY
    • Ubese bounty hunter Brene is exiled from her people's homeworld of Uba IV, eventually joining a crew led by Boushh.[1005]
    • Leia Organa performs her Day of Demand, an Alderaanian ceremony held on the sixteenth anniversary of her original Name Day, where she demands her right to the throne of Alderaan.[106]
    • The Rebellion attacks the Calderos Space Station, an Imperial deep-space waypoint, to conceal rebel shipping to an outpost on the planet Crait.[106]
    • A bombing is carried out by Saw Gerrera's Partisans on Onoam, one of Naboo's moons, to assassinate Imperial Moff Quarsh Panaka. Panaka had just been visited by Queen Dalné of Naboo and Princess Leia Organa of Alderaan, the two leaving his chalet mere minutes before it was bombed. On her way into the chalet, Organa took note of a civilian worker wearing a breath mask, and she sees the worker fleeing the scene after the bombing. Panaka's death prevents him from relaying to Palpatine his suspicions that Leia, adopted daughter of the House of Organa, is the biological daughter of the long-deceased Senator Padmé Amidala of Naboo.[106]
    • By this point, Dalné became Queen of Naboo, though the position was made ceremonial by the Galactic Empire.[106]
    • The Empire arrests many officials of the government of the planet Christophsis after discovering that they had backed rebel activity, also cracking down on the city of Tophen and orbitally bombarding the capital city of Chaleydonia.[106]
    • A fleet of rebel ships evacuate the planet Paucris Major after learning from Leia Organa that the Empire intended to send a fleet to the Paucris system.[106]
    • Leia Organa travels to the planet Garel with a data-tape holding information on the whereabouts of bases held by the Empire. Slipping past her Imperial escorts, she is able to contact Sabine Wren and lead her to the tape's location.[1006]
    • Inquisitors attempt to kidnap Force-sensitive younglings at Takobo but are stopped by the Spectres and Ahsoka Tano.[1007]
    • Kanan Jarrus and Ezra Bridger duel the Seventh Sister and the Fifth Brother on the planet Bilzen.[1008]
    • A fight takes place on the planet Imvur between two Rebel cells and the Empire. The Imperials target the Rebel base on the planet in retaliation for a previous raid on an Imperial base, launching a ground assault which initially knocks out the base's defenses, but are stopped by three MLC-3 light tanks and the shuttle Phantom working in conjunction. The rebel base is safely evacuated and destroyed during the retreat.[1009]
    • The imprisoned revolutonaries Mira and Ephraim Bridger stage a breakout in an Imperial prison on Lothal. The Bridgers, who had been imprisoned years earlier for broadcasting anti-Imperial messages on Lothal, are inspired to organize the attempt after they hear their son, Ezra Bridger, transmit a message of rebellion across the galaxy. Dozens of prisoners escape, including former Lothal Governor Ryder Azadi, but the Bridgers themselves are killed.[905]
    • The armed forces of the Empire launch an attack against Phoenix's Squadron's hidden base on the planet Garel. The Empire learned of the rebellion's location in the Garel City Spaceport after Ezra Bridger was overheard talking about the rebels' location. Agent Kallus and Admiral Kassius Konstantine of the Star Destroyer Relentless lead the assault against the rebels, while the Inquisitors known as the Seventh Sister and the Fifth Brother seek out Bridger and Kanan Jarrus. At least one rebel transport is downed and destroyed during the rebellion's retreat from Garel, but, due to the piloting of Hera Syndulla of the Ghost, the Empire's tractor beam is disabled and the Phoenix Cell is able to escape from the planet and into hyperspace.[905]
    • The Spectres raid an Imperial depot on the planet Lothal in an attempt to hijack three Sphyrna-class Hammerhead corvettes. These corvettes were delivered to Lothal by Princess Leia Organa of Alderaan, under the guise of a mercy mission, for use by the rebellion. The rebels fight against an Imperial garrison under the command of Lieutenant Yogar Lyste, who believe Organa to be a loyal Imperial and unable to prevent the ships from being stolen. With the help of Leia and the former Lothalian Governor Ryder Azadi, the rebels are able to steal the three corvettes, which bolsters Phoenix Cell.[1010]
    • As rebels of Phoenix Cell try to pass through the Concord Dawn system as part of a new hyperspace route to the planet Lothal, they are engaged in a dogfight by the Protectors of Concord Dawn, led by Fenn Rau and allied to the Empire. Three pilots of Phoenix Squadron are killed and the rebels are forced to retreat.[1011]
    • Kanan Jarrus and Sabine Wren travel to the third moon of Concord Dawn, intending to secure an alliance with Fenn Rau. Sabine invokes the Mandalorian code to challenge Rau to single combat, eventually capturing him, and he agrees to let the rebels pass through the system.[1011]
    • Phoenix Squadron gains safe passage through the Concord Dawn system from the Mandalorian Protectors.[1011]
    • Rescue of a rebel prisoner.[1012]
    • Mission to a mining planet.[1012]
    • A group of Lasat who survived the destruction of Lasan rediscover Lira San, the ancient homeworld of the Lasat, helped by the crew of the Ghost including Garazeb Orrelios, former captain of the Lasan High Honor Guard.[1013]
    • The Spectres conduct a heist at a Mining Guild Asteroid Belt Gas Refinery on an unidentified planetoid to obtain fuel for the Ghost, and the Phoenix Cell. The Gas Refinery is destroyed by Spectres with the help of a purgill herd.[1014]
    • Rescue of Imperial cadets.[1015]
    • The Spectres, with the help of Ryloth freedom fighters led by Cham Syndulla, hijack an Imperial bomber carrier and destroy a light cruiser as a symbol to rally the people of Ryloth.[1016]
    • The Wookiee chief Wullffwarro and his son Kitwarr, who where liberated two years before from Kessel by the Spectres, travel to the heavily forested planet of Oliu in order for Kitwarr to discover if he is Force-sensitive. At some point, Wullffwarro and Kitwarr become separated and Wulffwarro reaches out to the Spectres for help locating him. During a hard rain storm, Kitwarr is eventually found by the crew. Soon after, the storm clears and they are quickly discovered by the Ghost and Wullffwarro's Auzituck anti-slaver gunship.[1017]
    • The crew of the Ghost board an Imperial construction module orbiting Geonosis, following intelligence reports from the rebellion suggesting the Empire is constructing something over the planet. Unknown to the rebels, this is the Death Star, which has already been moved. When the rebels board, they are ambushed by Agent Kallus and several stormtroopers. The rebels fight their way back to the Ghost, but Garazeb Orrelios remains locked in combat with Kallus. The Ghost depart while Orrelios heads for an escape pod, but he is followed by Kallus, who also boards the departing pod. The Ghost and Imperial forces engage in a firefight outside of the module, which damages the escape pod and sends it crashing onto Bahryn, an icy moon of Geonosis. Orrelios and Kallus are forced to work together in order to survive. In the process, they learn more about one another's pasts and motivations, and Kallus begins to recognize the cold realities of serving in the Empire.[1018]
    • Kanan Jarrus and Ezra Bridger are ambushed by two Inquisitors on Oosalon while scouting for a rebel base.[1019]
    • Commander Jun Sato of Phoenix Cell and the crew of the CR90 corvette Liberator rescue Ezra Bridger from the Syvik Delegation, a band of Dug pirates, from their base on the planet Galzez.[1020]
    • Kanan, Ezra, and Ahsoka Tano head to the Lothal Jedi Temple to seek guidance on from Master Yoda to defeat Darth Vader and his servants[1019]
    • The Spectres steal fuel from the Imperial-controlled Horizon Base for Phoenix Cell. Chopper is accidentally left behind at the depot during the raid.[1021]
    • Admiral Kassius Konstantine launches an attack on the Phoenix Cell fleet, while its waiting for fuel to be delivered by the Spectres before it can jump into hyperspace in route to the Yost system, where the rebels intend to set up a base. Meanwhile, Chopper infiltrates the Imperial Cargo transport 241, and with the help of an Imperial inventory droid named AP-5, takes control of the ship. They learn that the Yost system is under Imperial control, and the rebels will be jumping into a trap, but they are able to discover a new location for their base, the planet Atollon. After receiving the coordinates, the rebels jump away from the attack and arrive safely to Atollon.[1021]
    • Phoenix Squadron establishes Chopper Base on Atollon; Rex and the Spectres secure the base against krykna.[1022][1023]
    • Sabine Wren travels to the Black Sun base on the planet Smarab to rescue her old friend, Ojo. She infiltrates the facility and locates Ojo in his prison cell, and explains that she has changed her ways since their previous encounter, where she captured him as a bounty hunter after he betrayed the syndicate. She convinces him to accept her help and fight for the Rebellion. She signals the Phantom for pickup, and Chopper blows the entrance up using its laser cannons. Ojo and Wren exit the building and board the shuttle, escaping to hyperspace.[1024]
    • Sabine Wren and Garazeb Orrelios travel to Thune City to recover cargo containers full of weapons and ship parts for the rebellion, fighting for them with the gangster Tomith Garbis of the Garbis family and his forces.[1025]
    • Mission in the Kinrah system.[1026]
    • Mission to Malachor[56]
      • Kanan Jarrus, Ezra Bridger, Ahsoka Tano, and Chopper travel to Malachor. There, they forge a temporary alliance with the former Sith Maul to fight several Inquisitors and to obtain a Sith holocron containing knowledge on how to defeat the Sith.[56]
      • Ahsoka Tano and the Jedi are confronted by Darth Vader.[56][59]
      • Destruction of the Malachor Sith temple; Jarrus, Bridger and Chopper escape back from Vader and the exploding temple to Atollon;[56] Ahsoka Tano is saved from Vader by Bridger through[1027] the World Between Worlds,[50] afterwhich the former Jedi parts ways with Bridger and embarks on a spiritual journey that changes the course of her life as she returns to Malachor and ventures beneath its Sith temple[58][1028] to find Morai,[1029] who leads her[1030] down[1031] into another portal of the World Between Worlds.[1032]
    • ISB agent Dedra Meero, at the direction of ISB Major Lio Partagaz, begin their effort to occupy Ghorman for the Empire where they begin construction of an armory and attempt a propaganda campaign to muddy its image among the wider galaxy. Meero uses her lover and Bureau of Standards member Syril Karn to infiltrate and manipulate the Ghorman Front rebels. Cassian Andor is sent by Luthen Rael to investigate the state of the planet's local rebels who are secretly influenced by imperial spies and information.[1033]
    • Saw Gererra and his partisans steal rhydonium from a pipeline on a planet with the help of Axis network agent Wilmon Paak.[1034]
    • Unhappy with Andor's report on Ghorman, Rael sends Vel Sartha and Cinta Kaz to help to gather information and assist the Ghorman Front instead. They lead a heist to steal weapons from the Empire. Due to the incompetence and insubordination of one of the Ghorman Front grunts, Kaz is accidentally killed, though the heist is successful.[1035]
    • Chandrilan businessman Davo Sculdun holds a party at his office on Coruscant for Senate Investiture week. Senator Bail Organa is leaving as senator Mon Mothma and her spouse, Perrin Fertha arrive at the party. Major Lio Partagaz of the Imperial Security Bureau is invited but passes his invitation to two of his subordinates, Lonni Jung and Avril Heert. During the party, Sculdun invites a select group of party guests to tour his art collection. During the tour, Orson Krennic and senator Mothma trade barbs over the Battle of Carmeen. At the same time as the tour, Kleya Marki is able to remove a listening device she had previously planted in the Tinian Codex, an artifact in Sculdun's collection.[1035]
    • Imperial doctor Gorst has his interrogation methods extended into a full program by Imperial Intelligence, convincing the Emperor he is too valuable to be left as a one-man Imperial Security Bureau asset. The Imperial Navy donates a facility on Coruscant to the ISB for the training, and Major Lio Partagaz assigns the work on the program to ISB Supervisors Avril Heert and Lonni Jung, who is, unknown to all, an undercover agent for the Axis network. The network dispatches Bix Caleen and Cassian Andor, who are living undercover on Corsucant, to assassinate Gorst. He enters the interrogation room and is surprised by Bix, who strapps him to the same torture device she was strapped on two years prior. Not long after, Cassian destroys his office with explosives.[1035]
  • 2 BBY
    • Brene joins Boushh's crew.[1005]
    • A coalition of rebel groups on the planets Batonn, Denash, and Sammun of the Batonn sector begin an insurgency against the rule of the Empire. They are led by Nightswan, a smuggler and insurgent leader working to overthrow the Empire.[885]
      • The Denash insurgents fight Admiral Kinshara's One Twenty-Fifth task force of the Empire. The 125th encounters little opposition since the Denash insurgents have evacuated most of their ships and materiel to the planet Batonn, as the Imperials learn after preliminary interrogation of captured insurgents. Kinshara reports his findings to Fleet Admiral Donassius and the other Imperial admirals, including Thrawn and Durril.[885]
      • The Imperial 96th Task Force, led by Commander Karyn Faro, acting under orders of Admiral Thrawn, fights the Sammun insurgents, who are defeated. Her forces destroy two enemy ships and capture four vessels and a considerable range of small arms ordnance.[885]
      • On the planet Batonn, local dissatisfaction with the rule of Governor Restos fuels the emergence of an insurgency led by Nightswan. The Batonn insurgents seize the Imperial garrison on Scrim Island.[885]
        • Fleet Admiral Jok Donassius dispatches Admiral Durril to take back the island. However, Donassius underestimates the tactics of the Batonn rebels and his One Oh Third Task Force sustains considerable damage. Admiral Thrawn and Commander Eli Vanto infiltrate Batonn and obtain intelligence on the Scrim Island insurgents' tactics and their leader, Nevil Cygni. Using this information, Thrawn's 96th Task Force neutralizes the rebels' defenses and forces their surrender.[885]
        • Following the battle of Scrim Island, the Batonn insurgents relocate to the Creekpath Mining and Refining complex near Paeragosto City. Admiral Thrawn's attempts to end the siege peacefully are rebuffed by Nightswan, who distrusts Governor Restos. Thrawn's management of the siege is also complicated by an Imperial Security Bureau operation by Agent Gudry to plant explosives inside the mine. The siege comes to an end when the Lothalian Governor Arihnda Pryce remotely blows up the mine's explosives and deflector shield generator to conceal Gudry's murder. The explosion destroys the complex and ends the Batonn sector insurgency, but kills many civilians.[885]
    • With Thrawn's help, Pryce rises to the rank of Governor of the Lothal sector.[885]
    • With Pryce's help, Thrawn is promoted by the Emperor to the rank of Grand Admiral and is given command of the Imperial Starfleet's Seventh Fleet.[885]
    • Eli Vanto and Thrawn discover that a steady stream of Imperial transports containing enslaved Wookiee continually traveled to an unknown location. Thrawn also discovers that extremely large amounts of Imperial resources have disappeared and been pooled into an unknown project.[885]
    • Thrawn sends Eli Vanto to Admiral Ar'alani to assist the Chiss in return for his services to the Empire.[885]
    • Governor Pryce solicits the services of Thrawn and his Seventh Fleet in dealing with the growing rebellion on Lothal.[1036]
    • Ezra Bridger, Sabine Wren and Garazeb Orrelios rescue the pirate Hondo Ohnaka from an Imperial prison on the planet Naraka.[1036]
    • A team of rebels from the Phoenix Cell, led by the newly appointed Lieutenant Commander Ezra Bridger, raids the Imperial Reklam Station. Despite losing the shuttle Phantom, they steal six BTL-A4 Y-wing assault starfighter/bombers and liberate several Ugnaught laborers. Under orders from Grand Admiral Thrawn, Governor Arihnda Pryce's Imperial forces allow the rebels to escape into hyperspace.[1036]
    • Maul kidnaps the crew of Ghost in order to secure their holocrons; after experiencing a vision revealing that his nemesis, Obi-Wan Kenobi, still lives, Maul parts ways.[1037]
    • A rebel GR-75 medium transport and flight of A-wings from Green Squadron are destroyed by Imperial forces led by Captain Vult Skerris, while en route to the planet Teralov.[1038]
    • The Imperial cadets Wedge Antilles and Derek Klivian escape from Skystrike Academy with the help of Sabine Wren and the rebels of Phoenix Cell. The escape begins after Antilles, Klivian, and Rake Gahree express sympathies towards the rebellion against the Empire, which is reported to Phoenix Squadron by Agent Kallus, secretly a rebel informant operating under the codename Fulcrum. Phoenix Squadron tasks Wren, a former Imperial cadet herself, with infiltrating Skystrike Academy and making contact with the would-be defectors. During the first escape attempt, Rake is killed and Wren and the cadets are captured. They make their way through the facility with the covert help of Agent Kallus, and are picked up by Kanan Jarrus and return to Chopper Base on Atollon, where Antilles and Klivian join Phoenix Squadron.[1038]
    • Hera Syndulla, Ezra Bridger, and Chopper infiltrate the Syndulla residence on the planet Ryloth, which had become the new Imperial headquarters in the Tann Province, to recover the Syndulla Kalikori. Meanwhile, the Free Ryloth Movement leader Cham Syndulla, Gobi Glie, Numa, and the other Spectres stage a diversionary attack in the Tulara Ravine. Although initially captured by Grand Admiral Thrawn, Hera gets Chopper to plant explosives throughout the building, and the subsequent explosions allow the two rebel factions to escape with the Kalikori.[1039]
    • Captain Rex and the Spectres travel to the planet Agamar to obtain proton bombs from a Separatist supply ship, where they are captured by a holdout of the Confederacy of Independent Systems by still-functioning battle droids led by the super tactical droid Kalani. The droid general takes Garazeb Orrelios hostage, and Rex, Ezra Bridger and Kanan Jarrus manage to overpower the droids and confront Kalani, but Imperial forces arrive and attack the Separatist supply ship, so rebels and droids join forces to defeat the Empire.[1040]
    • After losing contact with the Mandalorian Protectors, Fenn Rau, Sabine Wren, and Ezra Bridger travel to the third moon of Concord Dawn, finding the Protectors massacred by Imperial Mandalorians under Viceroy Gar Saxon.[1041]
    • After learning that the Empire was about to launch a crackdown on dissidents on the planet Mykapo, the Phoenix Cell and the Spectres evacuate rebel sympathizers and Iron Squadron of the Mykapo insurgents.[1042]
    • The Empire initiates a blockade over Synistahg.[1042]
    • The Spectres joined with the pirate Hondo Ohnaka and the crime lord Azmorigan loot an Imperial cargo ship drifting in an atmospheric storm on the planet Wynkahthu, recovering several cases of proton bombs for the rebellion.[1043]
    • Rebels Morad Sumar, Kanan Jarrus and Ezra Bridger infiltrate the Imperial Armory Complex on Lothal, obtaining the blueprints for a prototype TIE fighter, and escaping with the help of disillusioned ISB Agent Kallus.[1044]
    • Maul returns to the Spectres and attempts to take Ezra Bridger as his apprentice; after a skirmish on Dathomir proved Bridger's Jedi compassion, a disappointed Maul parts ways in order to find Obi-Wan Kenobi alone. From Maul's lair, Sabine Wren claims the Darksaber.[1045]
    • Hera Syndulla, Garazeb Orrelios and Rex rescue Ketsu Onyo from the Starlag XIX space station, a private intergalactic prison.[1046]
    • The Spectres rescue the argora, sacred bird to the Xendekians, while is being transferred to Palpatine's personal zoo in a cargo ship. After infiltrating the ship, the rebels return the bird to the Xendekians.[1047]
    • Saw Gerrera and a team of Partisans travel to the planet Geonosis investigating a major Imperial construction project previously posted in the Geonosis system.[32] They descend into an underground labyrinth inhabited by Klik-Klak, sole survivor of the Sterilization of Geonosis. Believing them a threat, he kills all partisans except Saw. Bail Organa and Jun Sato later dispatch the Spectres and Rex to Geonosis to find Saw and investigate the apparent disappearance of the Geonosian species. There they discover poison canisters used to exterminate the Geonosians.[5]
    • As Grand Admiral Thrawn deploys E-XD-series infiltrator droids to several Outer Rims, one of them, EXD-9, lands on Atollon, home of Phoenix Cell's Chopper Base. After the droid is damaged by several krykna spiders and knocked out, it's found by Garazeb Orrelios, who brings it to the base. To prevent the Empire from discovering the location of Chopper Base, the rebels send it back to its Star Destroyer with its proton warhead set to detonate when reconnected to the Imperial network. EXD-9 destroys the Star Destroyer, but the incident helps Trhawn narrow down his search for the rebel base to the 94 systems where the droids had been sent.[1048]
    • Sabine Wren undergoes training with the Darksaber in the fields of Lothal, under the tutelage of Kanan Jarrus.[1049]
    • Sabine Wren and the Spectres travel to the planet Krownest in the Mandalore sector with the objective of recruiting Mandalorian Clan Wren into the Rebellion. Together they battle the Imperial-allied Mandalorian Clan Saxon, led by Viceroy Gar Saxon and his Imperial Supercommandos. Sabine kills Saxon in single combat, and a Mandalorian Civil War, between revolting clans and Clan Saxon backed by the Empire, begins.[1050]
    • Members of Phoenix Cell launch a mission to rescue Agent Alexsandr Kallus after learning that Grand Admiral Thrawn is close to uncovering him as the rebel informant Fulcrum. Ezra Bridger, AP-5 and Chopper steal a shuttle and allowed it to be captured by Lieutenant Yogar Lyste's Arquitens-class command cruiser. After Thrawn summoned Lyste and Kallus for an audience aboard the Chimaera, Kallus and the rebels work to prevent Thrawn from discovering Chopper Base. To avoid being discovered as Fulcrum, Kallus frames Lyste. After the rebels escape, Thrawn and Colonel Wullf Yularen deduce Kallus is Fulcrum, but keep it secret to manipulate Kallus into helping them find the rebel base.[1051]
    • Ghorman Massacre occurs when Imperial troops respond to peaceful protestors on the planet Ghorman with violence.[1052] They successfully complete their takeover of the planet and deploy mining equipment to mine kalkite for the Death Star. After failing to assassinate the ISB's Dedra Meero during the event, Cassian Andor retrieves a damaged Imperial security droid and escapes.[1053]
    • Ghorman senator Dasi Oran is arrested in the Senate chamber on Coruscant for challenging the Imperial narrative on Ghorman, spurring senators Mon Mothma and Bail Organa to action.[1054]
    • Senator Mon Mothma delivers a speech in the Senate Chamber, denouncing the Empire's actions at the Ghorman Massacre as a genocide and declaring Emperor Palpatine a monster. After the ISB immediately puts out a warrant for her arrest, she flees Coruscant and the Imperial Senate with the help of Cassian Andor and the Axis network.[1054] Mothma is branded a traitor to the Empire.[1055][1056][32]
    • Phoenix Squadron dispatches Hera Syndulla and her crew to protect Mothma, who destroy a tactical infiltration pod sent to find her. They meet up with Mothma's shuttle, the Chandrila Mistress, and her escorts from Gold Squadron, part of the Massassi Group of rebels. After a brief skirmish with Imperial warships, the rebel teams travel through the Archeon Nebula using the Archeon pass, a dangerous hyperspace route they hope will dissuade the Empire from pursuing them. They are tracked, however, by a prototype TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter and its TIE/IN interceptor escorts. The rebels manage to elude their TIE pursuers and two Star Destroyers, commanded by Governor Arihnda Pryce and Admiral Kassius Konstantine, by firing proton torpedoes into the nebula's gas clouds to damage the Imperial ships. Mon Mothma is safely delivered to Dantooine.[1057]
    • Declaration of the Rebel Alliance;[567] Mon Mothma forms the Alliance to Restore the Republic above Dantooine, joining together scattered rebel cells[1057] to challenge the growing tyranny of the Galactic Empire[50] in one of the most important moments of the Galactic Civil War;[58] the war officially begins.[1023]
    • Imperial security droid K-2SO is reprogrammed by the Rebellion and speaks with Cassian Andor for the first time.[1054]
    • The Phoenix Cell dispatches the Spectres to recover Imperial codes from the Imperial Security Bureau station on Killun 71 in preparation for the Alliance's attack on Lothal.[1058]
    • Ezra Bridger and Chopper travel to the planet Tatooine to stop Maul from locating Obi-Wan Kenobi. Unknown to Ezra, Maul had lured him here with the intent of using him to draw Kenobi out of hiding. After being attacked by Tusken Raiders, Ezra and Chopper get lost in the desert, and are rescued by Kenobi. Maul attacks them but falls to Obi-Wan Kenobi's blade and dies.[1059]
    • A battle breaks out between the nascent Alliance to Restore the Republic with the Empire and the Force-sensitive being known as Bendu on and above the planet Atollon. It starts when Grand Admiral Thrawn discovers the location of Chopper Base, the headquarters of the Phoenix Cell. Thrawn's 7th Fleet blockades Atollon, trapping both Phoenix Squadron and General Jan Dodonna's Massassi Group. Thrawn's fleet inflicts heavy casualties on the two rebel forces. However, Commander Jun Sato's decision to ram Admiral Kassius Konstantine's Interdictor vessel allows Ezra Bridger and Chopper to escape and seek help. As his fleet holds the blockade, Thrawn launches a ground assault following an orbital bombardment of the base, while Governor Arihnda Pryce assumes command in space. Meanwhile, on Mandalore, Ezra succeeds in getting the help of Sabine Wren, Fenn Rau, and several members of Clan Wren in destroying Thrawn's second Interdictor vessel. Despite Thrawn attacking Chopper Base, Hera Syndulla and General Dodonna manage to escape with the surviving rebel fleet into space. The rebels and Imperials are both attacked by an angry Bendu, an enigmatic Force wielder, who uses his Force powers to destroy Chopper Base before being subdued by Thrawn. The surviving rebel forces flee to Yavin 4. A number of members of the Rebel Alliance, including Mon Mothma herself, see the defeat at Atollon as proof their organization is not yet ready for open war.[1060]
  • 2–1 BBY
    • Cassian Andor and K-2SO embark on a raid on an Imperial freighter where they free three Wookiees.[1061][1062]
    • The Sixty-First Mobile Infantry, nicknamed Twilight Company, fights a warrior group that had captured fellow rebel soldiers on Ferrok Pax, trekking through the ruins on the surface for several days in order to flank the enemy, eventually winning the ensuing battle and freeing the prisoners.[1063]
    • Twilight Company, led by Captain Micha Evon, confronts the Empire's forces on Allst Prime led by Governor Magé. During the battle, soldier Brand abandons her old life as a bounty hunter and is recruited into the 61st.[1063]
    • The 61st fights a series of battles at the planet Mygeeto,[1064] and an Alliance starfighter squadron earns the name Riot Squadron during the battles.[3]
  • 1 BBY
    • Alliance High Command sets up base on Yavin 4.[1023]
    • After sensing a disturbance in the Force on Batuu, on the edge of the Unknown Regions, Darth Sidious dispatches Darth Vader and Chiss Grand Admiral Thrawn to investigate the area.[779]
    • K-2S0 leads a Rebel Alliance group to infiltrate an Imperial facility on Mustafar for intelligence vital to the survival of the Rebellion.[1065]
    • The rebel mission to Mandalore occurs. Sabine Wren and Ezra Bridger rescue the former's father, Alrich Wren, from an Imperial prison. Aided by the Spectres, several Mandalorian clans unite and eliminate the forces of Imperial-allied Mandalorian Governor Tiber Saxon. Sabine Wren passes on the Darksaber to Bo-Katan Kryze, who leads as Mand'alor.[1066]
    • At some point afterward, the Empire would launch the Great Purge of Mandalore, destroying much of the planet and its people, with survivors scattering throughout the galaxy. The Imperial forces were led by Gideon,[1067] who eventually seizes the Darksaber.[1068]
    • Ezra Bridger, Sabine Wren and Chopper are send to to the planet Jalindi with the objective of hijacking the Empire's Jalindi Station in order to eavesdrop on Imperial transmissions sent through there. They sky-dive onto the station's dish from the Ghost to install a spike, but after the arrival of the Arquitens-class command cruiser Marauder, Saw Gerrera arrives and destroys both the dish and the Marauder.[1069]
    • Bridger and Wren, along with Chopper, wound up with Gerrera after the mission to Jalindi, as Gerrera invites them on board his U-wing. After Gerrera's U-wing and the Ghost are forced to separately flee into hyperspace due to the arrival of a Star Destroyer, and before Hera Syndulla can dock with Gerrera's ship to pick up her people, Gerrera asks them to assist with investigating a mysterious Imperial shipment passing through Faos Station, which Gerrera is convinced has a connection to the Empire's sterilization of Geonosis. Bridger, Wren, Gerrera, and Chopper sneak on board Freighter 2716 at the space station, and discover the supposed civilian freighter is actually an undercover Imperial vessel. They find a group of technicians, all specializing in power generators and reactors, imprisoned on board. A further search reveals the ship's special cargo: a giant kyber crystal. Gerrera, who wants to ride the freighter to its destination in the empty Tonnis sector, turns on Bridger and Wren, who want to get the prisoners to safety, and rigges the crystal up to the freighter's hyperdrive to create a bomb. Upon discovering that the freighter is only rendezvousing with a Star Destroyer, Gerrera damages the hyperdrive, beginning the process that will lead to the crystal's detonation, before releasing Bridger and Wren and departing in his U-wing. The rebels escape in a shuttle with the prisoners before the explosion, which destroys the freighter and the Star Destroyer. The freed technicians decide to join the Rebel Alliance.[1069]
    • Imperial forces led by Vader and Thrawn battle the Grysk Hegemony. Thrawn speculates that the Grysk would become a major threat to the Empire, and is concerned that the Chiss are on the verge of civil war as some Chiss had aligned themselves with the Grysk. Vader sees that Thrawn's true loyalty lies with Chiss, but despite their differences, Vader makes recommendations on Thrawn's TIE Defender program, which rivalled against Krennic's Death Star project that was endorsed by Tarkin, the Emperor, and Vader himself. Thrawn and Vader return to Coruscant, and Thrawn relays Vader's recommendations to Lothal.[779]
    • The Imperial Security Bureau conducts a raid on a suspected rebel cell on Lothal. During the raid, Old Jho attempts to help the cell escape, but is caught by the ISB and later executed. After Jho's death, the Empire gives his cantina, Old Jho's Pit Stop, to famous pilot Baron Valen Rudor.[1070]
    • The Spectres conduct a mission to Lothal to obtain intelligence about the Empire's prototype TIE Defender Elite and, if possible, prepare to aid Ryder Azadi and the Lothal resistance group in liberating the planet. They smuggle themselves onto the planet on Cikatro Vizago's starship, the Broken Horn, and quickly meet up with Lothal resistance member Jai Kell, who help them escape the town of Jhothal. The next day, Ryder takes Ezra Bridger, Sabine Wren and Garazeb Orrelios to stake out the remote airfield where the Defender Elite is supposed to have a test flight. After the prototype arrives, Sabine and Ezra decide to steal its flight data recorder, sneaking onto the base as Ryder and Zeb keep watch, but with the arrival of Thrawn and Governor Arihnda Pryce, Sabine decides to steal the entire prototype. She and Ezra are pursued by TIE Interceptors, and Pryce activates the prototype's kill switch, causing two of its wings to detach. It crashes and Ezra and Sabine escape into the wilderness with the flight recorder, eventually bringing it to Rebel Command.[1070]
    • Ezra sees a Loth-wolf, the first sighting of one for over 100 years.[1070]
    • Ezra Bridger, confident that Hera intends to return for the Spectres and lead an attack, insists to the Lothal resistance group to get in touch with the Alliance. When the Rebels detect an ore crawler in the vicinity of their new base, they decide to hijack it to use its long-range communications equipment. Ezra, Sabine Wren, Kanan Jarrus, and Garazeb Orrelios board the crawler, confronting its Trandoshan captain, Seevor, who activates an alarm and halts the vehicle. As Kanan and Zeb investigate the crawler's reactor room, Sabine works on activating the vehicle's old long-range transmitter. Kanan and Zeb discover several slaves, as well as Cikatro Vizago, who had been arrested by the Empire after smuggling them onto Lothal. Sabine attempts to activate the transmitter but discovers that Seevor has shut down the crawler's reactors. Ezra fights Seevor in the reactor room, and the captain eventually dies by falling into the smelter. The Rebels are able to contact Hera and Rebel Command, and discover that the Alliance leaders have authorized an attack to prevent the manufacture of Defender Elites on Lothal.[1071]
    • Hera Syndulla is able to persuade the rebel leadership to authorize a fighter assault on Lothal, to attempt to destroy the Imperial Armory Complex, where the Empire is planning on producing the TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighters. She is promoted to General and given command of a force of 24 fighters and bombers, a mixed group of T-65B X-wing starfighters and BTL-A4 Y-wing assault starfighter/bombers, with the goal of hitting the Lothal City fuel depot. On the ground, the Spectres and members of the Lothal resistance group destroy the anti-air defenses protecting the factories. At the space battle, the rebels sustain casualties at the hands of Imperial ace Commander Vult Skerris, before Hera lures him into a trap and shoots him down, triggering a chain of events that leads to the destruction of a Star Destroyer, and giving Hera's forces the opening they need to head for their targets. However, Thrawn had been anticipating this, and has another wave of TIE fighters at the ready. The entire strike force is shot down, several of the fighters crashing into Lothal City. Among the few survivors are Hera, Chopper, and Mart Mattin. Hera rescues Mart from stormtroopers attempting to arrest him, but they are pursued by Rukh, who has been sent to capture Syndulla alive. She is captured as Mart and Chopper flee on her orders. They escape the city and reunite with Kanan Jarrus.[1072]
    • Grand Moff Tarkin informs Thrawn that his TIE Defender program has been halted in favor of Krennic's Project Stardust. Considering that the loss of his TIE Defender program would outweigh the loss of Hera Syndulla, Thrawn leaves Lothal to the incompetence of Governor Pryce to plead his case to Emperor Palpatine.[1073]
    • Thrawn agrees to handle a gralloc problem for Krennic's Project Stardust in return for the continuation of funding for the TIE Defender program. While handling the gralloc infestation, Thrawn and the Chimaera traces a missing freighter to find that it had been raided by the Grysks. While battling a Grysk warship at the scene, the Chimaera is joined by Chiss Ascendancy forces led by Admiral Ar'alani. Thrawn reunites with the Chiss as well as Eli Vanto.[1073]
    • From the remains of the battle, Thrawn finds Un'hee, a Chiss girl who was kidnapped by the Grysk for her Force-sensitivity and was forced to serve as a navigator. Eli Vanto finds that many of the missing Imperial transports carried foodstuff which is used to feed Wookiee prisoners working on Project Stardust. They deduce that Imperial Governor Haveland had purposely arranged for similar disappearances as part of Project Stardust. To be rid of Brierly Ronan, a troublesome Imperial officer, Thrawn sends him and Vanto investigate Haveland's Aloxor system.[1073]
    • Dayja Collerand, sent by the Security Bureau's Colonel Yularen, joins Vanto on Aloxor. Processing gathered intelligence, Thrawn deduces that Grand Admiral Balanhai Savit was responsible for stealing supplies from Project Stardust in the guise of grolloc infestations. When confronted, Savit explains that he did so because he believed that Stardust was a waste of resources, and that he hoped to funnel the stolen supplies to aid the Imperial Starfleet.[1073]
    • Grand Admirals Thrawn and Savit thus commit their forces in a non-lethal battle. Savit is defeated and captured by the Chimaera, Collerand, Ronan, Captain Boulag of the Firedrake, and Captain Gilad Pellaeon of the Harbinger. With the help of Thrawn's TIE Defenders, the Chiss defeat the Grysks.[1073]
    • The Spectres mount a rescue of Hera Syndulla from the Imperial Complex on Lothal. Despite rescuing Hera, Kanan Jarrus is killed when he sacrifices his life to protect his fellow rebels from being incinerated by the exploding Lothal City fuel depot. Ezra Bridger enters[1027] the World Between Worlds[50] and saves Ahsoka Tano from Darth Vader's blade at Malachor. With the former Jedi's guidance under the eyes of Morai, he then lets go of his master as Tano did hers, resisting the temptation to prevent Kanan Jarrus' death and thus Darth Sidious and the dark side of the Force. Parting ways with Tano, Bridger returns from the mystical realm and rejoins the Spectres.[1027] Governor Arihnda Pryce's decision to fire on the fuel depot is deemed a catastrophic mistake; the destruction of the sector's fuel supply, combined with damage to the factories, derails the TIE Defender project, leading to the project being suspended and funding being redirected to Project Stardust.[1074]
    • Ronan gives an unfavorable report of Thrawn to Governor Tarkin, and Project Stardust becomes the sole recipient of funding over the TIE Defender program until Stardust is completed. Before such point, Vader is assigned to protect Stardust. Having received news that the situation on Lothal is "critical," Thrawn returns to the world with the escort of Captain Pellaeon aboard the Harbinger. Before leaving for Lothal, Thrawn takes the gamble of convincing Ronan to travel with Ar'alani back to the Chiss Ascendancy despite Ar'alani's warning that growing chaos amongst the Chiss threatened civil war. Thrawn also recommends Commander Faro to take command the Eleventh Fleet.[1073]
    • With Thrawn's loyalty to the Empire in serious doubt, the Emperor orders him to return for a long talk after handling the rebellion on Lothal. Palpatine also tells Thrawn to reconstruct a part of the Lothal Jedi Temple within the Chimaera to tempt Ezra Bridger, and further instructs the Chiss to persuade Bridger to join him.[1073]
    • The Liberation of Lothal occurs. Ezra Bridger lets go of a Force illusion of his parents, defying Darth Sidious' ploy before sacrificing himself to free Lothal, the planet, and the people he cared about, from Imperial forces under the command of Thrawn. Together with Thrawn, Bridger is taken by a pod of purrgil away[1075][59] to Peridea, in another galaxy.[10]
    • Jacen Syndulla is born on Lothal to Hera Syndulla and Kanan Jarrus.[21]
    • Armitage Hux is born on Arkanis to Commandant Brendol Hux and a kitchen woman, making Hux the commandant's bastard son.[814]
    • Farnay's mother dies.[1076]
    • The Alliance sends a Special Forces team to infiltrate the planet Iakar to steal medical supplies from the Empire.[32]
    • Two months before the Battle of Yavin, the the Sixty-First Mobile Infantry fights Imperial forces on the planet Crucival. The Empire arms civilians to help them defend the Crucival transmitter tower. However, soon after the battle begins, they abandon the armed civilians and hide under their shield, using the civilians as fodder for the rebels. After decimating the civilian mercenary force, the 61st Company shredds through the tower's defense. After the battle and the death of his friend Pira Ten, one of the civilian mercenaries named Hazram Namir joins Twilight Company, recruited by the Besalisk Gadren.[1077]
    • Around this point,[1078] Bansu Ro, having lived on Coruscant as the seventh generation of his family since they became established as a family of pilots, sets off to train at the Imperial Academy[1079] on Carida.[1080]
    • Rebel spy Lonni Jung leaks information about the Death Star to Luthen Rael, who is later confronted by ISD Supervisor Dedra Meero during a raid on his shop. Luthen attempts to commit suicide, but is kept alive by Imperial medics and taken to Lina Soh Hospital. In the aftermath, various members of the ISB, including Dedra Meero, are arrested due to the leaked information about the Death Star.[1081]
    • Kleya Marki infiltrates Lina Soh Hospital and disconnects Luthen Rael from medical equipment keeping him alive to prevent any secrets from getting out before being evacuated to Yavin 4.[1081]
    • After discovering Marki to be behind Rael's death at Lina Soh Hospital, the ISB tracks her to a Axis network safehouse and raid it in attempt to capture her. Rebel agents Cassian Andor, Ruescott Melshi, and K-2SO defy Alliance orders to rescue her[1082] and bring her back to Yavin 4 to tell the Alliance about the existence of the Death Star. With the ISB having failed to prevent the Death Star information leak from being contained, ISB Major Lio Partagaz commits suicide.[1083]
    • Alliance General Davits Draven sends Cassian Andor to investigate claims of an Imperial superweapon, after first receiving intel from Kleya Marki. Andor is contacted by Tivik, his source within the Partisans, and they meet in the Ring of Kafrene, where Tivik informs Andor that Imperial defector Bodhi Rook has information regarding an upcoming weapons test.[894]
    • Jyn Erso is freed from the Imperial Detention Center & Labor Camp LEG-817 in Wobani by Rebel Extraction Team Bravo, led by Ruescott Melshi. The Alliance hopes to use Jyn Erso to gain access to Saw Gerrera, after Bodhi Rook is captured by the Partisans on Jedha.[894]
    • A battle is fought in Jedha City, when Saw Gerrera's Partisans attack an Imperial convoy transporting Kyber crystals in Tythoni Square. Cassian Andor and Jyn Erso, along with Guardians of the Whills Chirrut Îmwe and Baze Malbus, after getting involved in the battle are taken prisoner by the Partisans and brought to the Catacombs of Cadera.[894]
    • Jedha City is destroyed by the Death Star. Saw Gerrera is killed and his Partisans' campaign to end the imperial occupation of the planet ends.[894]
    • Operation Fracture occurs.[894]
    • Cassian Andor and Jyn Erso travel to the planet Eadu with the objective of capturing Galen Erso. Unbeknownst to Jyn, Andor had received orders from General Draven to assassinate Galen on the spot, fearing Galen knows too much about the situation. While Andor hesitates in shooting him, Draven sends the pilots of Blue Squadron, who destroy the Eadu Energy Conversion Laboratory and kill Galen.[894]
    • Battle of Scarif. Jyn Erso, Baze Malbus, Chirrut Îmwe, Captain Cassian Andor, and Bodhi Rook die while on a mission to steal the blueprints for a secret Imperial superweapon called the Death Star. Destruction of the Imperial Scarif Deployment, Surface Command, and Orbital Command; first major Alliance victory against the Empire. The Death Star plans are transferred to Princess Leia Organa's Tantive IV[894]
      • Calys dies during the Battle of Scarif.[1084]
    • Secret mission to Tatooine. The Tantive IV escapes with the vital information for Obi-Wan Kenobi on Tatooine at the earlier behest of Bail Organa,[894] yet with the CR90 corvette's malfunctioning hyperdrive, the ship's signature is leaked and allows for Darth Vader's personal[1085] upgraded[1086] Imperial I-class Star Destroyer[92] Devastator to trace the Alderaanian craft;[1085] Above Tatooine, the Tantive IV is captured along with all its crew, including Leia Organa. However, the plans escape with R2-D2 and C-3PO planetside, the former of which was tasked by Organa to seek Obi-Wan Kenobi.[1087]
      • Owen and Beru Lars are murdered by Imperial stormtroopers; the Lars Homestead and its droid inhabitants are destroyed.[1087]
      • Luke Skywalker leaves Tatooine and begins training to become a Jedi.[1087]
    • Leia Organa is taken to the Death Star, where she is interrogated by Darth Vader and Grand Moff Wilhuff Tarkin in the hopes of extracting the location of the Rebel base from her, but she refuses.[1087][1088]
    • One week before the Battle of Yavin, Grand Moff Tarkin sends his protégé, Ellian Zahra, to the planet Ikkrukk to assassinate the warlord and terrorist Burnium Ro, who has connections with the Nihil of old. She destroys a substantial group of terrorists and believes her mission successful, but later finds out that Ro himself had never been present, and that the man she believed to be her target was simply a decoy.[1088]
    • The Imperial Senate is dissolved by a decree of Emperor Palpatine.[1087]
    • Tarkin threatens to use the Death Star to destroy Alderaan if Organa does not deliver the rebel base. She provides a false location, and although Tarkin believes her, he nonetheless destroys Alderaan to demonstrate the station's power to the galaxy, and in doing so, the price of any type of dissidence. Alderaanians off-world at the time will call Alderaan's destruction The Disaster.[1087]
    • The Sixty-First of the Rebel Alliance fights forces of the Empire in the honey fields of the planet Vir Aphshire. The news of Alderaan's destruction are given without hesitation by the 61st's Captain, Micha Evon.[3]
    • Protests erupt in Alderaanian immigrant areas of Coruscant, as citizens become angered at rumors of the Empire's part in the destruction of Alderaan. The Empire kills many rioters and arrests many more immigrants.[1089]
    • The Millennium Falcon travels to Alderaan so Obi-Wan Kenobi and Luke Skywalker can deliver the stolen Death Star plans to the Alliance. The Falcon is captured by the Death Star while in the remains of Alderaan, leading the ship's crew to hide with their passengers. In order to escape, Solo and Skywalker pose as stormtroopers, allowing the group to arrive to a hiding place within the Death Star. After Kenobi leaves to shut down the station's tractor beam, R2-D2 discovers that Princess Leia is aboard the station, held within Detention Block AA-23. The group decides to rescue the princess and make their way to the detention center, retrieving Organa and fighting their way back to the Millennium Falcon, while Kenobi successfully disables the tractor beam. Kenobi encounters Darth Vader, and sacrifices himself to give Skywalker and the others a chance to escape. The Millennium Falcon is able to flee the station and is pursued by a group of TIE/ln space superiority starfighters, which the crew manages to destroy.[1087]

Battle of Yavin[]

  • The Battle of Yavin. The Death Star is destroyed by the Rebel Alliance.[1087][679]

Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY)[]

Renewed hope (0–2 ABY)[]

  • 0 ABY
    • During the battle on Vir Aphshire, the 61st gets news of the destruction of the Death Star, and it boosts morale. The 61st defeats the Imperial forces that day, but the stormtroopers destroy the honey hives to minimize looting from the rebels.[3]
    • A medal ceremony is held in the Great Temple on Yavin 4 the morning after the Battle of Yavin, to honor Luke Skywalker, who fired the shot that destroyed the Death Star, as well as Han Solo and Chewbacca, who helped Skywalker during the battle. Skywalker and Solo are presented with medals of Bravery by Princess Leia Organa. Following the presentation of medals, the ceremony honors those who died when the Death Star destroyed Alderaan.[1087]
    • At the conclusion of the ceremony, General Dodonna announces that the Rebellion will be evacuating its base and finding a new one.[1090]
    • The Imperial credit encounters some turbulence.[1091]
    • One day after the Battle of Yavin
      • Imperial Security Bureau's Alecia Beck ambushes the Shrikes; special reconnaissance personnel who identified, secured, and prepared new Rebel Alliance locations, on the planet Tanaab, killing five of them, and only Lieutenant Caluan Ematt is able to escape. He contacts the Alliance on Yavin 4 for help as he travels to the planet Cyrkon.[2]
      • Leia Organa sends Han Solo and Chewbacca to extract Ematt.[2]
      • Against Jan Dodonna's orders, Leia Organa and Evaan Verlaine leave Yavin 4 overnight to search the galaxy for surviving Alderaanians.[1090][1092]
    • Organa and Verlaine travel to the planet Naboo, where they rescue a group of Alderaanian musicians called Melodic Order, before heading for Sullust. Unknown to Organa and her followers, the Empire is tracking their movements. Under duress, the Alderaanian Imperial officer Tula solicits information on Organa's whereabouts from her unwitting sister Tace. As a result, Jora Astane's Alderaan Enclave on Sullust is attacked, but the Imperials are defeated with the help of Organa, R2-D2, and Verlaine, who unleashed a pack of rockrenders. The rebels then depart with the Alderaanians aboard the Lord Junn.[1093]
    • Organa and her followers discover that the Empire is using Tula to solicit information from Tace. When Tula's Commander Dreed threatens to execute her, Organa agrees to surrender herself in order to save Tace's sister. She also dispatches Uwa Pareece and Astane on a mission to win over the Alder-Espirions, a hybrid species of Alderaanians who had settled on the planet Espirion. However, the mission falls apart due to Astane's xenophobic remarks towards Chief Beon Beonel. Meanwhile, Organa and Verlaine engineer a successful rescue mission which frees Tace and leads to the death of Dreed.[1094]
    • Dreed's deputy Venk attacks the Alderaanian refugee fleet that has gathered above Sullust under Organa's leadership. In desperation, Organa appeals to Chief Beonel for help. Beonel committs the Espirion warship Espirion Multi, which destroys the attacking Imperial Star Destroyer. While Organa returns to the Rebel Alliance, Verlaine is elected "Princess" of the Alderaanian refugee diaspora fleet.[1095]
    • Han Solo and Chewbacca successfully conduct the Cyrkon Extraction, earning the trust of Caluan Ematt.[2]
    • Solo is given his seventeen thousand credits by the Rebellion as promised by the late Obi-Wan Kenobi, though he loses it during a mission done as a favor for Luke Skywalker. Solo is then continually redirected by rebels across the galaxy to save lives.[2]
    • A few years before Operation Yellow Moon[1096]
      • The Empire garrisons Basteel with a token presence in response to rumors of a resistance movement there.[1096]
    • Chewbacca accidentally crashes his A-wing on Andelm IV, a world taken over by the gangster Jaum, who forces his workers to mass-produce dedlanite to secretly supply the Empire's war machine for profit. The Wookiee Chewbacca is persuaded by a local girl named Zarro to free Andelm IV from slavery.[1091]
    • Recalling his experience of being enslaved by Trandoshan hunters alongside other Wookiees,[1097] Chewbacca frees the enslaved miners on Andelm IV.[1098]
    • While Chewbacca and Zarro are captured by Imperials, the latter are misled into thinking that Jaum is a member of the Rebellion and no longer desire to expand their reach to Andelm IV. After making their escape, Chewbacca and Zarro part ways on Andelm IV, though not before the Wookiee gives Zarro the medal he himself earned at the Battle of Yavin.[1099]
    • With his A-wing repaired, Chewbacca proceeds to Kashyyyk to return the bandolier of a fallen Wookiee before leaving with the Millennium Falcon.[1099]
    • Several weeks after the Battle of Yavin[915]
      • Luke Skywalker emerges from hyperspace in the Llanic system with the intent of finding a smuggler on Llanic to open a secret weapon supply line to the planet Rodia for the Alliance. He sees one of Moff Abran Balfour's Star Destroyers, too far to cause harm to his Desert Jewel, but it had launched several TIE starfighters who are firing on an unidentified Kupohan starship. A although the ship's deflector shields are raised, the ship is close to being destroyed. Since the Kupohans had helped the Rebel Alliance in the past, Luke engages the Jewel's sole laser cannon and shoots down a TIE fighter, the remaining ones take evasive maneuvers but resume attacking the Kupohan ship instead of Luke's Desert Jewel. Luke tries to hail the Kupohan ship, but before he can establish a signal, the Kupohan ship flees into hyperspace. Luke leaves as well before putting the Desert Jewel on any Imperial watchlists.[915]
      • The Desert Jewel arrives with Luke Skywalker and rebel pilot Nakari Kelen to Rodia. At the planet, the Chekkoo clan, which makes weapons and armaments, has a rivalry with the ruling Chattza clan, which is allied with the Empire, so Leia Organa thinks that the Chekkoo clan might be willing to help the Rebel Alliance in order to overthrow the Chattza clan. Skywalker is greeted by Laneet Chekkoo, who guides them to her clan secret base at the Toopil complex and introduces them to Taneetch Soonta, sales executive for armament manufacturer Utheel Outfitters. Upon seeing Luke's lightsaber, Soonta tells him about her uncle, Huulik, a Jedi Knight during the Clone Wars who was killed by his clone troopers during Order 66. They visit Huulik's tomb, and when the pair are ambushed by a wild ghest near the mausoleum, Soonta retrieves Huulik's old lightsaber from his sarcophagus and gives it to Skywalker. He uses it to kill the ghest and the two return to the Chekkoo Enclave. Skywalker focuses on analyzing Huulik's lightsaber and trying to learn telekinesis, but a planetwide alert for the Desert Jewel forces them to leave Rodia immediately and meet up with Ackbar and Organa on the Promise.[915]
      • Luke Skywalker and Nakari Kelen go on a mission to the moon Fex, in order to find the lost collection crew of the Kelen Biolabs and their ship, the Harvester, and bring back any interests on the moon to Pasher. They find one survivor aboard the Harvester and successfully return with the ship, bringing along dead Fexian skullborer samples. They are paid from Fayet Kelen to upgrade the Desert Jewel in preparation for their mission to Denon.[915]
      • Nakari Kelen and Luke Skywalker travel to the planet Denon to liberate the Givin Drusil Bephorin, a cryptologist working for the Empire against her will, after it captured her family. They meet with their contact Sakhet, a Kupohan spy working for the Alliance. They are able to liberate Bephorin from Imperial custody, escaping ISB agents and leaving the planet.[915]
      • mission to Pasher.[915]
      • Mission to Omereth.[915]
    • Han Solo and Chewbacca take a step back from the rebel cause and return to smuggling. However, when given an offer too good to refuse by Leia Organa, Solo agrees to rescue[31] Alliance intelligence chief Airen Cracken's rebel spies—a network of informants assembled in the span of years with only one objective who, after completing that objective one month ago, began to be murdered by a traitor.[1100]
    • For the mission, Solo is to rescue the spies and capture the traitor while under the guise of competing in the Dragon Void race with the Millennium Falcon. With all of the spies aboard the Falcon, Solo competes in the race, though it is attacked by Imperial forces. Nevertheless, the Falcon perseveres, and its rebel crew discover the mole amongst them and detain the brainwashed traitor. While he could have gone through the race's endpoint—a gate that created a wormhole leading to the race's starting point—Solo instead allows Loo Re Anno, the last of her kind, to return home using the gate, which was created by Anno's kind for their exodus from the wider galaxy.[31]
    • Returning Han Solo's favor, Anno leads her people from between the seams of time and space to attack the Imperials pursuing the Dragon Void racers, allowing the latter to escape. Anno's people intends their return to be the last; before they leave the world forever.[31]
    • Having accomplished his mission, Solo returns to the Rebellion and realizes that he could not lie to himself and deny reality any longer; that he had to decide who he really is and what is worth living for. Han Solo thus chooses to remain with the Rebellion, reconciling his friendship with Leia Organa and renewing the spark of romance between them.[31]
    • A wedding is held at the mansion of Imperial Moff Jaccun Pereez between his daughter Famma and a rising young star of the Empire, Commander Yendiv Bensek.[1101]
    • Newly formed Inferno Squad undertakes a mission to the Tellik Four Station in order to recover and extract Bokk Naarg, a defecting member of the Partisans who offered intel to the Empire. They are unsuccessfull as three other partisans carry out a suicide bombing that kills Naarg. However Inferno Squad member Del Meeko's ID10 seeker droid manages to recover Naarg's intel on a datachip.[1101]
    • Inferno Squad infiltrates the Dreamers, a faction of Saw Gerrera's Partisans.[1101]
    • The Dreamers undertake a mission to Affadar to ruin the reputation of Emoch Akagarti, ephor of the continent of Pammur on Affadar. He and his government had struck an alliance with the Empire to fund a mining operation on the planet, despite it having a detrimental effect on the quality of its water. He is brought to the Affadar water purification plant and recorded admitting the water was unsafe for consumption, with the plant not actually operating.[1101]
    • Seyn Marana and Sadori Vushan of the Dreamers bomb an Imperial munitions factory on Anukara. The mission is planned to coincide with the grand opening of the factory, attended by Moff Rys Deksha and General Ivel Toshan, with both dying at the attack.[1101]
    • Inferno Squad undertakes a mission to locate and neutralize individuals who were blackmailing an Imperial Moff.[1101]
    • The heroes of Yavin infiltrate the Imperial Weapons Factory Alpha on Cymoon 1, with Han Solo posing as an emissary for Jabba the Hutt in a weapons deal with the Empire, and Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa posing as his bodyguards. Once inside the factory, the Rebels reveal themselves, attack the Imperials, free a number of slaves, and set the facility's power core to overload. Their plans change when Darth Vader arrives and orders the facility into full alert. The Rebels hijack an AT-AT to use in their escape, while Skywalker confronts Vader in lightsaber combat before the walker arrives. Solo crashes the AT-AT through the factory's walls, giving them an opening to escape the facility. The Rebels engage in combat with Vader and the Imperial forces, and the Dark Lord kills a number of the freed slaves. Skywalker takes out some of the Imperial forces using the weapons on a speeder bike, while Solo uses the AT-AT to fire on and injure Vader. The assault ends with the destruction of Weapons Factory Alpha, causing a significant setback in the operations of the Imperial Military, and the Rebels are able to escape.[1102][1103]
    • An Imperial Lambda-class shuttle carrying weapons and equipment supplied by Jabba the Hutt is attacked by a robotic CR90 corvette belonged to a group of pirates funded by the Crymorah Syndicate. Darth Vader arrives to save the shuttle, but it self-destructs as the crew killed themselves rather than let the pirates take the Empire's property. Vader, along with two other TIE fighters, succeeds in disabling the robotic pirate ship, and takes it captive in order to find the larger organization behind the attack.[1104][1105]
    • With information recovered from the corvette, Vader and his forces identify and attack the pirate's base, destroying it and all its inhabitants. During the attack is discovered that the pirates are backed by the Crymorah Syndicate, and Vader is able to plant false evidence in the station's computer incriminating Lieutenant Oon-ai, who was serving as Grand General Cassio Tagge's spy to Vader, as a traitor.[1104]
    • Chelli Aphra steals the Triple-Zero protocol personality matrix stored in Quarantine World III on a mission from the Droid Gotra. Vader, who was tracking her after identifying her as the provider of droidekas owned by the crymorah-backed pirates, finds and recruits her.[1106]
    • Having recruited Chelli Aphra and the assassin droids BT-1 and 0-0-0 to assist him in building an army of unquestionably loyal battle droids, Vader and his new team go on a mission to infiltrate the lair of Karina, a Geonosian queen who had utilized a droid factory to produce battle droids as her "children" after she was injured and left unable to produce offspring during the sterilization of Geonosis. They capture the droid factory and began producing BX-series droid commandos for Vader.[1106]
    • Vader and his secret forces attack Doctor Cylo's research base in order to discover Vader's rivals; soldiers engineered by Cylo to replace Vader, by orders of Palplatine.[1107]
    • A few months after the Battle of Yavin[1108][21]
      • The Rebel Alliance launches a mission to rescue underground rebel leaders from the planet Giju, before Imperial agents arrive. Red Squadron, led by Commander Arhul Narra, escorts a transport away from the planet, and fights several TIE fighters, destroying some before making the jump to hyperspace.[1108]
      • Luke Skywalker embarks on a mission to recover Imperial communication logs, but he is ambushed in the Tertiary Usaita system, forcing him to postpone his mission and return to Devaron to repair his ship.[1108]
      • At Devaron, Luke Skywalker engages in his first duel with a lightsaber in the Temple of Eedit.[1108]
    • Hired by Vader to uncover Luke Skywalker's identity, Boba Fett travels to Tatooine and duels the aspiring Jedi there.[1109]
    • Vader attacks the Son-tuul Pride on the planet Son-tuul, storming its base with stormtroopers and executing its leadership. The imperals discover a fortune amassed over the years, which they confiscate. Afterwards, Vader met with Son-tuul Pride competitor Sutha, who served Jabba the Hutt. They agree upon a mutually beneficial relationship between the Empire and the Hutt Cartel, with the Empire eliminating competitors to open up new markets in return for the Hutts supplying the Empire.[1110]
    • The Imperial light cruiser transporting the fortune confiscated to the Son-tuul Pride is Son-Tuul Pride robbed by Aphra and bounty hunters Krrsantan, Bossk, IG-90, and Beebox. Aphra is send by Vader in order to use the money for his own forces.[1110]
    • Imperial forces led by inspector Thanoth attack the Plasma Devils, an Alliance Starfighter Corps squadron, in the Anthan system. Almost all rebels are killed.[1111]
    • Luke Skywalker, Han Solo, and Chewbacca travel to the planet Rion to negotiate supplies for the Rebellion from the Besalisk Razian. Solo wins the Rion Swoop race and use the winnings to give to Razian for the supplies and future commissions.[1112]
    • Vader travels to the mining planet of Shu-Torun in order to ensure that ore quotas for Imperial building projects would be fulfilled. During the visit the King of Shu-Torun try to have Vader killed, but dies himself at the hands of BT-1 and 0-0-0. Vader then crownes the king's youngest and only surviving child, Princess Trios as the new queen, leaving her to see that future quotas are met.[1113]
    • Leia Organa and Luke Skywalker go on diplomatic mission to the planet Tibrin. Posing as diplomats from Naboo's House Berenko seeking to negotiate a trade deal, they aim to rally the native Ishi Tib to the Rebellion's cause.[1114]
    • Leia Organa, Han Solo and Sana Starros escape naval forces of the Galactic Empire during a dogfight in the Monsua Nebula.[1115]
    • Luke Skywalker is rescued from the custody of Grakkus the Hutt at Hutta Town on Nar Shaddaa, and Imperial forces led by Sergeant Kreel seeking to capture him, by the Heores of Yavin and Sana Starros.[1115]
    • The Battle of Vrogas Vas is fought between Vader and his secret forces, Imperial forces led by Commander Karbin, and the Alliance to Restore the Republic. Using intelligence provided by Aphra, Vader travels and crash-lands on the planet Vrogas Vas in search of Luke Skywalker. After inflicting heavy casualties on the rebels, Vader is attacked by Karbin and his forces, who wanted Skywalker for himself in order to curry the Emperor's favor. With the help of Aphra, Vader manages to defeat and kill Karbin, meanwhile the rebels escape offworld.[1116][59]
  • c. 0–1 ABY
    • Rebel spy Eneb Ray leads a failed plot to assassinate Palpatine, after Ray infiltrates the Arrth-Eno Prison Complex on Coruscant to free a group of Rebel-sympathetic senators scheduled for execution, and learns the Emperor was planning on being at the prison. Ray and six other Rebel spies on Coruscant attack and kill a man who was in reality a body double; the plot was a trap by the Emperor to kill the Rebel spies on Coruscant and the senators, and achieve a propaganda victory against the Rebellion. All the spies and senators are killed except for Ray.[1117][1118]
    • Eneb Ray carries out a prison siege at the Alliance's Sunspot Prison in retaliation for the deaths of the pro-rebel senators and Alliance operatives during the Arrth-Eno Mission, seeking to kill all Imperial prisoners holded in the station. He and his droids are thwarted by Leia Organa, Sana Starros, and Chelli Aphra. However, numerous Imperial prisoners are killed and the Alliance abandons the prison.[1119][1118]
    • Queen Trios, the recently appointed monarch of the Kingdom of Shu-Torun, controlled by the Galactic Empire, faces an uprising among the ore-dukes of her homeworld, leading to a planetary rebellion known as the Shu-Torun War.[1120]
      • Imperial forces battle a delving citadel controlled by the rebellious ore-dukes. By orders of Vader, the cyborg Tulon Voidgazer destroys the citadel using explosive drones, much to the horror of Queen Trios.[1120]
      • Forces of the rebellious ore-dukes attack Queen Trios and her Imperial allies in the ancestral retreat of the Shu-Torun royalty. Vader boards the dukes' vessel, the Lava Leviathan, and sinks it.[1121]
      • In the final battle of the war, Queen Trios leads her loyalist forces in an attack on the delving citadel of Duke Rubix, leader of the ore-dukes rebelling against her. With Imperial reforces, the side of Trios quickly claims victory. After Rubix surrenders, Trios executes him and names his daughter Bixene as successor to his position.[1122]
    • Doctor Cylo and his fleet attempt to hijack the Executor, an Executor-class Star Dreadnought, while it is still in construction in the shipyards of Kuat Drive Yards.[1123]
    • On the Ghost Moon, Imperial Task Force 99 led by Sergeant Kreel shoots down a Rebel Taylander shuttle, crashing it on the moon. The surviving Rebels are attacked and killed except for Admiral Verette, who is captured for interrogation.[1124][1118]
    • Rebel Alliance members, including the Heroes of Yavin and Red Squadron, take control of the Imperial I-class Star Destroyer Harbinger, after attacking it and forcing it's crew to abandon ship.[1125][1118]
    • After learning that the people of Tureen VII was providing aid to the Alliance, the Empire launches a siege on the planet by setting up a blockade in orbit and establishing bases on the planet's twin moons. The Alliance attempts three times unsuccessfully to break through the Imperial blockade.[1126][1118]
    • Leia Organa escapes from the Empire on the planet Skorii-Lei with the help of the engineer Pash Davane.[1127][1118]
    • Luke Skywalker undertakes a mission to the stonepower-strong world Yoda once journeyed to following the writings of Obi-Wan Kenobi; the Jedi-aspirant confronts Garro, a selfish rockhawker left bitter, power-hungry, and hoarding stonepower following Yoda's visit. Skywalker convinces Garro to let go of his power and sacrifice for his planet, restoring the balance of life.[552][1118]
  • c. 1 ABY
    • Aphra raids the Great Temple on Yavin 4 at the behest of her father, Korin Aphra, in order to obtain coordinates which he believed would lead to the lost Ordu Aspectu at the Citadel of Rur.[42] However, when Aphra's crew arrives at the Massassi Site, they find it occupied and under investigation by Imperial forces under the command of Captain Magna Tolvan.[42]
    • The twilight of the Ordu Aspectu occurs; Chelli Aphra obtains the Rur Crystal, which contains the artificial intelligence of Rur.[43]
    • Aphra and Luke Skywalker embark on a mission to the Citadel of Ktath'atn in order to seek an audience with the Queen of Ktath'atn.[1128]
    • Aphra's auction of Rur occurs; the artificial intelligence of Rur, the self-proclaimed "Eternal Rur," escapes from captivity.[1129]
    • The Imperial skirmish aboard the Sorca Retreat occurs; many wealthy and influencial parties are slaughtered by Rur and Imperial forces. Darth Vader confronts Rur as Aphra escapes.[1130]
    • Leia Organa and Luke Skywalker escape from an ocean planet, after they sought to draw Imperial forces away from a peaceful species that were caught in the middle of the war.[1131][1118]
    • Han Solo and Chewbacca deliver Grakkus the Hutt to a Alliance holding facility on the planet Akiva.[1132][1118]
    • Task Force 99 goes on a mission to Horox III to destroy a rebel outpost, killing all rebels there.[1133][1118]
    • R2-D2 concludes his successful mission to rescue C-3PO from an Imperial Star Destroyer, after he was captured by SCAR Squadron during the siege on Tureen VII.[1134][1118]
    • Imperial stormtroopers on Zeitooine arrest a local family after the parents violate a ban on independent publications, forcibly dragging them out of their home. When the mother pleads for the stormtroopers not to imprison her daughters, the captain slamms his blaster rifle into her face while another trooper cuffes her.[649]
    • The Imperial Star Destroyer Devastator conducts an evacuation of the Imperial garrisons on Ivarujar, a planet undergoing a massive volcanic eruption. Lieutenant Commander Ciena Ree evacuates a stormtrooper garrison in the capital city and manages to evacuate forty civilians before the city is completely overrun by lava.[649]
    • The battle for Jedha occurs.[1135][1118]
  • 1 ABY
    • The Alliance considers Crait as its potential central base,[1023] and the mission to Crait[1136] occurs.[1137][1118]
