    • Assault on the Mako-Ta Space Docks occurs.[1137] Rogue Squadron is formed, and a large portion of the Alliance Fleet is lost when Darth Vader's[1138] Death Squadron[1139] attacks the Mako-Ta Space Docks. Alliance High Command suffers heavy casualties.[1138][1140]
  • 1–3 ABY
    • The Heroes of Yavin[1141] are stranded on the moon Hubin, and are taken in by Thane Markona and his clan;[1142] they eventually escape, leading to an attack on an Imperial Impound Yard.[1143][1144]
    • The rebels conduct a mission to Shu-Torun; seeking revenge, Leia Organa plans to bring the traitor, Queen Trios, to ruin. Beginning with Trios' Empire-allied world, Organa found her and her own forces' mistakes after dueling Trios, who died fighting against the vengeful Partisans' threat of destroying her world. Ultimately, Organa's team acts to save Shu-Torun, and the Heroes of Yavin emerge victorious.[1145][1144]
    • Han Solo and Leia Organa undertake a mission to the Core World Lanz Carpo to trick the Empire into suspecting the Carpo Crime Syndicate is working with the Rebellion.[1146]
    • Luke Skywalker and R2-D2 undertake a mission to protect a rebel refueling station on Sergia in the Inner Rim.[1146]
    • Chewbacca and C-3PO undertake a mission to K43 on the edge of Wild Space to divert the Imperial Starfleet.[1146]
    • Members of the Rebel Alliance gather on a new base on Hoth,[1147] the sixth planet in the Hoth system.[1148] The rebels' Echo Base is constructed over the course of two years up until the Battle of Hoth[15] in 3 ABY.[679] Months before the battle, Rogue Squadron begins to be stationed at Echo Base.[1149]
  • 0–3 ABY
    • Following an outbreak at a secret Imperial research facility on the southern continent of the planet Dandoran, where a bioweapon nicknamed the Sickness had been created, an undead stormtroopers invasion starts.[1150]
  • 2 ABY
    • Poe Dameron is born[55] to rebels Shara Bey and Kes Dameron.[1151]

Empire ascendant (3 ABY)[]

  • 3 ABY
    • Echo Base is established inside a mountain on Hoth by the Rebel Alliance.[1148][1152] The base is expanded[1151] after scouting and surveying work was done on several locations within Hoth's temperate zone.[15]
    • The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories. The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level.[3]
      • The Alliance's 61st Mobile Infantry, led by Captain Micha Evon, fights in the factory-deserts of the planet Phorsa Gedd.[3]
      • The Empire regains territory in the Mid Rim, pushing out the Alliance. The rebels decide to stop their offensive, and the Mid Rim Retreat begins. Some Mid Rim revolutionaries follow the Alliance, though many stay to continue their struggle locally.[3]
      • Imperial forces attack Alliance personnel on Ord Tiddell as they evacuate the planet. Rebel agent Caluan Ematt makes it out on the last transport. As they lift off, he sees the Empire light up the launchpad with plasmic artillery.[980]
      • The Sixty-First takes control of the Ducal Palace and the Chenodra spaceport on the planet Bamayar.[3]
      • Attack on Haidoral Prime; Imperial Governor and Ruling Council member Everi Chalis defects to the Rebel Alliance.[3]
      • The Sixty-First Mobile Infantry crosses paths with an Imperial reconnaissance squadron while traveling through the Kontahr sector. The rebels flee the battle before Imperial reinforcements can arrive.[3]
      • The 61st is send by the Rebel Alliance to protect the Coyerti and secure their world in an effort to distract Imperial forces from the rebel withdrawal.[3]
        • Two squads, led by First Sergeant Hazram Namir and Sergeant Zab, launch an attack on an Imperial scout post. They manage to overrun the defending Imperial forces and take the camp before retreating to avoid incoming TIE bombers.[3]
        • The 61st assaults a strategic Imperial fortification. Consisting of at least a dozen squads, the plan is devised by one of the squad leaders, Carver. Following a lengthy effort to take out an Imperial AT-ST, Namir and his squad watch the flashes of the battle erupt from afar. The attack subsequently ends in a victory for the Sixty-First.[3]
        • Twilight Company attacks Coyerti's Distillery, a biotoxin and chemical refining facility and hub of the Empire's production on the planet. Rebel soldier Brand plants a microdetonator on one of the chemical barrels. When it explodes, it causes a sufficient distraction for the members of the 61st to attack, killing the remaining stormtrooper guard. The rebels enter the Distillery and clear the bunkers, placing detonators as they go. Once the rebel troops exite the distillery, they set off the detonators and destroy it.[3]
        • During the final actions of the campaign, Sergeant Maximian Ajax's squad is pinned down by an AT-ST while fighting the planet's Imperial garrison, the X-wings unable to fly low enough enough to shoot the vehicle. They are saved by the Coyerti people, who storm in great numbers and destroy the Imperial garrison.[3]
      • Rebel groups are sent throughout the Mid Rim to conduct missions designed to distract Imperials from the Mid Rim Retreat. The Twenty-First travel to Bestine IV on one such mission.[3]
    • At some point before the Battle of Hoth[1153]
      • The Hidden Hand crime syndicate puts together a crew—amongst them Arr-Nine-Nineteen, Dengar, Chio Fain, Gita, Honnah, and Beilert Valance—to hunt for Vader.[1154] The crew fails with the betrayal of Dengar[1155] and the Hidden Hand is silenced.[1156]
      • Valance arranges for his people on Chorin, including his lover, Yuralla Vega, to be sheltered by rebels on Lowik in exchange for supplying the Hand's weapons to the Rebellion.[1157]
    • Weeks before the Battle of Hoth
      • Luke Skywalker begins to fly snowspeeders with Dak Ralter at Hoth's Echo Base.[1158]
      • Death Squadron is misled in their search for the Rebellion's main base of operations by Chelli Lona Aphra to[1159] the long-lost Jedi world of Tython.[15] With the Empire redirected from probing Hoth, the Rebel Alliance is given an extra few weeks to prepare for an imminent Imperial intrusion.[1159][1160]
      • An Imperial Star Destroyer, following a hypermatter trail from the 61st's corvette Thunderstrike, ambushes the rebels in a lifeless star system in the Metatessu sector, but they are able to escape.[3]
      • An Imperial light cruiser, backed by a squadron of TIE fighters, attacks the starships of the 61st. However, the 61st obliterates the Imperial forces, wiping out the entire crew of the cruiser.[3]
      • The 61st starships are attacked by around a dozen TIE Interceptors, who damage the starboard side of the corvette before the 61st leaves the system, discovering the hypermatter leak.[3]
      • The Sixty-First, in order to obtain the resources needed for the repairs of the Thunderstrike, travel to the Redhurne system, where they raid an Imperial heavy freighter by launching boarding pods at the ship, each one carrying a squad of Rebel troopers. They tear through the defending stormtroopers while Sergeant Fektrin leads a trio of engineers to extract the resources they need. An Imperial Gozanti-class cruiser arrives in the system, forcing the rebel soldiers to get back to their pods. Once all of the survivors have returned, the Thunderstrike and Apailana's Promise jump out of the system.[3]
    • Darth Vader leads an invasion aimed at destroying the Rebel Alliance's Echo Base hidden on the remote ice world of Hoth and capturing Luke Skywalker. The base's location was discovered when a Viper probe droid, deployed by Darth Vader's Death Squadron, landed on the planet, prompting the Rebels to begin an evacuation. The Imperial attack force consisted of primarily AT-AT walkers, commanded by General Maximilian Veers. His army is tasked with destroying Echo Base's main power generator to allow orbital bombardment of the planet and for Darth Vader to arrive and lead ground forces into the base. Spearheading the defense of the generator are the T-47 airspeeders of Rogue Squadron, commanded by Luke Skywalker. However, the snowspeeders do not have the necessary firepower to bring down the walkers, so Skywalker suggests to trip the walkers with the tow cables issued to every snowspeeder. The power generator is destroyed by Veers' main AT-AT. The remaining base personnel proceedes to evacuate, as the 501st Legion, led by Vader, enters the base. The overwhelming Imperial invasion forces the Rebellion to scatter their fleet across the galaxy.[1161]
    • Following the Battle of Hoth, General Octavion Sorin of the Imperial Army is tasked with hunting down the remaining rebels left behind on the planet. One squad manages to fend off Sorin's forces and escape the planet on a stolen Imperial shuttle they stole from Sorin. The Rebel squad flees to Haven, a village on the planet Ison, but are tracked by Sorin. Believing the defenseless village to be the rebel stronghold, Sorin prepares to leave Hoth and jump to Ison. Finding an Imperial transceiver on the shuttle, the rebels discover Sorin's preparations to attack Haven. The group returns to Echo Base and operate the still functioning ion cannon in an attempt to destroy Sorin's Star Destroyer before it leaves for Haven. They temporaly disable the shields, so are able to fly the shuttle to the Star Destroyer's exposed launch bay, crashing along the hangar floor. However, the squad is too late and the ships jumps to hyperspace.[1162]
    • Above Ison, the rebels make their way to the bridge, where they confront Sorin. He escapes as the squad takes control of the bridge and crash the Star Destroyer onto the planet's surface. Having landed outside the gates of Haven, the rebels exit the ship and take one final stand against General Sorin and his forces. The group fendesd off waves of Imperial troops, including Sorin's experimental Mark III Repulsor Tank. They eventually defeat Sorin's forces, and kill the General, saving Haven from destruction.[1162]
    • The Thunderstrike and the Apailana's Promise arrive at a rendezvous in the Elochar sector with a flotilla of a dozen rebel ships, including the Sixmoon. The light freighter Trumpet's Call jumps to the flotilla from a battle, where Imperial spies have secretly replaced the crew. The ship's life support had failed along the way leaving the rebel crew dead with an Imperial infiltration team disguised as injured crew. Members of the 61st rescue the Imperial spies and take them onto the Thunderstrike. Inside, the spies break loose and storm the bridge, killing Lieutenant Sairgon, Commander Paonu and the rest of the bridge crew. However, Sairgon had already sent a message about the infiltration and all of the other ships except the Apailana's Promise scatter to other star systems. Brand and other twilight members take back control before the Imperial II-class Star Destroyer Herald arrives, and the 61st flees the system.[3]
    • By around this point, Lando Calrissian had freed the enslaved people held at the Fantanine mining colony.[1163]
    • Following advice from the Force spirit of Obi-Wan Kenobi, Luke Skywalker heads to Dagobah to begin his training with Jedi Master Yoda.[1161]
    • Darth Vader hires bounty hunters Boba Fett, Bossk, Dengar, IG-88B, Zuckuss, and 4-LOM to track down the Millennium Falcon, which travels across the Anoat sector, eventually reaching Bespin's Cloud City from the Hoth asteroid belt.[1161]
    • Vader sets a trap for Skywalker on the Cloud City. He captures Han Solo and Leia Organa to torture them, hoping to lure Skywalker out of hiding. He sees a vision of his friends' suffering while on Dagobah, while training with Master Yoda, and rushes to Cloud City to help them, despite the objections of both Yoda and Obi-Wan Kenobi.[1161]
    • Darth Vader had planned to put Skywalker in carbonite, in order to be delivered to the Emperor personally. Hoping to avoid his death, Vader chooses Solo as a test subject for the modified freezing chamber. Solo, Organa, Chewbacca, and C-3PO are brought to the carbon-freezing facility, despite Chewbacca's attempt to set him free, Solo insists there will be another time to rescue him. Solo is then frozen, and found to have survived. Afterwards, Vader is informed that Skywalker's X-wing has just landed.[1161]
    • Skywalker makes his way to the carbon-freezing chamber and finds Vader waiting for him, and the two engage in a lightsaber duel throughout the chamber. The duel progresses through the city's processing vane and onto a maintenance catwalk, where Vader disarmes Skywalker by slicing off his weapon hand. He then temptes the Jedi apprentice to join him and embrace the dark side. When Luke refuses, Vader reveals that he is the boy's own father, Anakin Skywalker. Rather than allowing himself to be captured, Skywalker throws himself off the platform where they had fought, before landing and hanging helplessly at the bottom of the city.[1161]
    • Han Solo is taken by Boba Fett to be delivered to Jabba the Hutt. Calrissian issues a Code Force Seven and arranges for the Bespin Wing Guard to ambush the Imperial team escorting himself, Organa, Chewbacca, and C-3PO to Vader's ship. They rush to intercept Fett, reaching his landing platform just in time to see the Slave I take off, so decide to go for the Falcon and escape the city. Leia hears Luke pleading for help and orders the ship around. Finding Skywalker dangling from the vane, they pull up beneath it, and Calrissian pulls him to safety.[1161]
    • Before leaving, Calrissian manages to warn the citizens of Cloud City about the recent arrival of Imperial forces, causing many to panic and flee. The city is occupied by the Empire, with several trades, such as cybernetic implants, being suppressed, and a skeleton garrison stationed there.[1161]
    • Darth Vader embarks on a quest for revenge against those who hid his son Luke Skywalker from him.[1164] With the forensics droid ZED-6-7,[1165] Vader returns to the Lars moisture farm and later to Padmé Amidala's apartment on Coruscant for clues.[1164]
    • "The Contessa" is faced with rebuilding her rebel[1166] Corona Squadron after it fought at the Battle of Hoth and regrouped aboard the Liberty.[649] Tasked by Mon Mothma to organize the starfighter defense at Rendezvous Point Delta-Three, "the Contessa" assigns Wedge Antilles to reform Red Squadron from Rogue Squadron veterans and other individuals.[1166]
    • Under Commander Wedge Antilles, Red Squadron attacks a pirate base, whose proximity to the Alliance Fleet's rendezvous point following the evacuation of Hoth threatened their safety. The base and all pirate forces are destroyed.[1166]
    • Dengar gambles with and is indebted to the Ohnaka Gang, whose original leader, Hondo Ohnaka, had been replaced by Skragg.[1167]
    • Having cracked the Rebel Alliance's codes, which were top secret and known only to a few individuals, such as R2-D2, the Empire tracks down rebels regrouping from the Battle of Hoth who initiated communications with other Alliance divisions.[1168]
    • The Falcon arrives at a battle at Rendezvous Point Delta-Three. The new arrivals aide in the rebels' escape from Imperial forces led by Ellian Zahra,[1168] who was reassigned by Darth Vader and the Emperor from a non-essential post at the spice mines of Kessel to hunt down remnants of the Rebellion.[1169] Following the incident, Commander Zahra received explicit instructions from Lord Vader: Luke Skywalker was not to be harmed.[1168]
    • After taking a fork-nosed ice worm fossil from Imperial forces on Hoth to sell to the Shadow University through Sava Estat, Chelli Lona Aphra embarks on a job to find the Rings of Vaale, which allegedly grants eternal fortune, immortality, and abilities to rival the most powerful Force user when used together. Doctor Aphra, archaeology student Detta Yao, the bounty hunter Black Krrsantan, and the mercenary "Just Lucky" pursue the rings against Ronen Tagge, scion of the Empire's aristocratic Tagge family. After losing years of research to Tagge at the Ruins of Kolkur, Professor Eustacia Okka and her droid, TA-418, who had done research on the rings, join Aphra aboard the Ark Angel III,[1170] traveling to Vaale, Dianth, the home of the The Architects of Vaale who made the rings. They discover murals of High Republic iconography warning that when used independently, one ring would grant eternal life at the cost of the user's madness, while another would grant fortune at the cost of hastening the user's eventual demise.[1171]
    • With the assistance of Just Lucky, Tagge's mercenaries trace Aphra's crew to Vaale, taking the Ring of Fortune before destroying the city of living bone with Aphra and her crew captured.[1172] Aphra's crew is taken to Ronen Tagge's Private Penthouse in Canto Bight, where they defeat Ronen, who was wearing the two rings, and escape from after stealing several priceless artifacts.[1173]
    • Nakano Lash resurfaces near Kessel.[1174] Having been informed by Aphra and Black Krrsantan on Burnin Konn that she has resurfaced, Bossk races to find Lash. The latter is also sought by her former protégé, Beilert Valance, as well as Boba Fett, who deviated from delivering Han Solo in carbonite to settle a personal score with Lash. Lash had torn apart the group of hunters years ago on Corellia, when she killed their client and the sole heir of the Mourner's Wail Syndicate, Khamus, as well as causing the death of Krynthia, the sole heir of the Unbroken Clan, instigating a continuation of violence between the two groups, which formed the three crime syndicates of Corellia with the addition of Lady Proxima's White Worms.[1175]
    • Leaving her lover, Losha, and her peaceful life on the Moon of Logal Ri behind, T'onga pursues Lash to avenge the death of her brother, T'ongor, while Thisspiasian hunter Ooris Bynar pursues Lash with the help of the bondsman Syphacc.[1174]
    • Amanaman agrees to notify Lando Calrissian should Boba Fett appear at Jabba's Palace. Jabba himself forces Calrissian to agree to spy on the Rebellion on his behalf with the threat of death.[266]
    • Having deduced alongside Commander Grek's Fourth Division they have saved at Rendezvous Point Delta-Three that the Empire was using Alliance codes to hunt down rebels at rendezvous points, the crew of the Millennium Falcon leave the EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate Redemption at Backup Rendezvous Point Gamma-Nine to return to Cloud City.[1176]
    • While the Liberty and Corona Squadron, previously at Rendezvous Point Delta-Three, did not join the rebels at Backup Rendezvous Point Gamma-Nine. Instead, Corona Squadron undertake a series of reconnaissance missions for the Rebellion, charting the planet D'Qar shortly before the Battle of Endor.[649] D'Qar would house only a small Alliance outpost during the Galactic Civil War before becoming the base of the Resistance decades later.[15]
    • Luke Skywalker seeks and fails to find his lost lightsaber, whilst Leia Organa, in her resolve to rescue Han Solo, infiltrates and is captured by the local Imperial garrison in her quest to learn more of the carbon-freezing mechanism. Meanwhile, Lando Calrissian finds his friend Lobot and sabotages the surrounding area of refined tibanna.[1177]
    • Skywalker lets go of his weapon following a vision through the Force. He then joins with Calrissian and Lobot to rescue Leia Organa from the Empire. In doing so, they accidentally free the Bespin Wing Guard, who are admitted into the Rebel Alliance.[1177]
    • Luke Skywalker parts ways with the crew of the Millennium Falcon. With R2-D2, he travels to the planet he envisioned earlier in search of a woman, whom Skywalker also witnessed in his vision, for help in his Jedi future and the fate of the Rebellion.[1178]
    • Skywalker meets Verla, a former disciple the Jedi Ferren Barr who rejected the Jedi, the Sith, and the Force in order to live her life. On Luke Skywalker's promise that he would not disturb her again be it the will of the Force, Verla gives the aspiring Jedi the location of a yellow-bladed lightsaber for him to claim for the extinct Jedi Order. After a duel of wills against the phantom Grand Inquisitor, who found himself tied to a fate worse than death as he is bound by Darth Vader to the mortal realm despite dying years ago over Mustafar, Skywalker asserts his understanding that the Jedi believed in self-sacrifice; compassion—dying for a greater cause.[1179]
    • Darth Vader and ZED-6-7 trail a transmitter from Padmé Amidala's apartment and travel to Vendaxa[1165] after being led to a false finding of Luke Skywalker.[1178] On Vendaxa, the pair encounter a group[1165] of Amidalans—individuals seeking to avenge the mysterious death of Padmé Amidala[1180]— being attacked by native fauna whilst investigating the cause of Amidala's death.[1165]
    • Darth Vader and his droid finds the group's sole survivor: Sabé, a handmaiden of the late Padmé Amidala. Sabé's group intent on avenging Amidala's death, Vader tells the handmaiden that he as well sought revenge on those who hid the cause of Amidala's death, and Sabé joins Vader and leads him to Naboo, where she had hidden the security recordings taken from Amidala's apartment.[1165]
    • Now on a hunt for the truth of Padmé Amidala's death, Darth Vader comes across Sabé's associates, former Royal Naboo Security Forces guards of Amidala Captains Gregar Typho and Tonra, who long suspected the senator was murdered after leaving for Mustafar during the last moments of the Clone Wars. With their presumption that Darth Vader had killed Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker confirmed by the Sith Lord himself, Sabé's group ambush him, though their confrontation is interrupted by an[1181] elder sando aqua monster.[81][1180]
    • Darth Vader slays the sando aqua monster after fleeing with ZED-6-7, thereby increasing the species' chances of extinction within a century to eighty-three percent. Vader then leads his death trooper unit to battle the Amidalans, who guarded the tomb of Padmé Amidala under the leadership of Ric Olié. With memories of the group's leader teaching Anakin Skywalker to operate Amidala's Royal Naboo Starship surfacing, Vader spares Olié and proceeds to Amidala's tomb, although he is stopped by Sabé and other handmaidens of the former Naboo Queen and senator. Unable to kill them, he immobilizes Padmé Amidala's bodyguards and enters her tomb.[1180]
    • Darth Vader and ZED-6-7 trace a med implant at Amidala's tomb to Polis Massa, which had been attacked and abandoned by this point. Vader and his droid are followed and attacked by the Amidalans, who are defeated. Finding a holorecording of his wife's death at the maternity ward, Vader destroys ZED-6-7 in his rage before wallowing in grief, hearing his lover's last words that there is still good in Anakin Skywalker as he returns to the Imperial Palace on Coruscant, where he submits to the Emperor.[1182]
    • Mourner's Wail kills Unbroken Clan soldiers in a bombing on Chorgad. In retaliation, General Vukorah of the clan attacks[1183] a Mourner's Wail spice run[1184] in the Kessel Sector,[1183] prompting the latter group to bomb a Caviness IV settlement.[1183]
    • Ooris Bynar is killed by Nakano Lash after tracing the latter to Ruusan, and Bossk is left on the graveyard world of Galmerah by Boba Fett. Valance and T'onga reach Lash, who reveals that she only killed Khamus because they were sent to murder a pregnant Krynthia. Lash further reveals that she had been raising Khamus and Krynthia's child, Cadeliah, in the hope that she would unite the two warring syndicates as their joint heir.[1184]
    • While T'onga's lets go of her desire for vengeance, she is mortally wounded by Boba Fett, who fulfills his desire to arrange Lash's death by leaving her on her starship, which is destroyed by Vukorah of the Unbroken Clan. Fett escapes aboard the Slave I, and Valance vows to protect Cadeliah as per Lash's dying wish, taking his ship, the Broken Wing, alongside Cadeliah.[1185]
    • Vukorah finishes purging those loyal to the old line of the Unbroken Clan and hires Zuckuss and 4-LOM to hunt down Valance and Cadeliah to secure her own place as leader of the clan.[1186]
    • Remy delivers a replica of the Nihil Path engine to De'Rruyet Industries on Corellia before assisting Sana Starros with a drop-off on the world.[1187]
    • The Gortus 4 colony signs over rights to their moon, prompting the Tagge Construction Division to be tasked with establishing an ore factory within a week.[1188]
    • The strikes on the Keshk lunar colony continue for the eighth day as a result of wage disputes with the Keshk Corporation.[1188]
    • Beol De'Rruyet reveals that his De'Rruyet Industries has found a way to replicate the Nihil Path engine with the potential to completely change the galactic economy or end the galactic war.[1188]
    • Lucky reports Ronen's fall to Lady Domina Tagge,[1173] who captures Aphra to Tagge Space after bounty hunter pursuits through Taris, Burnin Konn, and Arveesh Station. The head of Tagge Corporation hires Aphra to gather more information on the De'Rruyet Path engine replica.[1188]
    • Aphra enlists the help of Sana Starros to gain an audience with Lady Proxima at the Den of the White Worms on Corellia, who directs them to the Unbroken Clan. After clan leader Vukorah finished interrogating Remy, Aphra and Starros steal a datapad containing a transcript of Remy's interrogation, following a lead to[1187] an ancient battlefield on Dol'har Hyde. There, Aphra and Starros are attacked by Vukorah and the Unbroken Clan.[1189]
    • Working alongside "Just Lucky" in Canto Bight, Ariole Yu offers to let Koz go if the latter pays his debt to Wen Delphis, leader of the Sixth Kin, although Kin enforcers are ambushed by traitors.[1190] With treason apparent amongst the ranks of the Kin, Wen Delphis sends "Just Lucky" and Ariole Yu to eliminate the alleged traitor Gallin Crae and his contact off-world.[1191]
    • Emperor Palpatine instructs Domina Tagge to capture Beol De'Rruyet's Nihil Path engine replica.[1190] Aided by Lapin Tagge, Domina Tagge discovers that slicers had stolen confidential data from TaggeCo as well as its competitors.[1191]
    • For wallowing in grief, Vader is deposited by Darth Sidious to the black sands of Mustafar, on the spot where the Sith apprentice was immolated decades prior. To turn Vader's anger back into fear and the fear into pain, Sidious sends Ochi of Bestoon, assassin of the Sith, as a reminder.[1192]
    • The Eye of Webbish Bog calls out to Vader, reminding the cyborg of his inner conflicts between his personal desire for power, his current servitude to the Sith and destined servitude to the Force, and his personal needs for family. After receiving a wayfinder to Exegol from the Eye,[1193] Vader defeats Ochi's droid army and departs Mustafar holding Ochi captive in an escape pod retrofitted onto his green Jedi starfighter.[1194]
    • Darth Vader and Ochi reach the vicinity of Exegol,[1194] where they fly through an Imperial blockade led by Administrator Sly Moore as well as a creature of a summa-verminoth sub-species. After receiving a vision of his son, Luke Skywalker, striking him down and taking his place at the Emperor's side, Vader makes a forbidden use of the Force to subdue the beast, thereby violating his master's command. Through fear of the Emperor's plans, Vader had become angered and was fueled by hatred, leading to a demonstration of power whereby the cyborg gained control over the beast and rode it to Exegol's Sith Citadel.[1195]
    • Vader confronts his master but is subdued by pain. Vader's beast lies dead, and Ochi loses his eyes; overwhelmed by the power of the Emperor, Vader chooses to yield to his Master. Vader, the Emperor, Ochi, Sly Moore, and Mas Amedda then leave Exegol—leaving its Sith cultists, minions, experiments, and a growing fleet of planet-destroying Star Destroyers.[1196] Dathan escapes from Exegol by hiding in Vader's shuttle.[1197]
    • The Rebellion's Fourth Division joins forces with the Seventh Division after battling Ellian Zahra's assault group[1088] at Elessia.[1198]
    • Operation Starlight begins; the Fourth Division aims to reassemble the scattered Alliance Fleet.[1198]
      • The Pathfinders of the Rebel Alliance conduct a heist at Coruscant's Imperial Museum with help from Lando Calrissian and Lobot to steal the Autonomous Translator Module, Mark II droid, through which the Fourth and Seventh Divisions intend to establish new secret communications with other rebels.[1198]
      • Starlight Squadron is established. Consisting of Wedge Antilles, Freyta Smyth, L'ulo L'ampar, Shara Bey, Evaan Verlaine, and Mart Mattin, it attempts to contact other rebel groups and establish new secret communications through the codes provided by the Autonomous Translator Module, Mark II linguistics droid, which is kept operational by Lobot.[1199]
      • Starlight Squadron finds the ruins of the Sixth Division instead and is ambushed by Imperial probe droids. Amidst the chaos, Lobot begins to die in his efforts to keep the rebels' linguistics droid operational. While Lando Calrissian remains adamant in telling Lobot to cease maintaining the droid, Kes Dameron, fearing for his love, Shara Bey, demands Calrissian to let Lobot die in exchange so that Bey may be saved.[1199]
      • Calrissian is detained by order of Leia Organa. After secretly learning its language, C-3PO deactivates the Rebellion's linguistics droid in its refusal to yield its self-interest to save Lobot. With command of the droid's language, communications are reestablished with Starlight Squadron, which escapes from Zahra and her flagship, Tarkin's Will, by exploiting the Empire's disregard for its own droids.[1200]
      • However, Shara Bey, Starlight's commanding officer, is captured by Zahra's forces. While a despaired Kes Dameron is counseled by Leia Organa, Lando Calrissian is reunited with Lobot and, seeing that the Rebellion would not care for them, offers to pay off his debt to Jabba the Hutt by stealing the deactivated linguistics droid and its embedded rebel codes.[1200]
      • Having traced the location of Tarkin's Will, the Rebellion plans to strike back at the Empire.[1200]
    • Aphra, Starros, and the Unbroken Clan attempt to seize the replica on Midarr. The engine is destroyed during the resulting firefight, but Aphra steals the crystal core of the original Path engine and gives it to Domina Tagge. In refusing to let Aphra and Starros go yet again, Vukorah loses an eye while chasing them.[1191]
    • Valance and Cadeliah flee to meet Valance's lover, Yuralla Vega, at a rebel outpost on Lowik.[1186] Valance leaves Cadeliah in the care of Yuralla Vega as Zuckuss and 4-LOM attack the Lowik rebels,[1201] who are separated from Rebel Command as the Empire had broken the rebel security codes. While Valance distracts the hunters by visiting random ports throughout the galaxy, the Lowik rebels evacuate before the Empire's arrival, though they ask Valance to save a lone transport that was lost in the process. Valance does so to settle his debt to Han Solo, but is shot down by the Ohnaka Gang, which is led by Skragg, advised by a captive Dengar,[1202] and in pursuit of the locations of the rebel armada.[1167]
    • Working for Jabba alongside the Hutt's majordomo, Bib Fortuna, the hunter Bossk joins the hunted in the Great Hunt of Malastare, sparing Grummgar and killing the InterGalactic Banking Clan Vice Chair Exum Jermit, who had been refusing to do business with Jabba.[1203]
    • Hunt for Han Solo
      • While en route transporting Han Solo to Jabba on Tatooine, Boba Fett loses Solo to mysterious figures on Nar Shaddaa after killing the Kanji Criminal Enterprise's pit fighting champion, Wyrmen Lictor, in the Garkata Fighting Arena.[1204]
      • Crimson Dawn resurfaces under Lady Qi'ra. They steal Han Solo from Boba Fett[1205] with the help of Deva Lompop, who in doing so betrays Jabba.[1206] Qi'ra aims to bring the great factions of the galaxy together in an auction for Han Solo—hosted by Crimson Dawn on Jekara for a frozen figure wanted by all.[1205]
      • Jabba places a large bounty on Boba Fett,[1207] believing that he had betrayed him by selling Solo to another party.[1208] T'onga is thus motivated to take up arms again against Fett, and T'onga's wife, Losha, follows.[1207]
      • Valance battles Skragg's Ohnaka Gang, saves the rebel transport, and secures the rebel locations for the Rebel Alliance's Private Blanch Sproull. Hearing of Han Solo's capture by Boba Fett from Dengar, Valance takes Dengar captive to rescue Solo.[1167] Aboard a Corona-class armed frigate stolen from the Ohanak Gang, the two hunters pursue Boba Fett and his Slave I.[1203]
      • Vader, believing that any attempts to rebel against the Emperor is futile, changes his strategy of keeping Luke Skywalker and his friends alive; Vader decides that killing his son is the only way, and sets out to hunt for Han Solo using the services of Bokku the Hutt and Ochi of Bestoon.[1209]
      • Aboard Jabba's yacht, the Star Jewel, alongside Bib Fortuna, Bossk learns of Jabba's bounty on Boba Fett.[1207]
      • Intent on killing Fett and rescuing Solo, Valance is tracked down by Zuckuss and 4-LOM in the Y'Toub System. Dengar arranges a deal between the four of them to postpone pursuing the Unbroken Clan's bounty on Cadeliah and instead hunt down the wanted Fett.[1207]
      • Boba Fett is confronted by Zuckuss and 4-LOM. They are defeated and left for dead on Nar Shaddaa and Tatooine, respectively.[1205]
      • Chewbacca hears from Sagwa a lead on Han Solo's location.[1210]
      • Working with Dengar to trail Boba Fett, Valance seeks to help Han Solo since the latter saved his life. The pair run into Chewbacca and C-3PO on Nar Shaddaa. Chewbacca warns Valance not to cross paths with him again on their search for Solo, and they part in peace. Valance and Dengar learn from Devono Vix that Crimson Dawn had returned and stolen Solo from Fett, but Vix is immediately killed by the assassin Deathstick.[1208]
      • Valance and Dengar are pursued by Deathstick at Qi'ra's behest. In a brief interlude, Dengar contacted Bib Fortuna, seeking reassurance that[801] his love interest, the Aruzan[1211] Manaroo, remained safe under Jabba's captivity[801] while the bounty hunter remained indebted to the Hutt.[1212]
      • Valance and Dengar quietly escape to Canto Bight. While he remains indebted to Wen Delphis of the Sixth Kin, Dengar reclaims his ship, the Punishing One, on the condition that he and Valance stir up trouble at the Crimson Dawn gathering.[1213] Before they leave, Dengar promises to give Deathstick the location of Cadeliah.[1212]
      • Crimson Dawn attacks the Mourner's Wail. T'onga and Losha are cuaght amidst the attack.[1208] After repelling the Dawn, T'onga informs Mourner's Wail leader Khamdek that he has a granddaughter—Cadeliah. Khamdek asks T'onga to keep Cadeliah safe from the Dawn.[801]
      • T'onga and Losha recruit gladiator champion Tasu Leech, who seeks a worthy challenge, and Zuckuss, who was left bitter by the fact that his own friend, 4-LOM, had lost memory of him and attempted to kill him.[1213]
      • On Nar Shaddaa, General Vukorah is invited to the Crimson Dawn gathering by Deathstick.[1213]
      • R2-D2, C-3PO, Luke Skywalker, Chewbacca, and Sagwa are ambushed by the Kanji gang on Nar Shaddaa, and the rebels escape aboard the Falcon. The gang reports their sighting of Luke Skywalker to Imperial Garrison on Vandor.[1210]
      • R2-D2 informs Skywalker of the locations of dozens of Jedi outposts. Sagwa joins the Rebellion, and the Falcon is recalled to the Rebel Fleet by General Leia Organa after an unknown party contacted the Alliance regarding Solo.[1210]
      • Invitations are sent out to various individuals throughout the galaxy, including Jabba the Hutt, Black Sun's Boss Gyuti,[1214] and Imperial General Hurst Romodi.[1215]
      • Sly Moore and her court hire IG-88 to destroy Vader in the fear that he could overpower the Emperor. After defeating IG-88 at Zee-Nine City Seven in Hutt Space, Vader, Ochi, and Bokku find a Crimson Dawn lead to Han Solo. Bokku is tasked by Vader with obtaining Solo,[1216] as is Sly Moore.[1215]
      • Bounty hunter Boushh and his crew, hired by Crimson Dawn, attempt to assassinate the corporation's executive board. In the ensuing fight, Domina Tagge roots out Tagge Corporation's mole, Silas Tagge, and kills him. She then persuades Boushh's crew to work for her, promising as payment to take them back to their homeworld, Uba IV, from which they had been exiled by Ubese society.[1217]
      • Domina Tagge sends Aphra and Starros to track down Ebann Drake, who had left TaggeCo[1191] for Crimson Dawn's auction. Aphra and Starros betray the bounty hunter Durge, who had also taken a bounty on Drake, and take Drake's invitation to Crimson Dawn's auction, which they attend per Domina Tagge's instructions.[1214]
      • Amilyn Holdo, who had been working amongst the crime syndicates, informs Leia Organa that Solo was no longer with Fett, and is instead held at an auction hosted by Crimson Dawn. Organa asks Chewbacca to reach out to Lando Calrissian about the matter, and C-3PO accompanies the Wookiee to handle urgent tasks relating to Starlight Squadron's efforts to find the remainder of the Alliance Fleet.[1218]
      • The Imperial II-class Star Destroyer Ultima II under the command of Admiral Kalaxo traps the Alliance Eleventh Division on the surface of Ab Dalis. The Rebel Ground Defense Command under Colonel Chouch organizes the surface defense against Imperial walkers and tanks. Following Ground Defense Command's destruction, Starlight Squadron arrives and destroys Ultima II by causing an eruption of the volcano directly below it, saving High Command's Admiral Gial Ackbar and Alliance Chancellor Mon Mothma.[1219]
      • Calrissian agrees to help rescue Solo, although he also promises Jabba via Fortuna that his package would be delivered over Jekara. Upon the Falcon's arrival over the world, Calrissian secretly jettisons the rebels' linguistics droid into space. As the droid drifts away, it attempts to bargain with Calrissian, temporarily repairing Lobot's implants and restoring his personality.[1218]
      • Calrissian regrets his actions and vows to revive Lobot, although Chewbacca, Organa, and C-3PO pilot the Falcon down to Jekara.[1218]
    • Auction for Han Solo and aftermath
      • On Jekara, Boba Fett leaves Bossk for dead after the Trandoshan attempted to capture him for Jabba.[1205]
      • Fett infiltrates the auction venue, and Jabba successfully outbids everyone for Solo with one million credits.[1220] Back stage, Crae battles Ariole Yu and Lucky. Darth Vader arrives claiming Solo as his own.[1221]
      • Qi'ra duels Lord Vader while Imperial General Romodi takes Han Solo aboard an Imperial shuttle headed for the Executor.[1222]
      • Wishing to maintain the Hutts' relationship with the Empire, Jabba lets his prize go before leaving the auction halls himself. Dissatisfied that the Hutts' reputation is at risk, Bokku leads dissident Hutt Council members to take back Solo from Romodi's Imperial shuttle with a Hutt fleet.[1222]
      • The rebels pursue the same shuttle aboard the Millennium Falcon.[1222]
      • Wishing to save Han Solo, Valance teams up with his hated rival, Boba Fett, to pursue the same shuttle aboard the Slave I, leaving Dengar stranded on Jekara.[1222]
      • Vader, sensing the presence of Luke Skywalker above Jekara, pursues the Jedi-trainee in orbit.[1222]
      • Chelli Lona Aphra and Sana Starros steal a Black Sun necklace containing data crystals from the Crimson Dawn gathering and make their escape.[1223] While escaping, work alongside Ariole Yu and Lucky, and Aphra kills Crae.[1224] Aphra's electro-tattoos are fried after activating the Thought Dowser to incapacitate a pursuing Deathstick.[1223]
      • Ariole Yu and Just Lucky are stranded at the Crimson Dawn venue by Aphra and Starros, who escape and find that a network of Crimson Dawn spies had infiltrated Black Sun, the Empire, the Rebellion, the Sixth Kin, as well as the Tagge Corporation.[1223] Ariole Yu and Just Lucky eventually depart as well, back to Canto Bight.[1225]
      • Most guests leave the venue after fighting broke out over Han Solo, but General Vukorah remains with Crimson Dawn, impressed with their confrontation with Vader.[1212]
      • T'onga, Losha, Tasu Leech, and Zuckuss track Dengar and Valance to Jekara, where they find Bossk, who frees himself using Zuckuss' knife.[1212]
      • Dengar is recued by Deathstick, to whom he hands the location of Cadeliah as Deathstick promises to pay off Manaroo's debt to Jabba.[1212]
      • Qi'ra continues pulling her strings as Crimson Dawn's leader, anticipating the coming of the "end" while holding the a Sith holocron.[1222]
        • At the Dawn's urging, the Hutt Council armada attacks the Executor, although the shuttle containing Han Solo is safely delivered to the Imperial flagship.[1226]
        • Certain that his son's fear is keeping him from being a Jedi, Darth Vader leaves Luke Skywalker and, at Emperor Palpatine's command, personally executes the Hutt Council, including Bokku, over Jekara.[1226] Vader thus also learns that Palpatine wants Skywalker alive and is fearful of losing the boy.[1227]
        • Ochi apprehends Sly Moore for attempting to save Skywalker from Vader, but the Sith Lord lets Moore go to fulfill his next mission from the Emperor—to hunt down Crimson Dawn's operatives within the Empire.[1227]
        • Crimson Dawn also arranges for the crew of the Millennium Falcon to board the Executor and rescue Solo. However, he is instead taken by Boba Fett, who injures Valance and leaves him for dead aboard the Executor.[1226]
      • Crimson Dawn, the rebels, and Boba Fett depart Jekara.[1226]
      • Working for Crimson Dawn, Deva Lompop has IG-88 restored to take Solo back from Boba Fett. While IG-88 fails, the droid retreats to a junk world in order to bide his time and improve his systems.[1228]
      • Boba Fett sells Han Solo to Jabba the Hutt on Tatooine, where Fett remains. Having become the sole leader of the Hutt clans, Jabba remains unfettered in regard to the Hutts' relationship with the Empire.[1226]
    • Declaring the success of the "dawn," Qi'ra gathers warriors such as Ren's Knights of Ren in preparation for the reign of her organization.[1226]
    • Having had her group of Amidalans killed by Imperial forces,[1229] Sabé infiltrates Crimson Dawn, and on Polis Massa, the former handmaiden reactivates a repaired ZED-6-7 so that the droid could work for her in uncovering Vader's interest in Padmé Amidala.[1230]

Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY)[]

  • 3–4 ABY
    • By this point, the Archivist had visited Yoda on Dagobah, having been tasked by Qi'ra to find him so that the Jedi Master could assist in her plans. However, Yoda taught the Archivist, and she reports to Crimson Dawn the lie that Yoda was dead.[1231]
    • Ochi is ordered by Vader bring his assassin associates under the Sith Lord's fold. However, on Saki, Ochi kills his fellow master assassins to free an imprisoned Lady Qi'ra, joining Crimson Dawn.[1227]
    • Vader restores Valance and presses him into his service, tasking him with killing Admiral Tarl Sokoli.[1232]
    • Vader and Ochi hire three Assassin's Guild members on Vincorba and recruit three anti-Dawn Revengers on Calior, and they are joined by Valance on Laecor.[1233]
    • Domina Tagge tasks Aphra and Starros with recovering Ascendant technology similar to the Thought Dowser,[1234] but the pair run into rogue archaeologist Kho Phon Farrus, who works for the Archivist of Crimson Dawn and has been assembling Ascendant technology as well, in the hopes of finding the Spark Eternal.[1235] Aphra, Starros, Kho Phon Farrus, and Iglan'tine Nos enter the Unyielding Heart on Bar'leth, where the Spark Eternal possesses Aphra.[1236]
    • Starros and Kho Phon Farrus inform Domina Tagge of the Spark Eternal, and Tagge tasks them with retrieving both the artifact and Aphra. An ambitious Ronen Tagge also wishes to acquire the Spark Eternal.[1236]
    • Vader leads his group to attack Laecor's Crimson Dawn forces and retrieve a list of their collaborators in the Empire. Unbeknownst to them, Sabé and ZED-6-7 had planted the list there.[1233]
    • Vader and his group purges the suspects on Coruscant and on the Executor.[1230]
    • Sabé and ZED-6-7 question Ochi and Sly Moore—fellow Crimson Dawn collaborators—about Vader's motives. In a later mission to Tranchar, Vader uncovers Ochi's betrayal—intending to use him as a pawn against the Dawn—and follows him to Sabé's place on Naboo.[1229]
    • Vader and Sabé join together, united by their belief in making the galaxy orderly—[1229]Sabé concludes that Vader was Anakin Skywalker,[1237] who had fathered Luke Skywalker alongside Padmé Amidala.[1230]
    • Ochi also helps General Romodi, who has been unhappy about some of his soldiers being killed by Vader's crew, and informs Romodi's forces about the location of Vader's crew. While Romodi's forces attack the crew, they are occupied by Crimson Dawn forces called in by Ochi, allowing the treacherous assassin to take Vader's crew away from the site. Vader then orders Romodi's forces to battle the Dawn until their end, and a pulse bomb annihilates all the combatants.[1237]
    • On Gabredor III, Vader and Sabé reunite with Kitster Banai and Wald and target the corrupt Imperial Governor Tauntaza, a Crimson Dawn collaborator; while she escapes, Vader, Sabé, and Ochi discover that she had been developing technology that drains power from living things to the extent of entire planets—the world Karolia had already been devestated.[1238]
    • Believing that Sabé has been trying to bring him back to the light side, Vader pursues the governor himself in anger. He orders the Executor to incinerate Tauntaza's fortress but makes efforts to save his allies. While the governor escapes again, Sabé saves Vader in turn. They discover that Tauntaza had been acting upon direct orders from the Emperor, who is testing his apprentice again.[1239]
    • Vader, accompanied by Sabé, confronts Darth Sidious. However, Vader, still troubled by his suffering in his former self as Anakin Skywalker, turns her over to the Emperor, who asserts that the former handmaiden could be a powerful asset. She and Vader are sent to quell an uprising on Skako Minor.[1240]
    • Luke Skywalker and R2-D2 search the galaxy for Jedi sites, and on Al'doleem, they find a holocron by Yoda that leads them to Gazian, where Skywalker speaks with an imprint of Jedi Elzar Mann and obtains a sacred Jedi text.[1241]
    • Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn initiate the Syndicate War in order to disrupt the Empire and specifically target the Sith—Darth Sidious and Darth Vader—in a war against the Sith.[1242]
      • Chanath Cha and the Orphans sow mistrust and chaos among the galaxy's leading criminal syndicates by secretly attacking their facilities.[1242]
      • Qi'ra informs the surviving Rebel Fleet—assembled from the Fourth, Seventh, and Eleventh Divisions—that Zahra's hunter fleet had destroyed all but one remaining rebel division—the Second Division on Panisia. The Rebellion also strikes a deal with Crimson Dawn to organize its supplies, which have been disrupted by the Syndicate War, through Leia Organa's friend Amilyn Holdo.[1243]
      • Kes Dameron leads Starlight Squadron and the Pathfinders on an unauthorized mission to rescue Shara Bey from Zahra's flagship, Tarkin's Will, which the Pathfinders infiltrate.[1243]
      • Battle of Panisia[1244]
        • Deathstick alerts the Empire to Panisia's rebel base, captures Cadeliah from the rebels, and brings her to Qi'ra.[1245]
        • The Tarkin's Will is destroyed by the Pathfinders, who also rescue Shara Bey successfully, but not before the Star Destroyer eliminated the entire rebel base and several of the incoming Rebel Fleet's vessels—Zahra had relished forcing the rebels to kill some of their own members in their hopes of saving the rebel base.[1244]
        • Leia Organa duels Ellian Zahra and leaves her to the mercy of Panisia's animals, stating that the Rebellion only kills as a necessity and always for justice.[1246]
      • Ochi assassinates ten of the Emperor's Royal Guards in the Imperial Palace.[1245]
      • The Knights of Ren raid Fortress Vader to acquire the Screaming Key.[1247]
    • The Alliance organizes a robbery on Westhill Palace, a mansion in the Westhill cliffs of the city of Canto Bight on Cantonica owned by the Zerek Besh criminal syndicate leader Sliro Barsha, to rescue the captured rebel operative Asara Deyn. During the fight, Kay Vess steals the Trailblazer and escapes Cantonica.[502]
    • Imperial Governor Thorden stages a plot along with Crimson Dawn and the Pyke Syndicate traitor Tizlak Sharr to ovethrow the Pyke Syndicate underboss Gorak Palas, in an attempt to seize control over the moon of Toshara.[502]
    • Kay Vess and the mechanic Waka, under the commission of Crimson Dawn, infiltrate the Imperial Fuel Station Visavi, with the objective of deleting debt records to increase tension between the Pyke Syndicate and the Empire, and extract Bosnok from the facility.[502]
    • Crime lord Jaylen Vrax gathers a group of outlaws to steal beskar ingots from Sliro Ruback's vault at Canto Bight. However, with the real intent to reunite with Ruback, who was his brother, to get revenge on him.[502]
      • Kay Vess and her team infiltrate a private Crimson Dawn club known as the Wellspring in Kijimi City to recover a stolen Ashiga Clan artifact, with the hopes of earning Queen Ashiga's favor, and convince her to free Ank Parako, the bombmaker selected for their heist.[502]
      • Crimson Dawn leader Qi'ra, along with the associate Krisk Ashiga, request Kay Vess to infiltrate the Ashiga weapons factory on Kijimi as a part of their plot in taking over the Ashiga Clan. With the aid from Crimson Dawn, she is able to infiltrate and rescue Ank Parako from the factory.[502]
      • Kay Vess' crew and Crimson Dawn attack the Ashiga Clan stronghold on Kijimi to take control of the Ashiga Clan with the origin strand, the genetic code used by the Ashiga Clan to create more Melitto for the hive, and remove the Clan's relations with the Empire. Kay Vess and Nix initially infiltrate and disable the outer defenses of the stronghold to assist Crimson Dawn, but later become independent after realizing Crimson Dawn's true intentions.[502]
      • Retrieval of krayt dragon pearl.[502]
      • Kay Vess recovers Nix, her merqaal taken from her by the Sullustan gunslinger Hoss and sold to the Hutt Cartel, by infiltrating Jabba's Palace and freeing the imprisoned merqaal from the palace menagerie, but is later intercepted by Jabba the Hutt and his forces. Along with Hoss and the Zerek Besh bounty hunter Vail Tormin, Kay Vess and Nix are ordered to be executed by massifs and later the rancor Pateesa. Though Vess, Nix, and Tormin are able to survive, they are unable to escape from the Palace. However, Jabba the Hutt is impressed by the demonstration of strength, returning Nix to Vess, alongside constraining the group to work for him in exchange for redemption.[502]
      • Jabba the Hutt coerces Vail Tormin and Kay Vess to infiltrate Fort Sunfire, an Imperial stronghold, in order to identify a Zerek Besh spy planted within the Hutt Cartel. They are able to find the data containing the information of the spy within the fortress, but are discovered and pursued by the Empire after an alarm is triggered. Vess, Tormin, and Nix escape with speeder bikes and reach Mos Algo to regroup with the Hutt Cartel, but the place is abandoned by the Cartel after they find themselves tracked by Imperial forces. They are overwhelmed by the Imperial forces, but ND-5 pilots the Trailblazer to push back the Imperials and rescues the trio. They are able to successfully transfer the data to Jabba, the Zerek Besh spy is killed, and Kay Vess' crew is redeemed by Jabba.[502]
      • Kay Vess and her team rescue the Rodian droidsmith Gedeek Obaz from the Imperial facility of Research Station 57, in order to recruit a crewmember that can surpass the droid gatekeeper of Westhill Palace. After arriving on Akiva to gain intel from the Sullustan crime lord Surat Nuat, Vess and Nix make their way towards Research Station 57. Near the facility, Vess and Nix are able to breach the defenses of the exterior, and eventually infiltrate inside the station. After navigating past the Imperial forces on-site, the two are attacked by a turret model designed by Gedeek Obaz, which they deactivate. Ultimately, Vess confronts Obaz in his workshop at the facility, when she enters a standoff against a protocol droid reprogrammed by the droidsmith. Nevertheless, Vess convinces Obaz to join the crew, and later they escape from the facility.[502]
      • Kay Vess' crew infiltrates the Derosha Six space station to steal access codes needed for breaking into Director Sliro Barsha's vault at Canto Bight. At their time there, Kay Vess and Riko Vess learn that Sliro Ruback is Sliro Barsha, an ISB Director, and that Zerek Besh is an undercover intelligence gathering division of the Imperial Security Bureau charged with infiltrating the galactic underworld, to gather intelligence from various criminal organizations and uncover the Rebel Alliance.[502]
      • The heist on Sliro Barsha's vault on Cantonica is conducted. Afterwards, Jaylen Vrax reveals that they had really stolen Sliro's database, not unmarked beskar ingots, and betrays the crew.[502]
    • A major battle is fought in the Akiva system, where the Imperial-class Star Destroyer Revelator is infiltrated by Kay Vess' crew. Inside, they rescue ND-5 and Asara Deyn, and in the subsequent battle both Sliro Barsha and Jaylen Vrax are killed. Later, the Destroyer is attacked and destroyed by the Alliance to Restore the Republic.[502]
    • The Rebel Alliance and Kay Vess' team attack the Imperial facility of Kassina Outpost on the planet Akiva, with the aim of removing the Empire's data on the Alpha Droid model of the Viper probe droid, as well as reprogramming the model to aid the Akivan Rebels.[502]
    • A sabacc tournament is held aboard the casino cruiser Morenia. The tournament's grand prize is the coordinates to the legendary hidden moon of Okala Five, said to contain two point eight trillion tons of pure cyphrite ore. Participants include Brayzak, EY-E9, Kay Vess, Lando Calrissian, Mosh, Rauvin Vashiri and Ultus. The prize is stolen by the Starfallers, a gang of shipjackers led by the bandit Aksali Noll.[502]
    • Forces for the Galactic Empire raid a workshop used by the Starfallers in the Lost Steppe region on the moon Toshara. Governor Thorden acquires the encrypted coordinates of Okala Five.[502]
    • The Rebel Alliance and Kay Vess' crew hunt for the coordinates to Okala Five, in an effort to recover it from the Starfallers and later Governor Thorden. After Thorden manipulates Kay Vess to allow the Starfallers to steal the map to Okala Five from the sabacc tournament, Lando Calrissian convinces Kay Vess' crew to join him in intercepting the map from being delivered to Thorden. By gaining intel from the former Imperial bookkeeper, Bosnok, Kay Vess and Nix start by looking for Aksali Noll on Toshara. They discover that the Starfallers are attempting to sell the map to the Galactic Empire, and find the location of the exchange through an Imperial listening outpost. Vess arrives during the Imperial raid, and the Empire extracts the map away from Vess and the Starfallers. She is able to learn the new location of the map after rescuing the captured Noll from the Empire. At the Tosharan capital of Mirogana, Calrissian executes a plan to arrange a handover meeting with Thorden, and smuggles Vess inside the Mirogana Spaceport within a crate claimed to be volatile Tolium gas. As the Rebel stalls the Governor in an extensive conversation, Vess progresses through the Spaceport and later infiltrates Thorden's bunker. Arriving at the terminal of the bunker, Vess extracts the map from the data archive, and detonates the entire facility to create a distraction for Calrissian. She escapes from the base and entrusts the map to Calrissian.[502]
    • The Rokana Raiders and later Kay Vess' crew occupy and plunder the Khepi Tomb . Near the High Republic Era, a species known as the Khepi constructed a burial chamber hidden inside an asteroid amid the Sayma Nebula, in reponse to the foretold destruction of their world. Eventually, pirates around the galaxy would spread rumors of an immense treasure left behind in the Tomb. Under the guidance of the Imperial Doctor Jessik, a pirate group known as the Rokana Raiders are the first to reach the Tomb, establishing a base inside the asteroid, but they are ultimately unable to open the tomb through force. The Rokana Raiders' leader, Stinger Tash, discovers the security of the Tomb is controlled by the Architect's Scepter, consisting of two parts of a hilt and a shank. While the Raiders own the shank, the hilt is kept by the pirate Hondo Ohnaka, who attempts to pursuit the treasure himself but is captured by the Rokana Raiders. Ohnaka previously delivered a message to Jet Kordo, but is recieved instead by Kay Vess. While the Rokana Raiders capture Kay Vess' crew during their initial mission to the system, they are able to escape from the Tomb with Ohnaka, with Vess agreeing to create an alliance with Ohnaka. Ohnaka and Vess operate near the Kijimi system, Khepi system, and Tatoo system to acquire a disguise, the R93 refractor Cutlass, and the hilt to ultimately infiltrate the base. After returning to the base with the disguise, Ohnaka and Vess attempt to uncover Stinger's plan and win the favor of the crew through a vote, but they accidentaly activate the dormant Khepi guardian droids provoking the death of Tash and the escape of the Rokana Raiders. ND-5 arrives with the Trailblazer allowing Vess' crew to escape the Tomb.[502]
    • T'onga, Losha, Tasu Leech, Bossk, Zuckuss, and 4-LOM attempt to rescue Cadeliah from Crimson Dawn. However, Cadeliah chooses to stay with Qi'ra, who further offers T'onga's crew employment.[1248]
    • Realizing the purposeful threat to the Sith, the Emperor instructs the Imperial Military to target Crimson Dawn; in response, Lady Qi'ra instructs her agents to cause chaos in the Empire.[1249]
      • Palpatine speaks with the leadership of the Hutts, Black Sun, the Pykes, the Droid Gotra, and Zerek Besh and instructs them to cease their infighting and defeat Crimson Dawn.[1250]
      • T'onga's crew accept a Crimson Dawn mission to Bestine IV, and Vader tasks Valance to stop the Dawn there.[1251] T'onga reveals that the entire rebel base containing Valance's loved ones had been eliminated by the Empire, save for Cadeliah, turning a guilt-ridden Valance to destroy the Imperial naval base. In retaliation, Vader's forces bomb the Bestine insurgents[1252] and sends Inferno Squad to track down Valance and wipe his memory—his cybernetic memories had glimsped plans for a new Death Star.[1253]
      • Taking Imperial bounties, IG-88 eliminates Son-tuul Pride leaders and hunts Vukorah, leader of the Unbroken Clan.[1251]
    • The Spark Eternal, using Aphra, visits Crimson Dawn in search of knowledge and power, and speaks with the Archivist. Starros, Kho Phon Farrus, Eustacia Okka, Detta Yao, Korin Aphra, and Magna Tolvan assemble to retrieve Doctor Aphra. However, they are being tracked by Ariole Yu and Just Lucky, working for Ronen Tagge along with their old crew[1254] in the hopes of scoring their last job and retire safely.[1236]
    • Starros' crew finds 0-0-0 and BT-1 on Birukay, while Ariole Yu and Just Lucky find Doctor Aphra and the Spark with Crimson Dawn. Both groups confront the Spark, but it convinces Kho Phon Farrus to let it go and leaves with the Null Blade and 0-0-0 and BT-1 in her service,[1255] with the aim of destroying the Sith.[1256]
    • The Archivist and the Knights of Ren recover the Fermata Cage for Crimson Dawn using the Screaming Key.[1249] They are joined by Kho Phon Farrus as they prepare the Fermata Cage to defeat the Sith.[1250]
    • The Imperial Unity Day parade on Bar'leth is incinerated by a hit-and-run attack by the Rebellion—the event has been broadcast across the entire Empire.[1257]
    • The Imperial Security Bureau is alerted to an incident on Al'doleem, which Major Sharin, inspecting the second Death Star, intends to investigate.[1257]
    • At this point, Emperor Palpatine and Darth Vader are reported by the Rebellion to be aboard the Executor in the Mid Rim.[1258]
    • Death Star workers Bevelyn and Jon Melton, as Crimson Dawn agents, kill Major Sharin and take her shuttle in order to inform the Rebellion the existence of the new superweapon. The couple bring with them their children, Reen and Zelly, hail the Rebel Alliance for help,[1257] and flee to the Coruscant underworld. They are rescued by Luke Skywalker, and Alliance High Command learns of the new Death Star's construction.[1258]
    • Amilyn Holdo bring Leia Organa, Luke Skywalker, Lando Calrissian, Chewbacca, and Lobot to a resort on Spira for a vacation. They steal a Nihil Path engine from an auction and enter No-Space, in pursuit of much-needed fuel from the long-lost Kezarat Convoy.[267]
    • The other former handmaidens of Queen Amidala work alongside Ochi in order to retrieve—or stop—Sabé from Vader's service. However, when Sabé has herself captured by nationalist revolutionary leader Jul Tambor, Vader intercepts the handmaidens and attempts to turn them to his service too.[1259] Although he fails, the Sith Lord rescues Sabé, who chooses to join him and leave her sisterhood.[1260]
    • Sabé finds a diplomatic solution; she unveils to the Skakoan refugees Jul Tambor's true colors as a power-hungry individual willing to sacrifice his own people—he has ordered his forces to bomb a city despite being informed that his people were present there along with Vader, discrediting Tambor. His refugees are granted amnesty by the Empire, saving hundreds of lives.[1260]
    • Vukorah resigns from the Unbroken Clan, allowing IG-88 to eliminate its leaders and leaving unscathed herself.[1261]
    • Having been rescued by Ariole Yu and Just Lucky just prior to the Empire's attack, Starros' crew enter the service of Ronen Tagge, who had joined Crimson Dawn.[1256] Ronen's Crimson Dawn forces attempt to eliminate Domina Tagge but they are thwarted, with Starros' crew aiding Domina instead and thus clearing their debt to the Tagges.[1262]
    • The Empire finds and attacks Crimson Dawn's flagship. The Emperor speaks with Qi'ra, who loses the battle but orders the Fermata Cage to be activated, lying that she wishes to free an ancient Sith trapped by the artifact.[1250]
    • Threatened, Sidious and Vader pursue the Fermata Cage. The two Dark Lords arrive at the Amaxine Station aboard the Executor and they are attacked by Crimson Dawn's fleet.[1263]
      • Starros' crew and Kho Phon Farrus help Doctor Aphra expel the Spark Eternal from her body near the Fermata Cage. Starros successfully forces the "hopelessly emotionally constipated" Aphra and Magna Tolvan to have a real conversation, and the crew part ways afterward.[1264]
      • Vader joins Sidious before the Fermata Cage. Qi'ra activates it, beginning to trap the Dark Lords, but they are freed by the Knights of Ren.[1265]
      • Qi'ra acknowledges her failure and scatters the Dawn fleet. She leaves her remaining resources to Cadeliah, her protégé.[1265]
      • The Fermata Cage's activation causes a great disturbance in the Force, creating a Force Wave across the galaxy.[1266]
        • Vader loses control of his powers, and Sabé attempts to calm him down, taking him to an isolated world. However, while Vader demands her loyalty, Sabé refuses to give in to hate, and the Sith Lord casts her away in an ocean. The Emperor tasks his Empire to hunt for the rogue Vader.[1266]
        • Luke Skywalker is similarly affected, with his powers intermittently augmented uncontrollably or weakened. Nevertheless, with his help, the rebels recover much of the Kezarat Colony, who give their fuel to the Rebel Fleet, though some of its population decides to remain in No-Space and colonize the former Great Hall of the Nihil.[1267] Lando Calrissian and Amilyn Holdo had become romantically involved while in No-Space, and they reaffirm their commitment to the rebel cause.[1268]
    • Inferno Squad successfully wipes Valance's memories.[1269]
    • As the Alliance prepares for an aerial assault on Coruscant,[1270] Luke Skywalker travels to Christophsis to obtain a kyber crystal and build his own lightsaber along with R2-D2. He receives help from Gretta and Dr. Cuata, communicates with a Sith and Yoda, and leaves with a red and a green crystal.[1271]
    • Lando Calrissian, Chewbacca, Nien Nunb, Freyta Smyth, and Bryen Rowne battle Corusant's defenses and attack the Emperor's statue in Monument Plaza. The latter two rebels are lost, but the burning statue is broadcast on the HoloNet.[1270]
    • Vader acquires a shield of kyberite to focus his raw Force powers with Doctor Aphra's help on Ringo Vinda.[1272]
    • By Imperial mandate, ZED-6-7 leads a group of droids who had been wronged by Vader to hunt down the cyborg Sith. However, ZED helps Vader to defeat the droids and rebuild them to his allegiance, forming a droid army, and Aphra escapes, as does 0-0-0 and BT-1.[1273]
    • Aphra, Luke Skywalker, and R2-D2 find a Jedi temple on Sason[1274] and battle the Starweird.[1275]
    • T'onga reassembles her crew, wishing to save Valance's deteriorating mind. Boba Fett gives them coordinates to cyborg-fixer Tarr Kligson after they eliminate a rival Black Sun leader.[1269]
    • An ancient an powerful droid intelligence, that was one locked away in the Fermata Cage by the Sith, is released during the battle at Amaxine Station. It merges with the Spark Eternal, giving birth to the Scourge, an insatiable being wishing to spread and control other minds and bodies.[264]
    • Emperor Palpatine orders the Imperial forces at the Amaxine Station to retrieve all material and take them to Al'doleem, a moon in the Mid Rim Territories. The Scourge corrupts a KX-series security droid that had been deployed from the Star Destroyer Chelator as part of the recovery mission. Afterward, the Scourge spreads through the Destroyer from the security droid to astromech droids and protocol droids. The 2,518 droids are corrupted, catching the humans by surprise, and kill the 46,785 human crew members. The Scourge renames it the Scourge 01, that is gradually transformed into a monstrous vessel black in color and with many irregular spikes.[1276]
    • Ajax Sigma's Second Revelation droid movement attempts to destroy the Scourge.[1276]
    • Mon Mothma, Admiral Ackbar, General Madine, Princess Organa, Luke Skywalker, and Commodore Grek convene to discuss the mysterious second Death Star. Meanwhile, C-3PO succumbs to the Scourge, which wishes to control Force-wielders too.[1276]
    • Lando Calrissian and Lobot travel to the palace of Jabba the Hutt on Tatooine to retrieve a droid Lando had previously sold to Jabba, in order to save Lobot after his cybernetics begin failing and threaten to kill him. At the same time, the newly reassembled D-Squad make their way to Jabba's Palace to search for coordinates that can lead them to Ajax Sigma. Lando Calrissian escapes with the talky droid, Lobot is captured by Scourged droids and is also infected, and D-Squad learn Ajax Sigma is on Mechis III.[1277]
    • Forces of the Scoure attack Fortress Vader on Mustafar in order to inhabit Darth Vader for his knowledge of the Force. The attempt is ultimately a failure after Vader prevents himself from being infected, destroying multiple DT-series sentry droids, spider-droids and MSE-6 series repair droids.[1278]
    • Droids of the Second Revelation attack the Scourge 01 while the Scourge is attempting to corrupt Vader. During the battle, a number of the Second Revelation's droids are almost infected by the Scourge, but they destroy themselves to prevent their minds from falling into its hands. Ajax Sigma, the leader of the Revelation, is forced to escape having failed to destroy the Scourge.[1278]
    • Doctor Aphra and Just Lucky travel to a Tagge Corporation warehouse on Havel Prime to determine whether the prototype battle droids stored at the facility are experiencing a similar malfunction that another of the battle droids have suffered. As the two fight against the droids, which have been infected by the Scourge, they shut down, leading Aphra and Lucky to believe that they were running off of their backup power, explaining why they were able to operate for a short span of time before they powered down.[1279]
    • Aboard the Tagge Corporation research facility ship, the Casus Belli, employees of the corporation fight the ship's droid crew, which has been infected by the Scourge. Ultimately, Lady Domina Tagge deems the Casus Belli to be beyond saving and has it crashed onto the nearby moon Asheris, resulting in the destruction of the ship and all of the Scourged droids aboard.[1280]
    • Magna Tolvan leads a search for the missing Rebel Alliance supply-run ship Silver Arrow.[1280]
    • The Executor, flagship of Darth Vader, is targeted by droids corrupted by the Scourge in order to infect Vader himself. The droids, many of whom were part of the droid uprising recently reprogrammed to serve Vader's will, take over the vessel, compromising it, leading to Emperor Palpatine requesting, via Grand Vizier Mas Amedda, that Vader resecure it. During the battle, he is forced to fight the droids affected by the Scourge. Meanwhile, a hostile force of five Star Destroyers surround the Executor, which Amedda deems a lost cause and orders it destroyed to contain the Scourge. However, Admiral Corleque delays the order, giving Vader's own droids long enough to shut down the Executor's mechanical compliment. The Scourge seeds itself onto Corleque's flagship, the Unbreakable, which Admiral Firmus Piett destroys after giving his fellow admiral a mere four seconds to contain the infection.[1281]
    • On Mustafar, the Scourge corrupts an octuptarra tri-droid from an abandoned Techno Union droid factory. Having hijacked ZED-6-7, a droid servant of Vader, after the failed attempted to corrupt Vader aboard the Executor, the Scourge attacks Vader and his other droid servants with ZED and the octuptarra. Through the skirmish, Vader manages to re-establish his control over the Force, which he had struggled to command since the Force Wave returned. With his Force powers his to command once more, Vader destroys the corrupted octuptarra and ZED, who is later rebuilt.[1282]
    • R2-D2 reassembles D-Squad to combat the Scourge.[1283]
    • The Scourge attacks Coruscant, brought upon by Vader as part of a test for Emperor Palpatine. As part of his test, Vader collects a number of the Scourge's spider-droids, which it uses to infect droids and cyborgs. Arriving on Coruscant aboard his TIE Advanced x1 and speeding past an Imperial checkpoint looking for any sign of the Scourge, Vader is shot down but crashes into the Imperial Palace, where he is surrounded by a group of stormtroopers from a Lambda-class T-4a shuttle. Vader wipes out every trooper sent against him, including members of his own death trooper guard who were ordered to turn on Vader by the Emperor. The spider-droids escape Vader's TIE and infect the death trooper corpses as Vader struggles with Sidious. While Vader neck snapps each trooper and lifts them into the air to prevent their escape into the Lambda shuttle, he throws the spider-droids out into Imperial City, where the Scourge uses them to find and infect droids to massacre the populace. While Sidious strickes down several droids with his Force lightning, taking several civilians with them, the infected droids make it out into the city, where they quickly kill up to one hundred citizens. To protect his capital, Sidious orders Vizier Amedda to wipe out sector 35 and sector 36 under protocol 23. As laser weaponry levels both sectors, destroying any Scourged droids. Vader is allowed to leave Coruscant aboard the Lambda, where the Scourge uses a surviving spider-droid to tell Vader that, by bonding with it, he will finally be powerful enough to defeat Sidious. Vader willingly allows the Scourge to infect him but quickly separates his armor from the Scourge at large, protecting himself as he begins to create a Scourge infection of his own on Mirgoshirax.[1284]
    • Magna Tolvan is rescued from the Scourge on the galactic relay hub Epikonia by her former lovers Doctor Chelli Lona Aphra and Sana Starros, as well as Just Lucky. Chelli Aphra learns the Scourge plans to strike at the Tagge Corporation.[1285]
    • Tarr Kligson works with the Scourge to suppress Valance's consciousness. The Scourge abaondons Kligson and wishes to experiment with Valance, but T'onga and her crew attempt to kill their former friend.[1286]
    • A battle is fought in Tagge Space between the Tagge Corporation and the Scourge, who has infected the company's droids and cyborg employees. Lady Domina Tagge is rescued from the Scourge, and Doctor Aphra crashes the Acquisitor into Rocedila, destroying all Scourged droids.[1287]
    • Members of the Rebel Alliance battle the Scourge on Epikonia to rescue Lobot.[1288]
    • Ajax Sigma and the D-Squad carry out a second attack on the Scourge 01. Sigma destroys the Scourge.[1289]
    • Valance's memories are restored.[1290]
    • The Alliance to Restore the Republic holds a trial of Lando Calrissian in accordance with the Corellian code of military justice for treason, mutiny, desertion and insubordination against the Alliance.[1291]
    • Mon Mothma goes on a funding mission for the Alliance to the planet Uveron. Originally, Lando Calrissian was supposed to join Mothma on the mission, before being arrested.[1291]
    • Mon Mothma is kidnapped by Scarabi mercenaries while on Uveron. In a break in the negotiations, Mothma served as a magistrate for the Trial of Lando Calrissian via hologram. During the trial, the mercenaries, led by Hachi break into Uveron City and capture her. Keeping her hidden at Safe House Four, the Scarabi hold her at ransom for 200 million credits, and threaten to inform other third parties, such as the Empire, if the Rebel Alliance does not respond within a day.[1291]
    • The Alliance deploys the pathfinders to locate Mon Mothma. Unable to pay the price, a solution comes from Lando Calrissian, who knows the Scarabi leader, Hachi, from previous jobs and provides the Rebel Alliance with locations of various safehouses that can hold Mothma. With the help of Chewbacca, Calrissian is able to join the rebels on the rescue. The first safehouse is booby trapped with droidekas and yields no results for the Rebellion. The pathfinders move on to the second and third, and are finally able to locate Mothma at Safe House Four. Inside the safehouse, Mothma is attempting to convince Hachi on the principals of the Rebel Alliance and freedom. Hachi starts to believe it was a mistake to hold her captive, but she is killed by a fellow mercenary before she can act on it. Calrissian and Chewbacca then reach Mothma and free her from her captures.[1292]
    • Lando is found guilty of treason and insubordination and sentenced to a retributive fine of two hundred and fifty million credits, suspended until the end of the Galactic Civil War and subject to change depending on Lando's future conduct.[1293]
    • A crew of raiders working with the former Imperial Ellian Zahra attack the Survivor Fleet of Alderaanians who had escaped the destruction of their planet. The raiders destroy several of the fleet's ships and capture the vessel known as the Thranta before the rest of the fleet escapes. Zahra then uses the captured vessel to set up a trap for Leia Organa, which results in the Imperial's death.[1294]
    • Mission to Niraya.[1295]
    • Attack on a rebel base.[1296]
    • At Zee-Nine City Seven, the Alliance destroys the Imperial-class Star Destroyer Impervious and takes Imperial Lieutenant Enric Pryde prisoner.[1297]
    • Mission to Slandarvia M-9.[1298]
    • The Schism Imperial carries out an assault attempting to challenge Emperor Palpatine's power by attacking the Sith Citadel on Exegol, where he keeps his greatest treasures and secrets.[1299]
    • Skirmish on Caranthanax B.[1300]
    • Skirmish on Endoviara Seven.[1300]
  • 3–4 ABY (continued)
    • By this point, the 61st Mobile Infantry had conducted Operation Ringbreaker, a failed campaign against the Kuat Drive Yards formulated by the ex-Imperial Governor Everi Chalis. The operation involves a succession of attacks on weaker Imperial targets in order to force redeployments from the planet Kuat's security, weakening the shipyards for a planned final attack.[3]
      • Twilight Company attacks the Imperial warehouse world Mardona III, where they plant thousands of ion mines in tram rails, junctions, and warehouse entrances throughout the planet, forcing the Empire to shut down the whole system. It would take months for the Empire to clear them all.
      • The Sixty-First attacks the dockyards of the gas giant Najan-Rovi, which harbored Imperial luxury transports produced on Corellia for high ranking Imperial officers on the Imperial Ruling Council. The loss of these forces Corellia to produce more and detract supplies from Kuat.[3]
      • By ths point, the Obumubo Imperial garrison had been fighting Obumubo's sea creatures for months, and prove to be experienced when fighting the Sixty-First on the liquid-metal moon.[3]
      • The 61st fights in Nakadia, destroying the planet's plastoid factories where farm crops were made into armor-grade polymers and synthetic resins. During the fight, Rebel soldiers are exposed to the same bioweapons they encountered during the Coyerti campaign.[3]
      • Twilight Company fights in the icy mountains of the planet Naator, a contributor to the Empire's war effort.[3]
      • The 61st confronts Imperial forces in the canyons of Xagobah, sustaining some casualties but emerging victorious.[3]
      • 20 soldiers Twilight Company launch an attack on the Kuliquo belt, an Imperial mining asteroid field. The soldiers were personally selected by Captain Hazram Namir to sabotage machinery located in an airless and lightless death trap.[3]
      • Operation Ringbreaker ends with the Siege of Inyusu Tor on Sullust. The Inyusu Tor mineral processing facility is attacked and captured by the 61st, but they are sieged by the Empire's 97th Legion. The Rebels are victorious and a portion of the world is liberated from Imperial rule, but Operation Ringbreaker is called off following the destruction of the 61st' Thunderstrike.[3][1301]

Return of the Jedi (4 ABY)[]

  • 4 ABY
    • After eliminating a rebel cell in the aftermath of the Battle of Hoth,[1302] Squadron Five of Shakara Nuress's 204th Imperial Fighter Wing, also known as Shadow Wing, reclaim the Kudo system from the defecting Admiral Gratloe's private faction.
