    • Senator Lanever Villecham is elected Chancellor of the New Republic.[55]
    • Rapier Squadron of the New Republic responds to a distress signal from the Yissira Zyde, a freighter being hijacked by the First Order, and engages their forces above Suraz 4. However, the First Order successfully hijacks the Yissira Zyde.[1317]
    • Poe Dameron goes on an unsanctioned mission to find the captured Yissira Zyde. This brings him into contact with the Resistance leader General Leia Organa, who arranges for Dameron and Rapier Squadron to join the Resistance.[1317]
    • A Resistance team led by Captain Hoff, and including C-3PO, travels to the planet Taul aboard the Sphyrna-class Hammerhead corvette Sterdic Star. They obtain the location of Admiral Ackbar and transmit it to the Resistance.[1445]
    • Commander Poe Dameron along with the droids C-3PO and BB-8 rescue Admiral Gial Ackbar from the First Order Resurgent-class Star Destroyer Subjugator, where was being held hostage.[1445]
    • The New Republic obtains intelligence of the First Order, and Resistance sympathizers within the New Republic send pilot Kazuda Xiono and his squadron to rendezvous with Poe Dameron and deliver the data to the Resistance. They are ambushed by a First Order squadron led by Major Elrik Vonreg, and after defeating them with the help of Poe Dameron, Kazuda Xiono joins the Resistance.[1345]
    • Suspecting that the New Republic Senator Erudo Ro-Kiintor, with help from the Corporate Sector Authority, was secretly colluding with the First Order, Leia Organa dispatches Poe Dameron and his comrades Iolo Arana and Karé Kun on a mission to steal Ro-Kiintor's private yacht Hevurion Grace so that the Resistance can examine his ship's computers for incriminating information. Poe and his comrades manage to steal the Hevurion Grace and narrowly escape First Order forces. Senator Ro-Kiintor is captured and the Corporate Sector Authority dealings are exposed.[1317]
    • Poe Dameron and Black Squadron of the Resistance travel to the planet Ovanis to find Lor San Tekka to ask for his help in locating Luke Skywalker. During the course of the mission, Black Squadron comes into conflict with the First Order Agent Terex, who is after the same information. Following a dogfight and a skirmish against a large winged creature, Black Squadron manages to defeat Terex's forces with the help of the Crèche, a sect who worshiped a large egg known as the Crèche egg.[1446]
    • Poe Dameron and Black Squadron Black Squadron, in their search for Lor San Tekka, needed to question the Hutt crime lord Grakkus imprisoned in Megalox Beta Penitentiary, who had encountered Lor San Tekka previously. So they infiltrate the prision, but are confronted by Agent Terex. When Grakkus offers to reveal the information to whoever free him first, Poe gets BB-8 and the other astromech droids to disable the prison's gravity field, exposing Terex and the prisoners to the planet's high gravity. After rescuing Grakkus, the Resistance obtains more information about Lor San Tekka's whereabouts, but suspend the search to deal with a spy leaking information to the First Order.[1447]
  • 34 ABY
    • The junk boss Unkar Plutt is kidnapped by the Zendiat Collective in order to recover a J9 worker drone that was sold to him. The scavenger Rey tricks them, and is able to recover the junk boss and take him back to Niima Outpost, where Krynodd had taken over. For her efforts, Rey is awarded 30 portions.[1448]
    • The skirmish on Pheryon occurs.[1449]
    • Poe Dameron, C-3PO, BB-8 and the technician Oddy Muva travel to the planet Kaddak to obtain information that could lead to the First Order's Supreme Leader Snoke. They manage to rescue the BX-series droid commando N1-ZX from the Ranc Gang. The group is then pursued by Agent Terex, who had retaken control of the Ranc Gang. In an attempt to prevent Terex and the Ranc Gang from finding the Resistance base on D'Qar, Dameron and the droids flee to a desert planet.[1350]
    • Poe Dameron is followed by Terex and his Ranc Gang to the desert world. After Poe crash-landed his X-wing on the planet, a skirmish breaks out in the Fjor system between the Ranc Gang and the Resistance's Black Squadron. Terex's Ranc Gang is subsequently wiped out by First Order forces under Commander Malarus, who apprehend Terex for insubordination. During the battle, L'ulo L'ampar sacrifices himself to protect innocent lives.[1450]
    • The Resistance holds a funeral for L'ulo L'ampar at their base on D'Qar, conducted by Poe Dameron.[1451]
    • A mission to obtain fuel occurs.[1452]
    • Resistance members Karé Kun and Jessika Pava along with the journalist Suralinda Javos, travel to the planet Spalex where the First Order was attempting to mine the planet's thorilide, to get footage of First Order troops to use against the First Order and support the Resistance in the New Republic Senate. They are captured by First Order forces under Commander Malarus and Agent Terex. Oddy Muva lets them out of their cell and then sacrifices himself in a TIE fighter, destroying the light cruiser Enshado.[1453]
    • Leia Organa and Poe Dameron, along with Black Squadron and Suralinda Javos, travel to the planet Cato Neimoidia to rescue Lor San Tekka from a prison after he was arrested. However, he is taken prisoner by the First Order. Terex travels to the Absolution to meet with Captain Phasma.[1454]
    • Kylo Ren travels to the planet Pillio to learn where Lor San Tekka had taken the map to Luke Skywalker, and learns Tekka took the map to the planet Bayora. After Ren leaves Pillio, former Inferno Squad member Del Meeko is confronted by Gideon Hask, now a First Order officer, who mocks his former comrade before executing him at point-blank range. He orders Gleb and the Jinata Security to bury the bodies of Meeko and his crew, but to leave the Corvus alone in a bid to draw out Iden Versio. [1309]
    • Black Squadron rescues Lor San Tekka from the Great Forveen Nebula after he is dropped into the nebula by Terex, evading First Order jet troopers and TIE Fighters in the process.[1455]
    • The Resistance conducts a series of missions, after learning that the First Order is trying to take control of the Cassander sector by supplying weapons to the pirates in the sector.[1456]
      • The Resistance first target in the sector is to destroy a pirate controlled spaceport in the world of Mamkoda.[1456]
      • The Resistance conducts a relief mission to the planet Caraxl. The mission ultimately is aborted after Resistance pilot Tallissan Lintra spots First Order TIE fighters approaching, the Resistance trying to avoid problems with the New Republic Senate orders their pilots to avoid contact with First Order starships.[1456]
      • The bombers of Cobalt Squadron, supported by the A-Wings of Tallie Torchers, destroy the pirate base on Sheh Soahi. With the defeat of the pirates, the First Order plans in the Cassander sector are foiled.[1456]
    • Sent by Commander Dameron, Kazuda Xiono and CB-23 embark on a mission to spy on First Order activity at the Colossus refueling platform on Castilon, racing with local ace Torra Doza.[1345]
      • The pirate Kragan Gorr and his gang use a low-visibility storm, codenamed "triple dark", to launch an attack on the Colossus on behalf of their employers, the First Order. The Ace Squadron, the platform's defenders, are outnumbered by the pirates. Kazuda Xiono broadcast a grating feedback loop over the pirates' communications channel, preventing them from coordinating their attack, and allowing Ace Squadron to repell the pirate gang.[1457]
      • Baron Elrik Vonreg attempts to coerce the station's Captain Imanuel Doza to accept First Order protection from Kragan Gorr.[1458]
      • The First Order hunts two children, siblings Eila and Kel, who are the sole survivors of the massacre of their village on their homeworld Tehar, with the intent to kill them so they can not reveal the atrocity to anyone else. As part of their search, they placed a large bounty on the children. Kazuda Xiono heards of the bounty, and with assistance from Neeku Vozo and the Colossus Chelidae engineering staff, is able to save the children by faking their deaths in front of the First Order's Commander Pyre, who reports their deaths believing his mission was complete.[1459]
      • Poe Dameron and Kazuda Xiono investigate a derelict Darius G-class freighter broadcasting a distress signal, that had been attacked by members of Kragan Gorr's pirate gang, led by Synara San. The freighter was carrying a cargo of Kowakian monkey-lizards, and a giant Kowakian ape-lizard killed and ate most of the pirates, but San was able to survive by locking herself in a crate. She is discovered by Dameron and Xiono, who assume her to be a crewmember of the freighter, and rescure her, fighting off pirate ships coming in response to the distress signal's shuttle. When San wakes up Castilon, she claims to be a victim of the attack, and after contacting Gorr he orders her to blend in until he contacts her with an assignment.[1460]
      • The Warbird gang launches a raid on the platform, after Synara San informs Gorr that the Ace Squadron is off-platform and the targeting computer for the platform's defense turbolasers is down. The pirates are forced to retreat when Jarek Yeager and Kazuda Xiono fix the computer, allowing Captain Imanuel Doza to activate the defenses and repel the pirates. However, the damage from the attack forces Doza to reconsider the First Order's offer of protection for the platform, contacting Commander Pyre to ask about the details of the offer.[1461]
      • The Platform Classic, a major sky race, is held on the Colossus by Captain Imanuel Doza, who invites famous racer Marcus Speedstar to participate. Speedstar wins the race, defeating the pilots of Ace Squadron and Team Fireball.[1462]
      • Kazuda Xiono infiltrates Doza Tower trying and find out how Captain Imanuel Doza might be colluding with the First Order, assisted by Doza's daughter Torra, who had snuck out of the tower after her father had attempted to lock her in her room while he had a meeting with Commander Pyre. Xiono manages to obtain information about Doza's meeting with Pyre from Doza's office, discovering that the Captain was a former Imperial officer.[1463]
      • Kazuda Xiono and Poe Dameron travel to the Unknown Regions to investigate Station Theta Black, a space station utilized by the First Order to mine and process dedlanite to make blasters. They destroy the station, defeating Elrik Vonreg's squadron in the process.[1464]
      • The All Aces Battle Royale, a sky race hosted every ten cycles for ace pilots on Castilon, takes place on the Colossus, and many pilots, including Pamarthens, racers from Hosnian Prime, and the First Order operative Marnia Un'la'na are among the aces who travel to Castilon to participate. Under the pseudonym "Bolt-Shatterer-K," Kazuda Xiono flies in the race using a borrowed backup racer attempting to remain close to Un'la'na's ship to intercept a transmission she is sending to the First Order. After successfully capturing her data transfer using an encryption recorder, Xiono is knocked out of the race and crash-lands. He later takes the transmission captured and delivers it to the Resistance.[1465]
      • Bibo, a juvenile rokkna, is accidentally brought on board the Colossus inside of a wrecked Z-95 Headhunter salvaged by Synara San. Neeku Vozo decides to adopt him, unaware that Bibo's mother, a full-grown, enormous, adult of the species with the capability to destroy the platform, is heading to the Colossus intent on recovering her child. Eila and Kel, being force-sensitive, had dreamed of the attack, and warn Vozo and Tamara Ryvora the only way to save the platform is to give Bibo to the attacker. After retrieving her child, the mother peacefully leaves.[1466]
      • First Order operative Teroj Kee tries to steal a phase connector from Flix and Orka's shop, the Office of Acquisitions, on the Colossus. After luring the temporary shop keeper Kazuda Xiono into a cargo container, Kee steals the phase connector and attempts to escape aboard his Darius G-class freighter. However, Xiono manages to sabotage the ship's engine, causing it to crash into the sea.[1467]
      • Gorr's Warbird gang abducts Torra Doza, Captain Doza's daughter,[1468] forcing him to accept the First Order occupation of the Colossus.[1469]
    • Occupation of the Colossus.[1469]
      • After the First Order occupies the Colossus, Kazuda Xiono helps Synara San, a spy for the Warbird gang, escape from the station, before she can be captured, in the last of the platform's escape pods. San returns to the Warbirds while Xiono avoids being captured for his role in the escape.[1469]
      • Following an incident in which Kel and Eila knock out a First Order stormtrooper, Kazuda Xiono disguises as the stormtrooper and infltrates the First Order headquarters on the platform, in order to find out if he reported the incident to the First Order. There Xiono steals a data rod and finds out a full garrison of First Order troops is coming to the Colossus.[1470]
      • Poe Dameron and Kazuda Xiono travel to the Dassal system, where Xiono had identified possible First Order activities. They discover that the Dassal system's sun is missing, and all of its planets had been cored out. On a formerly inhabited moon of the only perfectly-cored planet, Dameron and Xiono discover an abandoned village, which had been the site of a massacre. They are spotted by a probe droid, and its destruction drew in Major Elrik Vonreg and his squadron of TIE fighters. Dameron and Xiono escape their pursuers by luring them into the gravity well in the center of the cored-out planet, destroying Vonreg's squadron. Xiono returns to the Colossus and Dameron departs on a mission to Jakku.[1471]
      • Kazuda Xiono and Torra Doza rescue several inhabitants of the Colossus that had been captured by the First Order. Hype Fazon and Aunt Z escape the platform on a stolen transport and head to Takodana, while Grevel and Nod escape custody.[1472]
      • Kazuda Xiono deactivates the First Order communication jammer on top of Doza Tower so he and his allies can contact the Resistance and ask for help fighting the First Order occupation of the Colossus. Kazuda's transmission reaches General Organa, who informs them the Resistance is unable to send reinforcements, so in turn he rallies the inhabitants of the Colossus into forming the Colossus resistance.[1473]
    • Tuanul, a spiritual village on the planet Jakku, is sacked by First Order forces led by Kylo Ren, who had been seeking the map to the location of Luke Skywalker. As four First Order squadrons descend on Tuanul with a force of eighty stormtroopers, Poe Dameron receives the map from Lor San Tekka. Dameron attempts to flee prior to the assault but his T-70 X-wing starfighter is disabled by the attacking stormtroopers, despite BB-8's warning just before the assault began. Dameron has BB-8 escape the village with the map. Subsequently, Tekka is killed by Kylo Ren, Dameron captured, and the villagers killed.[1474]
    • Poe Dameron is locked up on the Resurgent-class Star Destroyer Finalizer by Kylo Ren. There, he is tortured and interrogated by the latter for information about what he did with the map to Luke Skywalker, but he reveals no information.
    • Stormtrooper FN-2187, who had decided to desert the First Order, helps Poe Dameron escape. They steal a TIE fighter, and evade stormtroopers in the Destroyer's hangar and its turbolasers to escape to Jakku. While piloting the TIE, FN-2187 is rechristened as Finn by Dameron. Their ship is shot down by missiles, and the blast knocks Dameron unconscious, while Finn ejects from the TIE shortly before it crashes on the planet's surface. Dameron recovers consciousness just in time to set the craft down more or less intact, and finds himself alone and sets off to find a ship and contact the Resistance.[1474]
    • The scavenger Rey, the former First Order stormtrooper Finn, and the Resistance astromech droid BB-8 escape from Jakku aboard the retrieved Millennium Falcon, previously stolen by Unkar Plutt, to evade the First Order.[1474]
    • Aboard their freighter, the Eravana, Han Solo and Chewbacca manage to recover the Millennium Falcon, in which Finn and Rey had escaped from Jakku. Parties of the Guavian Death Gang and Kanjiklub board the freighter to collect debts owed to them by Solo. While Han tries to talk the boarding parties down, Rey accidentally release a trio of rathtars from the cargo hold. The rathtars storm through the freighter, killing several gangsters and giving Han, Chewbacca, and the fugitives a chance to escape on board the Falcon. Launching away from the freighter at lightspeed, they manage to escape the freighter and kill a rathtar that had attached itself over the cockpit.[1474]
    • Shortly after the death of Del Meeko, Shriv Suurgav finds the Corvus abandoned on Pillio with the crew and Del gone, and contacts Iden Versio, who makes her way to the Corvus with her daughter Zay. The three begin their search for Del, which leads them to the planet Athulla, where they find by a Jinata Security fleet commanded by Leema Kai kidnapping Athullans. Iden and Zay manage to disable the fleet and eliminate Kai, with the J-Sec fleet surrendering and telling them about Project Resurrection.[1309]

First Order-Resistance War (34 – 35 ABY)[]

  • 34 ABY (continued)
    • In the Hosnian Cataclysm, the Hosnian system is destroyed by the First Order, marking the annihilation of Courtsilius, Raysho, Hosnian, Cardota, and Hosnian Prime, the capital of the New Republic, by Starkiller Base, a superweapon on Ilum inside the Unknown Regions that is capable of destroying entire star systems. The massive sub-hyperspace ripple created by the destruction of the system makes the event visible in real time around the galaxy, including the planets of Takodana and Vardos. With the destruction of Hosnian Prime comes the destruction of the Galactic Senate and the Home Fleet of the New Republic Defense Fleet as the conflict between the First Order and the Resistance escalates into full open warfare. With the New Republic political structure decimated and its most important fleet in ruins, the First Order and its armed forces establishes itself as the primary power in the galaxy as both the Resistance and the weakened New Republic are left vulnerable.[1474]
    • The Cosmic Force is reawakened as individuals, such as Rey and Finn experience an awakening in their connection to the Force.[55]
    • Iden Versio, Zay Versio and Shriv Suurgav travel to the world of Vardos in search of Iden Versio's former mentor Protectorate Gleb. However, in Kestro City they discover Gleb's body shot by Gideon Hask, who tells them he had personally executed Del Meeko before taking them into custody. Hask orders the Resurgent-class Star Destroyer Retribution to shoot down the Corvus and the First Order troopers to burn the city. A battle erupts between Inferno Squad, Jinata Security forces, and the First Order. Shriv and the Versios escape the battle into the Retribution in TIE/sf space superiority fighters.[1309]
    • The Colossus resistance led by Kazuda Xiono, Jarek Yeager, and Captain Doza expel First Order forces from the Colossus and take the station into hyperspace. Tamara Ryvora, a mechanic who had been working for Yeager, joins the First Order.[1475]
    • Finn, Han Solo, Chewbacca, and Rey, seeking to return the map to the Resistance, stop at the castle of Maz Kanata on Takodana. There both a Resistance spy and a First Order informant identify BB-8 and inform their respective allies, drawing both forces to Takodana to recover BB-8 and the map.[1474]
    • Maz Kanata's castle is attacked by Kylo Ren. Flying in with TIE/fo space superiority fighters and Atmospheric Assault Landers, the First Order sieges the castle and levels it, and Ren corners and captures Rey. During the fighting at the castle, Finn, Han Solo and Chewbacca are forced to surrender until Resistance X-wings led by Poe Dameron arrive, and a dogfight ensued resulting in the destruction of many First Order TIEs and the deaths of many stormtroopers from X-wing strafing runs. Kylo Ren orders the First Order divison to pull out, saying they had what they came for, and BB-8 ends up in the hands of the Resistance.[1474]
    • The Battle of Starkiller Base, the first major battle in the First Order-Resistance War, is fought. Resistance T-70 X-wing starfighters of Blue Squadron and Red Squadron, led by Poe Dameron attack Starkiller Base with the goal of disable and destroy the superweapon in order to prevent the First Order, which is charging its main weapon, from destroying the Resistance base on D'Qar. Starkiller Base is destroyed, and the First Order sustains mayor casualties and is significantly weakened, despite both Kylo Ren and General Armitage Hux surviving the battle. Rey escapes custody with the help of Han Solo and Finn, and discovers she is Force-sensitive. Kylo Ren murders his father, Han Solo, and is defeated by Rey in a duel.[1474]
    • After learning the fate of Del Meeko on Vardos, Iden Versio leads Zay Versio and Shriv Suurgav aboard Gideon Hask's Resurgent-class Star Destroyer, the Retribution, to retrieve intelligence on the First Order and eliminate Hask for Meeko's death. The skirmish that follows results in the deaths of Versio and Hask, as well as the successful retrieval of First Order intelligence and specifications on the Mandator IV-class Siege Dreadnought.[1309]
    • Captain Phasma, the First Order TIE fighter pilot TN-3465, and the BB unit BB-K8 to hunt down Lieutenant Sol Rivas for his alleged "treason" against the First Order. In truth, Phasma initiates the operation since he was the only one who knew that Phasma had betrayed the First Order to save herself by lowering the shields of Starkiller Base. Following the Battle of Starkiller Base, Sol Rivas flees to the uncharted planet of Luprora where he was captured by the R'ora, a species indigenous to the planet. Captain Phasma, TN-3465 and BB-K8 followe Rivas on a TIE/sf space superiority fighter. Over the next four days, Phasma enlists the help of the humanoid Lupr'or in attacking the R'ora stronghold. Using generators salvaged from an abandoned Lupr'or starship, Phasma and her Lupr'or army manage to electrocute the Tsw'ells in the lake guarding the R'ora stronghold. While the Lupr'or fight the R'ora, Phasma executes Rivas. Before departing Luprora, Phasma destroys BB-K8 and kills TN-3465 in order to eliminate anyone who knew of her true role in Starkiller Base's destruction. Phasma then fabricates a report claiming that she had eliminated Rivas for his alleged treachery and that her comrades had perished in the process.[1476]
    • With the exposure of their base on D'Qar, the Resistance begins evacuating, and in the early hours of the evacuation, Leia Organa holds a funeral for her fallen husband Han Solo. The escape was interrupted by the arrival of a First Order fleet consisting of three Resurgent-class Star Destroyers and one Mandator IV-class Siege Dreadnought, the Fulminatrix. To buy time for the Resistance transports to escape, Poe Dameron attacks the dreadnought, eliminating its surface cannons and clearing the way for a squadron of Resistance bombers to attack. The resultant assault obliterates the Fulminatrix, but all bombers are lost along with four of their escorting starfighters. However, Dameron's attack buys enough time for the Resistance fleet to jump to hyperspace.[1437]
    • General Leia Organa sends Inferno Squad, Black Squadron and Resistance operative Vi Moradi on separate missions to the Outer Rim Territories to find allies of the Resistance and gather any surviving New Republic commanders.[1309][1477]
      • Members of Black Squadron are unintentionally involved in a local battle on the world Pastoria. Visiting the planet in the hopes of persuading King Siroc into supporting the Resistance, the Squadron, led by Temmin Wexley, is instead tricked into assisting with the death of the king's primary rival. Black Squadron fails to persuade King Siroc to ally with the Resistance against the First Order.[1477]
      • Vi Moradi travels to Batuu with the objective of forming a Resistance cell and establishing a base on the planet.[1478]
      • Shriv Suurgav and Zay Versio recruit Carlist Rieekan and some of Iden Versio's old Imperial friends who did not join the First Order into the Resistance.[1418]
    • With the First Order in need of refueling stations to supply their of conquest the galaxy, Commander Pyre and Agent Tierny aboard the Star Destroyer Thunderer hunt for the Colossus, which roams the galaxy for a safe haven. In order to supply the Colossus, its crew steal coaxium from the wreckage over D'Qar.[1479]
    • Rey and Chewbacca find Luke Skywalker on Ahch-To, a planet in the Unknown Regions. Skywalker briefly trains her before she departs to try to redeem Kylo Ren.[1437]
    • The First Order fleet, having deployed their recently developed means of tracking a ship through hyperspace, ambushes the fleeing Resistance fleet upon entering the Oetchi system. Comprising thirty Resurgent-class vessels and the Supreme Leader's flagship, the Mega-class Star Dreadnought Supremacy, the First Order fleet destroys the Vakbeor-class cargo frigate Vigil before it can activate its deflector shields.[1437]
    • Before the Resistance pilots can scramble their fighters, the Raddus hangar is destroyed by a torpedo fired from Kylo Ren's TIE/vn space superiority fighter. Several pilots die in the attack, including Tallissan Lintra, and Dameron narrowly survives. Ren has an opportunity to destroy the bridge of the command ship but is unable to open fire on his mother. However, a member of Kylo Ren's personal squadron, Lieutenant Poldin LeHuse hits the bridge. The attack obliterates the Resistance High Command, killing Admiral Gial Ackbar and other Resistance officers with the exception of Organa, who calls upon the Force to survive the vacuum of space and return to the Raddus.[1437]
    • Vice Admiral Amilyn Holdo takes charge of the Resistance after the incapacitated Leia Organa. Shut out from the Vice Admiral's plans, Poe Dameron devises his own plan to save the fleet with the help of Resistance technician Rose Tico and Finn. Using the information Finn acquired from his past service aboard the Supremacy, the trio intends to disable the hyperspace tracker so the Resistance can jump to lightspeed without being tracked. Needing a Master Codebreaker, Maz Kanata points them to the casino city of Canto Bight on Cantonica.[1437]
    • Finn, Tico, and BB-8 arrive to the Canto Casino, where they then find the Master Codebreaker, but before they can approach him are arrested by the Canto Bight Police Department for landing in an illegal area. In prison they meet a man named DJ, a codebreaker, who manages to unlock the prison cells. Finn and Tico escape through the sewers into the fathier stables. They release the fathiers and ride them out, trampling over the police and escaping in the Yacht Libertine, piloted by DJ and BB-8 after they steal it.[1437]
    • Rey is captured by the First Order whilst trying to redeem Kylo Ren, and brought before Supreme Leader Snoke aboard his flagship, the Supremacy. A wavering Ren betrays and murders Snoke, then attempts to forge a new order for the galaxy with himself and Rey as its new leaders. However, she declines and escapes after a fight in the throne room.[1437]
      • Rey's lightsaber is destroyed during the fight.[1437]
    • The Resistance fleet is effectively wiped out in the Crait system while fleeing the First Order fleet, but are able to score a victory of their own as Vice Admiral Amilyn Holdo launches a suicide attack which heavily damages the Supremacy and destroys many Star Destroyers.[1437]
    • Battle of Crait. Resistance forces that escaped to the surface of Crait are pursued by remaining First Order forces in the system. The two factions engage briefly, before the remaining Resistance members escape aboard the Millennium Falcon.[1437]
    • Luke Skywalker dies with peace and purpose on Ahch-To, becoming one with the Force in a sacrifice that inspires hope for the rebel fighters, who have Rey amongst them to ensure that the spark of rebellion would not die, and that Skywalker with his legendary bloodline would not be the last Jedi.[1437]
    • Hunt for the Colossus
      • Following the flight of the Colossus from the planet Castilon, the platform begins to run low on food and other basic supplies, and Aunt Z suggest to capture a giant jakoosk from the moon of Celsor 3 for meat, enough for the Colossus' population. Captain Kragan Gorr, along Kazuda Xiono and Torra Doza travel to the moon to hunt the jakoosk, but the creature destroys several pirate airspeeders and they are forced to retreat. A second expedition with Kazuda Xiono, Torra Doza and Tamara Ryvora manages to shoot the creature in its unprotected underside, killing it.[1480]
      • The First Order sends the Nikto engineer Nenavakasa Nalor to sabotage the Colossus. Posing as a stranded spacer, Nena manages to gain the trust of the Colossus crew and attempts to draw a wedge between the station inhabitants and the Warbird gang. Synara San and Kazuda Xiono discover Nena as a First Order spy, and although she escapes, Kaz and Neeku manage to undo her sabotage and escape the First Order forces.[1481]
      • After Nalor's sabotage, the platform is short on fuel, so members of Team Colossus travel to Flix's homeworld of Drahgor III to obtain fuel from members of Flix's family. They descend into their fuel refinery and face three 3 Karnex Dragons to retrieve the fuel.[1482]
      • Team Colossus attempts to resupply from Ashas Ree, but barely escape First Order Raiders in search of the world's Sith relic with the help of archaeologist Mika Grey.[1483]
      • On Torra Doza's birthday, the Colossus attempts to meet Torra's mother, Venisa, who was serving the Resistance, but the station is chased away by the First Order, and Venisa Doza is captured aboard the Thunderer. She escapes after encountering cadets Tamara Ryvora—whom she advises about the failings of the First Order—and Jace Rucklin. Ace Squadron celebrates Torra's birthday.[1415]
      • With the Colossus in need for credits, Ace Squadron takes part in the Voxx Vortex 5000, a sky race held in the Voxx Cluster, against the racing droids owned by the Hutt casino owner Vranki the Blue. Kazuda Xiono and Torra Doza win their relay race, gaining 500,000 credits for the Colossus.[1484]
      • The Colossus station fights against forces of the Guavian Death Gang in an asteroid field at the edge of Guavian-controlled space. The space station had passed through the asteroid field in an attempt to exit Guavian space unnoticed by the criminal organization, but after being spotted by a scout, the station is detained by Guavian forces, who demand they pay a toll of 1 million credits or have the station seized and its population turned over to the First Order. Imanuel Doza refuses the Guavian leader's demands, and Ace Squadron engage and is able to destroy most of the Guavian starships as the Colossus reaches open space, allowing the station to escape.[1485]
      • Xiono and his best friend and engineer, Neeku Vozo, and CB-23 infiltrate the Titan to secure a trans-binary deflector necessary to prevent cosmic radiation from killing all those aboard the Colossus, disrupting a meeting between General Hux, Commander Pyre, and other officers. Unbeknownst to Xiono, Tamara Ryvora secretly helps him escape.[1486]
      • Team Colossus rescues Resistance agent Norath Kev on Varkana, with them all narrowly escaping First Order capture.[1487]
      • Kragan Gorr's Warbird gang stages a mutiny on the Colossus for their own gain, using B2 battle droids and a B1 repaired by Neeku Vozo; however, Vozo had reprogrammed the droids, with Xiono and Synara San helping to foil the mutiny and expel the pirates.[1488]
      • A First Order Resurgent-class Star Destroyer attacks and destroys an Aeosian village on Aeos Prime as part of their campaign to deny the Resistance access to former Rebel Alliance bases, leading to the abandonment of the village and several wounded Aeosians.[1489]
      • With Captain Doza hopeful that Aeos Prime would provide a suitable home for the Colossus, Ace Squadron scouts the surrounding ocean but are attacked by hostile Aeosians. Thanks in part to Xiono, their queen calls off the attack and makes peace with Doza, negotiating the Colossus' stay. Xiono remains restless and does not wish to stay in their new home, and wishes to continue fighting the First Order.[1489]
      • Xiono decides to leave and rejoin Norath Kev and the Resistance near Batuu. However, he encounters a First Order probe droid over Aeos Prime, and the Thunderer promptly attacks the Colossus. It flees with the help of the Aeosians.[1490]
      • The First Order blockades the planet Dantooine, cuting off all communication and stranding Resistance recruits. Resistance's Jade Squadron led by Venisa Doza, and members of Ace Squadron including Torra Doza and Kazuda Xiono, extract shuttles carrying the recruits through the blockade and successfully escort them to the Colossus. During the fight, one of the shuttles is destroyed, and First Order Lieutenant Galek is killed, along with most of his TIE fighter squadron.[1491]
      • A First Order fleet, led by Commander Pyre and Agent Tierny, bombards a section of Aeos Prime in retaliation for assisting the Colossus. Tamara Ryvora, horrified by the massacre, decides to defect from the First Order.[1492]
      • Jarek Yeager and Kazuda Xiono arrange to rendezvous with Tamara Ryvora on the planet Castilon, who travels there under the guise of leading a training mission. However, First Order Security Bureau Agent Tierny discovers Ryvora's defection and dispatches the Star Destroyer Thunderer after them. Despite being captured by the First Order and exposing the Colossus to First Order pursuers, the team manages to sabotage the Star Destroyer's shield generator and escape the warship.[1492]
      • Kazuda Xiono and Tamara Ryvora contact the Colossus, warning them that the First Order is coming for them. When the Thunderer arrives, Commander Pyre deploys several TIE squadrons against the space station, but the Colossus residents were ready. Commander Venisa Doza leads Jade Squadron and Ace Squadron against the Thunderer, bombarding its engines and eventually destroying the ship, and with it Commander Pyre and all it's crew, ending the Hunt for the Colossus. Agent Tierny is executed by Supreme Leader Kylo Ren for her failure.[1492]
    • After First Order declares to the inhabitants of the planet Ikkrukk that they now fall under their control, Black Squadron and eventually its commander Poe Dameron, arriving shortly after the Battle of Crait, fight against the invasion and Ikkrukk First Order sympathizers at the Battle of Grail City. The city's defenses are able to destroy the attacking Maxima-A class heavy cruiser Fortitude and Grail City is saved, but multiple cultural centres within the city are destroyed by the First Order sympathizers.[1493] Prime Minister Grist alleges there is too much damage to Grail City to afford supporting the Resistance and that such aid would only give the First Order more cause to return.[1418]
    • The Resistance's Falcon, Zay Versio and Shriv Suurgav, and Black Squadron reunite. They are joined by rebel veterans Carlist Rieekan, Orrimaarko, Phantom Squadron, Wedge Antilles, and Norra Wexley, and they shelter at Yendor's Ryloth Defense Authority cave base. They launch a series of missions to start rebuilding the Resistance.[1418]
      • A mission to Corellia completed by two separate teams; Poe Dameron leads Corellian Team One in securing a list of people targeted by the First Order and being sold at a secret auction; and Wedge Antilles leads Corellian Team Two in rescuing former New Republic Senator Ransolm Casterfo from First Order enslavement.[1418]
      • Dross Squadron, led by Shriv Suurgav, travels to the junkyard planet of Bracca to take much-needed starships. There they are helped by a group of workers from the Scrapper Guild who are resisting the First Orber's occupation of the planet, and are able to obtain multiple X-wing starfighters before fleeing the planet.[1418]
    • The First Order launches an attack after discovering the Resistance is set up in the Ryloth Defense Authority cave base. In the fighting, at least 1 pilot and Yendor's daughter Hahnee Brethen are killed, and Rey leads the defense against the First Order forces while the Resistance evacuates the planet.[1418]
    • After fleeing Ryloth, the Resistance move to a Collective safe house[1418] on Helmaxa,[21] beginning to recruit more people to the cause.[1418] Having received supplies from Tah'Nuhna, the Resistance then relocates to Anoat.[1494]
    • Rebuilding his command, Kylo Ren discovers the First Order's reserve forces under the leadership of Allegiant General Enric Pryde, who is given a seat on the Supreme Council.[50]
    • First Order forces, led by General Armitage Hux aboard the Star Destroyer Finalizer, annihilate the planet Tah'Nuhna for assisting the Resistance. TIE bombers destroy the capital city and the government tower.[1494]
    • The pilots of Black Squadron, led by Poe Dameron, secure munitions that the New Republic had impounded from pirates and stored in Base 354-23X on the moon of Avedot. To retrieve them they are forced to fight the criminals of the Kendoh Gang, led by Kendoh Voss, and lose three of their members.[1494]
    • Leia Organa leads a mission to Mon Cala, securing their people's support and Aftab Ackbar's ships just as the First Order invades the waterworld.[1494]
    • Blockade of Kashyyyk; the Resistance, among them Chewbacca and Beaumont Kin, battles First Order jet troopers subjugating the Wookiee world.[1495]
    • Poe Dameron and Finn travel to the planet Tevel, to liberate the Tevel bacta production plant from the First Order and it's Tevellan forced workers. Allied with Tevellan conspirators led by Karkan, the Resistance defeats the First Order forces led by Major Kander Varak and retake the plant for the Tevellans.[1496]
    • First Order forces led by Commander Branwayne Spiftz go on a mission to the planet Minfar to obtain the Echo Horn, a weapon created in the Imperial Era by the scientist Glenna Kip as a way to control the Zixon, the native species of Minfar, for the Galactic Empire. Resistance members led by Poe Dameron, and including Rey and Rose Tico travel to the planet, where they ally themselves with Glenna Kip and the Zixon to drive away the First Order from Minfar. In the fight, Commander Spiftz is killed and a First Order Star Destroyed elliminated by Resistance bombers.[1497]
    • Director Orlok from the First Order Security Bureau, serving on the planet Garel, develops and deploys a new technology allowing him to control the entire planet's communications. Likana, a slicer from Garel, contacts the Resistance alerting them and asking for help, so Leia Organa and Poe Dameron go on a mission to Garel to stop Orlok and sabotage his technology.[1498]
    • Poe Dameron, Finn, and Gannis Ducain travel to the outpost of Horizon Base in order to recruit the Oruan resistance group who had fled there after the First Order took their homeworld, Oru. Mezlo, the leader of the Oruans, is hesitant to join the Resistance, but eventually promises to provide assistance should they ever ask. After a few brief encounters with First Order trooper|stormtroopers on the station, the Resistance crew escapes in the Millennium Falcon.[1499]
    • Leia Organa deploys Poe Dameron and Finn to the planet Kamil to secure livestock for the Resistance. They get a lead on the Octani Dhar Wat who is selling spare parts, ditch the livestock and track down the train Wat is on. He reveals the First Order had taken his parts and moved them to another car. Dameron and Finn set out to locate the parts but quickly run into First Order officers. A chase ensues throughout the train before they are able to knock out the First Order forces, Finn locates the spare parts and unlocks the car from the rest of the train. They also find livestock on their car, which they return to the Resistance.[1500]
    • Strono Tuggs, having been forced to leave Maz Kanata's castle after the Battle of Takodana, sets up shop at Batuu's Black Spire Outpost with a Sienar-Chall Utilipede Transport and publishes his cookbook The Official Black Spire Outpost Cookbook.[1501][1502]
    • Four months after the Hosnian Cataclysm, Resistance spy Vi Moradi and former stormtrooper Archex journey to Batuu and establish a Resistance base at the galaxy's edge. Their presence draws the attention of the First Order.[1502][1503]
    • Battle of Batuu[59][1503]
      • Six months after the Hosnian Cataclysm,[1502] the First Order's 709th Legion occupies Black Spire Outpost. Kylo Ren leads the search for Resistance fugitives.[1504]
      • The Resistance and the First Order compete over Batuu.[1502][1505]
      • The Finalizer is crippled in battle above Batuu and is retired from service. Half a year after the Starkiller Incident, Kylo Ren transfers his command to Allegiant General Pryde's flagship, the Steadfast.[50]
    • At some point, Kylo Ren rummages through the galaxy using the Steadfast, haunted by his defeat by Luke Skywalker's phantom at the Battle of Crait. He orders the ruins of Skywalker's Jedi Temple to be bombarded.[1506]
  • 35 ABY
    • The voice of the former Galactic Emperor, Sheev Palpatine, threatens revenge via a broadcast transmitted throughout the galaxy.[1507][1508]
    • Determined to eliminate any threat to his power, Kylo Ren travels to Mustafar for a calling Force-imbued Sith wayfinder. Ren travels to the co-ordinate pre-defined within the wayfinder—the planet Exegol—and discovers a resurgent Darth Sidious. Initially intending to bring an end to the dark power he found behind Snoke, Ren hears Sidious reveal that the voices of Snoke and Darth Vader in his head were that of his doing. Sidious then unravels the Final Order, a massive fleet of Xyston-class Star Destroyers built by the Sith Eternal, trying to persuade Ren to join forces and kill Rey, although he actually intended to make her his successor as she was his granddaughter.[1507]
    • The Mission to Sinta Glacier Colony occurs; the First Order Supreme Council recognizes a hidden spy within a their ranks.[1507]
    • The First Order tracks down Rey, Finn, Poe Dameron, BB-8, C-3PO, and Chewbacca to Pasaana during the its once-every-forty-two-years Festival of the Ancestors. While the five managed to evade the First Order thanks to Lando Calrissian, Chewbacca gets captured by the Order and presumed dead by his friends.[1507]
    • Rey, Finn, and Poe Dameron travel to the planet Kijimi to hack C-3PO's memory in order to obtain the location of the Emperor's wayfinder. During the mission, they team also learn that Chewbacca is alive, and mount a rescue on the Steadfast, the Star Destroyer of Kylo Ren, when it arrives above the planet. Armitage Hux reveals himself to be the spy who has been helping the Resistance and lets them escape, with Rey discovering that Darth Sidious is her grandfather thanks to Kylo Ren. However, Allegiant General Enric Pryde sees through Hux's deception and executes him, taking control of the First Order military.[1507]
    • Rey, Chewbacca, Poe Dameron and Finn undertake a mission to the Kef Bir moon in the Endor system. Having acquired the Blade of Ochi of Bestoon at Kijimi, they discover the location of the Emperor's wayfinder, and determined to find it, Rey enters the ruins of the Second Death Star and finds the wayfinder in the Emperor's Throne Room. However, she was followed to Kef Bir by Kylo Ren, who destroys the wayfinder in the hope of pushing Rey to the dark side of the Force. A lightsaber duel ensues between them, and during the fight Ren's focus wavers as he senses the death of his mother across the galaxy, allowing Rey to pierce him with his own lightsaber. Realizing that anger had driven her actions, Rey uses the Force to heal Ren before departing from Kef Bir, seeking exile on Ahch-To. The Force dyad between them, along with the loss of Ren's mother and the memory of his father, ultimately restore Ben Solo to the light side of the Force.[1507]
    • Rey, in fear after learning of her true lineage and from her tendencies of falling to the dark side of the force, takes Ren's starfighter and attempts to exile herself on Ahch-To, following the footsteps of Luke Skywalker, but is persuaded by the latter to face her destiny and confront her grandfather. Bestowed the lightsaber of Leia Organa and Skywalker's submerged X-wing starfighter, Rey uses the wayfinder aboard the late Kylo Ren's ship to uncover the path to Exegol, throneworld of the Darth Sidious' Sith Eternal, his personal Sith cult.[1507]
    • Allegiant General Enric Pryde assumes control of the First Order and pledges himself to Darth Sidious.[1507]
    • Kijimi, a world affiliated with the Resistance, is destroyed by the axial superlaser of the Xyston-class Star Destroyer Derriphan, at the behest of Pryde in a demonstration of loyalty to Sidious.[1507]
    • The Battle of Exegol occurs.[1507]
      • The Resistance confronts the Sith Eternal army and the Sith fleet. In face of apparent defeat, Lando Calrissian leads a Citizens' Fleet of allied starships gathered from across the galaxy against the Sith Eternal forces.[1507]
      • Ben Solo arrives at the Sith Citadel to assist Rey, battling through defending Knights of Ren into the throne room.[1507]
      • Darth Sidious fights against Rey and Ben Solo before the Throne of the Sith. Rey dies destroying Sidious, the last of the Sith.[1507]
      • Ben Solo sacrifices himself to revive Rey by giving her what remains of his life Force. Solo becomes one with the Force together with his mother, Leia Skywalker Organa Solo.[1507]
      • The allied forces win the battle against the remaining Sith forces.[1507]
    • The Resistance and their allies hold celebrations[1507] and mourn their losses on Ajan Kloss.[1506]
    • An uprising against the First Order takes place across the galaxy.[1507]
      • Uprising on Cloud City.[1507]
      • Uprising on Corellia.[1509]
      • Uprising on Coruscant.[1509]
      • Uprising on Endor.[1507]
      • Uprising on Jakku.[1507]
      • Uprising on Lothal.[1509]
      • Uprising on Naboo.[1509]
      • Uprising on Thyferra.[1509]

New Jedi Order (35 ABY–)[]

  • 35 ABY (continued)
    • Rey finalizes the construction of a yellow-bladed lightsaber—her new personal weapon.[1329]
    • Rey and BB-8 arrive at Tatooine with the Millennium Falcon. Rey buries the Skywalker twin's lightsabers by the Lars Homestead and, with the blessing of Luke Skywalker and Leia Skywalker Organa Solo, recognizes herself as a Skywalker.[1507]
    • With the destruction of the Sith, a new era is ushered in, to be shaped by the Force in ways as yet unknown.[1510]
    • Some months after the Battle of Exegol, Beaumont Kin leads an excavation team to uncover and explore the ancient hidden secrets of the Sith Citadel, the Sith Order, and Palpatine.[873]
  • Rey Skywalker's new blade would go on to honor the Skywalker legacy,[1509] and she works to restore peace in the galaxy as a Jedi Master.[59]
  • 50 ABY[1511]
    • Rey Skywalker builds a new Jedi Order.[1512]

Thereafter[]

  • A long time after the Battle of Yavin (exact chronology undefined)
    • Fey Zara operates a cantina in a distant corner of the galaxy which hosts a series of holotables upon which patrons may play games based on pivotal conflicts of galactic history.[1513]
    • An artist named Gammit Chond[1514] creates maps based on tales of the galaxy, and his works end up in the Shadow Stacks of the Graf Archive. Later, the drawings are exhumed by a trainee archivist and published by the archive[679] as the Galactic Atlas.[1514] An updated version of the Galactic Atlas is later published when more of Chond's notes and drawings are discovered by Tollan Magzax.[1515]
    • An archival task force led by archive droid TR-33NA scours the Graf Archive in a restoration effort for artifacts of historical importance. Within the library, they find an old illustrated journal of galactic species and cultures. TR-33NA identifies the drawing style to be similar to that of Gammit Chond.[1516]
    • A Whill is asked by other Whills to chronicle events that transpired in the galaxy into the Journal of the Whills. After a lifetime of preparation, the Whill begins writing an entry titled "Episode IV" for an audience far away from the galaxy.[1]
    • A dissatisfied Whill instead writes about how Chewbacca's family celebrated Life Day in another journal[1] while the former Whill goes on to write how the Empire strikes back in the next episode.[1517] Next, that Whill goes on to write the episode about the return of the Jedi and how it marked the beginning of the Empire's fall. As the writing process begins, the two Whills find common ground in their appreciation for the Ewoks and agree to come together to write the story of their caravan of courage, with the writing Whill also permitting the previously dissatisfied Whill the chance to write the episode about the blockade of Naboo.[1518]

Behind the scenes[]

"[W]e're exploring a lot of different storylines. This really spawns from what George Lucas said years ago, that he created Star Wars to move forward and backward along a mythological timeline."
―Lucasfilm president Kathleen Kennedy[1519]

Star Wars creator George Lucas had intended the fictional universe to be a mythology, with stories taking place throughout a timeline.[1519] Since 2014, Lucasfilm has been creatively aligning all storytelling in the current canon, aiming to keep all content consistent and interconnected in all aspects. Some content draw inspiration from the Expanded Universe that is now branded as a separate continuity—Star Wars Legends[1520]—which has its own timeline of galactic history on Wookieepedia, an unaffiliated online Star Wars encyclopedia maintained by volunteers.

Lucasfilm's Star Wars eras for the current canon continuity comprise of "Dawn of the Jedi," "The Old Republic," "The High Republic," "Fall of the Jedi," "Reign of the Empire," "Age of Rebellion," "The New Republic," "Rise of the First Order," and "New Jedi Order."[1521]

  21. ↑ 21.000 21.001 21.002 21.003 21.004 21.005 21.006 21.007 21.008 21.009 21.010 21.011 21.012 21.013 21.014 21.015 21.016 21.017 21.018 21.019 21.020 21.021 21.022 21.023 21.024 21.025 21.026 21.027 21.028 21.029 21.030 21.031 21.032 21.033 21.034 21.035 21.036 21.037 21.038 21.039 21.040 21.041 21.042 21.043 21.044 21.045 21.046 21.047 21.048 21.049 21.050 21.051 21.052 21.053 21.054 21.055 21.056 21.057 21.058 21.059 21.060 21.061 21.062 21.063 21.064 21.065 21.066 21.067 21.068 21.069 21.070 21.071 21.072 21.073 21.074 21.075 21.076 21.077 21.078 21.079 21.080 21.081 21.082 21.083 21.084 21.085 21.086 21.087 21.088 21.089 21.090 21.091 21.092 21.093 21.094 21.095 21.096 21.097 21.098 21.099 21.100 21.101 21.102 21.103 21.104 21.105 21.106 21.107 21.108 21.109 21.110 21.111 21.112 21.113 21.114 21.115 21.116 21.117 21.118 21.119 21.120 21.121 21.122 21.123 21.124 21.125 21.126 21.127 21.128 21.129 21.130 21.131 21.132 21.133 21.134 21.135 21.136 21.137 21.138 21.139 21.140 21.141 21.142 21.143 21.144 21.145 21.146 21.147 21.148 21.149 21.150 21.151 21.152 21.153 21.154 21.155 21.156 21.157 21.158 21.159 21.160 21.161 21.162 21.163 21.164 21.165 21.166 21.167 21.168 21.169 21.170 21.171 21.172 21.173 21.174 21.175 21.176 21.177 21.178 21.179 21.180 21.181 21.182 21.183 21.184 21.185 21.186 Star Wars: Timelines
  22. ↑ 22.0 22.1 22.2 22.3 Star Wars: The Last Jedi: The Visual Dictionary
  23. ↑ 23.0 23.1 Thrawn Ascendancy: Chaos Rising states that the Galactic Republic fought against the Sith Empire. As the Empire was destroyed by the time of the modern Republic's founding per Star Wars Propaganda: A History of Persuasive Art in the Galaxy, and the Republic that existed prior to the Sith's fall is referred to as the Old Republic by Star Wars Propaganda, it can be found that the Old Republic was the Galactic Republic, only that the government of the time before the Sith's fall came to be commonly known as the Old Republic.
  24. ↑ 24.00 24.01 24.02 24.03 24.04 24.05 24.06 24.07 24.08 24.09 24.10 24.11 24.12 24.13 24.14 The High Republic: The Rising Storm

  41. ↑ According to Unlimited Power, the Blood Monarchy was founded 20,000 years prior to the founding of the Galactic Republic. As the Republic was founded at the end of the Jedi-Sith War, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 1032 BBY. As such, the Blood Court's founding occurred around 21,032 BBY.
  42. ↑ 42.0 42.1 42.2 42.3 Doctor Aphra (2016) 2
  43. ↑ 43.0 43.1 Doctor Aphra (2016) 4
  44. ↑ Doctor Aphra (2016) 6
  45. ↑ Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 58 Guide to the Galaxy: Visiting Rodia
  46. ↑ 46.0 46.1 Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 48 Guide to the Galaxy: Visiting Ord Mantell
  47. ↑ According to Nexus of Power, which is set in the Imperial Era, the Jedi credited Ossus as having been their cultural center for a 12,000 year period. Since the Imperial Era began in 19 BBY and ended in 5 ABY per Star Wars: Timelines, Ossus must have come to be the Jedi's cultural center around 12,000 BBY.
  48. ↑ According to Nexus of Power, the Qel-Droma Epics dates the devastation of Ossus to 4000 years before the Imperial Era, which began in 19 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. Since the Epics also say that Ossus' devastation took place one thousand years after the Great Hyperspace War, the war occurred around 5000 BBY.
  49. ↑ According to Nexus of Power, the Qel-Droma Epics dates the devastation of Ossus to 4000 years before the Imperial Era, which began in 19 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. Since the Epics also say that the Great Jedi Library on Ossus stood for nearly a millennium, the Library was established around 5000 BBY.
  50. ↑ 50.00 50.01 50.02 50.03 50.04 50.05 50.06 50.07 50.08 50.09 50.10 50.11 50.12 50.13 50.14 50.15 50.16 50.17 50.18 50.19 50.20 50.21 50.22 50.23 50.24 Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary
  51. ↑ The Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states the original Rammahgon was thought to have been destroyed over 5000 years at the time of the Starkiller Incident, which is dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Timelines. Thus, the original Rammahgon was thought to have been destroyed at around 5000 BBY.
  52. ↑ Star Wars: Journey Through Space
  53. ↑ Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 23 Guide to the Galaxy: Yavin 4's Massassi Temple
  54. ↑ 54.00 54.01 54.02 54.03 54.04 54.05 54.06 54.07 54.08 54.09 54.10 54.11 54.12 54.13 54.14 54.15 54.16 54.17 Thrawn Ascendancy: Lesser Evil

  57. ↑ 4,000 years before the Starkiller Incident equates to around 3966 BBY, since the Incident is dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Timelines.
  58. ↑ 58.0 58.1 58.2 58.3 58.4 58.5 58.6 58.7 Ultimate Star Wars, New Edition
  59. ↑ 59.00 59.01 59.02 59.03 59.04 59.05 59.06 59.07 59.08 59.09 59.10 59.11 59.12 59.13 59.14 59.15 59.16 59.17 59.18 59.19 The Star Wars Book

  63. ↑ 63.0 63.1 63.2 63.3 Star Wars Rebels: The Visual Guide
  64. ↑ Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 47 Guide to the Galaxy: Visiting Taanab
  65. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that the citizens of Begamor had lived in peace for thousands of years, the time of peace must have began by around 2200 BBY.
  66. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that the Land & Sky Corporation, which ruled over the Aakaash system, had legitimately ruled for several millennia, their reign must have been began by 2200 BBY.
  67. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that Fire had existed for two thousand years, Fire must have been constructed around 2200 BBY.
  68. ↑ 68.00 68.01 68.02 68.03 68.04 68.05 68.06 68.07 68.08 68.09 68.10 The Vow of Silver Dawn
  69. ↑ Since according to Star Wars: Timelines, the time of the Galactic Empire began in 19 BBY, thousands of years before the Empire's time is equivalent to over 2019 BBY.
  70. ↑ Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary, New Edition states that the Klorri-clan battle shield dated to two thousand years before the Clone Wars. Since Star Wars: Timelines places the war between 22 and 19 BBY, the shield must have been introduced between 2022 and 2019 BBY.
  71. ↑ 71.0 71.1 Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary, New Edition
  72. ↑ Doctor Aphra 2 takes place soon after the Battle of Yavin, the end of which Star Wars: Timelines establishes to be the beginning of 0 ABY. Since Doctor Aphra (2016) 1 takes place immediately prior to Doctor Aphra 2, the events of Doctor Aphra 1 must have taken place in or shortly after 0 ABY. Since the comic issue states that farmers had lived on Boothi XII for thousands of years, Boothi XII's settlement by the farmers must have begun by 2000 BBY.
  73. ↑ 73.0 73.1 73.2 Doctor Aphra (2016) 1
  74. ↑ In Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Overlords", it is said that the Jedi code sent from Mortis has not been used for "over 2,000 years." As the Clone Wars is dated to around 20 BBY by Star Wars: Timelines, the Jedi code received by Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Ahsoka Tano during the war was last used around 2000 BBY.
  75. ↑ Battlefront: Twilight Company states that the Arakein Monks were active "almost two thousand standard years ago" at the time of the novel's events set twenty-two years after the Clone Wars, which ended in 19 BBY, according to Star Wars: Timelines. Thus, the section of the novel that mentions the Monks is set in 3 ABY, meaning that the Monks were active around 2000 BBY.
  76. ↑ 76.00 76.01 76.02 76.03 76.04 76.05 76.06 76.07 76.08 76.09 76.10 76.11 76.12 76.13 76.14 Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel
  77. ↑ Darth Vader (2017) 22

  95. ↑ Star Wars Helmet Collection: Boba Fett Databank A-Z: Alzoc III–Antilles
  96. ↑ 96.00 96.01 96.02 96.03 96.04 96.05 96.06 96.07 96.08 96.09 96.10 96.11 96.12 96.13 96.14 96.15 96.16 96.17 96.18 Thrawn Ascendancy: Chaos Rising
  97. ↑ 97.0 97.1 97.2 97.3 The High Republic: A Test of Courage
  98. ↑ 98.0 98.1 Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order to 14 BBY. Since Fallen Order states that the Jedi Temple on Ilum was constructed "a thousand years ago," the temple must have been constructed around 1014 BBY.
  99. ↑ Doctor Aphra (2016) 33
  100. ↑ According to Tarkin, the Galactic Senate was founded during or after the Jedi-Sith War, which ended in 1032 BBY according to Star Wars: Timelines.
  101. ↑ 101.00 101.01 101.02 101.03 101.04 101.05 101.06 101.07 101.08 101.09 101.10 101.11 101.12 101.13 101.14 101.15 101.16 101.17 101.18 101.19 101.20 101.21 101.22 101.23 101.24 101.25 Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones
  102. ↑ Nexus of Power, set during the Imperial Era that began in 19 BBY as dated by Star Wars: Timelines, states that the Jedi Temple had served as the Jedi Order's operational and spiritual headquarters for just over a thousand years, and that the temple's spire had existed for a thousand years. As such, the Jedi Temple began serving as the operational and spiritual headquarters of the Jedi Order in approximately 1032 BBY, and the Temple Spire was built at around that time.
  103. ↑ Star Wars: Galactic Atlas establishes that the mission to Utapau occurred in 19 BBY. As "The Big Bang" states that the Yavin Convention occurred approximately 1,000 years prior, also took place in 19 BBY. Thus, the Convention took place around 1019 BBY.
  104. ↑ Nexus of Power states that the Bardottan Royal Palace was a thousand years old at the time of the Imperial Era, which is dated to have begun in 19 BBY by Star Wars: Timelines. As such, the palace was built around 1000 BBY.
  105. ↑ Since Star Wars: Timelines places Leia Organa's birth in 19 BBY, and the events of Leia, Princess of Alderaan commence just after Organa's sixteenth birthday, it can be deduced that Leia, Princess of Alderaan is set in 3 BBY. As the novel states that the Royal Palace of Alderaan was "the work of more than a millennium," the palace must have begun construction around 1000 BBY.
  106. ↑ 106.0 106.1 106.2 106.3 106.4 106.5 106.6 106.7 Leia, Princess of Alderaan
  107. ↑ Star Wars: Han Solo, set shortly after the Battle of Yavin, states that what would be remembered as the most unpredictable and thrilling instance of the Dragon Void race until the race involving Han Solo occurred a millennia ago. Per the dating system used in Star Wars: Timelines, the race occurred around 1000 BBY.
  108. ↑ It is stated in Aftermath: Empire's End, which takes place one year after the Battle of Endor and thus in the year 5 ABY since the latter battle is dated by Star Wars: Timelines to 4 ABY, that the Anchorite sect of Jakku bound itself to the Jedi Order a thousand years before. Thus, those servants of the light side of the Force that believed in suffering being the basis of life bound itself to the Jedi around 1000 BBY.
  109. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of War of the Bounty Hunters 1 to 3 ABY. As Marlo stated to be nearly 1000 years old within the issue, he must have been born around 997 BBY.
  110. ↑ According to Poe Dameron: Free Fall, Kijimi City was constructed a thousand years prior. As Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Free Fall to 18 ABY, the city must have been constructed around 982 BBY. Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that Kijimi City was initially named Kijimiko and built around the Dai Bendu Monastery.
  111. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary dates Maz Kanata birth to 1007 years before the the Hosnian Cataclysm. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the the Hosnian Cataclysm to 34 ABY, the event must have taken place in 973 BBY.
  112. ↑ According to Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary, the Dai Bendu Monastery was constructed on Kijimi a thousand years prior to the planet's destruction depicted in Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker. As events of The Rise of Skywalker took place a year after the Starkiller Incident, as established by The Visual Dictionary, and because Star Wars: Galactic Atlas states that the Incident occurred in 34 ABY, the monastery was constructed around the time of 965 BBY.
  113. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. The novel states that the Sky Three starship engine model was created 700 years ago, which means first made in 900 BBY.
  114. ↑ Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 49 Starship Fact File: Acclamator-class Assault Ship
  115. ↑ Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide establishes that the Capitol was constructed 700 years before the events of the The Bad Batch Season 1, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 19 BBY.
  116. ↑ The High Republic: Path of Deceit—which Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates to 382 BBY—establishes that Thelj was settled nearly three centuries earlier. Therefore, it must've happened around 682 BBY.
  117. ↑ The segment of "A Closed Fist Has No Claws" depicting a Jedi's death onboard the Gaze Electric takes place around 379 BBY per the reasoning here. Marda Ro met the Faers after the Jedi's death; therefore, she must have met them no earlier than 379 BBY. The starship being three centuries old is established during their encounter, meaning it must have been created around 679 BBY.
  118. ↑ Star Wars Helmet Collection: Boba Fett Databank A-Z: Alzoc III–Antilles
  119. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Convergence to 382 BBY. Convergence states that the Greatest Age was 200 years ago during the events of the book, therefore it must have been the time around 582 BBY.
  120. ↑ The High Republic: A Test of Courage states that Leric Schmireland was sent on a mission to catalogue planets around three hundred years before the events of the novel. As A Test of Courage takes place concurrently with The High Republic: Light of the Jedi, it must be set in the same year, 232 BBY, and therefore Schmireland's mission must have begun circa 532 BBY.
  121. ↑  The Citadel in the Databank (backup link) says that the Citadel was built "five hundred years before the Clone Wars," which started with the First Battle of Geonosis, dated to 22 BBY by Star Wars: Timelines. Thus, the Citadel was constructed around 522 BBY.
  122. ↑ Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 75 Guide to the Galaxy: Lola Sayu and the Citadel states that it was five centuries before the Battle of Lola Sayu since the last Jedi was imprisoned within the Citadel. As the Battle of Lola Sayu takes place in 1 BFE according to Star Wars: Battles that Changed the Galaxy, which corresponds to 20 BBY as established by Star Wars: The Rebel Files, it can be deduced that it had been since approximately 520 BBY since the last Jedi was held in the Citadel.
  123. ↑ 123.0 123.1 Star Wars: Complete Locations states that the Kallidahin surveys of Polis Massa had begun for 500 years by the time of Luke Skywalker's birth, fifty years before which the discovery of Wiyentaah occurred. Since Star Wars: Timelines dates Skywalker's birth to 19 BBY, the Kallidahin surveys began in 519 BBY, and the discovery of Wiyentaah took place in 69 BBY.
  124. ↑ The prologue of Lost Stars, which mentions the first settlers to Jelucan arrived "nearly five hundred years before," takes place when Thane Kyrell and Ciena Ree are both eight years old. The two are established in the same novel to have been born the year the Galactic Empire was founded, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 19 BBY. Thus, the prologue is set in 11 BBY, setting Jelucan's colonization around 511 BBY.
  125. ↑ According to Star Wars Character Encyclopedia: Updated and Expanded, Yaddle was 477 years old at the time of the invasion of Naboo. Since Star Wars: Timelines dates the invasion to 32 BBY, Yaddle was born around 509 BBY.
  126. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines (bonus content)
  127. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Convergence to 382 BBY. Path of Deceit states that the Path of the Open Hand was founded over a century prior, meaning it must have been founded by 483 BBY.
  128. ↑ 128.0 128.1 The Art of Star Wars: The High Republic: Volume II establishes the flashbacks of The High Republic Adventures: Quest of the Jedi take place a hundred years before the events of The High Republic Phase II, which Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates to 382 BBY.
  129. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic (2022) 1 to 382 BBY. The High Republic (2022) 1 states that Mac has served the Jedi Order for fifty-one years and was around four years old when he joined the Order, meaning he was around fifty-five years old. Therefore, he must have been born on Kiffex around 437 BBY.
  130. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic (2022) states Vildar Mac is four years old at the attack on his village, placing it at 433 BBY if he is born at 437 BBY.
  131. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The High Republic: The Rising Storm to 231 BBY. Pan Eyta is stated to be around 200 years old and to hail from the planet Dowut, meaning he must have been born there around 431 BBY.
  132. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The High Republic: Mission to Disaster to 230 BBY. The Galactic Republic survey of the Maawat Mountains is stated to have been conducted over two centuries prior, meaning it must have happened by around 430 BBY.
  133. ↑ The section of The High Republic: The Eye of Darkness where Bell Zettifar reflects on Hintians takes place shortly after the one year anniversary of the destruction of Starlight Beacon, which equates to 229 BBY per Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia. Per the former work, Hintis's death began two hundred years earlier and therefore around 429 BBY.
  134. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The High Republic Adventures (2022) 8 to 382 BBY. As The High Republic Adventures (2022) 8 establishes Kaktorf was forty-six years old, he must have been born around 428 BBY.
  135. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Convergence to 382 BBY. As Convergence states that Creighton Sun is roughly forty years old, he must have been born around 422 BBY.
  136. ↑ The High Republic: Convergence establishes that Axel Greylark was eighteen years old thirteen years prior to its events. As Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of Convergence to 382 BBY, he must have been born] in 413 BBY.
  137. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Convergence to 382 BBY. Gella Nattai is thirty years old in The High Republic: Convergence, dating her birth to approximately 412 BBY.
  138. ↑ 138.0 138.1 The present day events of Star Wars: The High Republic — The Blade take place between the wedding of Phan-tu Zenn and Xiri A'lbaran and Porter Engle earning his title the "Blade of Bardotta," both of which are dated to 382 BBY by Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia. Therefore, the flashback sequence featuring a young Engle in the first issue, taking place twenty-five years prior, must have occurred around 407 BBY.
  139. ↑ 139.0 139.1 The High Republic — The Blade 1
  140. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Convergence to 382 BBY. As Phan-tu Zenn is established to be around twenty-five years old during the novel's events, he must have been born around 407 BBY.
  141. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Quest for the Hidden City to 382 BBY. Quest for the Hidden City establishes Dietrix Jago is twenty years old and dating her birth to approximately 402 BBY.
  142. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Convergence to 382 BBY. As Convergence states that Enya Keen is twenty years old, she must have been born around 402 BBY.
  143. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Quest for Planet X to 382 BBY. The novel states that Fel Ix is nineteen years old during its events, meaning that he must have been born around 401 BBY.
  144. ↑ Thomas Toov says he has "only lived 400 years" in Doctor Aphra (2016) 12, which is set between issues 7 and 40 of the Star Wars: Doctor Aphra (2016) comic-book series. The events of issue 7 take place after the Battle of Yavin, which marks the beginning of 0 ABY, according to Star Wars: Timelines, while the rebel base on Hoth is still intact during the events of issue 40. Given that Timelines dates the destruction of the base to 3 ABY, Doctor Aphra (2016) 12 must take place between 0 ABY and 3 ABY, and Toov was born around 400 BBY.
  145. ↑ The High Republic — The Blade 2
  146. ↑ The present day events of Star Wars: The High Republic — The Blade take place between the wedding of Phan-tu Zenn and Xiri A'lbaran and Porter Engle earning his title the "Blade of Bardotta," both of which are dated to 382 BBY by Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia. The flashback in the second issue takes place "seventeen years earlier"—in 399 BBY.
  147. ↑ Rooper Nitani is established to be fourteen years old in The High Republic: Quest for the Hidden City, while The High Republic: Quest for Planet X is set shortly after her fifteenth birthday. As Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates both novels to 382 BBY, she must have been born in 397 BBY.
  148. ↑ The High Republic Adventures (2022) 1 establishes that Sav Malagán is fifteen years old during Star Wars: The High Republic Adventures, which Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of to 382 BBY. Therefore, Malagán was born around 397 BBY.
  149. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Quest for Planet X to 382 BBY. As Quest for Planet X establishes Sky Graf was fifteen years old during its events, they must have bee born around 397 BBY.
  150. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Convergence to 382 BBY. As Convergence establishes that Phan-tu Zenn's biological mother died on Eiram fifteen years before its events, she must have passed away around 397 BBY.
  151. ↑ The High Republic: Convergence establishes that Axel Greylark was eighteen years old thirteen years prior to its events. As Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of Convergence to 382 BBY, he must have been born in 413 BBY. Greylark is seventeen years old in "The Queen's Bloom," dating its events to around 396 BBY.
  152. ↑ "The Queen's Bloom" — The High Republic: Tales of Light and Life
  153. ↑ 153.00 153.01 153.02 153.03 153.04 153.05 153.06 153.07 153.08 153.09 153.10 153.11 153.12 The High Republic: Convergence
  154. ↑ The High Republic: Convergence establishes that Axel Greylark was eighteen years old thirteen years prior to its events. Axel was also eighteen years old during the mission, so it must have also taken place thirteen years before its events Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of Convergence to 382 BBY, which means the mission took place around 395 BBY.
  155. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Quest for the Hidden City to 382 BBY. Dass Leffbruk is twelve years old in Quest for the Hidden City, placing his birth in approximately 394 BBY. His mother is stated to have died when he was a baby, dating her death to around the same year.
  156. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Path of Deceit to 382 BBY. As Path of Deceit establishes that Jerid is eleven years old during its events, he must have been born around 393 BBY.
  157. ↑ "Shield of the Jedi" establishes that Rooper Nitani is around eighteen years old during its events. During the events of The High Republic: Quest for Planet X, which Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates to 382 BBY, Nitani is fifteen years old. Therefore, "Shield of the Jedi" must take place around 379 BBY. "Shield of the Jedi" also establishes that Nitani became a Padawan ten years before its events, meaning she became a Padawan around 389 BBY.
  158. ↑ The High Republic (2022) 3 takes place after Vildar Mac's arrival on Jedha and before the Battle of Jedha. As Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates both events to 382 BBY, The High Republic (2022) 3 must take place in that year. Additionally, the issue states that the flashback takes place five years before the present event, placing it in 387 BBY.
  159. ↑ The High Republic: Path of Deceit—which Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates to 382 BBY—establishes Kevmo Zink has been the Padawan of Zallah Macri for almost two years. Therefore, he must have began his apprenticeship around 384 BBY.
  160. ↑ 160.0 160.1 160.2 160.3 160.4 160.5 160.6 Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Path of Deceit to 382 BBY.
  161. ↑ 161.0 161.1 The High Republic: Quest for the Hidden City
  162. ↑ 162.0 162.1 The High Republic — The Blade 4
