  164. ↑ The High Republic Adventures (2022) 2
  165. ↑ The High Republic Adventures (2022) 3
  166. ↑ The High Republic (2022) 1
  167. ↑ The High Republic (2022) 2
  168. ↑ 168.0 168.1 168.2 168.3 The High Republic: The Battle of Jedha
  169. ↑ The High Republic (2022) 3
  170. ↑ The High Republic (2022) 4
  171. ↑ The High Republic (2022) 5
  172. ↑ 172.0 172.1 172.2 172.3 172.4 172.5 172.6 The High Republic: Path of Vengeance
  173. ↑ The High Republic Adventures (2022) 6
  174. ↑ 174.0 174.1 174.2 174.3 174.4 174.5 The High Republic: Cataclysm
  175. ↑ The High Republic Adventures (2022) 8
  176. ↑ The High Republic: Quest for Planet X
  177. ↑ The High Republic Adventures – The Nameless Terror 1
  178. ↑ The High Republic Adventures – The Nameless Terror 4
  179. ↑ Per The High Republic: Quest for Planet X, the Path is dissolved in the immediate aftermath of the Night of Sorrow, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 382 BBY.
  180. ↑ Per The High Republic: Path of Vengeance, Marda Ro creates her new organization in the immediate aftermath of the Night of Sorrow, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 382 BBY.
  181. ↑ 181.0 181.1 The High Republic: The Edge of Balance: Precedent
  182. ↑ 182.00 182.01 182.02 182.03 182.04 182.05 182.06 182.07 182.08 182.09 The High Republic – Shadows of Starlight 1
  183. ↑ "A Closed Fist Has No Claws" establishes that Marda Ro visited Dantooine around a year after taking possession of the Gaze Electric, an event that Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates to 382 BBY. Therefore, Ro's trip to Dantooine must have occurred around 381 BBY.
  184. ↑ 184.0 184.1 "A Closed Fist Has No Claws" — The High Republic: Tales of Light and Life
  185. ↑ 185.0 185.1 185.2 185.3 185.4 185.5 185.6 185.7 The High Republic: Mission to Disaster
  186. ↑ "A Closed Fist Has No Claws" establishes that the section depicting the Jedi's death aboard the Gaze Electric takes place when Marda Ro is roughly eighteen years old. The High Republic: Path of Deceit, which Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of to 382 BBY, establishes both that members of the Path of the Open Hand receive full membership at the age of fifteen and that Ro had recently become a full member, meaning that she is around fifteen years old. Therefore, the section of "A Closed Fist Has No Claws" depicting the Jedi's death must take place around 379 BBY.
  187. ↑ 187.0 187.1 "Shield of the Jedi" — The High Republic: Tales of Light and Life
  188. ↑ "Shield of the Jedi" establishes that Rooper Nitani is around eighteen years old during its events. During the events of The High Republic: Quest for Planet X, which Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates to 382 BBY, Nitani is fifteen years old. Therefore, "Shield of the Jedi" must take place around 379 BBY.
  189. ↑ 189.0 189.1 "Tales of Enlightenment: Missing Pieces" — Star Wars Insider: The High Republic: Tales of Enlightenment
  190. ↑  RETURN TO THE HIGH REPUBLIC AS GEORGE MANN AND PARTHA PRATIM EXPLORE "PATHFINDERS" on Dark Horse Comics' official website (backup link)
  191. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic: Chronicles of the Jedi establishes that the cold war began 100 years prior to the Eiram–E'ronoh crisis, which took place in 257 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines
  192. ↑ The flashbacks of Thrawn Ascendancy: Lesser Evil take place before the main events of Thrawn Ascendancy: Chaos Rising, which take place in 19 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. As Lesser Evil's flashbacks establish that the battle took place "three hundred twenty years ago," it must have taken place in 339 BBY.
  193. ↑ 193.0 193.1 193.2 193.3 193.4 Queen's Peril
  194. ↑ The events of Queen's Peril overlap with that of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 32 BBY. Queen's Peril states that the Jafan family had ruled the eponymous planet for over 300 years after leading a group of people to colonize the Chommell sector world, which corresponds to the colonization taking place around 332 BBY.
  195. ↑ 195.0 195.1 By the time of the events of The High Republic: Light of the Jedi, the mining industry of Elphrona is over a century old. Since  Star Wars: The High Republic | Announcement Trailer on the official Star Wars YouTube channel (backup link) (Posted on StarWars.com) states that the events of Star Wars: The High Republic, which include the events of Light of the Jedi, took place 200 years prior to the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 32 BBY, Elphrona's mining industry must have been established by 332 BBY.
  196. ↑ 196.0 196.1 196.2 196.3 The Rise of Kylo Ren 2
  197. ↑ According to Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary, the Aionomica forgery scandal took place 300 years before the fall of the Jedi Order, which took place in Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith, the events of which is dated to 19 BBY by Star Wars: Timelines. Hence, the scandal occurred around 319 BBY.
  198. ↑ Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary, New Edition states that the Techno Union came to own Mustafar over three hundred years before the end of the Clone Wars, which is dated to 19 BBY by Star Wars: Timelines. Thus, the Union came to own the planet around 319 BBY.
  199. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines places the main events of Thrawn Ascendancy: Lesser Evil in 18 BBY. As the creation of the modern Chiss Syndicure is stated to have occurred around 300 years earlier, it must have occurred around 318 BBY.
  200. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. The book states that the disappearance of the group of Gotal took place 100 years prior, thus the event took place in 300 BBY.
  201. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The High Republic: The Fallen Star to 230 BBY. In The Fallen Star, Thandeka is described as being around fifty years old, meaning she must have been born around 280 BBY.
  202. ↑ The section of The High Republic: The Eye of Darkness where Bell Zettifar reflects on Hintians takes place shortly after the one year anniversary of the destruction of Starlight Beacon, which equates to 229 BBY per Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia. Per the former work, the evacuation took place around fifty years earlier and therefore around 279 BBY.
  203. ↑ The High Republic: Seeds of Starlight states Orlis' age during the present day events of the audiobook to be 50 standard years. As those events occur in 228 BBY per the reasoning here, he must have been born around 278 BBY.
  204. ↑ The Star Wars Book establishes that events of the novel Bloodline occur 28 years after those of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to the year 0 ABY; therefore, Bloodline takes place in 28 ABY. Given that Bloodline says Pamarthe had not faced invaders for about three hundred years, the last time Pamarthe was faced with invaders until the novel's events must have been around 272 BBY.
  205. ↑ Lourna Dee is stated to be roughly 40 years old in The High Republic: Light of the Jedi, which is set in 232 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. Therefore, she must have been born around 272 BBY.
  206. ↑ The High Republic: Tempest Runner establishes that the insurrection on Aaloth took place between Lourna Dee's birth and the attack on the Zygerrian camp. The High Republic: Light of the Jedi, which Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of to 232 BBY, establishes that Dee is around forty years, therefore placing her birth around 272 BBY. Timelines also dates the attack on the Zygerrian camp to around 255 BBY.
  207. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The High Republic: The Fallen Star to 230 BBY. The High Republic: Midnight Horizon is set concurrently to The Fallen Star. The incident with "Corellia for Corellians," at which time the song was forty years old, occurred prior to the events of Midnight Horizon, meaning the song must have been released by around 270 BBY.
  208. ↑  Star Wars: The High Republic Show: High Republic Phase II Deep Dive, Star Wars Celebration, and More! on the official Star Wars YouTube channel (backup link) (Posted on StarWars.com) dates the Attack on the Zygerrian camp to around 255 BBY. Because the The High Republic: Tempest Runner script states that Dal Azim is fifteen at the time of the attack, he must have been born around 270 BBY.
  209. ↑ During the Halcyon's 275th anniversary cruise in Star Wars: Galactic Starcruiser, Gaya tells guests that the Hayananeya had been missing for 300 years, which corresponds to 266 BBY since Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Galactic Starcruiser to 34 ABY.
  210. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker to 35 ABY. As Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that Faddaff Davenspon was three hundred years old during the film, Davenspon must have been born around 265 BBY.
  211. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The High Republic: Light of the Jedi to 232 BBY. As Light of the Jedi establishes Avar Kriss to be around thirty years old, she must have been born around 262 BBY.
  212. ↑ 212.0 212.1 212.2 212.3 212.4 The High Republic: Tears of the Nameless
  213. ↑ 213.0 213.1 213.2 213.3 213.4 213.5 213.6 The High Republic: Into the Dark
  214. ↑  Star Wars: The High Republic | Announcement Trailer on the official Star Wars YouTube channel (backup link) (Posted on StarWars.com) states that the events of Star Wars: The High Republic, which include the events of The High Republic: Into the Dark, take place two-hundred years before the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace. Since Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace to 32 BBY, and the Eiram–E'ronoh crisis is stated to have occurred twenty-five years ago in Into the Dark, the crisis must have occurred around 257 BBY.
  215. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The High Republic: The Rising Storm to 231 BBY. The High Republic: Out of the Shadows takes place in the immediate aftermath of The Rising Storm, meaning it must also take place around 231 BBY. As Xylan Graf is stated to be 22 years old in the novel, he must have been born around 253 BBY.
  216. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The High Republic: Light of the Jedi to 232 BBY. As Light of the Jedi establishes Innamin to be just over two decades old, he must have been born around 252 BBY.
  217. ↑ 217.0 217.1 The High Republic: Eye of the Storm 1
  218. ↑ "The Lie" chapter in The High Republic: Eye of the Storm 1, which includes Shalla Ro's death, features Asgar Ro's ascension to Eye of the Nihil, an event dated to 252 BBY by Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia.
  219. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The High Republic: The Rising Storm to 231 BBY. The High Republic: Out of the Shadows takes place in the immediate aftermath of The Rising Storm, meaning it must also take place around 231 BBY. As Jordanna Sparkburn is stated to be 20 years old in the novel and to have been born on Tiikae, she must have been born around 251 BBY.
  220. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic: Chronicles of the Jedi establishes that Pra-Tre Veter had served on the Jedi High Council for more than two decades by the time of the destruction of Starlight Beacon, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 230 BBY. Therefore, Veter must have joined the Council by 250 BBY.
  221. ↑ Yoda 1
  222. ↑ 222.0 222.1 The High Republic: Light of the Jedi establishes that Bell Zettifar was eighteen years old and had been with the Jedi Order for fifteen years. Since Light of the Jedi takes place in 232 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. Therefore, Zettifar must have been born around 250 BBY and joined the Jedi in 247 BBY.
  223. ↑  Star Wars: The High Republic | Announcement Trailer on the official Star Wars YouTube channel (backup link) (Posted on StarWars.com) states that the events of Star Wars: The High Republic, including The High Republic: Into the Dark, take place 200 years prior to the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 32 BBY. In Into the Dark, Reath Silas and Affie Hollow are stated to be seventeen years old, thereby placing their births around 249 BBY.
  224. ↑  Star Wars: The High Republic | Announcement Trailer on the official Star Wars YouTube channel (backup link) (Posted on StarWars.com) states that the events of Star Wars: The High Republic, which include the events of The High Republic: A Test of Courage, took place 200 years prior to the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 32 BBY. Thus, it can be deduced that the events of The High Republic: A Test of Courage took place around 232 BBY. According to Justina Ireland in this interview, Vernestra Rwoh is sixteen years old during A Test of Courage, thus setting her birth around 248 BBY.
  225. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The High Republic: A Test of Courage to 232 BBY. A Test of Courage states that Imri Cantaros is fourteen years old, thus placing his birth around 246 BBY.
  226. ↑ The Star Wars Book dates the launch of the Starlight Beacon to 232 BBY. As The High Republic: Light of the Jedi depicts the Beacon's launch, the novel's events must take place in approximatley that year. The High Republic: Light of the Jedi states that Ronn Blythe was 13 years at the time of the story. Therefore, he must have been born in 245 BBY.
  227. ↑ The High Republic (2021) 2 states that Sedri Minor was colonised twelve years ago. Since The High Republic 2 takes place shortly after the events of The High Republic (2021) 1, which include dedication of the Starlight Beacon that is dated by The Star Wars Book to 232 years before the events of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, the events of The High Republic 2 must be set around 232 BBY. Therefore, it can be found that the Sedri Minor colony was founded around 244 BBY.
  228. ↑  Star Wars: The High Republic | Announcement Trailer on the official Star Wars YouTube channel (backup link) (Posted on StarWars.com) states that the events of Star Wars: The High Republic, which include the events of The High Republic: A Test of Courage, take place two-hundred years before the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace. Since Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The Phantom Menace to 32 BBY, it can be deduced that the events of The High Republic: A Test of Courage take place around 232 BBY. As Justina Ireland has stated that Avon Sunvale is twelve years old during A Test of Courage, Starros was born around 244 BBY.
  229. ↑ The Star Wars Book dates the launch of the Starlight Beacon to 232 years before the events of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, which corresponds to 232 BBY in the dating system used by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. The events of The High Republic: A Test of Courage take place shortly before the launch, meaning they must take place around that year. The novel states that Avon Sunvale and Honesty Weft are both twelve years old, meaning they must have been born around 244 BBY.
  230. ↑ Per the reasoning here, the events of The High Republic: Beware the Nameless, which take place one and a half years after the destruction of Starlight Beacon, must take place in 228 BBY. As Beware the Nameless establishes that Haze Delik is sixteen years old during its events, she must have been born on Helikoprion around 244 BBY. Since the novel establishes the civil war on Helikoprion happened roughly around the time Delik was in her "pup stage," the civil war must have happened around 244 BBY.
  231. ↑ 231.0 231.1 231.2 231.3 The events of The High Republic: Escape from Valo take place one year after the destruction of Starlight Beacon, which equates to 229 BBY per Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia. As Driggit Parse, Zyle Keem, and Gavi are fourteen years old during the novel's events, they must have been born around 243 BBY; as Kildo is roughly the same age in the book, he must have been born around that year as well.
  232. ↑ The events of The High Republic Adventures – Dispatches from the Occlusion Zone 1 take place not long after the events of The High Republic Adventures: Crash and Burn, which occur in 228 BBY per the reasoning here. As Dispatches from the Occlusion Zone 1 establishes Emelsine is fifteen years old during its events, they must have been born around 243 BBY.
  233. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of The High Republic: Light of the Jedi to 232 BBY. As Serj Ukkarian is estimated to be around ten years old in Light of the Jedi, he must have been born around 242 BBY.
  234. ↑ 234.0 234.1 234.2 234.3 234.4 234.5 234.6 234.7 The High Republic: Tempest Runner
  235. ↑ The High Republic Adventures — The Monster of Temple Peak 4
  236. ↑  Star Wars: The High Republic | Announcement Trailer on the official Star Wars YouTube channel (backup link) (Posted on StarWars.com) states that the events of Star Wars: The High Republic, including The High Republic: Into the Dark, take place 200 years prior to the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 32 BBY. Into the Dark states that Dez Rydan passed his Jedi Trials eight years before, placing Rydan's knighting to around 240 BBY.
  237. ↑ 237.0 237.1 237.2 237.3 The High Republic (2021) 4
  238. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that Xu Bo was thirty-seven years old, Xu Bo must have been born in 237 BBY.
  239. ↑ Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates the events of The High Republic: Out of the Shadows to 231 BBY. Out of the Shadows states that Sylvestri Yarrow is eighteen years old at the time, dating Sylvestri Yarrow's birth to circa 249 BBY. Out of the Shadows dates Yarrow's year living with Lourna Dee to when Sylvestri was thirteen years old, which must therefore have been around 236 BBY.
  240. ↑ The Star Wars Book dates the launch of the Starlight Beacon to 232 BBY. The events of The High Republic: A Test of Courage take place shortly before the launch, meaning they must take place around that year. The novel states that J-6 was given to Avon Starros three years before, which corresponds to 235 BBY.
  241. ↑ The Star Wars Book dates the launch of the Starlight Beacon to 232 BBY. The High Republic: Light of the Jedi depicts the launch, so its events must occur around that year. The High Republic: The Rising Storm says that Bell Zettifar was 15 years old when he became Loden Greatstorm's Padawan. As Zettifar is 18 years old in Light of the Jedi, he must have become Greatstorm's apprentice around 235 BBY.

As Star Wars: Timelines dates both the Great Hyperspace Disaster and the dedication of the Starlight Beacon to 232 BBY, the attack on Tanalorr must take place within that year.

As  Star Wars: The High Republic Show: Trail of Shadows, a Tale From Last Call, and More! on the official Star Wars YouTube channel (backup link) (Posted on StarWars.com) establishes that "Crash and the Crew Do What They Do" takes place after the events of The Rising Storm, it must take place around the same year.   352. ↑ 352.00 352.01 352.02 352.03 352.04 352.05 352.06 352.07 352.08 352.09 The High Republic: The Eye of Darkness
  353. ↑ "Light in the Darkness" — The High Republic: Tales of Light and Life
  354. ↑ "All the Republic" — Revelations (2023) 1
  355. ↑ The High Republic Adventures (2023) 1
  356. ↑ The High Republic Adventures (2023) 2
  357. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates Starlight Beacon's destruction to 230 BBY. The implementation of the Guardian Protocols, which Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia places in 229 BBY, took place one week after Starlight Beacon's fall according to The High Republic – Shadows of Starlight 1. As The High Republic Adventures (2023) 4 takes place one year and two weeks after the destruction of Starlight Beacon, it must be set in 228 BBY. Since the events of The High Republic Adventures (2023) 6 are set immediately following the fourth issue and depict the expansion of the Stormwall, the expansion must have taken place in the same year. Meanwhile, the events of "Tales from the Occlusion Zone: No Big Deal" include the Stormwall's expansion and also depict the start of Pikka Adren's pregnancy. A later story, "Tales from the Occlusion Zone: Sparks Fly," states that Adren is eight months pregnant during its events, indicating that it is set eight months after "No Big Deal," and therefore one year, eight months, and two weeks after the fall of Starlight Beacon. "Sparks Fly" is set during the Battle of Eriadu, an event that begins in The High Republic Adventures Phase III - The Wedding Spectacular. As  Star Wars: The High Republic Chronological Reader's Guide on StarWars.com (article) (backup link) places The High Republic: Defy the Storm between the events of "No Big Deal" and The Wedding Spectacular, its events must also be set in 228 BBY.
  358. ↑ 358.0 358.1 The High Republic Adventures (2023) 5
  359. ↑ "Tales from the Occlusion Zone: No Big Deal" — Star Wars Insider 222
  360. ↑ "Tales from the Occlusion Zone: Friend or Foe?" — Star Wars Insider 223
  361. ↑ 361.0 361.1 361.2 361.3 361.4 361.5 The High Republic: Defy the Storm
  362. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates Starlight Beacon's destruction to 230 BBY. The implementation of the Guardian Protocols, which Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia places in 229 BBY, took place one week after Starlight Beacon's fall according to The High Republic – Shadows of Starlight 1. As The High Republic Adventures (2023) 4 takes place one year and two weeks after the destruction of Starlight Beacon, it must be set in 228 BBY. Since the events of The High Republic Adventures (2023) 6 are set immediately following the fourth issue and depict the expansion of the Stormwall, the expansion must have taken place in the same year. Meanwhile, the events of "Tales from the Occlusion Zone: No Big Deal" include the Stormwall's expansion and also depict the start of Pikka Adren's pregnancy. A later story, "Tales from the Occlusion Zone: Sparks Fly," states that Adren is eight months pregnant during its events, indicating that it is set eight months after "No Big Deal," and therefore one year, eight months, and two weeks after the fall of Starlight Beacon. "Sparks Fly" is set during the Battle of Eriadu, an event that begins in The High Republic Adventures Phase III - The Wedding Spectacular. As  Star Wars: The High Republic Chronological Reader's Guide on StarWars.com (article) (backup link) places "Chronicles of Corellia: Heir Apparent" between the events of "No Big Deal" and The Wedding Spectacular, its events must also be set in 228 BBY.
  363. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates Starlight Beacon's destruction to 230 BBY. The implementation of the Guardian Protocols, which Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia places in 229 BBY, took place one week after Starlight Beacon's fall according to The High Republic – Shadows of Starlight 1. As The High Republic Adventures (2023) 4 takes place one year and two weeks after the destruction of Starlight Beacon, it must be set in 228 BBY. Since the events of The High Republic Adventures (2023) 6 are set immediately following the fourth issue and depict the expansion of the Stormwall, the expansion must have taken place in the same year. Meanwhile, the events of "Tales from the Occlusion Zone: No Big Deal" include the Stormwall's expansion and also depict the start of Pikka Adren's pregnancy. A later story, "Tales from the Occlusion Zone: Sparks Fly," states that Adren is eight months pregnant during its events, indicating that it is set eight months after "No Big Deal," and therefore one year, eight months, and two weeks after the fall of Starlight Beacon. "Sparks Fly" is set during the Battle of Eriadu, an event that begins in The High Republic Adventures Phase III - The Wedding Spectacular. As  Star Wars: The High Republic Chronological Reader's Guide on StarWars.com (article) (backup link) places "Chronicles of Corellia: Cracking the Mask" between the events of "No Big Deal" and The Wedding Spectacular, its events must also be set in 228 BBY..
