  390. ↑ 390.0 390.1 390.2 390.3 390.4 390.5 The High Republic: Trials of the Jedi
  391. ↑ The High Republic – Fear of the Jedi 1
  392. ↑ The section of The Acolyte - Kelnacca 1 in which Yarzion Vell tells Kelnacca about his tattoos occurs three years after the Destruction of Starlight Beacon, which Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia dates to 230 BBY. The section in which Vell is knighted takes place after that, and so both must occur around in 227 BBY.
  393. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that Fire had been plagued by the Blue Dragon for decades, the series of events must have began by around 220 BBY.
  394. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that Zhang Jincheng began to ascend the hierarchy of Fire, a city in the Aakaash system, twenty years before. Therefore, Zhang must have began ascending the ranks around 220 BBY.
  395. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that Jamie Brasson had risen to power for twenty years, the politician's rise must have taken place around 220 BBY.
  396. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that Ma, an executive of the Land & Sky Corporation that rules the Aakaash system, died of suspicious circumstances twenty years ago, Ma must have died around 220 BBY.
  397. ↑ Star Wars: Tales from the Galaxy's Edge – Last Call
  398. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. In the novel, Sean is stated to be seventeen years old, which would place his birthyear around 217 BBY.
  399. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. In the novel, Sean is stated to be seventeen years old, which would place his birthyear around 217 BBY. Zhang Yuanying was said to be around Sean's age, so she was born around the same year.
  400. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that the Mandalorian gatekeeper at Jamie Brasen's estate had been employed for five years, the Mandalorian must have been employed around 205 BBY.
  401. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that Huang had been working as a member of Li Yu's Knights of Silver Dawn to pay off his debts for five years, Huang must have joined the Knights around 205 BBY.
  402. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that Zhang Jincheng ascended to the position of the Fire station's manager five year ago, the promotion must have occurred around 205 BBY.
  403. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since Sean was seventeen years old in The Vow of Silver Dawn, and the webnovel states that Sean was taken by Jedi Master Mostima as her Padawan learner when Sean was fourteen years old, Sean's apprenticeship began around 203 BBY.
  404. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that Bellon had worked as a chef under Jamie Brasson for over three years, Bellon must have been employed in that capacity in 203 BBY.
  405. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that the clash on Fire involving the dragon head escaping with his life occurred one year ago, the event must have taken place around 201 BBY.
  406. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that Nan Moyou and Nan Holi last went on a short vacation at the Nan family villa one year ago, the vacation must have taken place around 201 BBY.
  407. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that Mostima and Sean studied the Oplovis sector a year ago, it can be calculated that they studied the sector around 201 BBY.
  408. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY. Since the webnovel states that the industrial output of the Aakaash system exceeded that of Metalorn's a year ago, it can be calculated that the event occurred around 201 BBY.
  409. ↑ "The Lost Stories, Part 1" — Star Wars Adventures (2017) 30
  410. ↑ Star Wars: Card Trader Set: Base Series 1, Card: Chewbacca - Kashyyyk Warrior
  411. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates The Vow of Silver Dawn to 200 BBY.
  412. ↑ Chelli Lona Aphra states in Doctor Aphra (2020) 4 that the sewers of Vaale had not been used for a few hundred years, and, in Doctor Aphra (2020) 2, that the Architects of Vaale had inscribed Old Republic iconography in their sacred workshop in Vaale, which warned of the dangerous potential of the Rings of Vaale. Since the comics are set soon after the Battle of Hoth, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 3 ABY, it can be found that the past events relating to Vaale as Aphra claimed had occurred by around 196 BBY.
  413. ↑ Moving Target: A Princess Leia Adventure states that the carved, cathedral-like ceiling of the landing pads of Basteel's Eladro City had existed for centuries. Since the events of the novel take place shortly before the Battle of Endor, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 4 ABY, the ceiling on Basteel must have been made by 196 BBY.
  414. ↑ Moving Target: A Princess Leia Adventure states that the fertilizer farmers of fertile Jowloon had settled on Jaresh because they did not want change, and had done so for centuries. Since the events of the novel take place shortly before the Battle of Endor, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 4 ABY, Jowloon must have been settled by the farmers by 196 BBY.
  415. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide specifies that Indara was fifty-four years old during the main events of Star Wars: The Acolyte, which took place in 132 BBY. Therefore, she must have been born around 186 BBY.
  416. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Taibik Kajok is fifty-two years old during the main events of Star Wars: The Acolyte, which take place in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. Therefore, he must have been born around 184 BBY.
  417. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Wiltoo Kajok is forty-nine years old during the main events of Star Wars: The Acolyte, which take place in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. Therefore, he must have been born around 181 BBY
  418. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide states Sol's age during the present day events of The Acolyte to be 48 standard years. As those events occur in 132 BBY, he must have been born around 180 BBY.
  419. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes Sol is forty-eight years old during the present day events of The Acolyte, which occur in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. Therefore, he must have been born around 180 BBY. "Destiny" establishes that Sol left his family to join the Jedi Order when he was four years old, placing that event around 176 BBY.
  420. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Torbin was forty-one years old at the time of the events of Star Wars: The Acolyte, which occur in 132 BBY. Therefore, he must have been born around 173 BBY.
  421. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Torbin was forty-one years old at the time of the events of Star Wars: The Acolyte, which occur in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. It also establishes Torbin was given to the Jedi Order when he was three years old, equating to around 170 BBY.
  422. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Cabuck was 34 standard years old during the events of The Acolyte. As such, Cabuck must have been born around 166 BBY.
  423. ↑ The main events of Phasma takes place a few weeks before that of Galaxy's Edge: Black Spire, which in turn takes place concurrently to Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens. Since the latter's events are dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, and it is established in a chapter set ten years before the main events of Phasma that the Con Star Mining Corporation established mining colonies on Parnassos one hundred and eighty-six years prior, it can be deduced that the corporation's arrival occurred around 162 BBY.
  424. ↑ The prologue of Lost Stars, in which it is mentioned the second wave of settlers to Jelucan arrived "a hundred and fifty years ago", takes place when Thane Kyrell and Ciena Ree are both eight years old. Both of them are mentioned to have been born the year the Galactic Empire was founded, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 19 BBY. Thus, the prologue is set in 11 BBY, setting Jelucan's second wave of colonization in 161 BBY.
  425. ↑ 425.0 425.1 The Acolyte: Wayseeker
  426. ↑ 426.00 426.01 426.02 426.03 426.04 426.05 426.06 426.07 426.08 426.09 The events of Star Wars: The Acolyte take place in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Indara is fifty-four years old in The Acolyte and also states that she is twenty-six years old during the events of The Acolyte: Wayseeker. Therefore, the events of Wayseeker must take place around 160 BBY.
  427. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Fillik is twenty-six years old during the events of Star Wars: The Acolyte, which take place in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. Therefore, he must have been born around 158 BBY.
  428. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Mog Adana is twenty-six years old during the events of Star Wars: The Acolyte, which take place in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. Therefore, he must have been born around 158 BBY.
  429. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Yord Fandar was twenty-five years old at the time of the events of Star Wars: The Acolyte, which occur in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. Therefore, he must have been born around 157 BBY.
  430. ↑  "Lost/Found" Episode Guide | The Acolyte on StarWars.com (backup link) states that the events of Star Wars: The Acolyte take place 100 years before the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 32 BBY. Therefore, The Acolyte takes place in 132 BBY. "Lost / Found" states that Mae and Osha were eight years old at the time of their separation, and "Revenge / Justice" establishes the events on Brendok occurred sixteen years prior to the events of the episode, placing their birth in 156 BBY.
  431. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Jecki Lon is twenty-one years old during the events of The Acolyte, which take place in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. Therefore, she must have been born around 153 BBY.
  432. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Bazil was nineteen years old during the events of Star Wars: The Acolyte, which take place in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. Therefore, he must have been born around 151 BBY.
  433. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Tasi Lowa was 19 years old at the time of the events of Star Wars: The Acolyte, which occur in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. Therefore, she must have been born around 151 BBY.
  434. ↑ Thrawn Ascendancy: Greater Good takes place around 18 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. In Greater Good, Xodlak'ji'iprip dates the Coduyo family's family emergency to thirty-two years prior, and Coduyo'po'nekri says the Coduyo had not been a Ruling Family for 100 years before that. Thus, the family's removal took place around 150 BBY.
  435. ↑ The events of The Acolyte: The Crystal Crown take place in 134 BBY per the following reasoning. The Acolyte: The Crystal Crown establishes that Jecki Lon is the Padawan of Sol and Yord Fandar has yet to undertake his Jedi Trials. The novel also states that Lon has been Sol's Padawan for at least two years, as well as that Sol had not taken a Padawan for years after his former Padawan, Verosha Aniseya, left the Jedi Order. From this, at least four years have passed since Aniseya left the Order. "Lost / Found"—the events of which take place in 132 BBY per the reasoning here—establishes Aniseya left the Order six years before its events, equating to 138 BBY. The episode further states Fandar passed his Jedi Trials two years before its events, meaning he must have passed his Trials in 134 BBY. As The Crystal Crown states that Rhos Arthyst was sixteen years old at the events of the novel, they must have been born in 150 BBY.
  436. ↑ 436.0 436.1 Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Kelnacca was 149 years old at the time of the destruction of Starlight Beacon, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 230 BBY. Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide also establishes Kelnacca was 230 years old during the present day events of The Acolyte - Kelnacca 1, meaning the events of the comic must occur around 149 BBY. Additionally, it establishes that Kelnacca was 231 years old when he completed Ordea's training, meaning it was completed around 148 BBY.
  437. ↑ 437.0 437.1 Star Wars: The Acolyte — "Choice"
  438. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte — "Destiny"
  439. ↑ 439.0 439.1 439.2 439.3 439.4 Star Wars: The Acolyte — "Lost / Found"
  440. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Torbin was forty-one years old at the time of the events of Star Wars: The Acolyte, which occur in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. It also establishes Torbin was twenty-six years old when he passed his Jedi Trials, equating to around 147 BBY.
  441. ↑ "Revenge / Justice"—which takes place in 132 BBY per the reasoning here—establishes Torbin took the Barash Vow at least ten years prior to its events. Therefore, he must have taken the vow by 142 BBY. The episode also establishes that Torbin was stationed on Brendok sixteen years before its events, equating to 148 BBY.
  442. ↑  "Lost/Found" Episode Guide | The Acolyte on StarWars.com (backup link) states that the events of Star Wars: The Acolyte take place 100 years before the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 32 BBY. Therefore, The Acolyte takes place in 132 BBY. "Lost / Found" establishes that Osha Aniseya left the Jedi Order six years before its events, equating to around 138 BBY.
  443. ↑ The events of Star Wars: The Acolyte take place in 132 BBY per the reasoning here. Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes Chippin Jat hatched four years before the events of The Acolyte, meaning he must have hatched in 136 BBY.
  444. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide establishes that Cabuck Junior was 3 years old during the events of The Acolyte.  "Lost/Found" Episode Guide | The Acolyte on StarWars.com (backup link) states that the events of Star Wars: The Acolyte take place 100 years before the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 32 BBY. Therefore, The Acolyte takes place in 132 BBY. As such, Cabuck must have been born around 135 BBY.
  445. ↑ 445.0 445.1 445.2 445.3 The events of The Acolyte: The Crystal Crown take place in 134 BBY per the following reasoning. The Acolyte: The Crystal Crown establishes that Jecki Lon is the Padawan of Sol and Yord Fandar has yet to undertake his Jedi Trials. The novel also states that Lon has been Sol's Padawan for at least two years, as well as that Sol had not taken a Padawan for years after his former Padawan, Verosha Aniseya, left the Jedi Order. From this, at least four years have passed since Aniseya left the Order. "Lost / Found"—the events of which take place in 132 BBY per the reasoning here—establishes Aniseya left the Order six years before its events, equating to 138 BBY. The episode further states Fandar passed his Jedi Trials two years before its events, meaning he must have passed his Trials in 134 BBY.
  446. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte — "Day"
  447. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte — "Revenge / Justice"
  448. ↑ 448.0 448.1 Star Wars: The Acolyte — "Day"
  449. ↑ Star Wars: The Acolyte — "Night"
  450. ↑ 450.0 450.1 Star Wars: The Acolyte — "The Acolyte"
  451. ↑ Star Wars: Galactic Atlas states that the Imperial Era began in 19 BBY, thus placing events that took place nearly a century prior to the birth of the Galactic Empire to around 119 BBY.
  452. ↑ 452.0 452.1 452.2 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Old Friends Not Forgotten"
  453. ↑ Adventures in Wild Space: The Steal
  454. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines places the main events of Thrawn Ascendancy: Lesser Evil in 18 BBY. As the high-density electronics memory unit is stated to have been brought to the Universal Analysis Group 100 years previously, it must have been brought in around 118 BBY.
  455. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines places the main events of Thrawn Ascendancy: Greater Good in 18 BBY. As the Coduyo family is stated to have been removed from Ruling family status 100 years previously, they must have been removed in around 118 BBY.
  456. ↑ "Rules of the Game" — Canto Bight states that Kedpin Shoklop was born on Werma Lesser and had experienced a century and a half of life by the time of the novelella's events, which take place shortly before the events of Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi. Given that the events of The Last Jedi take place immediately after that of Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens, which are dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, Kedpin Shoklop must have been born around 116 BBY.
  457. ↑ Star Wars Propaganda: A History of Persuasive Art in the Galaxy states that Janyor had experienced fourteen decades of life by the time of the Hosnian Cataclysm, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY. Therefore, Janyor must have been born around 106 BBY.
  458. ↑ According to Star Wars: Absolutely Everything You Need to Know, Dooku was eighty years old at the time of the First Battle of Geonosis. Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the battle to 22 BBY, therefore establishing Dooku's birth to around 102 BBY. The script of Dooku: Jedi Lost establishes that Ramil, born on Serenno, was two years older than Dooku, placing Ramil's birth to around 104 BBY.
  459. ↑  Count Dooku in the Encyclopedia (original site is defunct)
  460. ↑ According to Star Wars: Absolutely Everything You Need to Know, Dooku was eighty years old at the time of the First Battle of Geonosis. Per Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, the battle took place in 22 BBY, thus placing Dooku's birth to around 102 BBY. Dooku: Jedi Lost establishes that Arath Tarrex was the same age as Dooku at the time of their Initiate Trials, thereby placing Tarrex's birth to around 102 BBY as well.
  461. ↑ The script of Dooku: Jedi Lost states that Jenza is eleven years old when Dooku is at the age of twelve. Star Wars: Timelines dates Dooku's birth to 102 BBY. Therefore, Jenza must have been born around 101 BBY.
  462. ↑  "Narkina 5" Trivia Gallery | Andor on StarWars.com (backup link) (Slide 6) dates "In the Name of the Rebellion" to the year 1 BBY. As the events of "Flight of the Defender," including the remark that there had been no reported sighting of the Loth-wolf in "a hundred-years," takes place shortly after "In the Name of the Rebellion", it can be placed around the year 1 BBY. Thus, the last reported sighting of a Loth-wolf happened around 101 BBY.
  463. ↑ The events of issues 1 to 8 of the Star Wars: Doctor Aphra comic-book series take place after the Battle of Yavin, which marks the beginning of 0 ABY, according to Star Wars: Timelines. Additionally, issue 8 is part of the Star Wars: The Screaming Citadel crossover arc, which is placed chronologically before the mutiny on Mon Cala. Star Wars: Battles that Changed the Galaxy dates the mutiny on Mon Cala to nineteen years after the formation of the Galactic Empire, which Timelines dates to 19 BBY. Therefore, the events of Doctor Aphra 2 must be set in 0 ABY. Since the comic issue states that the Cosmatanic Steppes had been a center of archaeological digs and scavenging for the last hundred years of the historical art rush, the period of the rush involving the Cosmatanic Steppes must have began around 99 BBY.
  464. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of issues 9 to 31 of the Star Wars: Doctor Aphra comic-book series to around 1 ABY. Therefore Doctor Aphra 16 must take place around 1 ABY. Since Doctor Aphra 16 establishes that Wat Tambor acquired the Triple-Zero protocol personality matrix nearly a century ago, the acquisition occurred around 99 BBY.
  465. ↑ Doctor Aphra (2016) 16
  466. ↑ According to the novelization of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story, Meggone was ninety-three at the time of the destruction of the Holy City, an event dated to 1 BBY by Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide Therefore, Meggone must have been born around 94 BBY.
  467. ↑ In Star Wars Forces of Destiny: Volume 3, while narrating her knowledge of Finn and Rose Tico's mission to Canto Bight, Maz Kanata finds a holocron she says she has not seen for 125 years. Since the mission to Canto Bight are dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Timelines. As Maz Kanata's narration took place after the mission, Kanata last saw her holocron in or after 91 BBY.
  468. ↑ In the short story "Orientation," which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the events of to 14 BBY, Pell Baylo was said to have been "well past 70". Additionally, in The Living Force, which is set in 33 BBY per the reasoning here, Baylo is stated to be in his late fifties. Therefore, he must have been born sometime between around 92 BBY and 85 BBY.
  469. ↑ The script of Dooku: Jedi Lost gives Gretz Droom's age as sixteen years when he is chosen as the Padawan of Jor Aerith. Qui-Gon Jinn, who is simultaneously made the Padawan of Dooku, is stated to have been twelve years old at the time in the novel Master & Apprentice. Skywalker: A Family at War places the novel's events eight years before the Invasion of Naboo, which is placed in 32 BBY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. As such, Master & Apprentice is set in 40 BBY, meaning that both Jinn and Droom became Padawans in 68 BBY. Therefore, Droom was born sixteen years earlier, around 84 BBY.
  470. ↑  "Rix Road" Trivia Gallery | Andor on StarWars.com (backup link) (Slide 2) dates Clem Andor's birth to 7896 C.R.C. and his death to 7959 C.R.C. Star Wars: Scum and Villainy: Case Files on the Galaxy's Most Notorious dates carbon-freezing of Han Solo to 7980 C.R.C., which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 3 ABY, therefore it can be concluded that 7980 C.R.C. is equivalent to 3 ABY, 7896 C.R.C. roughly equates to 81 BBY.
  471. ↑ Qui-Gon Jinn is forty years old in the novel Master & Apprentice. Skywalker: A Family at War places the novel's events eight years before the Invasion of Naboo, which is placed in 32 BBY by Star Wars: Timelines. Therefore, Master & Apprentice is set in 40 BBY, thereby placing Jinn's birth around 80 BBY.
  472. ↑ Qui-Gon Jinn is forty years old in the novel Master & Apprentice. Skywalker: A Family at War places the novel's events eight years before the Invasion of Naboo, which is placed in 32 BBY by Star Wars: Timelines. Therefore, Master & Apprentice is set in 40 BBY, thereby placing Jinn's birth around 80 BBY. The script of Dooku: Jedi Lost states that members of Jedi Initiate Clans are the same age, and as Ky Narec is in the same clan as Jinn, it can be deduced that Narec was also born around 80 BBY.
  473. ↑ 473.0 473.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Character Encyclopedia - Join the Battle!
  474. ↑ Star Wars: Scum and Villainy: Case Files on the Galaxy's Most Notorious states that Dannl Faytonni is 54 years old in 7955 C.R.C.. The book also places the Trade Federation's plot to assassinate Senator Amidala in 7955 C.R.C.. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the plot to 22 BBY, it can be deduced that 7955 C.R.C. took place in 22 BBY, thereby placing Dannl Faytonni's birth to 76 BBY.
  475. ↑ According to the Star Wars Encyclopedia booklet "Mace Windu and Other Users of the Force," Mace Windu was born 40 years before the Battle of Naboo, which Star Wars: Timelines places in 32 BBY. Therefore, he must been born in 72 BBY.
  476. ↑ Star Wars Journeys: Beginnings
  477. ↑ Skywalker: A Family at War places the events of Master & Apprentice eight years before the Invasion of Naboo, which is placed in 32 BBY by Star Wars: Timelines. Therefore, Master & Apprentice is set in 40 BBY. Since Rahara Wick is twenty-nine years old in Master & Apprentice, Wick was born around 69 BBY.
  478. ↑ "Rules of the Game" — Canto Bight states that Kedpin Shoklop had been selling vaporators for VaporTech for 102 years by the time of the novelella's events, which take place shortly before the events of Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi. Given that the events of The Last Jedi take place immediately after that of Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens, which are dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Timelines, Kedpin Shoklop must have began selling vaporators for VaporTech in 68 BBY.
  479. ↑ "Rules of the Game" — Canto Bight states that Anglang Lehet had been working for the Syndicate for 102 years by the time of the novelella's events, which take place shortly before the events of Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi. Given that the events of The Last Jedi take place immediately after that of Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens, which are dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Timelines, Anglang Lehet must have began working for the Syndicate in 68 BBY.
  480. ↑ Greater Good takes place around 18 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. Senior Captain Irizi'in'daro reflected that the Xodlak had been stripped of their Ruling Family status 50 years ago, dating their removal to around 68 BBY.
  481. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines places the events of Master & Apprentice in 40 BBY. Qui-Gon Jinn is established to be forty years old in Master & Apprentice, placing his birth to around 80 BBY. Given the battle on Shurrupak occurs when Jinn is twelve years old, the event must have occurred around 68 BBY.
  482. ↑ Justice (episode) takes place after Qui-Gon Jinn became Dooku's padawan. According to Star Wars: Timelines, Qui-Gon becomes Dooku's padawan in 68 BBY and becomes a Jedi Knight around 58 BBY. Therefore Justice must take place between those two years.
  483. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines places the main events of Master & Apprentice in 40 BBY. The novel also establishes that Qui-Gon Jinn was forty years old during its main events and fourteen years old in the Numidian Prime mission, which dates the endeavor to around 66 BBY.
  484. ↑ Star Wars Complete Vehicles, New Edition states that the Radiant VII was a veteran of 34 years in the Republic Diplomatic Corps at the time of the Invasion of Naboo. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas states that the invasion took place in 32 BBY, the Radiant VII's service with the Diplomatic Corps began in 66 BBY.
  485. ↑ Star Wars: Galactic Atlas places the events of Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens in 34 ABY, and Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi takes place immediately after The Force Awakens. Since Star Wars: The Last Jedi: Incredible Cross-Sections states that the Libertine was built a century before the events of The Last Jedi, the starship was built in 66 BBY.
  486. ↑ Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide states that Raddus was sixty-five years old at the time of the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story and was born on Mon Cala. As Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Rogue One to 1 BBY, Raddus must have been born around 66 BBY.
  487. ↑ Star Wars Jedi Master Magazine 2
  488. ↑  Ric Olie in the Databank (backup link)
  489. ↑  Naboo Royal Guards in the Databank (backup link)
  490. ↑ 490.0 490.1  Cosbaby By Hot Toys (@cosbaby) on Instagram (post on April 11, 2025) (backup link)
  491. ↑ Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide states that Jan Dodonna was 64 years old in 1 BBY. Therfore, he must been born around 65 BBY.
  492. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Force Collector to 32 ABY. As Force Collector establishes that Karr Nuq Sin is seventeen years old, he was born around 15 ABY. As Naq Med is established to be eighty years older than Karr, he was born around 65 BBY.
  493. ↑ Per his character folio, Hallan Krintu was born forty–four years before Begun, The Clone Wars Have!, which takes place in 21 BBY. Therefore he must been born in 65 BBY.
  494. ↑ Star Wars: Scum and Villainy: Case Files on the Galaxy's Most Notorious establishes that Cad Bane was 41 years old in the year 7956 C.R.C., eleven years after the 7945 C.R.C. Boonta Eve Classic of Neva Kee's disappearance. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates that podrace to 32 BBY, it can be calculated that Bane was born in the year 62 BBY.
  495. ↑  Cad Bane in the Databank (backup link)
  496. ↑ Thrawn Ascendancy: Greater Good establishes that Ar'alani is three years older than Mitth'raw'nuruodo. Star Wars: Timelines states that Thrawn was born around 59 BBY. Therefore, Ar'alani must have been born around 62 BBY.
  497. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates Nim Pianna's passing to 48 BBY. As Master & Apprentice establishes that she was around fourteen years old at the time of her death aboard the Advent, she must have been born around 62 BBY.
  498. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines establishes that Thrawn was born in 59 BBY. Thrawn Ascendancy: Chaos Rising establishes that Cohbo'rik'ardok was two years older than Thrawn, placing her year of birth in 61 BBY.
  499. ↑ Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 22 Secrets of Spaceflight: The Hanx-Wargel Main Computer
  500. ↑ "Orientation" — Star Wars Insider 157 states that Pell Baylo founded the Defiance Flight Training Institute, which he had been running for nearly fifty years by the time of the short story's events that took place in 14 BBY. Ergo, Baylo began running his Flight Training Institute around 60 BBY.
  501. ↑ Star Wars Rebels: The Visual Guide states that Kanan Jarrus and Hera Syndulla met on Gorse, as depicted in the novel A New Dawn, six years prior to the events of Star Wars Rebels. Star Wars: Galactic Atlas states that the events of Rebels began in 5 BBY, thereby dating the events of A New Dawn to 11 BBY. It is revealed in A New Dawn that Denetrius Vidian was once Lemuel Tharsa via hologram footage from twenty years before. Therefore, the footage was taken in 31 BBY, and since Tharsa is shown to be "just shy of thirty years old" in the footage, he must have been born around 60 BBY.
  502. ↑ 502.00 502.01 502.02 502.03 502.04 502.05 502.06 502.07 502.08 502.09 502.10 502.11 502.12 502.13 502.14 502.15 502.16 502.17 502.18 502.19 502.20 502.21 502.22 502.23 502.24 502.25 Star Wars Outlaws
  503. ↑  Obi-Wan Kenobi in the Databank (backup link)
  504. ↑ Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge takes place in 34 ABY per Star Wars: Timelines. Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge — Star Wars: Datapad states that Brookish Boon was 91 years old during the events of Galaxy's Edge, thereby placing his birth to around 57 BBY. The Datapad also establishes that Boon was born on Sy Myrth.
  505. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates Obi-Wan Kenobi's birth to 57 BBY In Padawan, Kenobi considers Audj Seedol to be around his own age, dating her birth to approximately 57 BBY.
  506. ↑ Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide dates Galen Erso's birth on Grange to 56 years before the the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story to 1 BBY, the event must have taken place in 57 BBY.
  507. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge to 34 ABY. Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge — Star Wars: Datapad states that Kral Grek was 90 years old during the events of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge, meaning he was born in around 56 BBY.
  508. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines places the events of Master & Apprentice in 40 BBY. Master & Apprentice establishes Cady to be a year or two younger than Kenobi, which places her birth around 56 BBY.
  509. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge to 34 ABY. Star Wars: Datapad — "Flight Crews Wanted" (screenshot) states that Bok Askol is age 90 during the the events of Galaxy's Edge, meaning Askol was born around 56 BBY.
  510. ↑ Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide states that Baze Malbus was born 53 years before the mission to steal the Death Star plans, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 1 BBY. Therefore, he must have been born in 54 BBY.
  511. ↑ Master & Apprentice gives Fanry's age as fourteen years old. The book also states that Obi-Wan Kenobi was 17 years old and according to Star Wars: Card Trader Set: Base Series 1, Card: Obi-Wan Kenobi - Padawan Obi-Wan was born in 57 BBY, placing the novel around 40 BBY. Using math we can deduce that Fanry was born around 54 BBY.
  512. ↑ Master & Apprentice
  513. ↑  Darth Maul in the Encyclopedia (original site is defunct)
  514. ↑ Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide states that Chirrut Îmwe was born 52 years before the mission to steal the Death Star plans, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 1 BBY. Therefore, he must have been born in 53 BBY.
  515. ↑ Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide dates Orson Krennic's birth to 51 years before the the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story. As Star Wars: Timelines dates the the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story to 1 BBY, the event must have taken place around 52 BBY.
  516. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Brotherhood to 22 BBY. The novel establishes that Rokura is thirty years old, therefore placing her birth around 52 BBY.
  517. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the First Order's occupation of Batuu to 34 ABY. Star Wars: Datapad — "Flight Crews Wanted" (screenshots: 1, 2) states that Maneeli Tuun is age 86 during the occupation, meaning he was born around 52 BBY.
  518. ↑ A New Dawn establishes that Skelly was born on Corellia forty standard years before the events of A New Dawn, which takes place in 11 BBY per the Star Wars: Timelines. Therefore he must have been born around 51 BBY.
  519. ↑ Star Wars Jedi Master Magazine 2
  520. ↑ Thrawn Ascendancy: Greater Good takes place in 18 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. After Yoponek described the events in which the Coduyo family invoked the family emergency to protect their holdings on Massoss as "around thirty years ago," Senior Aide Lakjip identified the events as "thirty-two years ago." Therefore, they must have happened in 50 BBY.
  521. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Character Encyclopedia - Join the Battle! places the birth of Asajj Ventress and her subsequent selling to Hal'Sted to 50 years before the events of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, which corresponds to 50 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines.
  522. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that Babu Frik's age was eighty-five years old during the events of Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker, which are established by The Visual Dictionary to have taken place one year after the Hosnian Cataclysm. Since the Cataclysm is dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, The Rise of Skywalker must take place in 35 ABY, and Babu Frik must have been born in 50 BBY.
  523. ↑ 523.00 523.01 523.02 523.03 523.04 523.05 523.06 523.07 523.08 523.09 Queen's Shadow
  524. ↑ 524.0 524.1 524.2 "Choices" depicts Mace Windu gaining a seat on the Jedi High Council. Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith established that Anakin Skywalker was the youngest to ever serve on the Council. Star Wars: Timelines dates the film to 19 BBY and Skywalker's birth to 41 BBY. Therefore, Windu must have been at least twenty-two years old. According to the Star Wars Encyclopedia booklet "Mace Windu and Other Force Users," Mace Windu was born forty years before the Battle of Naboo, which Star Wars: Timelines places in 32 BBY. Therefore, Windu gained a seat on the Council no earlier than around 50 BBY. Master & Apprentice further establishes that Windu is part of the Jedi Council at the time of Nim Pianna's death, which Timelines dates to 48 BBY. Therefore, the events of "Choices" must take place between these two years.
  525. ↑ Obi-Wan 1
  526. ↑ Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide dates Lyra Erso's birth on Aria Prime to 47 years before the the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story to 1 BBY, the event must have taken place in 48 BBY.
  527. ↑ Visit a Galaxy Far, Far Away with our Star Wars liveblog direct from San Diego Comic-Con 2024! on Popverse: "We're beginning our journey three years after the Battle of Yavin" (backup link) dates the events of Star Wars Outlaws to 3 ABY. Since Riko Vess is stated to be 50 years old in the events of the game, we can determine that her birth year is around 47 BBY.
  528. ↑ The events of "The Final Order" occur parallel to the events of Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 3 ABY. In "The Final Order" Canonhaus is established to be fifty years old. Therefore, he must have been born around 47 BBY.
  529. ↑ The novel Tarkin establishes that Wilhuff Tarkin was eighteen years old when Q'anah died in the Greater Seswenna raids, and 64 years old at the time of his own death at the Battle of Yavin. This can therefore be used to establish a 46 year gap between the two deaths, placing the novel's events at 46 BBY.
  530. ↑ 530.0 530.1 530.2 530.3  Padmé Amidala Biography Gallery in the Databank (backup link) (Slide 1)
  531. ↑ Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide dates Antoc Merrick's birth on Virujansi to 46 years before the the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story to 1 BBY, the event must have taken place in 47 BBY.
  532. ↑ Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide dates Mon Mothma's birth on Chandrila to 46 years before the the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story to 1 BBY, the event must have taken place in 47 BBY.
  533. ↑ Star Wars Rebels: The Visual Guide states that Cikatro Vizago was born on Devaron, and was 40 years of age at the beginning of Star Wars Rebels. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the start of Rebels to 5 BBY, it can be calculated that Vizago was born around 45 BBY.
  534. ↑ In Shadow of the Sith, Beaumont Kin estimates that Lor San Tekka is around 40 years his senior. According to Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary, Kin was 40 years old one year after the "Starkiller Incident." Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the Starkiller Incident to 34 ABY, Beaumont was born in 5 BBY, meaning that San Tekka must have been born around 45 BBY.
  535. ↑ 535.0 535.1 Doctor Aphra (2016) 36 states that Pitina Mar-Mas Voor was born twenty-five years before Palpatine's rise to absolute power, which is dated to 19 BBY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. As such, Voor was born in 44 BBY. As per Doctor Aphra 36, Voor was married off on her eighteenth birthday, allowing the calculation that Pitina Mar-Mas Voor married in 26 BBY.
  536. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary dates Landonis Balthazar Calrissian's birth on Socorro to 77 years before the the Hosnian Cataclysm. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the the Hosnian Cataclysm to 34 ABY, the event must have taken place in 43 BBY.
  537. ↑ Yoda 6
  538. ↑ Padawan
  539. ↑ "Life Day" — Star Wars Adventures (2020) 3
  540. ↑ Jedi Fallen Order - Dark Temple 1
  541. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of "Chapter 1: The Mandalorian" to 9 ABY. Since Grogu is fifty years old in Chapter 1, he must have been born around 41 BBY.
  542. ↑ Star Wars Rebels: The Visual Guide states that Kanan Jarrus and Hera Syndulla met on Gorse, as depicted in the novel A New Dawn, six years prior to the events of Star Wars Rebels. Star Wars: Galactic Atlas states that Star Wars Rebels begins in the year 5 BBY, thereby dating the events of A New Dawn to 11 BBY. Rae Sloane is thirty years in A New Dawn, thus placing her birth in 41 BBY.
  543. ↑ 543.0 543.1 543.2 543.3 543.4 A New Dawn
  544. ↑ 544.0 544.1 544.2 544.3 Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 32 Guide to the Galaxy: The History of Mandalore
  545. ↑ Star Wars: The Mandalorian — "Chapter 18: The Mines of Mandalore"
  546. ↑ According to The Official Star Wars Fact File Part 98 MAN 1-4: Mandalore, the Mandalorian Civil War took place around 20 years before the Clone Wars. Star Wars: Galactic Atlas establishes that the Clone Wars took place starting in 22 BBY through 19 BBY. Therefore, the Mandalorian Civil War can be dated to around 42 BBY to 39 BBY. By the time of Padawan, which Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of to 41 BBY, author Kiersten White establishes that the novel's plot was explicitly written to convey that he had not met Satine Kryze. As Obi-Wan Kenobi is established to have met Kryze during the Mandalorian Civil War in Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Voyage of Temptation", the war must have taken place between around 41 BBY and 39 BBY.
  547. ↑ 547.0 547.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Voyage of Temptation"
  548. ↑ 548.0 548.1 548.2 548.3 Master & Apprentice
  549. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines places the events of Master & Apprentice in 40 BBY.
  550. ↑ Star Wars (2015) 26
  551. ↑ Star Wars (2015) 29
  552. ↑ 552.0 552.1 Star Wars (2015) 28
  553. ↑ 553.0 553.1 553.2 553.3 553.4 553.5 553.6 553.7 553.8 Jedi Knights 1 states the events of Star Wars: Jedi Knights take place during the time Finis Valorum is Supreme Chancellor of the Galactic Republic, while Jedi Knights 5 establishes the series is set at least two years before the Battle of Naboo. As Star Wars Propaganda: A History of Persuasive Art in the Galaxy states that a Supreme Chancellor served four-year terms, and the flashback section of Tarkin that mentions Valorum's re-election is dated to 36 BBY by Star Wars: Timelines, Valorum must have begun serving as Chancellor in 40 BBY. Timelines also dates the Battle of Naboo to 32 BBY, meaning Jedi Knights must take place between 40 BBY and 34 BBY.
  554. ↑ "The Moon of Death" — Free Comic Book Day 2025: Star Wars 1
  555. ↑ Star Wars: Complete Vehicles (2018)
  556. ↑ According to "Laina" — From a Certain Point of View, Ryland is 38 years old during this events. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas: Updated Edition dates the Destruction of Alderaan to 1 BBY, he must have been born in around 39 BBY.
  557. ↑ Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 47 Starship Fact File: Naboo J-Type 327 Nubian
  558. ↑  Wald in the Databank (backup link)
  559. ↑ According to the script of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, as seen in Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide, Amee is 6 years old during the events of the film. As Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of the film to 32 BBY, she must have been born in around 38 BBY.
  560. ↑ According to Inquisitor: Rise of the Red Blade (p. 25), Zeeth was 16 years old as of 22 BBY, he must have been born in around 38 BBY.
  561. ↑ Age of Republic - Qui-Gon Jinn 1
  562. ↑ 562.0 562.1 Thrawn graduated from Taharim Academy around 37 BBY per the following reasoning. Thrawn Ascendancy: Lesser Evil establishes that Thrawn was a cadet at the Academy for two years. Per the reasoning here, Thrawn entered the academy around 39 BBY. Therefore, he must have graduated as a lieutenant in the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet around 37 BBY. Thrawn and Thrass met the Stybla family Senior Aide Lappincyk on Naporar just after his graduation, placing their encounter in the bistro around 37 BBY as well.
  563. ↑ Star Wars Rebels: Visual Guide: Epic Battles
  564. ↑ According to Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide, Ellis Mathes was 28 in 9 BBY, thus placing their birth around 37 BBY.
  565. ↑ Aftermath: Life Debt establishes that Gallius Rax joined the Naval Intelligence Agency, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to around 16 BBY, at the age of twenty. Therefore, Rax was born around 36 BBY.
  566. ↑ "Inbrief" — Star Wars Insider 161
  567. ↑ 567.0 567.1 567.2 567.3 567.4 Star Wars: The Rebel Files
  568. ↑ In Ahsoka, Kaeden Larte is established to have passed her sixteenth birthday one year prior to the novel's events. As the novel also establishes its events to have taken place one year after the founding of the Galactic Empire, and the Empire's founding is dated to 19 BBY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, Larte was born around 35 BBY.
  569. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary dates the Battle of Batuu, which includes the First Order's occupation of Batuu, to six months after the Hosnian Cataclysm, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY. Star Wars: Datapad — "Flight Crews Wanted" (screenshots: 1, 2) states that Wazellman is age 69 during the occupation, meaning he was born around 35 BBY.
  570. ↑ Star Wars: Scum and Villainy: Case Files on the Galaxy's Most Notorious
  571. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines establishes that Ahsoka Tano was born in 36 BBY. As the main story of "Life and Death" take place a year afterwards, they must be set in 35 BBY.
  572. ↑ The Secret Jedi: The Adventures of Kanan Jarrus: Rebel Leader
  573. ↑ "The Weapon" — Age of Republic Special 1
  574. ↑ 574.0 574.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Cat and Mouse"
  575. ↑  The Clone Wars Episode Guide: Cat and Mouse on StarWars.com (original site is defunct)
  576. ↑ 576.0 576.1 Age of Republic - Darth Maul 1
  577. ↑ Star Wars Helmet Collection: Rebel Trooper Databank A-Z: Saw Gerrera–Commander Gree states that the Gozanti-class cruiser was introduced "about 15 years before the rise of the Empire," which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 19 BBY.
  578. ↑ 578.0 578.1 578.2 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Nightsisters"
  579. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the event that Asajj Ventress became Ky Narec's padawan to 44 BBY. Per Dark Disciple, Ventress and Narec helped the people of Rattatak for ten years, meaning that Ventress had trained under Narec for ten years until Narec's death on Rattatak. Therefore, Narec was killed in 34 BBY.
  580. ↑ "A Certain Point of View" — Star Wars Insider 200 states that the Eriadu Conference took place 33 years prior to the events of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, which corresponds to 33 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines.
  581. ↑ 581.0 581.1 Star Wars: Complete Vehicles
  582. ↑ Darth Maul (2017) 1
  583. ↑ 583.0 583.1 Darth Maul (2017) 2
  584. ↑ Darth Maul (2017) 5
  585. ↑ Jango Fett 1
  586. ↑ Jango Fett 2
  587. ↑ Jango Fett 3
  588. ↑ Jango Fett 4
  589. ↑ Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide establishes that Cassian Jeron Andor was twenty-seven years old during the events of Star Wars: Andor, which the book dates the events of to 5 BBY. Cassian Andor in the Databank (backup link) establishes that Andor was nine years old when he left Kenari, with  "Reckoning" Episode Guide | Andor on StarWars.com (backup link) establishing that this occurred before the Confederacy of Independent Systems had formed. Rise of the Separatists establishes that the Raxus Address and the following informal forming of the CIS occurred two years before the First Battle of Geonosis, which Star Wars: Timelines and other sources state to take place in 22 BBY. For all this information to be true, Cassian Andor had to have been born in 33 BBY, with his departure taking place after his birthday, but before the Raxus Address in 24 BBY, and the events of Andor taking place before his birthday in 5 BBY.
  590. ↑ Alphabet Squadron
  591. ↑ 591.0 591.1 591.2 591.3 591.4 591.5  Poggle the Lesser Biography Gallery on StarWars.com (backup link)
  592. ↑ Star Wars: Card Trader Set: Base Series 1, Card: Lott Dod - Senator
  593. ↑ 593.0 593.1 593.2 593.3 593.4 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "The Lost One"
  594. ↑ 594.0 594.1 Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi — "The Sith Lord"
  595. ↑ 595.0 595.1 "Dark Vengeance: The True Story of Darth Maul and His Revenge Against the Jedi Known as Obi-Wan Kenobi" — The Clone Wars: Stories of Light and Dark
  596. ↑ 596.0 596.1 Age of Republic - Jango Fett 1
  597. ↑ 597.0 597.1 597.2 Star Wars: The Bad Batch — "Bounty Lost"
  598. ↑ 598.0 598.1 598.2 598.3 Solo: A Star Wars Story The Official Guide
  599. ↑ In Ahsoka, Miara Larte is established to be fourteen years old. As the novel also establishes its events to have taken place one year after the founding of the Galactic Empire, and the Empire's founding is dated to 19 BBY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, Larte was born around 32 BBY.
  600. ↑ Blue Shadow Virus" involves the raid on Nuvo Vindi's laboratory in which Gregar Typho states that the Separatist scientist disappeared "ten years ago." As the raid is dated to 21 BBY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, Nuvo Vindi must have disappeared around 31 BBY.
  601. ↑ Star Wars Rebels: The Visual Guide states that Kanan Jarrus and Hera Syndulla met on Gorse, as depicted in the novel A New Dawn, six years prior to the events of Star Wars Rebels. Star Wars: Galactic Atlas states that Star Wars Rebels begins in the year 5 BBY, thereby dating the events of A New Dawn to 11 BBY. During the Gorse Conflict, Jarrus, Syndulla, Zaluna Myder, and Skelly, investigating Denetrius Vidian's identity, discover that he was once Lemuel Tharsa via hologram footage taken twenty years before during his visit to the Introsphere facility. Thus, the visit took place around 31 BBY.
  602. ↑ In Ahsoka, the family of Kaeden Larte is established to have moved to Raada when Kaeden was four years of age. As the novel also establishes its events to have taken place one year after the founding of the Galactic Empire, and the Empire's founding is dated to 19 BBY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, the Lartes moved to Raada around 31 BBY.
  603. ↑ According to Star Wars Character Encyclopedia: Updated and Expanded, Apailana became Queen of Naboo aged twelve, towards the end of the Clone Wars. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas establishes that the war was a four-year conflict spanning from 22 BBY to 19 BBY, Apailana became Queen of Naboo in either 20 or 19 BBY. Therefore, her birth, which took place twelve years earlier according to the encyclopedia, must have occurred around 31 BBY.
  604. ↑  Queen Apailana in the Databank (backup link)
  605. ↑  "Mystery of a Thousand Moons" Episode Gallery on StarWars.com (backup link) states that Jaybo Hood is 10 years old in Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Mystery of a Thousand Moons". Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of this episode to 21 BBY, meaning that Jaybo Hood was born in 31 BBY.
  606. ↑ Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide establishes that Reva Sevander was twelve years old at the time of Order 66, which the book dates to 19 BBY. Therefore, Sevander must have been born around 31 BBY.
  607. ↑ Hidden Empire 3 states that Chanath Cha was twelve years old when her parents were killed in Darth Vader (2017) 11, which Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of to 19 BBY. Therefore Cha was born around 31 BBY.
  608. ↑ "Tobias Beckett and Other Characters of the Underworld" establishes that Qi'ra was born in 31 BBY. As Crimson Climb states that Rebolt was approximately the same age as Qi'ra, he must have been born] around 31 BBY.
  609. ↑ "Tobias Beckett and Other Characters of the Underworld" establishes that Qi'ra was born in 31 BBY. As Crimson Climb states that Jagleo was approximately the same age as Qi'ra, he must have been born around 31 BBY.
  610. ↑ "Tobias Beckett and Other Characters of the Underworld" establishes that Qi'ra was born in 31 BBY. As Crimson Climb states that Syke was approximately the same age as Qi'ra, he must have been born around 31 BBY
  611. ↑ "Tobias Beckett and Other Characters of the Underworld" establishes that Qi'ra was born in 31 BBY. As Crimson Climb states that Salbee was approximately the same age as Qi'ra, she must have been born around that 31 BBY.
  612. ↑ Per his character folio, Hawk was born 10 years before Begun, The Clone Wars Have!, which takes place in 21 BBY.
  613. ↑ In "I Have Friends Everywhere", Thela mentions that he was 12 years old when the Tarkin Massacre occurred. According to "Ever Been to Ghorman?", the Tarkin Massacre occurred 16 years before the events of the episode. As the events of the episode are established to occur in 3 BBY, the Tarkin Massacre must have occurred in 19 BBY. Therefore, Thela must have been born around 31 BBY.
  614. ↑ Queen's Shadow, which has the effects of the groundquakes at Bromlarch as its central plot point, is set four years after the Invasion of Naboo, dated to 32 BBY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. Thus, the events of Queen's Shadow occurred around 28 BBY. Since Padmé Amidala is established to have been serving the second of her two two-year terms as Monarch of Naboo when she stepped down that year, Amidala's second term began around 30 BBY.
  615. ↑ Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide states that Lyra Erso studied at the University of Rudrig twenty-nine years before the events of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story. As Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Rogue One to 1 BBY, meaning Eros's study must have taken place in 30 BBY.
  616. ↑ Star Wars: Scum and Villainy: Case Files on the Galaxy's Most Notorious establishes that Rinnrivin Di was 57 years old in the year 8004 C.R.C.. From this his birth can be calculated to tge year 7947 C.R.C.; because Scum and Villainy also states that the escape from Cloud City took place in 7980 C.R.C., and Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates that event to 3 ABY, 7947 C.R.C. converts to 30 ABY.
  617. ↑ The events of Thrawn Ascendancy: Greater Good take place around 18 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. Lakris was described as twelve years old during that novel's events, so she must have been born around 30 BBY.
  618. ↑ Age of Republic - Obi-Wan Kenobi 1 takes place in between Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas places in 32 BBY, and Star Wars: Obi-Wan and Anakin. Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide dates Anakin Skywalker's birth to 41 BBY, and since Anakin is 12 years old in Star Wars: Obi-Wan and Anakin, it must take place in 29 BBY. Therefore, Age of Republic - Obi-Wan Kenobi 1 must take place in between 29 BBY and 32 BBY.
  619. ↑ "Anakin Skywalker" — Star Wars Encyclopedia
  620. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of "Part II," including the appearance of the clone trooper veteran, to 9 BBY.  Part II Trivia Gallery | Obi-Wan Kenobi on StarWars.com (backup link) states that Nax is "20 or so" years old. As such, he would have been born in or around 29 BBY.
  621. ↑  Clone Troopers in the Databank (backup link)
  622. ↑ The script of Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith, as seen in Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith Illustrated Screenplay, states that Zett Jukassa is 10 years old. As Star Wars: Timelines dates Revenge of the Sith to 19 BBY, Jukassa must have been born around 29 BBY.
  623. ↑ Star Wars: Scum and Villainy: Case Files on the Galaxy's Most Notorious states that Bom Vimdin was 28 years old in 7977 C.R.C., also giving that date for the Battle of Scarif. Since Star Wars: Timelines dates the battle to 1 BBY, Vimdin must have been born around 29 BBY.
  624. ↑ Thrawn Ascendancy: Greater Good states that Che'ri's tenth starday occurred between the Battle over Primea and the Mission to Rapacc. The battle over Primea was portrayed in Thrawn Ascendancy: Chaos Rising, which took place in 19 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. Greater Good took place around 18 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. Therefore, Che'ri must have had her tenth star day between those two years. A star day celebrated a Chiss' first sight of the stars, which required them to already be born. Therefore, Cheri must have been born by around 29 BBY.
  625. ↑  Star Wars: Padawan's Pride on Audible.com (September 19, 2024) (backup link) states that Padawan's Pride is set three years after the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 32 BBY. Therefore Padawan's Pride must take place in 29 BBY.
  626. ↑ Obi-Wan and Anakin 1
  627. ↑ Adventures in Wild Space: The Cold takes place shortly after the events of Adventures in Wild Space: The Dark, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 18 BBY, and before those of Adventures in Wild Space: The Rescue, which is also set in 18 BBY according to Timelines. Since Lina was 10 years old in Adventures in Wild Space: The Cold, she must have been born around 28 BBY.
  628. ↑ 628.0 628.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "The Zillo Beast"
  629. ↑ The events of "The Final Order" occur parallel to the events of Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 3 ABY. In "The Final Order" Canonhaus says he became a midshipman with the Galactic Republic thirty years earlier.
  630. ↑ 630.0 630.1 Solo: A Star Wars Story: Expanded Edition
  631. ↑ Battlefront II: Inferno Squad establishes Gideon Hask is twenty-seven years old. As this is mentioned three days after the Battle of Yavin, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 0 ABY, it places Hask's birth in 27 BBY.
  632. ↑ Adventures in Wild Space: The Escape is set a "year or so" after the end of the Clone Wars, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates as 19 BBY; thus, it can be deduced that the story takes place around 18 BBY. It can also be deduced that Milo Graf was born circa 27 BBY, since Adventures in Wild Space: The Escape establishes that he was nine years old during that time.
  633. ↑ The events of "Grounded" occur parallel to the events of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 0 ABY. In "Grounded," Kase is said to be "three years shy of thirty," which places her birth in 27 BBY.
  634. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines establishes that Mitth'raw'nuruodo captured a Vagaari gravity well projector around 27 BBY.
  635. ↑ Age of Republic - Count Dooku 1
  636. ↑ Star Wars Rebels: The Visual Guide states that Kanan Jarrus and Hera Syndulla met on Gorse, as depicted in the novel A New Dawn, six years prior to the events of Star Wars Rebels. Star Wars: Galactic Atlas states that Star Wars Rebels begins in the year 5 BBY, thereby dating the events of A New Dawn to 11 BBY. In the novel, Denetrius Vidian reminisces that he reinvented himself fifteen years before, thus setting the date of said reinvention to 26 BBY.
  637. ↑  The Clone Wars Episode Guide: The Deserter on StarWars.com (original site is defunct) states that Shaeeah Lawquane was five years old during the events of "The Deserter." Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the events of the Star Wars: The Clone Wars episode "Landing at Point Rain" to 21 BBY, and Star Wars Helmet Collection: Commander Ponds Highlights of the Saga: The End of Endurance dates the episode "Lethal Trackdown," to the same year. Since  Star Wars: The Clone Wars Chronological Episode Order on StarWars.com (backup link) places "The Deserter" between "Landing at Point Rain" and "Lethal Trackdown," its events must take place in the same year. Because Shaeeah was five years old in 21 BBY, she must have been born in 26 BBY.
  638. ↑  "Innocents of Ryloth" Episode Guide | The Clone Wars on StarWars.com (backup link) establishes that Numa was five years old by the time of the Battle of Ryloth. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas places the Battle of Ryloth to 21 BBY, Numa must have been born in 26 BBY.
  639. ↑  Numa in the Encyclopedia (original site is defunct)
  640. ↑ As stated in TK-462, the eponymous protagonist was twelve years of age in the year Wilhuff Tarkin was promoted to Grand Moff. According to the novel Tarkin, this occurred 14 years before the Battle of Yavin. Thus, the birth of TK-462 must have occurred around 26 years before the battle, which, per the calendar system of used in Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, is around 26 BBY.
  641. ↑ In Aftermath: Life Debt, Gallius Rax's prelude and epilogue chapters are set three decades before the Battle of Jakku of 5 ABY.
  642. ↑ 642.0 642.1 Star Wars: Rogue One: Rebel Dossier
  643. ↑ 643.00 643.01 643.02 643.03 643.04 643.05 643.06 643.07 643.08 643.09 643.10 643.11 643.12 643.13 643.14 Bloodline
  644. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge to 34 ABY. Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge — Star Wars: Datapad states that Sarco Plank was 59 years during the events of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge, meaning he was born in around 25 BBY.
  645. ↑ Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge — Star Wars: Datapad
  646. ↑  The Clone Wars Episode Guide: The Deserter on StarWars.com (original site is defunct) states that Jek Lawquane was four years old during the events of "The Deserter." Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the events of the Star Wars: The Clone Wars episode "Landing at Point Rain" to 21 BBY, and Star Wars Helmet Collection: Commander Ponds Highlights of the Saga: The End of Endurance dates the episode "Lethal Trackdown," to the same year. Since  Star Wars: The Clone Wars Chronological Episode Order on StarWars.com (backup link) places "The Deserter" between "Landing at Point Rain" and "Lethal Trackdown," its events must take place in the same year. Because Jek was four years old in 21 BBY, he must have been born in 25 BBY.
  647. ↑ Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide establishes that Cassian Jeron Andor was twenty-seven years old during the events of Andor Season 1, the events of which are dated to 5 BBY by that same book.  Cassian Andor in the Databank (backup link) establishes that Andor was nine years old when he left Kenari, with  "Reckoning" Episode Guide | Andor on StarWars.com (backup link) establishing that this occurred before the Confederacy of Independent Systems had formed. Rise of the Separatists and The Star Wars Book establish that the formation the CIS occurred two years before the First Battle of Geonosis, which Star Wars: Timelines and other sources date to take place in 22 BBY. For all this information to be true, Cassian Andor had to have been born in 33 BBY, with his departure taking place after his birthday, but before the Raxus Address in 24 BBY, and the events of Andor taking place before his birthday in 5 BBY.
  648. ↑ Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel gives the time in which the construction and overhaul facility was partially engineered by Orson Krennic as five years prior to his visit to Kartoosh to discuss mining and research in the Western Reaches, which occurs in 18 BBY according to Star Wars: Timelines. Therefore, Krennic's work on the shipyards occured in 23 BBY.
  649. ↑ 649.00 649.01 649.02 649.03 649.04 649.05 649.06 649.07 649.08 649.09 649.10 Lost Stars
  650. ↑ Star Wars: Scum and Villainy: Case Files on the Galaxy's Most Notorious establishes that Ketsu Onyo was 18 standard years old four years before the Battle of Scarif. Star Wars: Timelines dates this event to 1 BBY. It can then be calculated that Onyo was 18 years old in 5 BBY and was born around 23 BBY.
  651. ↑ As stated in TK-462, the eponymous protagonist was twelve years of age in the year Wilhuff Tarkin was promoted to Grand Moff. According to the novel Tarkin, this occurred 14 years before the Battle of Yavin, which corresponds to 14 BBY per Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. Thus, the birth of TK-462 must have occurred around 26 BBY. Since Xea is established to have been born when TK-462 was four years old in the aforementioned short story, Xea was born around 22 BBY.
  652. ↑ 652.0 652.1 Star Wars Helmet Collection: Sidon Ithano Databank A-Z: Darth Tyranus
  653. ↑ 653.0 653.1 Star Wars Helmet Collection: Guavian Death Guard Databank A-Z: Hera Syndulla–Ahsoka Tano
  654. ↑ Hyperspace Stories 1
  655. ↑ 655.0 655.1 Queen's Hope
  656. ↑ 656.0 656.1 656.2 656.3 656.4 Brotherhood
  657. ↑ 657.0 657.1 657.2 657.3 657.4 Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Brotherhood to 22 BBY. At the end of Brotherhood, Jedi Knights were officially made generals; their promotion occurs prior to the Operation on Hissrich per the opening crawl of Jedi of the Republic – Mace Windu 1.
  658. ↑ Jedi of the Republic – Mace Windu 1
  659. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "The Deserter"
  660. ↑ 660.0 660.1 "501 Plus One" — Age of Republic Special 1
  661. ↑ 661.0 661.1 661.2 661.3 "501 Plus One" depicts Anakin Skywalker without a scar during the Battle of Arantara, while Star Wars Adventures: The Clone Wars – Battle Tales 1 and Age of Republic - Anakin Skywalker 1 establishes that Skywalker has the scar by the time of the mission to Benglor and the Battle of Corvair. Thus, the Battle of Arantara must have occurred prior.
  662. ↑ 662.0 662.1 Star Wars Adventures: The Clone Wars – Battle Tales 1
  663. ↑ Age of Republic - Anakin Skywalker 1

  679. ↑ 679.00 679.01 679.02 679.03 679.04 679.05 679.06 679.07 679.08 679.09 679.10 679.11 679.12 679.13 679.14 Star Wars: Galactic Atlas
  680. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Cloak of Darkness"
  681. ↑ 681.0 681.1 681.2 681.3 681.4 681.5 681.6 681.7 Star Wars Helmet Collection: Senate Guard Highlights of the Saga: The Betrayal of Captain Argyus dates the events of the Star Wars: The Clone Wars episode "Cloak of Darkness" to 21 BBY and Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the episode "Blue Shadow Virus" also to 21 BBY. Thus, events of the episodes that take place between the episodes "Cloak of Darkness" and "Blue Shadow Virus" per  Star Wars: The Clone Wars Chronological Episode Order on StarWars.com (backup link) must also have taken place in 21 BBY.
  682. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Lair of Grievous"
  683. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Dooku Captured"
  684. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "The Gungan General"
  685. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Jedi Crash"

  687. ↑ 687.0 687.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Trespass"
  688. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Blue Shadow Virus"
  689. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Mystery of a Thousand Moons"
  690. ↑ Star Wars: Galactic Atlas places the raid on Nuvo Vindi's laboratory to 21 BBY. As the mission to Iego occurs concurrently to the raid, it must also have taken place in 21 BBY.
  691. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Storm Over Ryloth"
  692. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Innocents of Ryloth"
  693. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Liberty on Ryloth"
  694. ↑ 694.0 694.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Holocron Heist"
  695. ↑ 695.0 695.1 695.2 695.3 695.4 Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates both the conclusion of the Battle of Ryloth and the second mission to Rodia to 21 BBY. As the two events are depicted in Star Wars: The Clone Wars episodes "Liberty on Ryloth" and "Children of the Force," the events of the episodes between the aforementioned, as chronologically ordered by  Star Wars: The Clone Wars Chronological Episode Order on StarWars.com (backup link), must have taken place in 21 BBY.
  696. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Cargo of Doom"
  697. ↑ 697.0 697.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Children of the Force"
  698. ↑ 698.0 698.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Bounty Hunters"
  699. ↑ 699.00 699.01 699.02 699.03 699.04 699.05 699.06 699.07 699.08 699.09 699.10 699.11 699.12 699.13 699.14 699.15 699.16 699.17 699.18 699.19 699.20 699.21 699.22 699.23 Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates both the end of the Battle of Ryloth, featured in "Liberty on Ryloth," and the Battle of Kamino, featured in "ARC Troopers," to 21 BBY. Thus, events shown in episodes chronologically placed between "Liberty on Ryloth" and "ARC Troopers" per  Star Wars: The Clone Wars Chronological Episode Order on StarWars.com (backup link) must also have occurred in 21 BBY.

  718. ↑ 718.0 718.1 718.2 718.3 718.4 The Star Wars Book dates the Invasion of Kamino and the Confederate–Republic peace initiative, featured in "ARC Troopers" and "Heroes on Both Sides" respectively, to 21 BBY. Therefore, events of the episodes Star Wars: The Clone Wars television series chronologically placed between "ARC Troopers" and "Heroes on Both Sides" per  Star Wars: The Clone Wars Chronological Episode Order on StarWars.com (backup link) must also have occurred in 21 BBY.
  719. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Hostage Crisis"
  720. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Hunt for Ziro"
  721. ↑ Star Wars Forces of Destiny — "The Padawan Path"
  722. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Heroes on Both Sides"
  723. ↑ Star Wars: Absolutely Everything You Need to Know, Updated and Expanded
  724. ↑ Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel establishes that Jyn Erso was born to Galen and Lyra Erso on Vallt one year after the First Battle of Geonosis, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 22 BBY. Therefore, Erso was born in 21 BBY.
  725. ↑ In Ahsoka, the parents of Kaedan and Miara Larte is established to have been killed in a farming accident that also injured Selda when Kaeden was fourteen years old. As the novel, in which Kaeden is seventeen years old, also establishes its events to have taken place one year after the founding of the Galactic Empire, and the Empire's founding is dated to 19 BBY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, the accident occurred around 21 BBY.
  726. ↑ 726.0 726.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Pursuit of Peace"
  727. ↑ 727.0 727.1 The Star Wars Book dates the Confederate–Republic peace initiative, featured in "Heroes on Both Sides," to 21 BBY. Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of "Senate Murders" to 21 BBY. Thus, events shown in episodes chronologically placed between "Heroes on Both Sides" and "Nightsisters" per  Star Wars: The Clone Wars Chronological Episode Order on StarWars.com (backup link) must have occurred between 21 and 20 BBY.
  728. ↑ 728.0 728.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Senate Murders"
  729. ↑ 729.0 729.1 Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of "Senate Murders" to 21 BBY.
  730. ↑ Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide states that the Death Star began construction over Geonosis twenty years before the Rogue One mission. Since the latter is dated to 1 BBY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, the Death Star must have began construction over Geonosis in 21 BBY.
  731. ↑ 731.00 731.01 731.02 731.03 731.04 731.05 731.06 731.07 731.08 731.09 731.10 731.11 731.12 731.13 731.14 731.15 731.16 731.17 731.18 731.19 731.20 731.21 731.22 731.23 Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the events of both "Nightsisters" and "Water War" to 20 BBY. Thus, events shown in episodes chronologically placed between "Nightsisters" and "Water War" per  Star Wars: The Clone Wars Chronological Episode Order on StarWars.com (backup link) must have also occurred in 20 BBY.

  752. ↑ 752.0 752.1 752.2 752.3 752.4 Star Wars Helmet Collection: Commander Neyo Highlights of the Saga: The Unlikely Heroes dates the events of "Secret Weapons" to 20 BBY. Star Wars: Galactic Atlas also dates the events of "Crisis on Naboo" to 20 BBY. As such, episodes chronologically placed by  Star Wars: The Clone Wars Chronological Episode Order on StarWars.com (backup link) between the two aforementioned episodes must also take place in 20 BBY.
  753. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Bounty"
  754. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Brothers"
  755. ↑ "Hide and Seek" — Star Wars Adventures (2017) 20
  756. ↑ 756.0 756.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Revenge"
  757. ↑ 757.0 757.1 Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Tipping Points"
  758. ↑ 758.0 758.1 758.2 Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the partnership between Ahsoka Tano and the Onderon rebels, which was first depicted in "Front Runners," to 20 BBY. Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Character Encyclopedia - Join the Battle! dates the events of "The Gathering" to be 20 years before the events of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, which corresponds to 20 BBY according to Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. As  Star Wars: The Clone Wars Chronological Episode Order on StarWars.com (backup link) establishes that the events of "Tipping Points" occurred between the events of Front Runners and The Gathering, it also must have happened in 20 BBY.
  759. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "The Gathering"
  760. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "A Test of Strength"
  761. ↑ 761.0 761.1 761.2 761.3 761.4 According to  Star Wars: The Clone Wars Chronological Episode Order on StarWars.com (backup link), the events of "A Test of Strength," "Bound for Rescue," and "A Necessary Bond" occur after "The Gathering." Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Character Encyclopedia - Join the Battle! dates the events of that episode to be 20 years before the events of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, which corresponds to 20 BBY according to Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. The chronological episode guide also says those episodes are set before "Secret Weapons," which occurs in 20 BBY according to Star Wars Helmet Collection: Commander Neyo Highlights of the Saga: The Unlikely Heroes, meaning they all occur in 20 BBY. Furthermore, the guide dates "A Sunny Day in the Void," "Missing in Action," "Point of No Return," and "Revival" to occur after "Secret Weapons" but before the end of the Clone Wars, which Galactic Atlas says was in 19 BBY. Therefore, the events of these episodes must occur 20 or 19 BBY.

  831. ↑ Star Wars: The Bad Batch — "Rescue on Ryloth"
  832. ↑ Star Wars: The Bad Batch — "Infested"
  833. ↑ 833.0 833.1 Star Wars: The Bad Batch — "War-Mantle"

  835. ↑ Star Wars: Andor — "Ever Been to Ghorman?"
  836. ↑ 836.0 836.1 836.2 "Truth and Consequences" features the official formation of the Stormtrooper Corps, which Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide dates to 18 BBY. Since the events of "The Clone Conspiracy" occur directly before "Truth and Consequences," it must also take place in 18 BBY. Therefore, all season 2 of Star Wars: The Bad Batch episodes proceeding "The Clone Conspiracy" occur between 19 BBY and 18 BBY, and all season 2 episodes set after "Truth and Consequences" must occur during or after 18 BBY.
  837. ↑ 837.0 837.1 Star Wars: The Bad Batch — "Spoils of War"
  838. ↑ Star Wars: The Bad Batch — "The Solitary Clone"
