  861. ↑ Adventures in Wild Space: The Escape
  862. ↑ Star Wars: Adventures in Wild Space books are set in 18 BBY according to Star Wars: Timelines.
  863. ↑ Kanan 5
  864. ↑ Darth Vader Annual 2
  865. ↑ The novel Tarkin mentions that the Antar Atrocity took place one year after the Clone Wars ended.
  866. ↑ 866.0 866.1 Darth Vader (2017) 13
  867. ↑ 867.0 867.1 Darth Vader (2017) 15
  868. ↑ Darth Vader (2017) 16
  869. ↑ 869.0 869.1 Darth Vader (2017) 17
  870. ↑ 870.0 870.1 870.2 870.3 870.4 Age of Rebellion - Darth Vader 1
  871. ↑ "Rebel Rousers: Our Top Ten Rebels" — Star Wars Insider 203 states that Mon Mothma began organizing an anti-Empire coalition eighteen years before the events of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, which corresponds to 18 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. Therefore, Mothma must have began organizing the anti-Empire coalition in 18 BBY.
  872. ↑ "Mustafar and Other Planets in the Outer Rim" — Star Wars Encyclopedia
  873. ↑ 873.0 873.1 873.2 Star Wars: The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire
  874. ↑ Star Wars: The Clone Wars — "Victory and Death"
  875. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge to 34 ABY. Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge — Star Wars: Datapad states that Gwellis Bagnoro was 51 years during the events of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge, meaning he was born in around 17 BBY.
  876. ↑ Battlefront: Twilight Company
  877. ↑ "The Eye" takes place days after "Kassa," which dates itself to 5 BBY. As "The Eye" establishes Leonart Beehaz to be 12 years old, he must have been born around 17 BBY.
  878. ↑ "True Love" — Tales from a Galaxy Far, Far Away: Aliens: Volume I
  879. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Resistance Reborn to 34 ABY. As Resistance Reborn establishes that Nifera Shu is fifty years old, she must have been born around 16 BBY.
  880. ↑ "Time of Death" — From a Certain Point of View
  881. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of "Chapter 14: The Tragedy" to 9 ABY. In "Chapter 14: The Tragedy," Boba Fett states in that his chain code was encoded into his armor twenty-five years prior to the events of the chapter. Therefore, Boba's chain code was encoded into his armor around the year 16 BBY.
  882. ↑ "Coruscant: Whispers of Rebellion" states the Axis network was founded a decade before the events of Star Wars: Andor, which takes place in 5 BBY per the reasoning here.
  883. ↑ Star Wars Helmet Collection: Commander Neyo Databank A-Z: Admiral Kilian–Kowakian states that Kitwarr was ten years old during the events of Star Wars Rebels: Spark of Rebellion, which takes place in 5 BBY according to Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. Therefore, Kitwarr was born in 15 BBY.
  884. ↑ Alphabet Squadron states that Wyl Lark is nineteen years old during the events of the novel, which is placed in 4 ABY due to the fact that it takes place shortly after Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi, which is dated to 4 ABY in Star Wars: Galactic Atlas.
  885. ↑ 885.00 885.01 885.02 885.03 885.04 885.05 885.06 885.07 885.08 885.09 885.10 885.11 885.12 885.13 885.14 885.15 885.16 885.17 Thrawn
  886. ↑ Per the reasoning here, the events of Thrawn begin around 15 BBY.
  887. ↑ Darth Vader (2017) 22
  888. ↑ "TK-462" — Star Wars Insider 166
  889. ↑ As stated in TK-462, the eponymous protagonist was twelve years of age in the year Wilhuff Tarkin was promoted to Grand Moff. According to the novel Tarkin, this occurred 14 years before the Battle of Yavin, which corresponds to 14 BBY per Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. Thus, the birth of TK-462 must have occurred around 26 BBY. Since TK-462's mother is established to have been dead by the time TK-462 was twelve years old in the aforementioned short story, the mother died around 14 BBY.
  890. ↑ 890.0 890.1 890.2 890.3 Lords of the Sith
  891. ↑ 891.0 891.1 891.2 891.3 In Star Wars canon, the "Story of Star Wars" timeline of novels places the events of Lords of the Sith as having taken place before the events of Tarkin, both having taken place approximately 5 years after the events of Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith.
  892. ↑ 892.0 892.1 892.2 892.3 892.4 Tarkin states that "five standard years have passed since Darth Sidious proclaimed himself galactic Emperor," an event shown in Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the events of Revenge of the Sith to 19 BBY, the events of Tarkin occurred in 14 BBY.
  893. ↑ As stated in TK-462, the eponymous protagonist was twelve years of age in the year Wilhuff Tarkin was promoted to Grand Moff. According to the novel Tarkin, this occurred 14 years before the Battle of Yavin, which corresonds to 14 BBY per Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. Thus, the events of TK-462, which include protagonist's application to become a stormtrooper, took place around 14 BBY.
  894. ↑ 894.0 894.1 894.2 894.3 894.4 894.5 894.6 894.7 894.8 Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
  895. ↑ "Mercy Mission" — The Rise of the Empire
  896. ↑ Most Wanted
  897. ↑ 897.00 897.01 897.02 897.03 897.04 897.05 897.06 897.07 897.08 897.09 897.10 Solo: A Star Wars Story
  898. ↑ Han Solo - Imperial Cadet 3
  899. ↑ Han Solo - Imperial Cadet 5
  900. ↑ As stated in TK-462, the eponymous protagonist graduated to the Imperial Academy of Eriadu after a year at the Junior Academy.
  901. ↑ Darth Vader (2017) 23
  902. ↑ Darth Vader (2017) 24
  903. ↑ Darth Vader (2017) 25
  904. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that Fortress Vader was completed forty-six years before the Starkiller Incident, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY. Therefore, the fortress's construction, as well as the Battle of Fortress Vader, must have happened in 12 BBY.
  905. ↑ 905.0 905.1 905.2 Star Wars Rebels — "Legacy"
  906. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Shadow of the Sith, in which Dathan is thirty-three years old, to 21 ABY, therefore placing Dathan's birth on Exegol in 12 BBY.
  907. ↑ Star Wars Jedi: Battle Scars dates itself to a few years after the events of Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order, which take place in 14 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. Therefore, the events of Jedi: Battle Scars must take place in 12 BBY at the earliest. Additionally, the novel dates itself within the years before Star Wars Jedi: Survivor, the events of which take place in 9 BBY per the reasoning here. Within Jedi: Survivor, it is also noted the Stinger Mantis crew has been broken up for "years." As the crew are together in Jedi: Battle Scars, the latest it can take place is 11 BBY. Therefore, the events of Jedi: Battle Scars occur between 12 and 11 BBY.
  908. ↑ Star Wars Jedi: Battle Scars
  909. ↑ 909.0 909.1 909.2 909.3 909.4 Star Wars Jedi: Survivor
  910. ↑ Enfys Nest's mother died after her daughter turned sixteen in 11 BBY but before the raid on Gargon, which Star Wars: Scum and Villainy: Case Files on the Galaxy's Most Notorious dates to 7966 C.R.C., which corresponds to 11 BBY. This means her death also took place in 11 BBY.
  911. ↑ Star Wars: Women of the Galaxy
  912. ↑ In Thrawn, the Cyphar Incident, the Attack on the Sempre, and the Battle at Lansend Twenty-Six take place between the battle over Umbara and the battle for Botajef, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 11 BBY and 9 BBY respectively.
  913. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire
  914. ↑ 914.0 914.1 Star Wars: Rogue One: Secret Mission
  915. ↑ 915.0 915.1 915.2 915.3 915.4 915.5 915.6 915.7 Heir to the Jedi
  916. ↑ Lando - Double or Nothing 2
  917. ↑ Lando - Double or Nothing 5
  918. ↑  Solo: A Star Wars Story Books and Comics Revealed on StarWars.com (backup link) places Star Wars: Lando - Double or Nothing right before Solo: A Star Wars Story, which Solo: A Star Wars Story The Official Guide places nine years after the founding of the Galactic Empire, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 19 BBY. Therefore, Star Wars: Lando - Double or Nothing takes place in 10 BBY.
  919. ↑ Solo: A Star Wars Story The Official Guide dates Han Solo's expulsion from the Imperial Academy in Solo: A Star Wars Story to nine years after the formation of the Galactic Empire. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the Empire's founding to 19 BBY, Solo's expulsion must have occurred in 10 BBY.
  920. ↑ 920.0 920.1  Star Wars: Beyond Victory - A Mixed Reality Playset from ILM – Exclusive Reveal on StarWars.com (backup link) establishes that Star Wars: Beyond Victory - A Mixed Reality Playset takes place around the time of the events of Solo: A Star Wars Story, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 10 BBY. Therefore, Star Wars: Beyond Victory must take place around 10 BBY.
  921. ↑ Dawn of Rebellion establishes that the Death Star was over Scarif months after the sterilization of Geonosis.
  922. ↑ "By Whatever Sun" — From a Certain Point of View
  923. ↑ In "By Whatever Sun" — From a Certain Point of View, which takes place at the Royal Award Ceremony, Miara Larte reminisces that her promotion to captain took place nine years before. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas states that the ceremony took place in 0 ABY, Miara Larte's promotion could be dated to around 9 BBY.
  924. ↑ 924.0 924.1 Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi — "Part I"
  925. ↑ Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi — "Part II"

  931. ↑ War of the Bounty Hunters – Boushh 1, set between Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back and Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi, the events of which are respectively placed in 3 ABY and 4 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, establishes that Keln would have been twelve years old, so Keln must have been born in 8 BBY.
  932. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Thrawn: Treason to 1 BBY. As Thrawn: Treason establishes that Un'hee is seven years old, she must have been born around 8 BBY.
  933. ↑ Star Wars (2015) 20

  950. ↑  "Narkina 5" Trivia Gallery | Andor on StarWars.com (backup link) (Slide 6) establishes that the events of "Narkina 5" take place in 5 BBY, while the events of "Rix Road" take place in 7972 C.R.C., which corresponds to 5 BBY per the dating systems of Scum and Villainy: Case Files on the Galaxy's Most Notorious and Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. As the events of "Nobody's Listening!" take place in between these two episodes, dated to 5 BBY, and the episode states that the theft at Lozash took place a year prior, the theft of the Imperial masking device must take place in 6 BBY.

  955. ↑ According to Star Wars: The Force Awakens Beginner Game Tiras Or'un was eleven years old when the Galactic Empire and New Republic fleets battled above Jakku. Star Wars: Galactic Atlas placed the Battle in 5 ABY, thus Tiras Or'un must had been born in 6 BBY.

  1060. ↑ Star Wars Rebels — "Zero Hour"
  1061. ↑ "Tales from Wild Space: Adventures in Wookiee-Sitting" — Star Wars Adventures (2017) 3
  1062. ↑ "Tales from Wild Space: Adventures in Wookiee-Sitting" takes place after the formation of the Rebel Alliance, but prior to the Battle of Scarif. Star Wars: Timelines dates the foundation of the Rebel Alliance to 2 BBY, and the Battle of Scarif to 1 BBY. Therefore Tales from Wild Space: "Adventures in Wookiee-Sitting" takes place some time between Between 2 and 1 BBY
  1063. ↑ 1063.0 1063.1 Star Wars: Timelines dates the Alliance's founding to 2 BBY and the Battle of Scarif to 1 BBY. Thus the events the Alliance and its 61st Mobile Infantry took part in, which the Rogue One: A Star Wars Story novelization establishes occurred before the Battle of Scarif, including the battles mentioned in Battlefront: Twilight Company, must have occurred between 2 BBY and 1 BBY.
  1064. ↑ Battlefront: Twilight Company sets the battles at Mygeeto prior to when Hazram Namir joined the Sixty-First Mobile Infantry. The novel also establishes that Namir was part of the company two months before the destruction of the Death Star, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to the end of 1 BBY. The battle of Mygeeto must therefore have occurred by 1 BBY, but after the Alliance's founding, which Timelines dates to 2 BBY.
  1065. ↑ Star Wars: Secrets of the Empire
  1066. ↑ Star Wars Rebels: Heroes of Mandalore
  1067. ↑ Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett — "Chapter 5: Return of the Mandalorian"
  1068. ↑ 1068.0 1068.1 Star Wars: The Mandalorian — "Chapter 8: Redemption"
  1069. ↑ 1069.0 1069.1 Star Wars Rebels — "In the Name of the Rebellion"
  1070. ↑ 1070.0 1070.1 1070.2 Star Wars Rebels — "The Occupation"
  1071. ↑ Star Wars Rebels — "Crawler Commandeers"
  1072. ↑ Star Wars Rebels — "Rebel Assault"
  1073. ↑ 1073.0 1073.1 1073.2 1073.3 1073.4 1073.5 1073.6 Thrawn: Treason
  1074. ↑ Star Wars Rebels — "Jedi Night"
  1075. ↑ Star Wars Rebels — "Family Reunion – and Farewell"
  1076. ↑ Farnay's mother's died a year before the events of The Weapon of a Jedi: A Luke Skywalker Adventure, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 0 ABY. Therefore, Farnay's mother died in 1 BBY per Timelines.
  1077. ↑ The section of Battlefront: Twilight Company where the Battle of the Tower takes place is set two months prior to the Battle of Yavin, which corresponds to 1 BBY per Star Wars: Timelines. Therefore, the Battle of the Tower must take place in 1 BBY.
  1078. ↑ The flashback sequence involving an adolescent Bansu Ro in Tie Fighter 4 is set in troubling times, during the Imperial Senate's existence, and years before the death of the Emperor as mentioned in Tie Fighter 5. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas: Updated Edition states that the Senate was dissolved in 1 BBY and dates the death of the Emperor to 4 ABY, it can be deduced that the flashback sequence takes place around 1 BBY.

  1084. ↑ Star Wars Outlaws establishes Calys died at the Battle of Scarif, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 1 BBY.

  1104. ↑ 1104.0 1104.1 Darth Vader (2015) 2
  1105. ↑ The events of this issue take place between the Battle of Yavin, which marked the beginning of 0 ABY per Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, and Vader Down 1, the events of which, specifically the Battle of Vrogas Vas, is placed in 0 ABY by The Star Wars Book. As such, the events of this issue must also take place in 0 ABY.

Thus, the assault took place in 1 ABY. Hence, this event occurred in 0–1 ABY.

Therefore, this event must take place between 1 ABY and 3 ABY.

  1151. ↑ 1151.0 1151.1 Empire Ascendant 1
  1152. ↑ Star Wars: Galactic Atlas states that Echo Base was constructed in 3 ABY.
  1153. ↑ Star Wars: Target Vader is set before Empire Ascendant 1, which in turn takes place before the Battle of Hoth.
  1154. ↑ Target Vader 1
  1155. ↑ Target Vader 4
  1156. ↑ Target Vader 5
  1157. ↑ Target Vader 6
  1158. ↑ "Against All Odds" — From a Certain Point of View: The Empire Strikes Back
  1159. ↑ 1159.0 1159.1 Doctor Aphra (2016) 40
  1160. ↑ Doctor Aphra (2016) 39 depicts an intact rebel base on Hoth, and is set prior to Empire Ascendant 1, which in turn took place before the Battle of Hoth. Star Wars: Galactic Atlas states that Echo Base was constructed and destroyed in 3 ABY, and places the Battle of Hoth in the same year, meaning the events of Doctor Aphra 39 are set in 3 ABY. Thus, events set shortly after and prior to Doctor Aphra 39, including those of Doctor Aphra issues 37—40 and Empire Ascendant 1, must also take place in 3 ABY. Since Doctor Aphra 40 establishes that the Empire was delayed in their attack on the rebels on Hoth by a few weeks, the events of Doctor Aphra 37—40 and Empire Ascendant 1 also took place weeks from the Battle of Hoth.
  1161. ↑ 1161.0 1161.1 1161.2 1161.3 1161.4 1161.5 1161.6 1161.7 1161.8 Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back

  1302. ↑ TIE Fighter 1
  1303. ↑ Star Wars Forces of Destiny — "Bounty Hunted"
  1304. ↑ 1304.0 1304.1 Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that Maz Kanata helped Leia Organa acquire Boushh's armor on Ord Mantell thirty years before the Starkiller Incident, which corresponds to 4 ABY per Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. Additionally, Star Wars: Card Trader Set: Base Series 1, Card: Boushh - Bounty Hunter states that Boushh died prior to Leia Organa's rescue of Han Solo, which the Galactic Atlas dates to 4 ABY.
  1305. ↑ Star Wars: Card Trader Set: Base Series 1, Card: Boushh - Bounty Hunter
  1306. ↑ Age of Rebellion - Princess Leia 1
  1307. ↑ 1307.00 1307.01 1307.02 1307.03 1307.04 1307.05 1307.06 1307.07 1307.08 1307.09 1307.10 1307.11 1307.12 Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi
  1308. ↑ Return of the Jedi: Beware the Power of the Dark Side!
  1309. ↑ 1309.00 1309.01 1309.02 1309.03 1309.04 1309.05 1309.06 1309.07 1309.08 1309.09 1309.10 1309.11 1309.12 1309.13 Star Wars Battlefront II
  1310. ↑ Age of Rebellion - Luke Skywalker 1
  1311. ↑  15 Details Making the Locations in Star Wars™ Battlefront™ II Feel Alive on Electronic Arts' official website (backup link)
  1312. ↑ 1312.0 1312.1 Star Wars: The Mandalorian — "Chapter 5: The Gunslinger"
  1313. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of "Chapter 5: The Gunslinger" to 9 ABY.
  1314. ↑ 1314.0 1314.1 Return of the Jedi – The Rebellion 1
  1315. ↑  The Temperance in the Databank (backup link)
  1316. ↑ 1316.0 1316.1 Battle of Jakku — Insurgency Rising 1
  1317. ↑ 1317.0 1317.1 1317.2 1317.3 1317.4 1317.5 1317.6 Before the Awakening
  1318. ↑ Shattered Empire 1
  1319. ↑ 1319.0 1319.1 1319.2 Shattered Empire 2
  1320. ↑ 1320.0 1320.1 "A Race for Answers" — Star Wars Adventures (2017) 23
  1321. ↑ The Princess and the Scoundrel
  1322. ↑ Battle of Jakku — Insurgency Rising 2
  1323. ↑ Shattered Empire 2 places the events of Operation: Cinder on Naboo twenty days after the Battle of Endor. Shattered Empire 3 depicts this raid as taking place concurrently, so the date can be extrapolated from the second issue.
  1324. ↑ Shattered Empire 3
  1325. ↑ Shattered Empire 4 states that the mission to Vetine occurred three months after the Battle of Endor, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 4 ABY. Additionally, Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that the New Republic was officially founded from the Rebel Alliance one year after the Battle of Endor, and twenty-nine years before the Starkiller Incident, which Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY, thereby placing the formation of the New Republic from the Rebel Alliance in 5 ABY. Since the Alliance participated in the mission to Vetine in Shattered Empire 4, the time of 3 months after the Battle of Endor must have also taken place in 4 ABY.
  1326. ↑ 1326.0 1326.1 1326.2 Shattered Empire 4
  1327. ↑ 1327.0 1327.1 1327.2 1327.3 1327.4 1327.5 Victory's Price: An Alphabet Squadron Novel
  1328. ↑ 1328.0 1328.1 1328.2 1328.3 Alphabet Squadron
  1329. ↑ 1329.0 1329.1 Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: Expanded Edition
  1330. ↑ 1330.0 1330.1 Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary provides this information, and dates the Jedi training of Leia Organa as between her wedding with Han Solo and the birth of Ben Solo, with both having occurred thirty years before the Starkiller Incident, which is dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. As such, Organa's training must have taken place in 4 ABY, and specifically in between her wedding and the birth of Ben Solo. Moreover, Aftermath alludes to Organa not having been trained in the ways of the Force, thus the chronological placement of Organa's training as after the events of Aftermath.
  1331. ↑ 1331.0 1331.1 Star Wars: Uprising
  1332. ↑ "Blade Squadron: Zero Hour" — Star Wars Insider 160
  1333. ↑  The Overseer in the Databank (backup link)
  1334. ↑ 1334.0 1334.1 Shadow Fall: An Alphabet Squadron Novel
  1335. ↑ 1335.0 1335.1 1335.2  the Starhawk in the Databank (backup link)
  1336. ↑ 1336.0 1336.1  STAR WARS™: Squadrons Maps and Locations on Electronic Arts' official website (June 19, 2020) (backup link)
  1337. ↑ Star Wars: Galactic Atlas says Shara Bey and Kes Dameron moved to Yavin 4 in 5 ABY. Before the Awakening dates the move to 6 months after the Battle of Endor.
  1338. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that the New Republic was officially founded from the Rebel Alliance one year after the Battle of Endor, and twenty-nine years before the Starkiller Incident, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY, thereby placing the formation of the New Republic from the Rebel Alliance in 5 ABY.
  1339. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Shadow Fall: An Alphabet Squadron Novel to 5 ABY.
  1340. ↑ Battle of Jakku — Republic Under Siege 1
  1341. ↑ Battle of Jakku — Republic Under Siege 4
  1342. ↑ 1342.0 1342.1 1342.2 1342.3 Battle of Jakku — Last Stand 1
  1343. ↑ 1343.0 1343.1 1343.2 1343.3 1343.4 1343.5 1343.6 1343.7 Phasma
  1344. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that Luke Skywalker began searching for lost lore of the Force twenty-nine years before the Starkiller Incident, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY. Therefore, Skywalker began his search in 5 ABY.
  1345. ↑ 1345.0 1345.1 1345.2 1345.3 Star Wars Resistance — "The Recruit"
  1346. ↑ According to Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary, Rose Tico was twenty-four years old one year after the Starkiller Incident. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the Incident to 34 ABY, Tico was born in 11 ABY. Since The Last Jedi: Bomber Command states that Paige Tico was five years older than Rose, the former could be calculated to have been born in 6 ABY.
  1347. ↑ 1347.0 1347.1 The Last Jedi: Cobalt Squadron
  1348. ↑ 1348.0 1348.1 Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Phasma to 34 ABY. Phasma takes place a few weeks before the beginning of Galaxy's Edge: Black Spire, which begins during the events of Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens, which is dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. "Resistance Revisited" – Star Wars Insider 190 establishes that the events of Star Wars Resistance Season One take place thirty years after the Battle of Endor, which corresponds to 34 ABY according to Star Wars: Galactic Atlas, and Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary places the beginning of Star Wars Resistance half a year prior to Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens. Therefore, the year 34 ABY begins at least half a year prior to Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens, and since a few weeks is shorter than half a year, Phasma must be set between "The Recruit" and Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens, thereby placing it in 34 ABY. Phasma establishes that Phasma is the same age as Siv, who in a chapter set twelve years before the present day events, is stated to be sixteen. Therefore, Siv and Phasma were both born on Parnassos in 6 ABY.
  1349. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary gives Simero Quonit's age during the events of Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker as twenty-nine years old. The reference book also places the events of The Rise of Skywalker one year after the Hosnian Cataclysm, which is dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. As such, The Rise of Skywalker must take place in 35 ABY, and Simero Quonit must have been born in 6 ABY.
  1350. ↑ 1350.0 1350.1 1350.2 Poe Dameron 8
  1351. ↑ Marvel's New Star Wars Series to Explore the New Republic Erae by Schedeen, Jessie on IGN (January 21, 2025) (backup link) states that the events of Star Wars (2025) begin around two years after the events of Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 4 ABY. Therefore, the events of Star Wars must take place around 6 ABY.
  1352. ↑ "Past is Prologue" — Free Comic Book Day 2025: Star Wars 1
  1353. ↑ 1353.0 1353.1 1353.2 1353.3 Star Wars (2025) 1
  1354. ↑ Star Wars (2025) 2
  1355. ↑ 1355.0 1355.1 Star Wars (2025) 3
  1356. ↑ Star Wars (2025) 4
  1357. ↑ Star Wars (2025) 5
  1358. ↑ 1358.0 1358.1 Star Wars (2025) 6
  1359. ↑ 1359.0 1359.1 Star Wars (2025) 9
  1360. ↑ Poe Dameron: Flight Log
  1361. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of "Chapter 1: The Mandalorian" of The Mandalorian Season One to 9 ABY. In addition, "A Certain Point of View" — Star Wars Insider 228 also dates "Part Seven: Dreams and Madness" to nine years after the events of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, which corresponds to 9 ABY per Timelines. "Part Seven: Dreams and Madness" takes place after the liberation of Mandalore, which is the main event depicted in "Chapter 23: The Spies" and "Chapter 24: The Return," the final two episodes of The Mandalorian Season Three. Therefore, Seasons One through Three of Star Wars: The Mandalorian must all be set in 9 ABY as well.
  1362. ↑ Star Wars: The Mandalorian — "Chapter 1: The Mandalorian"
  1363. ↑ Star Wars: The Mandalorian — "Chapter 3: The Sin"
  1364. ↑ Star Wars: The Mandalorian — "Chapter 4: Sanctuary"

  1366. ↑ Star Wars: The Mandalorian — "Chapter 9: The Marshal"

  1380. ↑ 1380.0 1380.1 1380.2 1380.3 Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett — "Chapter 7: In the Name of Honor"
  1381. ↑ 1381.0 1381.1 1381.2 Star Wars: The Mandalorian — "Chapter 17: The Apostate"
  1382. ↑ 1382.0 1382.1 1382.2 Star Wars: The Mandalorian — "Chapter 19: The Convert"

  1384. ↑ 1384.0 1384.1 Star Wars: The Mandalorian — "Chapter 21: The Pirate"
  1385. ↑ 1385.0 1385.1 Star Wars: The Mandalorian — "Chapter 23: The Spies"

  1387. ↑ Star Wars: Ahsoka — "Part Four: Fallen Jedi"
  1388. ↑ Star Wars: Ahsoka — "Part Seven: Dreams and Madness"
  1389. ↑ 1389.0 1389.1 1389.2 1389.3 Star Wars: Ahsoka — "Part Eight: The Jedi, the Witch, and the Warlord"
  1390. ↑ 1390.0 1390.1 Star Wars: Skeleton Crew — "Way, Way Out Past the Barrier"
  1391. ↑ Star Wars: Skeleton Crew — "Very Interesting, As an Astrogation Problem"
  1392. ↑ Star Wars: Skeleton Crew — "You Have a Lot to Learn About Pirates"
  1393. ↑ Star Wars: Skeleton Crew — "Zero Friends Again"
  1394. ↑ Star Wars: Skeleton Crew — "We're Gonna Be In So Much Trouble"
  1395. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary gives CS-9147's age during the events of Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker as twenty-five years old. Since the events of The Rise of Skywalker are dated to 35 ABY by The Star Wars Book, CS-9147 must have been born in 10 ABY.
  1396. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary gives HF-3311's age during the events of Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker as twenty-five years old. Since the events of The Rise of Skywalker take place 35 ABY per the reasoning here, HF-3311 must have been born in 10 ABY.
  1397. ↑ According to Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary, Rose Tico was 24 years old one year after the Starkiller Incident. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the Incident to 34 ABY, Tico was born in 11 ABY.
  1398. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge to 34 ABY. As A Crash of Fate takes place on the day before and day of Izal Garsea's eighteenth birthday, it can be deduced that she was born in 16 ABY. The book also establishes that Ana Tolla was 5 years older than Garsea, therefore we can deduce that Tolla was born in 11 ABY.
  1399. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary gives TX-8080's age during the events of The Rise of Skywalker as twenty-four years old. The reference book also places the events of The Rise of Skywalker one year after the Hosnian Cataclysm, which is dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. As such, The Rise of Skywalker must take place in 35 ABY, and TX-8080 must have been born in 11 ABY.
  1400. ↑ 1400.0 1400.1 1400.2 1400.3 1400.4 1400.5 1400.6 1400.7 Poe Dameron: Free Fall
  1401. ↑ According to Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary, ST-I4191 was 22 years old one year after the Hosnian Cataclysm. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the Cataclysm to 34 ABY, ST-I4191 was born in 13 ABY.
  1402. ↑ According to Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary, Kaydel Ko Connix was 22 years old one year after the Hosnian Cataclysm. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the Cataclysm to 34 ABY, Connix was born in 13 ABY.
  1403. ↑ Star Wars Is BACK…. On TV with Star Wars Resistance! on Jedi News (backup link)
  1404. ↑ Star Wars: The Force Awakens: The Visual Dictionary states that Finn is 23 years old during the events of Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens, which are dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. Therefore, Finn was born in 11 ABY. Since Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that Finn was abducted from his family at the age of three by the First Order, he must have been abducted in 14 ABY.
  1405. ↑ According to Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary, ST-A1215 was 21 years old one year after the "Starkiller Incident." Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the Starkiller Incident to 34 ABY, ST-A1215 was born in 14 ABY.
  1406. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary
  1407. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary dates restoration of the Jedi Order to 19 years before the the Hosnian Cataclysm. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the the Hosnian Cataclysm to 34 ABY, the event must have taken place in 15 ABY.
  1408. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of A Crash of Fate to 34 ABY. As A Crash of Fate establishes that Julen Rakab is nineteen years old, it can be deduced that he was born in 15 ABY.
  1409. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Force Collector to 32 ABY, and also establishes that Karr Nuq Sin is seventeen years old. Therefore, Nuq Sin was born around 15 ABY.
  1410. ↑ A Crash of Fate takes place after the Hosnian Cataclysm, which is dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. As the novel takes place on the day before and day of Izal Garsea's eighteenth birthday, it can be deduced that she was born around 16 ABY.
  1411. ↑ A Crash of Fate
  1412. ↑ The Art of Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker states that Aftab Ackbar is nineteen years old during the events of Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to 35 ABY. Therefore, Aftab must have been born around 16 ABY.
  1413. ↑ Allegiance 2
  1414. ↑  Torra Doza in the Databank (backup link)
  1415. ↑ 1415.0 1415.1 Star Wars Resistance — "Rendezvous Point"
  1416. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge to 34 ABY. Jac Lodain was sixteen years old in A Crash of Fate, thus placing his birth in 18 ABY.
  1417. ↑ Skywalker: A Family at War states that the mission to Elphrona took place the year Ben Solo turned fourteen. Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary establishes that Solo was born twenty-nine years prior to the Hosnian Cataclysm, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY. Therefore, the mission took place in 19 ABY.
  1418. ↑ 1418.0 1418.1 1418.2 1418.3 1418.4 1418.5 1418.6 1418.7 1418.8 Resistance Reborn
  1419. ↑ 1419.0 1419.1 1419.2 Join the Resistance
  1420. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary dates mission to Pasaana to 13 years before the the Hosnian Cataclysm. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the the Hosnian Cataclysm to 34 ABY, the event must have taken place in 21 ABY.
  1421. ↑ "Hear Nothing, See Nothing, Say Nothing" — Canto Bight
  1422. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge to 34 ABY. As A Crash of Fate establishes that Shari is thirteen years old, it can be deduced that she was born in 21 ABY.
  1423. ↑ Phasma takes place a few weeks before the beginning of Galaxy's Edge: Black Spire which begins during the events of Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens, which is dated to 34 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. As Egil is killed on Parnassos in a chapter set twelve years before the main story of Phasma, it can be deduced that he was killed around 22 ABY.
  1424. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary dates Poe Dameron leaving the Spice Runners of Kijimi to 11 years before the the Hosnian Cataclysm. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the the Hosnian Cataclysm to 34 ABY, the event must have taken place in 23 ABY.
  1425. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Bloodline to 28 BBY. In Bloodline, Greer Sonnel states that she received her bloodburn diagnosis three years prior, and the short story "Scorched" is set when Sonnel is symptomatic but not yet diagnosed; this places "Scorched" in 25 ABY.
  1426. ↑ Age of Resistance - Rose Tico 1
  1427. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Age of Resistance - Rose Tico 1 as taking place from around 26 ABY to 34 ABY.
  1428. ↑ Star Wars: Scum and Villainy: Case Files on the Galaxy's Most Notorious establishes that the New Republic issued a wanted poster for Rinnrivin Di in the year 8004 C.R.C.. Because Scum and Villainy also states that the escape from Cloud City took place in 7980 C.R.C., and Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates that event to 3 ABY, 8004 C.R.C. converts to 27 ABY.
  1429. ↑ According to Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary, Temmin Wexley joined the New Republic Defense Fleet 7 years before the Starkiller Incident. Since Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the Incident to 34 ABY, Temmin Wexley must have joined the New Republic Defense Fleet in 27 ABY.
  1430. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary dates Poe Dameron entering the New Republic Academy to 7 years before the the Hosnian Cataclysm. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the the Hosnian Cataclysm to 34 ABY, the event must have taken place in 27 ABY.
  1431. ↑ Star Wars: Timelines dates the events of Bloodline to 28 ABY.
  1432. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary dates formation of the Resistance to 5 years before the the Hosnian Cataclysm. As Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the the Hosnian Cataclysm to 34 ABY, the event must have taken place in 29 ABY.
  1433. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that the Jedi Temple of Luke Skywalker was destroyed six years before the Starkiller Incident, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY. Therefore, the destruction must have taken place in 28 ABY.
  1434. ↑ The Rise of Kylo Ren 1
  1435. ↑ 1435.0 1435.1 The Rise of Kylo Ren 4
  1436. ↑ The Rise of Kylo Ren 3
  1437. ↑ 1437.00 1437.01 1437.02 1437.03 1437.04 1437.05 1437.06 1437.07 1437.08 1437.09 1437.10 1437.11 Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi
  1438. ↑ 1438.0 1438.1 1438.2 1438.3 1438.4 Happenings stated to have occurred five years before the Starkiller Incident in Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary must have occurred in 29 ABY. This is as Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates the Incident to 34 ABY.
  1439. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that BB-8 was assigned to Poe Dameron four years before the Starkiller Incident, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY. Therefore, BB-8 was assigned to Dameron in 30 ABY.
  1440. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary establishes Brendol Hux's killing as having occurred four years before the Starkiller Incident, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY. Hence, Hux died in 30 ABY.
  1441. ↑ "The Crimson Corsair and the Lost Treasure of Count Dooku"
  1442. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that BB-8 and Poe Dameron joined the Resistance three years prior to the Starkiller Incident, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY. Therefore, BB-8 and Dameron joined the Resistance in 31 ABY.
  1443. ↑ "Flight of the Falcon, Part 5: Grand Theft Falcon" takes place after the events of "The Crimson Corsair and the Lost Treasure of Count Dooku," as Kix is indirectly mentioned, but before Life Day 1, which Star Wars: Timelines dates to c. 31 ABY. "The Crimson Corsair and the Lost Treasure of Count Dooku" refers to the prologue as taking place 50 years prior to the main story. The prologue takes place in between "The Wrong Jedi" and Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith, both of which are placed in 19 ABY by Star Wars: Galactic Atlas. Therefore the prologue takes place in 19 BBY, and the main story takes place around 31 ABY. Therefore, "Flight of the Falcon, Part 5: Grand Theft Falcon" also takes place around 31 ABY.
  1444. ↑ Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary states that Poe Dameron began leading Black Squadron on missions two years before the Starkiller Incident, which Star Wars: Galactic Atlas dates to 34 ABY. The mission to Ovanis was the first mission that Black Squadron undertook as stated in Poe Dameron 1. Therefore, the mission must have occurred in 32 ABY.
